He tried not to think about his aunt and uncle; how they treated him was truly diabolical and Harry had to wonder if Dumbledore had something to do with the abuse never being notice by the authorities or Madam Pomfrey. ØThese payments have been stopped correct?Æ Harry asked, once he had finally calmed down. Ragnok nodded, shocked at the amount of control Harry had gained in such a short amount of time. ØIf you would like we should be able to reclaim some of what was taken and impose sanctions until their debts to you have been cleared,Æ Ragnok said. ØIf this can be done without their knowledge, do so. If not, then leave it, I do not what them knowing I am aware of their deceptions just yet,Æ Harry said. ØThe sanctions can be put in place without their knowledge,Æ Ragnok said. ØThen do so,Æ Harry replied. After taking a moment to gather his thoughts, which had been scattered by his anger, Harry remembered his other area of business. ØI would like to visit some of my vaults today, to see what they contain.Æ ØA wise decision,Æ Ragnok said. ØIf I may, I would recommend going to the Mortemis vault; it should hold information very important to you.Æ Nodding his head in understanding Harry thanked Ragnok and left the office, going with a teller who seemed to materialise out of the shadows to the vault carts. ØVault number one,Æ the teller intoned when Harry stated his destination. A flicker of thought had Harry pausing; the lower the vault numbers usually meant older vaults, but the sudden jerk of the cart sent his apprehension of visiting the oldest vault in Gringotts flying in the wind. ~ Chapter End Notes Thanks to my Beta miisticalwrites Hope you're enjoying the story so far! Remember to leave a comment & Kudos if you've liked it ~ Annie Chapter4:The masterof3 Chapter Notes See the end of the chapter for notes Previously ØVault number one,Æ the teller intoned when Harry stated his destination. A flicker of thought had Harry pausing; the lower the vault numbers usually meant older vaults, but the sudden jerk of the cart sent his apprehension of visiting the oldest vault in Gringotts flying in the wind. ~ After what seemed like forever, the cart finally came to a stop and Harry slowly exited from it. They were in the deepest part of the bank, far below anywhere Harry had ever been before; above them, Harry could hear other carts moving far in the distance. Shaking himself to try and gather his bearings, Harry approached the doors to the vault. After a moment of consideration, Harry placed his hand on the doors and felt his magic surge before they unlocked with a soft click. Harry had expected an eerie creak, but not a sound came as he pushed open the vault doors. Entering the nearly empty cavern, Harry noted that the vault was surprisingly clear and well lit. Approaching the table in the middle of the room, Harry's eyes were drawn to the book stand which displayed a very old looking, small book. Next to it, Harry noted something which made his eyes widen in shock: his invisibility cloak was on the table neatly folded, as well as Dumbledore's old wand. Harry had been wondering where the wand had vanished to, but in all the excitement the thought had been easily buried. His cloak too had disappeared, but Harry had assumed it would turn up; apparently, he was right. The next object was another thing that he recognised, having seen it only days before: it was the box for the Slytherin family ring. Opening the box, just to confirm it, Harry noted with little surprise that it was empty, and wondered if, when he died, it would appear here or in the Slytherin vault. Looking at the three items, Harry realised that, apparently, the story of the three brothers was true. Picking up the wand, Harry felt his magic surge yet again; the feeling was similar to how he had felt when he had first picked up this holly wand at eleven, but it was more natural. Before, if asked, he would have said that his holly wand was the perfect fit for him, but now having felt his magic blend with this wand he realised how untrue that statement would have been. Summoning a spare wand holder, Harry placed the wand in it before turning to his cloak. Shrugging his shoulders, Harry put it on over his robe. Finally done, Harry turned to the book and picked it up. Opening it, Harry began to read with fascination. ~ Congratulations, dear reader, on doing the impossible, If you are reading this, then you have done what thousands before you have tried to do but failed. You have collected and mastered the three Deathly Hallows. Items forged by Death himself. This, I know, means little to you at present, but once you truly comprehend the notion of how much power you now wield, you will understand what an honour and blessing this is to have befallen you. There are however a few limitations. But, they are best explained by the maker Himself. To summon Him, all you must do is try. You will find He always hears your call, He just doesnít always answer. Hello, farewell, and good luck. ~ Closing the book, Harry stood blankly for a moment, confused and thoroughly baffled. ØHow the hell do I try to summon Death?Æ he mumbled, running through countless ideas and dismissing them completely before they fully formed. ØYou must merely wish for my presence, Master,Æ spoke a deep voice from in front of Harry. Looking up sharply, Harry let out a high squeak that he didnít think would be biologically possible under normal circumstances. The being before him, as it was no man or creature, was easily 7ft tall and appeared to be draped in shadows. ØY-you-you're Death?Æ Harry stuttered out eventually, once his voice decided to return to him. ØI am known as many things but yes, I answer to Death,Æ said the voice. ØOh, okay,Æ Harry said lightly, feeling suddenly very light headed. Standing in silence, which seemed to stretch for hours rather than seconds, Harry tried not to squirm. Although he could make out no eyes under the shadowed cloak, Harry could almost feel the beingís gaze raking over him. Eventually, Harry dared to open his mouth again. ØThe book said I had mastered you,Æ he said, his words sounding stupid to him now he had been in Deathís presence; nobody could master such a being. ØYou have collected all three of my hollows, mastered them,Æ Death said. Harry nodded before speaking, ØAnd what does that mean for me?Æ ØIt means that I will help you, teach you, and aid you in whatever you wish. You are my master, but I am no slave. I will keep you alive until such a time you no longer desire to be; you will be my companion,Æ Death said, His voice giving no indication about what He thought of this. Harry considered Deathís words, and after a few moments he asked the question that had been haunting him. ØCould you bring somebody back to life? My soulmateîÆ he said, breaking off his softly spoken words. He wanted the bond complete as he didnít want to die, but bringing Voldemort back? Harry was torn. Death was silent for a moment. ØEven I have limits,Æ he said, Øthough it would be possible, bringing somebody back from the dead is never without cost. Once somebody is dead, it is better for them to stay that way. To take them from their peace is not something that should be done.Æ Harry let out a breath of air that he didnít even realise he had been holding and sagged at His words. ØOh,Æ he said, his disappointment tangible. Even though he was torn to hear it was better left impossible, it still made the ache in his chest grow. Silence fell then before Death spoke again, ØHowever, I can offer you something else.Æ Harry perked up at this, wondering what Death would have to say. ØI can feel your broken bond and it sings out in agony. I can send you back to a time it was not broken,Æ Death said. Harry stilled at his words and thought about the implications. ØYou can send me back in time?Æ he asked, just to clarify. ØYes,Æ Death verified. Harry thought about the possibilities and asked, ØIf I was sent back, would I still be me?Æ ØYou would merge with your younger self unless you went back to a time before you existed. Though not impossible, I do not recommend that path. It opens up endless loopholes and paradoxes that give me headaches to fix,Æ Death said. Harry almost laughed at how put out Death managed to sound, while still being utterly terrifying. ØBy merge, do you mean that I would take over my younger self? Would I keep my memories?Æ ØIndeed,Æ Death confirmed. Harry nodded as he thought, an idea blooming in his mind. ØWhat about my magic? It was bound when I was younger, if I returned would it be bound still?Æ he asked. ØI would never allow such a thing,Æ Death said scathingly. ØSo I would return how I am now basically, but in my younger body?Æ ØYes,Æ Death said. ØWhat about the Hallows? Back then theyíre still around, but I donít have them, or at least I donít have the wand or the stone. Would I still be your master?Æ Harry asked. ØYou would still be my master unless somebody else gains all three of them. However, you will obviously not have access to the wand or the stone. I am unsure, but I would think the wand would not work against you - it would know you are its true master. I would suggest you work on collecting all three once you have returned,Æ Death said. Harry thought about this and nodded his head. ØI donít have to take you up on this right away, do I? Itís not like a onetime deal?Æ Harry questioned. ØNo. You may summon me and ask for this at any point, though I am busy, so I may not always answer you the moment you call,Æ Death said. Harry nodded. ØI think that's all I needed to know. Thank you,Æ he said before awkwardly adding, ØErm, you can go now?Æ Death seemed to chuckle slightly, alarming Harry, before He simple vanished back into the shadows. Harry stood for a moment, standing in shock, before the realisation of how surreal his life was hit him. Laughing manically, Harry barely kept himself from falling to the floor in his state. Finally, after gaining a small semblance of control, Harry stood and straightened himself out. Deciding it was best not to wear his invisibility cloak, Harry focused on banishing it to his room in Grimmauld Place, trusting his magic to send it there. Finally. he returned to the cart outside and asked to be taken back to the surface, ignoring the goblinís curious look. Instead of going back into the main bank, Harry instead followed the paths towards Ragnokís office. Nodding to the guards, Harry knocked on the door and waited to be called in. ØI see you have returned,Æ Ragnok said once Harry had seated himself. ØDid you know?Æ Harry questioned. Ragnok withheld his smirk. ØI know many things, but even I cannot take a guess as to what you mean.Æ Harry narrowed his eyes and clarified, ØDid you know what being the Master of Death meant for me?Æ Ragnok sighed, ØIt has never been done before, so I did not know for certain, but I had an idea.Æ Silence fell after he spoke. ØDeath said I could go back,Æ Harry said after a few moments. ØGo back?Æ Ragnok questioned. ØI would be able to relive my life, taking the place of my younger self. I would have my memories and my powers; I would literally have a chance to do it all again, but better,Æ Harry said ØI see,Æ Ragnok said. ØI take it you are considering this?Æ ØI think I want to do it,Æ Harry confided, Øthe only thing that is currently keeping me going is the thought of revenge, and even that doesnít block out my longing for long. Itís always there. If I went back, I could change everything.Æ ØYou would side with Voldemort?Æ Ragnok asked, his voice holding no judgement. ØNo - yes - I donít know,Æ Harry admitted. ØI would try to fix him. His goals before they became so twisted with insanity were good; I refuse to believe the one person magic fated to be perfect for me was an insane snake-faced megalomaniac.Æ Ragnok smiled at that. ØGood.Æ Harry was shocked. ØYou want me to side with Voldemort?Æ he asked. ØNo, I want you to fix Voldemort, to build on what he started when he first began. I want you, Harry Potter, to change the world,Æ Ragnok said. Harry looked at Ragnok who, in just a few short days, had come to be a true friend. ØYou know if I go back, you wonít remember me,Æ he said. Ragnok smirked at that. ØI wouldnít be so sure, Harry.Æ ØWhat do you mean?Æ ØGoblin magic is different from that which wizardís wield,Æ Ragnok said. ØWhen you return, you may find yourself surprised with just how much we remember.Æ Harry smirked at this, glad he would have a confidant when he went back. ØYou have made your decision then,Æ Ragnok said, seeing the look on Harry's face. ØYes, I think so. There is just one thing I need to do first.Æ After leaving the bank, Harry made his way to Grimmauld Place. One of the only regrets he had about his decision to go back was Kreacher. He had really bonded to the elf in the weeks he had been at Grimmauld, however he knew that if all went to plan he would be seeing the elf a lot earlier than he had in this life when he returned. Going up to his room, Harry opened his trunk and summoned the object he had come home for: the Marauderís Map. Opening it up, he placed his wand again it and intoned, ØI solemnly swear that Iím up to no good.Æ Watching as the map came to life, Harry looked to the headmasterís office and saw it empty; searching the surrounding areas, Harry saw that, although not completely empty, the school was practically deserted. Putting on his invisibility cloak and tucking the map into his pocket, Harry returned to the receiving room and apparated to Hogsmeade. The walk to the castle filled Harry with regret; there were vast areas of destruction and Harry could see that it would take time to fix the damage. Hopefully things would be better after he returned; he never wanted to see Hogwarts in such a state ever again. Even if he hated that his memories of the place were tainted by Dumbledore and his manipulations, Harry just couldnít bring himself to hate the castle and all it represented for him. Once he got close enough to enter the castleís wards, Harry felt a shift in his magic. Breaching them, Harry had a baffling sensation of peace, almost like he was coming home. Making his way quickly towards the office, Harry withdrew the map. Even under his cloak with scent blocking and silencing charms, Harry didnít want to risk running into anyone. Seeing that the way was clear, Harry kept his pace and finally made it the gargoyle guardian blocking the way to the headmasterís - well, he supposed it would be headmistressí now .office. Harry considered his next move before a thought came to him. Remembering what he had once read in a book years ago, Harry decided to try something. He said, ØI, Harrison James Potter-Black, Lord of Slytherin and Gryffindor, do demand entrance to this room.Æ At first he thought it had not worked, as nothing seemed to happen, but slowly the gargoyle moved. Quickly casting a secrecy charm on the guardian so it could not be questioned about how he gained entrance should Harry be found, Harry continued his journey and climbed the stairs to the office. Standing, Harry let out a breath. Withdrawing his new wand Harry set about casting warning wards, as well as silencing charms and secrecy charms. Once he felt them settle, Harry removed his cloak, causing the many paintings of previous headmasters and headmistresses to splutter in shock. ØYou, boy, how did you get in here?Æ one male portrait asked. Harry believed him to be Walter Gagsworth, the headmaster from 1640 to 1679. Harry, however, chose to ignore him as he focused on the portrait of the man he had once worshiped and loved like a father. ØHarry?Æ the portrait of Albus Dumbledore asked, his voice sounding as mild and grandfatherly as ever. Unable to control himself at the benign tone, Harry felt the room shake slightly as his magic tried to rip free. The portrait of Dumbledore seemed shocked for a moment, then those twinkling eyes narrowed. ØI see you have found out about yourself then,Æ he said sagely, as though he had not manipulated Harry's entire life. Harry gritted his teeth. ØIndeed,Æ Harry said in an icy tone that promised pain. At this point the other portraits went silent, fearing the look in the eyes of the dark hair teen before them. The portrait of Albus sighed, ØIt really was for the greater good.Æ Harry, having had enough, barely kept his voice from breaking the sound barrier as he bellowed, ØThe greater good?! How is stealing from me, drugging me, and manipulating me for the greater good?!Æ ØIt was better this way,Æ Albus said before being cut off. Harry was not done ranting. ØTell me, how is blocking my soul bond for the greater good? Tell me that, Albus!Æ Harry demanded. Hearing Harry's shout, the other portraits seemed to forgot their fear and let out cries of shock and horror. ØYou didnít!Æ ØHow could you?!Æ ØWho do you think you are?!Æ And to Harry's great amusement, the portrait of Phineas Nigellus Black began ranting and swearing in such a creative and colourful way that Harry was sure even a seasoned sailor would have blushed. Eventually Albus was forced to shout, ØEnough!Æ Once the room had quieted down, although mutinous mutterings could still be heard, Dumbledore continued to speak, staring at Harry with a look of betrayal that made him want to laugh. ØYou needed to destroy Voldemort, you needed to support the light and follow in your parentís footsteps. You know the prophecy, it had to be you.Æ Harry growled and took a step forward. ØFollow in their footsteps. Die, you mean?Æ Harry said in a deadly tone. With narrowed eyes the portrait of Dumbledore seemed to puff himself up before speaking, ØFor the greater good, my boy, some sacrifices must be made.Æ ØHow is my death for the greater good?!Æ Harry screamed, before he took a breath. ØAnd donít you even think to use my parents against me! I doubt my parents would have wanted me to sacrifice my life after they lost their own lives saving mine!Æ ØThey believed in the light! The prophecy!Æ ØThe prophecy,Æ Harry spit the word out, Øitís always been about the prophecy. But tell me Albus, was there ever a prophecy, or did you just make that up to fit your desires?Æ Harry asked. Dumbledore seemed to vibrate in anger in his portrait, before he once again put on his sagely persona. ØHow could you ask me that, my boy? Your parents died for that prophecy.Æ ØThey died for me!Æ Harry shouted, ØThey died sacrificing their lives so that I could live mine!Æ ØThey were great people, true light followers! They knew that some sacrifices had to be made. Just look at you now, Harry, you practically ooze darkness - you're turning into Tom!Æ Dumbledore said, his voice comically rising in pretend concern. ØI'm exactly who I've always been Albus, just without your manipulations,Æ Harry said. ØThis is why you had to die, Harry. You're just like Tom, just as dark,Æ Albus finally said, losing his grandfatherly persona. ØDark does not mean evil!Æ Phineas Blackís portrait shouted, interrupting the conversation with the support of the other former headmasters and headmistresses. ØHeís right,Æ Harry said, drawing the conversation back to himself. ØDark does not mean evil. Something you have never understood.Æ Albus huffed arrogantly, ØYou think to tell me anything? I am Albus Percival Wulfric Brian Dumbledore, and you are just a boy!Æ Harry shook his head condescendingly. ØNo, youíre the portrait of a dead man,Æ Harry said icily. Albus huffed again, ØAnd youíre a dead man walking, Harry. Or did you forget? You killed your soulmate.Æ Albusí taunt made Harryís heart twinge momentarily and once again the other portraits exploded in anger. Harry, however, started to chuckle softly after the moment passed, eventually gaining the attention of the room when his guffaws became loud enough to notice. ØYou think this is funny?!Æ Albus demanded, wanting to know why Harry was not crying because of his words. Harry eventually calmed down and shot Albus a hard look. ØYes,Æ Harry said to everybodyís confusion. ØThere is no bringing him back this time, Harry. I made sure of that,Æ Albus taunted. ØYou will die just like him.Æ Harry, however, just smirked. ØAh, that, Albus, is where you are wrong,Æ he said. The portrait of Albus jumped to his feet and pushed his face closer to the frame. ØWhat have you done?!Æ he demanded. ØTell me Albus, what do you know about the Deathly Hallows?Æ Harry taunted. The portrait seemed to lose colour, something Harry didnít know was possible. ØImpossible,Æ he whispered. ØOh no, dear Albus, imagine my surprise when I did my blood inheritance test and I found myself to be the lord of five families and the Master of Death,Æ Harry said. The other portraits all froze at his words, shocked whispers running rampant. ØSo you're going to bring him back.Æ Albus said with disgust, looking at Harry with pure unadulterated hatred. Harry laughed again. ØOh no, whereís the fun in that? Even Death doesnít like to bring back those who have passed. No, what Iím planning is much more fun, dear Albie,Æ Harry continued to taunt. ØWhat, you demented boy?!Æ ØWhy, I'm going to go back,Æ Harry said. His words caused all the noise to stop before finally soft chuckling then full blown belly laughs were heard. Unsurprisingly, it was once again Phineas Blackís portrait. ØYou canít!Æ Albus screamed. ØOh, but I can, and it will be glorious,Æ Harry replied, his dark smirk promising revenge. ØI will have my revenge and save Tom,Æ he promised. ØHe canít be saved!Æ Albus tried to reason in vain, hoping to regain control of Harry. ØI refuse to believe that!Æ Harry replied, before his warning wards gave a twinge. Pulling out the map Harry saw McGonagall walking toward the office. With a smile Harry tucked it away and faced the portrait of Albus for the last time. ØIt seems our time together has come to an end, so I will just say this: I cannot wait to see you again, professor.Æ With that Harry turned and made his way to the door, ignoring Dumbledoreís cries and threats and Phineasí laughs. Harry had to let out a small chuckle as he heard Black shout, Øplease come and visit us when you return boy, I simply cannot wait to see who you become,Æ Exiting the office in time, Harry caught McGonagall just as she turned the corner. ØMr. Potter, what are you doing here?Æ she asked. Harry schooled his features and smiled at her. ØI came to talk to you actually, professor,Æ he said. ØWell then, why donít we take this up to Albusí office?Æ she asked. ØNo,Æ Harry said quickly. ØI mean, I would like to see the progress that's been made fixing the damage, if that's alright with you.Æ McGonagall nodded after looking at Harry with surprise at his quick rejection. ØThatís fine with me, Mr. Potter,Æ she said, leading the way away from the office. ØNow, what is it you wished to speak to me about?Æ she asked. ØI was wondering if you had heard about my decision to not return to Hogwarts in September?Æ he asked, gaining pursed lips from McGonagall before she replied. ØI had heard, and I cannot say I blame you, but I do wish you would reconsider,Æ she said. Harry felt a moment of anger well up in him as he wondered if she was in on Dumbledore's schemes. She was his deputy after all, and they always seemed close. ØOh professor?Æ Harry said hoping for her to elaborate so he could watch her response. ØI cannot even image all you had to go through in order to defeat Voldemort, but I will be honest with you Harry; you have a unique ability. You inspire those around you and I had hoped that, if you returned, maybe others would as well. However, I do understand why you would choose not to. I suppose it would seem silly to return to being a student when you have gone through so much,Æ she said. Harry's relief at her words was immense. Although it was not definite proof, Harry felt he could trust that she was not in on Dumbledore's schemes. They continued to make small talk as they view the grounds. The castleís damage was still extensive, but Minerva, as she now insisted on being called as she was no longer his teacher, assured Harry the school would be operational by September. Walking him to the gates so he could apparate, Minerva smiled at Harry. ØYou will visit?Æ she asked, although it sounded more like a demand. Harry smiled. ØI will be back before you know it,Æ Harry promised. ~ Grimmauld Place, 9th June 1998 Returning to Grimmauld Place for the last time, in this lifetime at least, had Harry looking about in nostalgia. He was ready now, he thought. Sitting down at the kitchen table, Harry, with a cup of tea Kreacher made, thought about Death to summon Him. After a few minutes, Harry noticed the shadows growing before a voice asked, ØYouíre ready now?Æ Harry swallowed the last of his tea before he turned and looked at Death. ØYes,Æ he said. ØI want to go back to just before I received my letter to Hogwarts.Æ Death seemed to nod, ØVery well.Æ Harry sat, wondering how this would all work, before suddenly he felt like his body was being forced through a blender. If he thought portkeys were bad, they were nothing compared to this; Harry felt like his very atoms were being ripped apart and rearranged. His last thought was that he was probably correct. ~ Chapter End Notes The scene between Harry and Albus' portrait is Literally my favourite to date. I loved writing it. Hope you enjoy, if so remember to comment & leave kudos ~ Annie Chapter5:Hello old world, I'm back Chapter Notes See the end of the chapter for notes Previously Harry sat wondering how this would all work, before suddenly he felt like his body was being forced through a blender. If he thought portkeys were bad, they were nothing compared to this. Harry felt like his very atoms were being ripped apart and rearranged. His last thought was that he was probably correct. ~ 4 Privet Drive, July 31st 1991 Harry sat up with a groan. His head was killing him and he felt like he had bruised every inch of his skin, twice. Looking around, Harry tried to make sense of his surroundings when he suddenly remembered what he had done. With a maniacal cackle, Harry looked around the dusty confines of his cupboard; it was as bad as he remembered he thought to himself. ØIt worked,Æ he whispered to himself. ØDid you doubt me?Æ asked Deathís deep, rumbling voice, making Harry jump as he had thought he was alone. ØNo, itís just shocking really being here.Æ Harry said, his prepubescent voice shocking him as he was used to his more mature rumble. Death seemed to accept that. ØI will leave you here,Æ he said, Øtry not to call on me unless you have dire need as I am not an errand boy for your amusement. I will check in from time to time.Æ Harry nodded his head, still lost in the surrealism of the situation. ØVery well,Æ Death said before promptly vanishing. Harry sat for a moment gathering his thoughts before deciding to test his magic; it wasnít that he didnít trust Death, it was just that he wanted to verify. Focusing on his magic, Harry held out his hand. With one thought of the lighting charm, Harry was happy to see his cupboard light up. Smiling, Harry's thoughts soon turned sour when he heard movement. In his joy he had forgotten about the muggles. As Vernon moved above him, coming down the stairs, the ceiling let out puffs of dust making Harry cough and splutter. Harry's eyes narrowed as he remembered everything he had been forced to live through at the hands of his so called caretakers. He would be damned if he would sit back and take it again. When his cupboard door was finally yanked open and Vernonís bellowing voice rang out, Harry had already decided what to do. ØYou had better be awake, boy! I want breakfast on the table in ten minutes or so help me God, you will rue the day your good for nothing mother whelped you!Æ Vernon roared, his face an unhealthy puce colour. Harry stood gracefully and moved out of the cupboard. He didnít know if the blood glamour had been removed, but he guessed it had as Vernon soon started spluttering at Harry. ØWhat did you do you little freak?!Æ he demanded, advancing on Harry with his fist raised. Normally this would have caused Harry to drop and cower in fear, but now Harry just smirked and tightened his fist, watching in fascination as Vernon froze, unable to move. The spell was one he had read about in the Black library - it would bind the victim and the more they struggled the tighter the bindings got. It was based on devilís snare, interestingly enough. Harry took a step forward and, with flourish, dropped his fist down, causing Vernon to drop painfully to his knees. ØNow uncle, letís not do anything weíll regret now,Æ Harry said, his voice mockingly soft. ØF-freak! Release me at once!Æ Vernon ordered, his voice not as loud as it had once been and his face taking on a distinctly fearful look. Harry watched in glee as he struggled, knowing he was causing himself further pain; and although he didnít like torture, he was suddenly gaining an understanding for it. Sighing in mock sympathy, Harry took another step forward so that now he was only a foot away from Vernon before he spoke again, ØI think itís time we established some new rules. Donít you agree?Æ ØS-stop it,Æ Vernon said. ØI asked you a question, uncle,Æ Harry said. ØWhat is it you want, boy?Æ Vernon spat out eventually. ØI want to be left alone,Æ Harry said. ØNo cooking, no cleaning, you will pretend like I donít exist and I will do the same for you and your family.Æ ØFine,Æ Vernon growled the word out. Stepping back, Harry released the spell. Vernon sank even further down before surging forward. Expecting this, Harry raised his hand and stupefied him before turning his eyes to his real audience. Petunia stood at the top of the stairs white faced and shaking, her hand covering her mouth in terror. ØLike I said, I no longer exist,Æ Harry said to his aunt, who quickly nodded her head. ØIs-is he d-dead?Æ Petunia whispered. Harry rolled his eyes. ØNo, not this time. However, I do think it would be wise of you to explain how our arrangement works, to both him and Dudley. It would be a shame if any unpleasant accidents happened because they forgot the new rules.Æ Petunia nodded again, her beady eyes darting between Harry and Vernonís prone form. ØYes, yes of course,Æ she said, practically vibrating in her fear. Happy, Harry turned and made his way towards the kitchen to make himself something to eat before he stopped just shy of the door. ØOh and aunty, I will be receiving my letter today, you know the one. So donít expect me back until tonight.Æ Harry's words made her pale even further and for a moment he thought she would actually faint. Bemused, Harry continued on his way before he froze and practically ran back to the hallway. Grabbing Petunia by the arm, Harry looked into her eyes and placed a secrecy jinx. She would be unable to tell anybody what she now knew apart from whoever Harry wished. This way she would be unable to tell Dumbledore or anybody else if they ever came sniffing about for answers - it would even work against Legilimens. He would do the same to Vernon once he woke up. Happy with what he had accomplished in just a few short minutes, Harry returned to his previous task and went about cooking his breakfast. Just as he was about to plate up, Dudley came running into the kitchen. Seeing Harry, he turned and made his way towards him. Sighing, as it was clear Petunia hadnít had a chance to talk to her oversized son, Harry wondered for a fleeting second if he could get away with just killing them. Harry didnít really condone casual murder, but when he thought about everything his family had done to him, every beating and nights left starving, he really considered just ending them now. Harry couldnít believe how passive the potions had made him, how they had made him ignore the beatings and be forgiving about the abuse. ØStop,Æ Harry said, the chilling tone making Dudley do just that, almost comically freezing. However, like his father, Dudley would never be considered smart. After a second, he narrowed his eyes and took a step forward. The look on his face, Harry decided, was supposed to be menacing, but looked vaguely like he was constipated. Turning back to the stove, Harry turned off the hob before turning back to Dudley. It was clear the boy was confused by Harry's actions and was wavering on what to do. Luckily for him, he picked the smarter option. ØMum!Æ he wailed, smirking when he thought about the trouble Harry would be in. Petunia appeared like a bat out of hell, almost panting in her effort to get to her son. Harry watched in amusement as she looked between them and grabbed Dudley. ØWhat is it, diddums?Æ she asked shakily. ØThe freak was cooking,Æ he said, sending a glare at Harry, who at this point had his omelette on his plate and was considering if he wanted to add sauce. Petunia squeaked in terror and looked at Harry, who just raised an eyebrow, smirking, before taking a bite of his omelette. ØDiddy-kins, I need you to come with me. We need to have a chat about how we treat Harry,Æ Petunia said, practically dragging him from the room as he protested. With that over, Harry made his way upstairs and into Dudleyís second bedroom. Looking around in disgust at the broken toys and rubbish cluttering the floor, Harry waved his hands and watched as it was banished. Next, he used a liberal about of scouring and repairing charms on the walls, floors, and door. Looking at the furniture, Harry attempted a few simple transfigurations, not anything too fancy as, even with his power increase, he was still working without a wand and with his limited knowledge of transfiguration. Whilst he had been practically living in the Black library, he hadnít studied up on it, focusing more on darker arts and soul magic in the hope of finding an answer to his problems. However, even with his limited knowledge, Harry was able to create a comfortable looking bed, with matching side table, desk, and wardrobe as well as a desk chair. Happy with the sparsely furnished room, Harry turned to leave, adding a muggle repelling ward to the doorway as an afterthought. Entering the living room, Harry noted how the Dursleys sat together and watched him warily. It would seem that Petunia had finally got around to warning Dudley. Making eye contact with Vernon and then Dudley Harry set the jinx, happy when he felt his magic settle, letting him know it was in place. ØI will be moving into Dudleyís second bedroom,Æ Harry stated, watching in fascination as both Dudley and Vernon recoiled as though struck. Dudley let out a horrified squawk, but before he could protest Petunia nodded. ØYes, of course,Æ she said quickly, sending both the male Dursleys a fearful look. ~ Waiting, Harry was almost bouncing with excitement when he heard the mail come. Walking to the door, Harry easily saw the letter that was for him, the parchment standing out against the muggle envelopes. Picking the letter up, Harry immediately felt the charms and compulsions on it try to take effect and smirked when his magic reacted and tore them apart. Taking the letter upstairs, Harry opened it after reading the address, gleefully ripping apart the purple wax seal bearing the schoolís coat of arms. ~ Mr. H. Potter The Cupboard under the Stairs 4 Privet Drive Little Whinging Surrey ~ HOGWARTS SCHOOL of WITCHCRAFT and WIZARDRY Headmaster: Albus Dumbledore (Order of Merlin, First Class, Grand Sorc., Chf. Warlock, Supreme Mugwump, International Confed. of Wizards) Dear Mr. Potter, We are pleased to inform you that you have been accepted at Hogwarts School of Witchcraft and Wizardry. Please find enclosed a list of all necessary books and equipment. Term begins on September 1. We await your owl by no later than July 31. Yours sincerely, Minerva McGonagall, Deputy Headmistress ~ Equipment list First-year students will require: Uniform: Three Sets of Plain Work Robes (black) One Plain Pointed Hat (black) One Pair of Protective Gloves (dragon hide or similar) One Winter Cloak (black, silver fastenings) Please note that all student's clothes should carry name-tags at all times. Books: The Standard Book of Spells, Grade 1 by Miranda Goshawk A History of Magic by Bathilda Bagshot Magical Theory by Adalbert Waffling A Beginner's Guide to Transfiguration by Emeric Switch One Thousand Magical Herbs and Fungi by Phyllida Spore Magical Drafts and Potions by Arsenius Jigger Fantastic Beasts and Where to Find Them by Newt Scamander The Dark Forces: A Guide to Self-Protection by Quentin Trimble Other Equipment: 1 Wand 1 Cauldron (pewter, standard size 2) 1 set of glass or crystal phials 1 telescope 1 set of brass scales Students may also bring an Owl, Cat, or Toad. PARENTS ARE REMINDED THAT FIRST YEARS ARE NOT ALLOWED THEIR OWN BROOMSTICKS ~ Reading the list, Harry thought about Hedwig for a moment; he could buy her again in this life he thought happily, pleading that his first friend would hopefully be with him again soon. Summoning a biro and some paper, Harry quickly penned his reply to McGonagall. It wouldnít do for him to use ink and a quill and gain any unwanted attention. ~ Professor McGonagall, Thank you for the letter, I accept my place at Hogwarts school as a first year student. Gratefully, Harry J Potter ~ With that done, Harry spent a moment and concentrated on his clothes, transfiguring his oversized t-shirt into a short day robe in a dark blue colour with pale green accents and his jeans into a pair of grey trousers. Harry then focused on his shoes, looking in disgust at the barely held together trainers, and transfigured them into shiny black ankle boots. Happy that his clothing would last until he managed to buy himself a proper wardrobe, Harry made his way down the stairs, glad to see the Dursleys going out of their way to ignore him. Even Vernon, who clenched his teeth and shook as he stared at Harry, but he still let him pass without challenge. Walking out of the door, Harry took a moment to observe the wards surrounding the property and felt his anger towards Dumbledore surge. There were no blood wards surrounding the house. The wards consisted of a simple protection ward, an anti-apparition ward, and an owl redirection ward. Luckily the old man hadnít thought to place any magical detection wards, or Harry would have some explaining to do. åI will make him payí, Harry vowed to himself. With a growl, Harry continued his journey and passed through the wards fully before spinning on his heel and apperating to just outside of the Leaky Cauldron. Entering the pub, Harry made sure to walk quickly and keep his scar covered. The Wizarding world hadnít yet met him and didnít know what he looked like, therefore he hoped that dressed in wizarding fashion and without Dumbledore's blasted blood glamour, he would go undetected. Going up to the bar, Harry gained Tomís attention. ØExcuse me, could you open the passage to Diagon Alley please?Æ he asked. ØCertainly, young sir,Æ Tom said. Nodding his in thanks as the entry way opened, Harry slipped into the bustling crowd. He remembered only days ago reminiscing about the magic of Diagon Alley, but now he felt he had exaggerated it as he struggled to get through the crowd without knocking into people. Like before, the crowd parted easily for him subconsciously but still, with the number of people milling about, the journey through wasnít without difficulty. Finally reaching the other end of the alley, Harry slipped into Gringotts, giving the guards a nod in greeting and happy to see he got one in return. After waiting in the small queue, Harry finally reached a free teller. Barely able to see up over the desk, Harry nodded in greeting and spoke, ØGreetings, master goblin. I wish to enquire about the state of my familyís estate and my inheritance.Æ Nodding, the goblin stood. ØGreetings, Mr. Potter, we at Gringotts welcome you on this day. If you would follow me, I will take you to Chief Ragnok, who is personally overseeing your accounts.Æ Harry let out a breath in relief when he heard this and hoped that Ragnok hadnít been wrong when he assured Harry he would remember. Taking the familiar route to Ragnokís office, Harry greeted the guards and bid his escort goodbye before entering the office. Seeing Ragnok behind his desk, Harry approached before stopping to glare at the chair he usually sat in. Although he could get into it, it wouldnít be easy as his eleven year old body was disturbingly small. Ragnok, who was watching, chuckled at seeing his friend, although he hadnít met him yet officially, struggle with the limitations of his new body. Harry, giving up on trying to sit down, simply focused his magic on the chair, using a slight shrinking charm. Harry watched happily as the chair reduced enough in size that he would be comfortable yet still able to see over the desk. Once he was settled, he looked up to see Ragnok smirking at him. ØIt is good to see you again, my friend,Æ the goblin said, Øalthough this is our first time meeting for me.Æ Harry let out a breath. ØI am glad to see you again as well,Æ he said. ØI take it you are here to withdraw money from your vaults?Æ Ragnok asked. ØPlease,Æ Harry said, before he added, Øwould it also be possible to make it so that only I can take money from my accounts?Æ ØSadly, you cannot ban Dumbledore from your vaults as he is your magical guardian, however you can make it so that only he can access your vaults and only in person,Æ Ragnok said ØPlease do, and could you possibly recall all of my vault keys?Æ Harry asked, remembering that Hagrid had one in his last life. ØRecalling your keys is easy, just say your full name and that you wish to recall your keys belonging to your name and they will appear,Æ Ragnok instructed. ØI, Harrison James Potter-Black, recall any and all vault keys belonging to my name.Æ Within seconds several keys appeared, shocking Harry. ØWhat do I do with them now?Æ Harry asked. ØThey will be disposed of for you if you wish,Æ Ragnok said. At Harryís nod he preceded, flicking his wrist so the keys were banished. ØWithout a key nobody else apart from Dumbledore can gain access to my vaults, right?Æ Harry checked. ØCorrect. Even with a written statement from Dumbledore, they would be unable to get in,Æ Ragnok confirmed. ØCan you make it so no new keys are given out? Perhaps, lie to Dumbledore, saying that itís a new policy or something? Maybe say that they can only be given out with the family or heirs permission?Æ Harry asked Ragnok considered the request before giving a sharp nod. ØIt can be done,Æ he said. ØThank you,Æ Harry said, before he frowned. ØI need to get into my trust vault, but I was wondering if there was anything other than a key that I could use,Æ Harry said. ØWearing the heir ring of your house will grant you access to your vault. As like a lordship ring, an heir ring can only be worn by the rightful heir, it cannot be fooled by potions or glamours,Æ Ragnok said. ØI see, do I have heir rings?Æ Harry asked as he had not had one last time, or at least he didnít know about them. ØYou do, however you only have two heir rings although you are still the heir of four houses. Heir rings are a newer trend, coming about around 150 years ago, therefore only the Potter and Black families had heir rings made,Æ Ragnok explained. Harry nodded his understanding and picked up the two boxes Ragnok produced. Putting on the heir rings, Harry felt the same tug he experienced when he claimed his lordships in his past life. ØThese rings have the same protections as their lordship counterparts and can work as a payment seal when making purchases. However, as there is no Black heir vault set up for you, only the Potter seal will work for payment; just think of that when placing the ring and it will show up,Æ Ragnok continued to say. ØThank you,Æ Harry said after his magic had settled. Being able to use his ring to buy things would make it easier for him to make large purchases. ØIt is no problem,Æ Ragnok dismissed. ØIf that is everything, I will have somebody take you down to your trust vault.Æ ØIt is,Æ Harry said, saying goodbye as he exited the office. Greeting his new escort, Harry asked about getting a money pouch attached to his trust vault, much like he had seen many purebloods using in his last life. After haggling the price down, Harry agreed with the goblin and accepted the pouch. This way he wouldnít need to take copious amounts of money out to buy everything he needed and the pouches were magical signature locked, so there was no worry about his money even if it was stolen. Entering his vault, Harry was greeted by the sight of piles of gold; he remembered being overwhelmed by the picture the first time around when he came in with Hagrid and snorted thinking about how he would have reacted to the sight of the Black family vaults. Grabbing a couple of coins just to keep on him, Harry was in and out of his vault quickly as he no longer needed to spend time counting out money now he had his pouch. Greeting the goblin who was waiting back at the cart, Harry soon left the bank. Shopping quickly and trying to be efficient about collecting his first year supplies from the various stores, Harry aimed to keep a low profile. The only time he deviated from the equipment list was when he was in a apothecary - he collected his own ingredients instead of buying the readymade up first year kit in order to have higher quality ingredients; he also added a few extra in case he found himself needing to brew anything. Harry also diverted from his list when in Flourish and Blotts, as he bought books on everything that fascinated him as well as his required texts. When looking at the books, Harry was reminded of the Black family library and cursed his own stupidity. Buying the books quickly, Harry practically ran back to Gringotts. When he re-entered the bank Harry did not go to a teller. Instead, he nodded greetings as he passed and made his way straight to Ragnokís office. Harry barely even waited to be called in when he bound through the doors. ØSirius Black!Æ he practically shouted to the startled Goblin chief. Ragnok blinked at the state Harry was in and wondered what Sirius Black had to do with anything. ØMass murderer, placed in Azkaban prison on November 4th 1981 for the murder of twelve muggles and one wizard, Peter Pettigrew, reported Death Eater, and betrayer of the Potter family,Æ Ragnok recited. ØNo, he's innocent and heís my godfather,Æ Harry said. ØInnocent?Æ Ragnok questioned, ØYouíre sure?Æ ØYes. In my third year it was confirmed, Pettigrew is alive; heís an animagus living with the Weasleys. He was Ron's pet rat while we were at Hogwarts. Heís the real traitor.Æ ØI see,Æ Ragnok said. ØWhat do you wish to do with this information?Æ ØI want him freed,Æ Harry said. ØHe never had a trial, he was just locked away. If he is freed I gain a new magical guardian, which solves my Dumbledore problem, and I will have access to the Black family library again.Æ Ragnok frowned. ØNo trial? That's unheard of, especially as he was the heir of an Ancient and Noble House,Æ Ragnok muttered, more to himself than Harry. ØCan it be done?Æ Harry asked. ØI can make inquiries,Æ Ragnok said. ØHowever, it will be a slow process; this screams of a cover up and the ministry will want this to remain buried. It would not look go on them to have locked up a noble heir without a trial for almost ten years.Æ Harry smiled in relief and asked, ØWould it help for me to acquire Pettigrew?Æ ØYes, but not immediately,Æ Ragnok said. ØIf, like you say, Pettigrew was at Hogwarts, then itís not unreasonable to believe Dumbledore knows. If he is to just disappear before we have sorted out a trial, Dumbledore may grow suspicious.Æ Harry frowned but nodded, pleased that Ragnok would start to work on getting Sirius a trial. ØI will make a contract for you with a reliable law wizard,Æ Ragnok said, Øthat way, once the gears are moving, we can use them as an intermediary, thus keeping the nature of our relationship hidden from the world.Æ ØBrilliant,Æ Harry said, awed by the goblinís cunning. Ragnok smiled toothily. ØNow, if that is all, it would seem I suddenly have a lot of work to do.Æ With that dismissal, Harry once again left the bank, making sure to say goodbye to the guards as he had rushed passed them when he had entered. Returning to his school shopping, Harry realised it was almost dusk and the shops were either closing or closed. With that in mind, Harry decided to get a room for the night at the Leaky Cauldron instead of returning to Surrey as he was still in need of his uniform and wand. Entering the pub, Harry once again gained Tomís attention, asking for a single room for the night and paying. Harry was happy to hand over the small cost as it included both dinner and breakfast and he was starving. He was determined to try and reverse some of the damage done to his body because of his relative's care. Going up to his room, Harry looked around and added a few extra wards before removing the few bits he had brought from his robe. Returning downstairs, Harry ordered the Sheppardís pie as well as a pot of tea and tucked in. Leaving a small tip when he was finished, Harry thanked Tom and returned to his room, trying to think of everything that happened in his last life and how he could change it for the better. It only took a moment for Harry to recall exactly what else had happened the day he had gone to Diagon Alley and he cursed his stupidity. The stone! Thinking back, however, Harry thoughts soon turned to suspicion. Why was it that Hagrid was sent for the stone on the day he had to pick up Harry? Surely the better option for Dumbledore would have been to send somebody who could actually do magic? If not pick it up himselfè. Thinking on the situation, Harry cursed his own stupidity and had to hand it to Dumbledore; he had well and truly played Harry. The entire situation was a setup - he was testing Harry and Harry had fallen for it every single time. Laughing in spite of himself, Harry wondered how Dumbledore would try to get Harry after the stone this time. Barely able to stay awake, Harry wondered why he was so tired but figured time travel probably left one sleepy and quickly stripped from his clothes and slipped into the bed. The next day Harry looked at his transfigured clothes. They were still usable but looked rather lacklustre; that was the problem with transfiguring clothing when you were not a professional: the charms faded quickly. Sighing, Harry transfigured them again, and vowed his first order of business after getting a wand would be clothes. After a breakfast of pancakes and bacon, Harry quietly joined the crowds of Diagon Alley, making his way straight to Ollivanderís. Unlike in his first life, the shop was not empty, and much to Harry's chagrin the person was none other than an eleven year old Hermione Granger. Narrowing his eyes in distaste, Harry barely stopped himself from cursing her just to get it over with; this was the first time he had seen somebody on his hit-list and he was not feeling magnanimous. He was surprised to see that she didnít have a school escort but was with her parents, who were standing looking shell shocked in the corner. Hearing the bell chime over the door as he stepped through, she had turned to look at him. Her brown eyes narrowing as she ran her eyes over him, looking at his casual wizarding clothing with distaste. Just then Ollivander spoke, ØAh, Mr. PîÆ Harry quickly cut the peculiar man off before he could say Harry's last name, ØPlease sir, call me Harrison.Æ ØThatís rude you know,Æ said Hermione in a matter of fact tone that had Harry clenching his fist to stop himself from lashing out. ØYou shouldnít interrupt people when theyíre talking,Æ Harry just looked at her with distaste. ØIndeed,Æ he said, unconsciously mimicking Lucius Malfoy, Øif youíre done Miss....Æ he let himself trail off. Hermione seemed to puff up at his tone with indignation. ØYes, actually, I am finished,Æ she said contemptuously. ØAnd my name is Hermione granger,Æ she added. Harry hummed, ØGranger, I've never heard of that family before.Æ He really didnít have a problem with muggleborns, not at all, but he hated how Hermione treated the Wizarding world. She thought their customs were outdated and scorned their culture just because it was different from the muggle world. She didnít try to understand them, she just ignored them and complained. ØThatís because I'm the first witch in my family,Æ Hermione replied, looking at Harry challengingly. Making sure to put as much distaste behind his words as possible, Harry said, ØI see.Æ Turning then, as though to dismiss her, Harry only had to wait a second before she exploded in self righteous indignation, ØWhat? You think you're better than me because you have a magical family?Æ she demanded. Harry hummed again, ØNo, I never said that. That belief is all yours, Miss Granger.Æ Just as Harry was sure she was about to explode, her parents seemed to pull themselves out of their fearful state and noticed she was done. ØOh, come on Hermione, weíve got to get going,Æ Mrs. Granger said, practically making a run for the door. Watching them leave, Harry let a small smirk form on his face when he saw Hermione struggle and glare at him. ØWell, that was interesting,Æ said Ollivander voice, drawing Harry's attention to the man who had been silently watching his interaction with Hermione. Harry shrugged. ØI really donít have a problem with muggleborns,Æ Harry said. ØI just hate people like her who come into this world and try to change it into a replica of the muggle one.Æ Ollivander hummed again and nodded, ØWell then, letís see what the wands make of you, Harrison.Æ Stepping forward, Harry watched as Ollivander seemed to lose focus before looking at Harry intently. ØYour wand arm, if you please,Æ he instructed. Handing out his right arm, Harry considered just telling him what wand was his but decided to go with the process. Maybe his wand would have changed. Eventually Harry decided that his wand hadnít changed as, like last time, after testing dozens of wands, Ollivander finally produced a familiar dusty old box. Picking up his holly wand, Harry felt his magic sing. Although he was now more than capable of wandless magic, the feeling of unity between a wand and its wizard was something special. ØAh, interesting,Æ Ollivander said, almost mirroring his speech from Harry's last life. ØEleven inches from the wood of a holly tree and containing the tail feather of a phoenix.Æ Nodding his head, Harry didnít ask what was interesting, instead he asked, ØCould I also buy a wand holder for my wrist, in black dragonhide if possible?Æ Ollivander nodded when Harry spoke and went to find the holster. After placing it on the till he looked at Harry. ØI will be expecting great things to come from you with that wand, Mr. Potter,Æ he said. Harry smiled slightly. ØI hope to achieve great things,Æ he said. ØThat will be twelve galleons and eighteen knuts in total,Æ Ollivander said eventually. Reaching into his money pouch, Harry thought of the amount of money he needed and withdrew his hand, the correct sum now in it. Passing it over, Harry attached his wand holster to his wrist and placed his wand in it before leaving the shop. Next, Harry headed for Madam Malkinís. Entering the shop, Harry was glad to find it empty. ØFirst year robes?Æ asked Madam Malkin herself as she appeared from behind a clothing display. ØYes please, could I also get a few day robes andîÆ Harry cut himself off with a sheepish rub of his neck. ØWell, actually, I need an entire wardrobe really,Æ he admitted. ØRight then dear, why donít you step up on the platform and Iíll measure you up,Æ Madam Malkin said. Standing up on the raised platform, Harry soon had his measurements taken. ØRight dear, I can have the school robes done in a few moments,Æ she said, indicating for Harry to get down. ØWe can pick out the rest and I can modify the fit for you.Æ ØThank you,Æ Harry said, going to look through the racks. Picking out a few pieces, Harry soon found his arms full with day robes, shirts, trousers, jumpers, underwear, and pajamas. All of it got added to the pile growing on the table - he even picked out shoes and a few accessories. Eventually, between himself and Madam Malkin, who had returned, Harry had a decent variety of clothing which Madam Malkin promised to charm to grow a few inches so it would last even if he had a growth spurt. ØI can have these modified and charmed by the end of lunch. Why donít you come back and pick them up at, say, around one o'clock? If that's convenient, of course. If not, I can have them owled to you,Æ she said. ØI can pick them up,Æ Harry said. It was almost lunch now and he still had to go to the Eeylops Owl Emporium to buy Hedwig. ØWould you like to pay now or on collection?Æ she asked. ØI will pay now,Æ Harry said. Malkin nodded and tapped her wand to the till. ØYour total will be 126 galleons and six sickles,Æ she said. Harry nodded at the price, soothing the ladyís nerves as she worried the child wouldnít be able to pay such a large sum. ØCan I use my heir ring to pay?Æ Harry asked. Producing the payment sheet, Madam Malkin handed it over. Focusing on the Potter crest, Harry placed his ring in the designated space and watched as the Potter seal appeared. Handing it back, Harry watched as Madam Malkinís eyes widened and then darted to Harry's forehead. Seeing this, Harry looked around the shop before carefully moving his fringe to show the famous scar. ØI would appreciate if you kept it quiet that I am shopping today,Æ Harry said. ØO-of course, Mr. Potter, anything,Æ she stuttered suddenly, making Harry want to sigh. He hated when people suddenly realised who he was and started to treat him like a celebrity. ØRight then Madam, I will see you after lunch,Æ Harry said, nodding his head politely in goodbye. Leaving the shop and ignoring the seamstresses stuttered farewells, Harry headed to Eeylops. Seeing Hedwig still outside in her cage, Harry smiled brightly. Approaching her large, knowing eyes Harry hoped that she would bond with him like she had in his last life. Seeing his interest, a shop assistant appeared as though summoned and asked if he wanted to buy her. Harry nodded and barely drew his eyes away from his old friend. ØSheís a temperamental one, doesnít seem to like anybody. Always takes a nip at whoever has to feed her,Æ the shop assistant warned as he looked at Hedwig in her cage. ØSheís perfect,Æ Harry said. ØCan I also get a large cage and some owl treats?Æ ØCertainly,Æ the assistant said before gathering the items and telling Harry the total. Shrinking them down, the assistant handed them over to Harry, who handed over the required gold in response. Carefully opening the cage Hedwig had been kept in, Harry watched as she looked at him with interest before she walked onto his arm. ØHello, beautiful,Æ he said softly, scratchy her in the way he knew she loved. ØWell, Iíll be damned, you must have a lucky touch,Æ the shop assistant said, watching Harry interact with the bird in awe. Nuzzling against his hand, Hedwig have a contented trill. ØMy nameís Harry and you and me? Weíre going to be great friends,Æ Harry told the bird. ØI'm going to call you Hedwig,Æ Harry continued, and watched as her intelligent eyes seemed to accept her new name. ØRight then,Æ Harry said after a moment, Øwhy donít you go and catch yourself some food and stretch your wings for a few hours? I'm currently living at 4 Privet Drive in Surrey so, when youíre done, you can return there,Æ Harry instructed the bird. He watched happily as she flew up in the air. Happy now that he had finished all of his shopping, Harry found a small cafe to have lunch before returning to collect his clothing. Glad he was done, Harry wandered through the Leaky Cauldron and apperated back to Surrey. He didnít want to risk forgetting anything about his first year again like he almost did with the stone, so he was going to make a list of everything that had happened. Thinking back as he wrote, Harry couldnít believe at the amount of things he had done that played into Dumbledoreís hands; but then again, that was probably exactly what he had wanted. Vowing things would be different, Harry started to plot. ~ Chapter End Notes So there it is, he's back! Hope you're enjoying the story so far, as always comments and Kudos welcomed! An update should be available in the next few days. It may take slighlty longer than you've become used to as I will be very busy over the next week ~ Annie Chapter 6: HoneyI'm home Chapter Notes See the end of the chapter for notes Previously Happy now that he had finished all of his shopping, Harry found a small cafe to have lunch before returning to collect his clothing. Glad he was done, Harry wandered through the Leaky Cauldron and apperated back to Surrey. He didnít want to risk forgetting anything about his first year again like he almost did with the stone, so he was going to make a list of everything that had happened. Thinking back as he wrote, Harry couldnít believe at the amount of things he had done that played into Dumbledoreís hands; but, then again, that was probably exactly what he had wanted. Vowing things would be different, Harry started to plot. ~ 4 Privet Drive, September 1st 1991 On the morning of September 1st, Harry could hardly contain his impatience and resentment. He had spent the last month reviewing everything that had happened in his last life, and he honestly feared that he would curse Dumbledore the moment he saw him. So much had been manipulated that Harry truly feared he had never once had a chance to act like himself, to be his own person. Doing one final check of his room, Harry was happy to see that he had not forgotten anything. When he had been packing his trunk, Harry had been careful to ward each compartment individually - especially his book compartment. Harry had returned to London a few times since his first visit in order to buy a variety of books; books that would not be approved of by many. He had travelled to Knockturn Alley a few times in his past life, but never extensively, so Harry had been anxious this time around, however, his presence was soon ignored. Harry had also been to a private healer and, after making him swear a privacy vow, had him perform an in depth scan. Harry had been pleased when he had learnt that, without the glamour, he had no need for his glasses, but he still wanted to be checked out. The healer had been horrified after the scans were done and had tried to get Harry to report the damage to the ministry. Many of his bones had breaks that had healed incorrectly and he had numerous scars. Harry had been given potions to fix the damage as well as an ointment to wear while sleeping. The healer had also worried for Harry's growth and development and given Harry potions to combat the damage, though he would never be tall, Harry hope to gain another inch or two. With a final glance around, Harry cast a tempus charm that said it was half 10. Deciding to get to the platform early and find a compartment, Harry released Hedwig from her cage. ØYou can fly to Hogwarts from here,Æ he said, not wanting her to be cooped up for the long journey. Stretching her wings, Hedwig swooped over to Harry and landed on his shoulder, pecking his ear affectionately before taking flight out of the open window. Walking down the stairs, Harry was not surprised when he saw Petunia standing awkwardly by the front door. He had had few interactions with the Dursleys in the last month; they had taken his warnings to heart after Harry had once again cursed Vernon, this time with Dudley watching, when the man had tried to hit Harry once he had returned from Diagon Alley the first time. ØYouíre going then,Æ she said. ØYes, I wonít be back until the summer. If I return at all,Æ Harry told her. Petunia swallowed again. ØRight then, good.Æ Walking past her with his school supplies shrunk down in his pocket, Harry walked a short distance before apparating onto the platform. Kings Cross Station, September 1st 1991 Arriving at the station, Harry walked quickly. There were a few families milling about that Harry chose to ignore in favour of getting onto the train. Finding an empty compartment, Harry entered and un-shrunk his trunk, levitating it above him before he sat down and conjured the book he had been reading: Mind Magics, Shields, and Webs. Even with his mind protected by his heir ring, Harry wanted to learn occlumency, if only so he would have better control of his emotions. He had so far developed the beginnings of a mindscape and was trying to meditate for at least an hour a day before bed. It was hard going, but he was positive he would have mastered it by the end of the year. Eventually Harry's solitude was disturbed by knocking on the door to his compartment. Looking up in time, Harry saw a young Susan Bones enter alongside Hannah Abbott. ØDo you mind if we join you?Æ Susan asked, standing just inside the door. Smiling Harry nodded. ØOf course,Æ he said. He had liked both of these girls in his last life, although they hadnít really been friends. He had known them and worked with them as part of the DA. He also knew they would never be part of Dumbledore's schemes; Susan in particular, as her aunt was the head of the DMLE. Sitting down opposite him, both girls exchanged a look before Susan held out her hand. ØIím Susan Bones,Æ she introduced herself. Taking her hand and kissing the air above it, Harry smiled slightly at her blushed cheeks. ØA pleasure, Miss Bones. My name is Harrison Potter.Æ When they heard his name both girls instantly looked at his forehead and to the place his fringe was covering. Hannah gulped before holding out her hand. ØI'm honoured to meet you Harry - I mean, Harrison. I'm Hannah Abbott.Æ Doing the same to Hannah, Harry kissed her hand before looking at both girls. They were staring at him with fascination that made Harry want to sigh. ØI understand that youíve grown up with stories about me, but honestly, I would rather you treat me like every other student. Iím really not that interesting, and please feel free to call me Harrison or Harry. I really donít like it when people act weird around me or treat me special for something I have no memory of doing.Æ Susan seemed to come to a decision then and smiled at him. ØSo, Harry,Æ she started, Øare you excited to start Hogwarts?Æ Happy that he would not be putting up with fangirls for the entire trip, Harry relaxed slightly in his chair. ØI am, I've read all about it,Æ Harry said, letting himself act as a soundboard to the two girls, who seemed to explode talking about all they had heard about Hogwarts. Just as the train started to pull away, another knock was heard. Turning, Harry saw that this time a very timid looking Neville Longbottom had entered and was now standing awkwardly, clutching his toad in his hands. ØD-do you m-m-mind if I sit with you?Æ he stuttered out. Without sparing the girls a glance, Harry agreed, indicating for Neville to sit next to him as he moved his satchel out of the way. ØHello,Æ Harry greeted, knowing that at this age Neville was a walking bag of nerves. It wasnít until 5th year that the Neville he knew gained any confidence. ØWhoíve you got there?Æ Harry asked, indicating to the toad. Neville seemed to calm down when Harry started to speak to him. ØT-Trevor. His name is Trevor, and my name is Neville, Neville Longbottom,Æ he said, looking around the compartment. Looking up, Harry gave the girls encouraging smiles, which they seemed to pick up on. Sitting forward, Hannah and Susan both introduced themselves. Once they had done, Harry smiled and introduced himself, ØPleased to meet you, Neville, my nameís Harrison Potter.Æ Neville spluttered and tried to say something, but no words came out. Deciding to save him, Harry smiled and said, ØI know, I know - Boy-Who-Lived and all that rubbish, but really? I'm just Harry.Æ Susan, who had watched this, laughed, ØI see what you mean, Harry. I bet it does get tiring being treated like that all the time.Æ Neville seemed to shrink in on himself for a moment before he realised they were not laughing at him, sending Harry a hesitant smile. ØDo you have the scar?Æ Neville asked hesitantly, reminding Harry that Neville was a true Gryffindor. The girls instantly stopped their quiet conversation to look at Harry with interest. Sighing, Harry moved his fringe and showed them before brushing his hair back into place. ØDoes it hurt?Æ Hannah asked tentatively. Harry smiled at the girl. ØNo, not usually. However, sometimes it can ache after I have dreams.Æ Susan frowned. ØDreams?Æ she asked. ØI think theyíre of that night,Æ Harry paused to shrug, ØI'm really not sure, and sometimes it just aches in general,Æ he said, wanting to spread this story for when his scar ached in Quirrellís presence, if it did this time around. ØOh,Æ was the quiet reply he got. ØWell, that was depressing,Æ Harry said, trying to brighten the mood. ØBefore you got here Neville, we were talking out the four houses. Do you know which house you want?Æ Neville seemed to shake himself before he smiled. ØMy gran thinks I destined for Hufflepuff,Æ he said sadly. ØAnd what's wrong with Hufflepuff?Æ the girls demanded simultaneously before they giggled. ØN-nothing,Æ Neville said, scared for a moment he had insulted them. Harry laughed. ØIgnore them Nev, theyíre both proud descendants of mighty åpuffs,Æ Harry said. ØWhat about you, Harry?Æ Neville asked. ØHonestly there's a chance Iíll be in Slytherin I think, but I want Ravenclaw,Æ Harry said, shocking them. ØS-S-Slytherin?Æ Neville squeaked. ØSure,Æ Harry said happily, ignoring their horrified looks. ØDo you really think that's possible?Æ asked Susan. ØItís the house of ambition and cunning,Æ Harry said, Øand I think that I'm ambitious.Æ ØBut, well, You-Know-Who....Æ Hannah trailed off. ØI donít think that an entire house should be judged just because of what happened in the last war. I mean, not all dark wizards are in Slytherin, there are dark wizards in any house, just like there are light wizards in any house, including Slytherin,Æ Harry said. Susan nodded. ØYou're right, Harry.Æ ØAnyway, I think I would prefer Ravenclaw, so I'm going to ask the hat for that.Æ ØAsk the hat?Æ Neville asked. Harry realised his mistake, remembering how wizards all tried to keep it a secret how you were sorted. ØOops,Æ he said sheepishly, looking around the compartment. Hannah and Susan shared a look and laughed. ØItís okay Harry, we know how it works,Æ Hannah said. ØYeah, my aunt Amelia told us because we were worried,Æ Susan continued, whispering conspiratorially. Neville just looked confused. ØI donít get it,Æ he said making them all laugh again. ØItís okay Nev, donít worry,Æ Harry said. ØYou never did say where you wanted to be placed, Neville,Æ Hannah said after she finished laughing. ØOh, well, I think I want to go to Gryffindor,Æ Neville said. ØMy parents were in Gryffindor, but, well, I doubt Iíll get in....Æ ØDonít be silly, Neville,Æ Harry said. ØI think you would do great in Gryffindor.Æ ØReally?Æ Neville asked eagerly. Just then the door to their compartment opened, causing them all to turn and look. ØCan we help you?Æ Susan asked. ØCan I -oh, itís you,Æ came the derisive voice of Hermione Granger. Seeing her look, Neville turned to Harry. ØDo you know her, Harry?Æ he asked. ØWe met briefly in Ollivanderís. Miss Granger, yes?Æ Harry said, saying her name as a question. ØHe was very rude to me actually,Æ Hermione said. ØDidnít your parents teach you not to be rude to people?Æ she added snidely. Harry couldnít believe his luck - with that comment she had doomed herself, not just to him, but to everybody who heard it. The silence that followed was consuming. Harry had to bite his lip to keep from laughing, which made the situation for Hermione worse as it looked like he was trying to contain his sadness, not his laughter. ØGet out,Æ said Neville, his voice so soft and deadly that even Harry was shocked for a moment. ØI beg your pardon?Æ Hermione said, shocked by his tone. ØI said leave,Æ Neville said, standing. ØButîÆ ØYou heard him,Æ Susan added, also standing and giving Hermione a withering stare. Looking around, Hermione stuck her nose up and turned, slamming the door in her haste to retreat. Silence fell before Hannah hesitantly spoke. ØAre you okay, Harry?Æ she asked. Taking another moment as he feared if he opened his mouth he would break out in laughter, Harry shut his eyes and centred himself. ØYes,Æ he said softly. Turning to Neville, he smiled at the boy, who was still standing and looking shocked. ØThank you, Neville,Æ Harry said. ØThank you all,Æ he added to the girls. ØItís okay Harry, that's what friends are for.Æ His hesitant use of the word friend made Harry sad; Neville never seemed to have real friends until he was older and, thinking back on it, that made Harry feel guilty as Neville was a true friend when given a chance. ØShe had no right to say that to you about your parents,Æ Neville added after seeing Harry's smile. ØI donít know what I did to her - in the shop, I mean. She just seemed to hate me as soon as she saw me; when I entered she made a snide comment and when I ignored her she started to argue with me,Æ Harry said, adopting a confused expression. Susan sat back and shook her head. ØShe's probably muggleborn,Æ she said before quickly adding, ØNot that I have a problem with muggleborns or think purebloods are better or anything, no, itís just that sometimes when they first enter our world they feel like they have something to prove. Then again, she could just be a terrible person.Æ Harry laughed. ØI think itís a combination of all of that, to be honest,Æ he said, easing Susanís fears that she would upset Harry when she mentioned muggleborns. Seeing the tension slowly ease, Harry sat back happily. He had worried when he thought about coming back to Hogwarts that he wouldnít be able to cope with being 11 again and surrounded by children, but now he had more confidence that he would manage. At least he hoped he would. ØAnd to think, Neville,Æ Harry said, Øyou didnít think you were Gryffindor material. What you just did was brave.,Æ Neville perked up. ØIt was, wasnít it?Æ he said, his excitement making the girls smile. Eventually Harry started to tune out the conversation and picked up his book again. Reading, Harry would add the occasional comment, but for the next hour or so he devoured the chapter in his book. ØAre you interested in mind magic?Æ Susan asked when Harry closed the book, having finished the chapter he had been on. ØYes, I find it to be quite fascinating,Æ Harry said. ØMy aunt has told me a little about it,Æ Susan said. ØItís really hard to master, but she promised to try and teach me some basic occlumency next summer; she said it would help me study.Æ Harry was not surprised by this, a lot of purebloods and halfbloods taught their children occlumency if they could. ØYouíve heard of occlumency, right?Æ Susan asked. ØYes, I find it fascinating. I read about it over the summer, which is what lead me to reading this book. I've started to do some of the exercises and think I've got a good starting base,Æ Harry said. ØThatís cool. You know, I'm sure my aunt wouldnít mind answering any questions if you have any. She's a Master Occlumens because of her job,Æ Susan said happily. ØThank you, I will remember that,Æ Harry promised. Once again the conversation drifted and Harry found his thoughts wandering. He joined in, adding comments in places, but he was more than happy to sit back and observe. ~ This time when the trolley lady got to their compartment, Harry once again bought a large amount of food and, like last time, he shared his treats before putting half away in his trunk. ØI heard itís a smart idea to buy a load now so they can last, weíre not allowed to go to Hogsmeade until 3rd year and well... in my case, I donít really have anyone to send me sweets,Æ Harry said at their curious looks. ØIs it true then? The rumours?Æ Neville asked. ØWhat rumours would those be? I've heard a lot recently.Æ ØWell... that you were raised by muggles?Æ Neville said. ØOh, that rumour,Æ Harry said, his voice losing some of its warmth, Øyes. My aunt and uncle.Æ ØDo you not like them?Æ Susan asked, picking up on Harry's tone. Harry was suddenly glad that she and Hannah had decided to sit with him; the odds are that she would tell her aunt anything he said. ØWell itís not that, itís just that, wellè. They donít like anything not normal, not muggle, and me, well, I'm not normal to them,Æ Harry said in a quiet voice. Hannah gasped quietly. ØThey donít like you?Æ she said, shocked. Harry just shrugged. ØThey donít, you knowè.Æ Susan asked hesitantly. Harry just looked up. ØItís not so bad now, since I got my letter,Æ is all he said. After letting that sink in and a silence fall, Harry had to congratulate himself. He doubted that it would change anything but it certainly wouldnít hurt. ØCould you not say anything to anybody about that?Æ Harry asked. ØI donít want people looking at me with pity or anything.Æ ØSure, Harry,Æ Neville said, Øit that's what you want.Æ Smiling, Harry couldnít wait to see what planting the seeds in Susanís mind would do. It helped that he really wasnít lying. ~ Arriving at Hogsmeade station, Harry joined the crowd of growing first years and looked out for the famous red hair of the Weasley family. Getting to the boats Harry, Hannah, Susan, and Neville got on together. Harry watched with amusement as Neville struggled with Trevor; at least he hadnít lost him on the train this time, he thought fleetingly. As the boats took off, Harry looked up to see with amusement that Ron had somehow managed to get himself trapped on a boat with Draco Malfoy and his two goons, Crabbe and Goyle. Making sure not to use his wand as he didnít want anybody to see him, Harry focused on his magic and sent a push towards the redhead. Harry barely contained his mirth when Ron fell, landing with a splash in the Black Lake. Some people screamed whilst others laughed; Harry thought for a moment Malfoy would join Ron with the amount he was laughing. ØEasy now,Æ shouted Hagrid from the first boat. ØHeíll be fine, the squid will rescue him.Æ His words seemed to incite more fear and people huddle close into the boats. Eventually Ron resurfaced with a tentacle around his waist and was plopped back in his boat. His white face made his freckles stand out even more. Harry had to bite his lip as he heard Malfoy taunt, ØBet that's the first bath youíve taken all year, weasel.Æ Eventually the boats reached the castle and Harry found himself reaching out with his magic instinctively. He felt the castleís magic sing within him and felt an overwhelming peace. Seeing his enraptured look the girls giggled. ØItís beautiful, isnít it?Æ Susan asked. Harry, dazed from the magical connection, merely nodded. ØYeah.Æ The crowd follow Hagrid into the entrance hall with Ron standing at the front next him, dripping lake water on the floor. The doors opened and Professor McGonagall entered.
