ØHey Harry, what are you doing for Yule? Or do you celebrate Christmas?Æ Hannah asked. Shrugging, Harry shut his book and put it in his bag, making sure the cover wasnít shown. ØIíll be staying here,Æ Harry said. ØAnd, honestly? I havenít really ever celebrated either.Æ ØOh, donít you want to go home?Æ Anthony said curiously. Harry made sure to cringe slightly before he answered, ØNo, I think I would enjoy it better here.Æ ØReally? Well, I canít wait to go home. My parents and I are going to Aspen to see my cousinsè,Æ Anthony went on to tell them all about his Yule plans. ØHey Harry, if you wanted, I'm sure I could owl my gran and ask if you could come back to the manor with me,Æ Neville offered quietly. Harry smiled lightly at that. Harry knew that Neville spent most of day visiting his parents and that he cherished that time. ØThank you Neville, but I think I want to stay here. Maybe next year or something?Æ Harry said. He was looking forward to having some alone time; not that he didnít like the friends he had made, it was just that they were still children. Yes, he was now eleven again, but he was also eighteen. He wanted some time to himself. Neville looked slightly concerned but nodded. ØIf youíre sure,Æ he said reluctantly. ØI do really mean it, thank you, Neville,Æ Harry said. ØAre you sure, Harry? My aunt would happily have you,Æ Susan offered, having heard their whispered conversation. Sending her a smile, Harry nodded. ØYeah, I'm sure.Æ ØOkay, then, but you had better owl me every other day to let me know you're okay,Æ she said. Laughing, Harry replied, ØI promise.Æ ~ A few days later, the castle emptied out as people returned home for the holidays and Harry was left alone with the few others who had remained. Like last time, this included the Weasleys. Harry was startled to realise he hadnít even spoken to the twins yet in this life and felt guilty for a moment before he realised that feeling bad was silly - they didnít even know him in this life. During breakfast on the first day, Harry, who was sitting with a 4th year Ravenclaw and two 7th years, was not remotely surprised when Ron appeared. ØHey mate, you want to hang out?Æ he asked. Harry, keeping his displeasure masked, said, ØSure, maybe. I just need to head up to the common room.Æ Ron gaped at Harry for a moment; clearly he had been expecting him to say no. ØReally?Æ he asked incredulously. ØGreat!Æ Harry kept from rolling his eyes. ØSure, there's no one else our age here,Æ Harry said. He wondered what Dumbledore was up to; Harry had almost hoped he would stop sending Ron as, each time he had, Ron had ended up either in trouble or causing an argument. Ron happily ignored Harry's comment. ØWe could play chess or go and explore the castle; I bet thereís loads of things hidden here,Æ Ron said. Harry barely stopped him from snorting. ØWhatever,Æ he mumbled instead. ØWe could go check out the dungeons now that the slimy snakes are gone,Æ Ron said. Harry didnít even hide his displeasure. ØThere's nothing wrong with Slytherins,Æ he said. Ron just snorted. ØRight, itís okay, Harry. You stick with me and Iíll show you, you just need to learn that they're evil,Æ he said. His certainty actually made Harry feel almost sorry for him. ~ Harry was exhausted after finally ditching Ron after a day of the boy trailing after Harry, telling him how evil Slytherins are, how Harry really should have been in Gryffindor, and how ådonít you think Snapeís been acting suspicious?í. It was like he was hiding something or going after something; at least he had figured out Ron's purpose, though. Dumbledore obviously didnít want his boy hero to forget about the stone and had sent the redhead to make sure Harry was suspicious of Slytherins and thinking about who was after it. Thinking about the stone, Harry had come to a conclusion. He couldnít let Quirrellmort get it. Letting Voldemort return now would be disastrous, as he would return insane like he had in Harry's fourth year, and Harry refused to allow that to happen. He would rather Tom be forced to wait, wandering as a spirit, than return as an insane Voldemort. However, Harry didnít really want Dumbledore to have the stone either; he didnít want to rescue it and go through all of the trials he had last time just to have the old coot destroy it. Instead, Harry had a better idea. He would take the stone and hide it away secretly. Then, after he had fixed Tom, which he would do, he could give it to him as a peace offering for delaying his return. With that in mind, Harry started to plan; he couldnít have to old coot growing too suspicious of him, now. ~ Chapter End Notes Here's the latest chapter! The redhead just would not take the hint that Harry had no desire for his presence. His persistence would have been commendable, if not for the fact he was ultimately trying to befriend Harry in the hopes of stealing from him and killing him. With the lack of students, Harry had taken to literally running everywhere he needed to go and checking around the corners. He would have just worn his invisibility cloak, but he didnít want people to become worried and therefore pay closer attention to him if they realised he hadnít been seen for hours on end. So, instead, he dodged and checked as if he was a criminal on the run. Harry had taken to trying to ignore him after a few days as it seemed that whenever he left his common room, Ron would appear. If it wasnít for the fact that Harry had checked twice, he would have thought he had a tracker on him, alerting the redhead of his movements. ØCome on, mate, stop being so boring. All you seem to do is want to read books and hang out in the library,Æ Ron whined as Harry continued to walk towards the library. He needed to check one final thing before he could go after the stone. ØWell, I am a Ravenclaw - and there's nothing wrong with the library,Æ Harry said. He almost cringed as he mimicked Hermione and then internally cringed again as he remembered her broken body. ØThe library, really?Æ Ron asked skeptically, before he developed a slightly odd look. ØWhy are you in there so much anyway? Are you researching something - a mystery?Æ Harry considered what to say for a moment before he concluded that putting Ron on the case would help him in the end. Dumbledore would once again be sure of Harry's path and Ron would hopefully leave Harry alone. ØWell, I have this theory,Æ Harry said, Øthat whatever was stolen from Gringotts is now in the school, I just donít know what. Itís probably on the 3rd floor, I saw a three headed dog there.Æ Harry watched as Ron's eyes lit up with glee. ØI can help you, Harry!Æ he practically shouted. ØThis can be our adventure.Æ Harry fought his eye roll and instead smiled. ØGreat, why donít you go look up animal books for me? That would really help,Æ Harry lied, relieved when the redhead ran off with an air of triumph. After that day, Ron became easier to distract; all Harry had to do was mention their so-called adventure and the redhead would run off and look up books on animals. Morning - December 25th 1991 Hogwarts Eventually Christmas morning came and Harry woke up to find he had a larger pile of presents than he had been expecting. He had sent Hedwig off earlier in the week to deliver Neville, Hannah, and Susanís presents and he had decided to send everyone in his study group a box of candy from Honeydukes. For Neville, he had ordered a book on rare and exotic plants and gotten him the clipping from one of the more obscure ones; for Hannah, Harry had got some silver hair pins with butterflies on it; and for, Susan, he bought gold hairpins with flowers. Looking at his pile, Harry was surprised to see the familiar sight of a Weasley present. Picking it up, Harry felt a compulsion charm attempt to take hold before it was nullified by his rings. With a snarl, Harry barely kept himself from destroying the package. After taking a few moments to collect his feelings and strengthen his Occlumency shields, Harry finally opened the package. Inside, there was an obviously handmade green jumper and a box of homemade fudge. Flicking his wand, Harry ran through his repertoire of detection spells and saw that the fudge set them all off. With a scowl, he nullified them before banishing the box. There was also a note resting innocently on the jumper. Picking it up, Harry just about contained his snort. ~ Harry, Ron mentioned that you were spending Christmas at Hogwarts, so I thought I would send you something to help keep your spirits up. I canít tell you how happy it makes me that Ron's finally found himself such a wonderful friend. Happy Christmas dear, Mrs. Weasley ~ Harry was impressed with the note; had he not known their motives, he would have felt truly guilty. Instead, he was offended by the not-so subtle manipulations. Discarding the present, Harry looked with interest at the rest. There were no presents from Hagrid or Dumbledore this year, or even a card with 50p from the Dursleyís. Picking up a package wrapped in silver, Harry opened it and found that inside was a pair of soft black leather gloves with HJP embroidered in the corner of the wrist. The gloves were very nice and Harry picked up the card inside to see who they were from. Reading the note, Harry was surprised to see they were from Terry, making him feel slightly bad about his lacklustre present to the boy. Moving on, Harry opened his present from Neville and saw the boy had given Harry a kit to start his own small herb garden, and had written his own instructions on what to do to get successful results. Next, Harry opened a present from Susan and saw she had gotten him a book on mind magic, he had not heard of; flicking through it briefly, he saw that it was an old publishing and Harry concluded it was probably no longer in print. Opening his gift from Hannah, Harry saw that she had bought him a beautifully illustrated copy of The Tales of Beedle the Bard. Opening it, Harry saw she had placed a note inside. ~ Happy Yule, Harry! I know itís a childrenís book, but I thought you would enjoy reading some of the stories, especially as you didnít grow up in our world! See you after the holidays! X, Hannah ~ After putting away his gifts, Harry picked his clothing for the day, trying to decide if he wanted to endure the itchy jumper from Mrs. Weasley. Eventually, Harry chose to go without as he really didnít care. Walking downstairs for breakfast, Harry was quick to take a seat close to the teachers and hoped that somebody would sit next to him before Ron appeared. To Harry's surprise, Snape took the seat, sitting down and ignoring everybodyís presence as he poured himself coffee. ØHappy Yule, sir,Æ Harry said, pouring his own coffee. He hadnít really drunk the beverage since returning to his preteen body, but Harry had plans for a long night and thought it would probably help. Severus inclined his head as he drank. ØIndeed, Mr. Potter, happy Yule.Æ Silence fell then as they started to eat, Harry glad that he was free from Ron's presence as the redhead appeared, looking mutinous between Fred and George. Snorting softly, Harry watched as they forced him to sit down away from Harry, smirking when he saw Ron's scowl. Severus watched at the dark haired boy snorted and smirked. ØWhy, Mr. Potter, one would think you donít want to spend your Yule with the youngest Mr. Weasley,Æ he said quietly Harry, not feeling the need to hide, shrugged subtly, knowing Snape would pick up on it. ØI donít,Æ he said. Severus smirked lightly at the curt reply. ØOh, and here I thought you two had become the best of friends,Æ he drawled. ØPlease,Æ Harry said dismissively, ØI would never befriend such an idiotic bigot.Æ ØIndeed,Æ Severus commented. ØYet you have spent the holiday with him.Æ ØI could hardly avoid him, I would think he was stalking me with how he keeps popping up,Æ Harry said in quiet exasperation. Severus hid is humour at the put-out tone of the eleven year old. ØHe has certainly been persistent,Æ he acknowledged. ØTell me about it,Æ Harry grumbled. ~ Evening- December 25th Hogwarts After having managed to avoid Ron for the entire day, excluding meals, Harry was finally ready to enact the first part of his plan. Putting his cloak on, Harry wandered the halls, eventually coming to the room he knew the Mirror of Erised was being kept. Entering, Harry knew that Dumbledore was watching, hidden in the shadows; keeping that in mind, Harry looked up to the mirror. Harry remembered how the mirror had shown him his parents for the first time. Yet, now when Harry looked, he saw himself aged around 20 in the arms of a slightly older man who he recognised from his meeting with Tom Riddle. He was tall, with dark brown hair shorter than Harry's, and styled. This was the man Tom could have been and Harry vowed that he would try to make that happen. Around the pair stood Sirius, Neville, and Harry's other friends. They all looked happy. Knowing he was being watched, Harry removed his cloak and sat down, repeating his actions from his former life. ØMom... Dad...?Æ he said longingly. This time when Dumbledore appeared, Harry wasnít shocked. ØDo you know what this mirror shows us harry?Æ he asked. Making sure to jump, Harry turned to look at the scheming old man. ØI'm sorry sir, I didnít see you there,Æ Harry stuttered out. ØItís okay, my dear boy. Many a man are drawn to the mirror,Æ Dumbledore said sagely. ØI donít know, sir,Æ Harry said, in reply to his earlier question. ØReally, you have no idea?Æ Dumbledore asked. ØWell, I guess it shows what we want?Æ Harry replied, making sure to sound uncertain. ØTake a closer look, Harry,Æ Dumbledore prompted. Walking to the mirror, Harry pretended to observe it before åfindingí the inscription. "Erised stra ehru oyt ube cafru oyt on wohsi," Harry mumbled before pretending to figure it out. ØI show not your face, but your heartís desire!Æ he exclaimed happily. ØVery good, Harry,Æ Dumbledore said, his tone just shying away from patronising but still setting Harry's teeth on edge. ØSo, I see my parents because theyíre what I want most?Æ Harry asked. ØYes, my dear boy,Æ Albus said, happy that his plans for the boy where falling into place even with the boy in Ravenclaw. He would still get his hero. ØWhat do you see, professor?Æ Harry asked. ØWhy, I see myself with a warm pair of socks,Æ Albus lied. ØDo you think I could come back here, professor? Itís just... I've never seen my parents before,Æ Harry made sure to mumble shyly. ØOh, my dear boy, you must not despair. You will see your parents again someday,Æ Dumbledore said, making Harry think about how Dumbledore was actively planning on reuniting them sooner rather than later. ØYou must remember though, Harry, the images the mirror shows are not real. It does not do well to dwell on them,Æ he warned. ØOkay, professor,Æ Harry said, conveying disappointment. ~ After that night, Harry had been busy preparing what was needed for him to work his plan. Ron had finally taken the hint and, although he would still come up to Harry to ask about their adventure, Harry was quick to tell him he hadnít found anything new. This left Harry with enough free time to finish his preparations. Harry pulled out a plain crystal he had ordered alongside his Christmas presents and placed it in the centre of a circle of runes he had spent an hour drawing. The ritual he was using was an old one used by conmen and thieves that had been banned by the ministry in the 1700ís, yet it suited Harry's purpose perfectly. Centring his magic, Harry started the chant he had been researching all week, making sure his pronunciation was as perfect as any mistake could have drastic effects. Once the ritual took hold, Harry watched in fascination as the crystal shimmered and took on the physical properties of the philosopherís stone. It wouldnít work as one, but it would fool anybody up until the moment they went to use it. It even had the same magical signature and presence. Feeling drained, Harry quickly hid the evidence of his ritual and placed the stone in his trunk, warding it with the strongest protections he could. The next day, Harry was still slightly exhausted and barely kept from snarling at Ron, who was trailing after him like a puppy. There were only two days left until the other students returned and Harry wanted to get the stone before then. ØYou sure you're alright, mate? You're looking kind of pale,Æ the redhead commented. Harry seeing his opening, he jumped at the chance. ØActually, I'm not feeling great, I think I'm just going to go back to bed,Æ he said. Ron looked disappointed. ØOh, yeah,Æ he grumbled, ØI suppose you should.Æ ØBye, then!Æ Harry said, practically racing away. Having spent the rest of the day resting, Harry soon found himself mentally and magically restored. Putting on a pair of black trousers and a dark grey jumper, Harry gathered up his invisibility cloak and his imposter stone. Putting the cloak on, Harry set his silencing and scent blocking charms before he made his way out of Ravenclaw tower and to the 3rd floor. Walking to the door, Harry once again observed the wards. Working carefully, Harry took his time manipulating them; he didnít want to break them as that would alert Dumbledore. Eventually, Harry found what he was looking for and managed to slip past them and into the room. Upon hearing the door open, all three of Fluffyís massive heads turned and let out growls. Lifting his wand, Harry flicked it and listened as a soft tune started to play. After a few minutes, the dog fell asleep, leaving Harry free to start his mission. Dropping into the pit of Devilís Snare, Harry kept his body loose and dropped through to the next level. Next, he came to the keys; reaching out, Harry tried a simple accio and watched as the key sailed through the air to him. Smirking, Harry unlocked the door before the other keys could embed themselves in him. Next, Harry approached the chess set and tried to move around without playing. However, it seemed like McGonagall actually anticipated this and Harry found his movements blocked. Sighing, Harry had to play his way across. Although not a chess master, Harry could actually hold his own in a game after years of watching Ron play. Harry managed to win the game without having to nobly sacrifice himself like the redhead had in his last life. Coming to the next room, Harry was hit with a foul odour. Knowing he couldnít kill or really harm the troll, Harry instead sent an overpowered sleeping charm, knowing that, with itís magical resistance, it should make the troll sleep for the next few hours. Snapeís task, Harry noted this time, was actually very clever and Harry wondered if the hat had considered Ravenclaw for the resident potions master. Picking up the solution that would allow him to pass through the flames, Harry entered the chamber. Approaching the mirror, Harry reached out and observed the carefully erected wards surrounding it. Stopping to find a way in, Harry eventually slipped passed without alerting them. The next part of his plan would be the hardest; Dumbledore's enchantments on the mirror where both subtle and in depth. Standing in front of the mirror, Harry thought about how he wanted the stone and didnít want to use it. Which was technically true - he didnít want to use the stone. Feeling the weight of it in his pocket, Harry took out his imposter stone and carefully manipulated the enchantments on the mirror. This was the hard part. After what seemed like hours, Harry's imposter stone finally disappeared, accepted by the enchantments. Going back over them, Harry spent time modifying the charms to perfectly mirror how he had found them. Eventually, Harry's task was done and he stood, exhausted. ØI see you are not wasting your new life being idle,Æ a deep voice said, startling Harry, who span, wand raised and ready to curse whoever had caught him. Seeing exactly who, or rather what, had snuck up on him, Harry let out a breath. ØDeath,Æ he greeted. ØMaster,Æ Death said, His grave voice seeming to fill the room without being loud. Harry stood and wondered why the powerful being was here; he knew he hadnít summoned Him. ØI am here as I said I would be. I did say I would check in from time to time,Æ Death said, making Harry wonder if the being could hear his thoughts even through his rings and Occlumency shields. ØOh,Æ Harry said, before he thought of a question to ask. ØDo you know of any way I can return Tomís sanity to him?Æ he asked. He had yet to find a solution. Death was silent, making Harry want to squirm as being in His presence was always overwhelming. ØThe magic he used to gain his immortalityîÆ Death managed to put an astonishing level of disdain behind the word, Øîis a magic I abhor. It splits the casterís very self, not just their soul, but their mind, magic, and emotion. To fix him, you must find a way to reconnect the pieces he cast off.Æ Harry withheld his sigh - he already knew that. ØYes, but do you know how to do that specifically?Æ he asked, barely stopping himself from sounding like he was whining. ØI do,Æ Death said. The silent åbutí easily heard. ØYet...?Æ Harry asked. ØI think that it would be best for you to work for the results,Æ Death said, making Harry want to cry with frustration. He was magically exhausted and Death was taunting him. ØI am not fond of those who use such magics. I think that your soulmate can suffer however long it takes you to find the solution.Æ ØBut there is a solution,Æ Harry wanted verified. ØYes,Æ Death confirmed. ØFine then, I will find it,Æ Harry vowed. He didn't blame Death for His decision, he just wasnít pleased. ØI have no doubt, master,Æ Death said. ØWhat about the stone?Æ Harry asked, thinking about Deathís words about those who sought immortality. Death seemed to come closer without really moving. ØAh, the famed Philosopherís Stone, you really have been quite busy, master. That stone I actually do like; Nicholas did spend such a long time working on it,Æ Death answered. ØSo, you wouldn't be angry if I used it one day? Or gave it to Tom?Æ Harry asked. ØNo,Æ Death said. ØI doubt you would wish to stay my master if your soulmate passed, and I would prefer him to use the stone over his other solution.Æ Harry smiled in relief. ØThank you.Æ ØI have done nothing,Æ Death said. ØI shall go now; I have seen you are not in need of me. Remember, you can call if need, but know I shall not always answer.Æ Like before, He disappeared into the shadows once again. Harry stood for a moment before crashing; he really was exhausted and the conversation hadnít helped. Barely able to stay awake, Harry just about managed to make his way back to Ravenclaw tower. Getting to his trunk, Harry pulled out the stone and observed it. For such a small item, it really had caused a lot of bother. Unlocking his trunk, Harry placed it in the most protected part and added numerous charms and even a few dark hexes to keep people out. At this point, Harry was barely awake, his magical energy was distinctly low and he struggled to undress before falling into a deep and dreamless sleep. The next day, Harry didnít wake up until well into the afternoon. Stretching, Harry felt refreshed; he was surprised at how drained he had been, but considering he never had need to do so much magic at once, he summarised that he shouldnít have been so shocked. Magic was like a muscle: it needed to be used and stretched. Yes, his magical core was large, but as he never had need to use much of it, especially as an eleven year old, it shouldnít surprise him that completing two such powerful rituals in just over 24 hours left him drained. Thinking about that, Harry considered ways of training up his magical stamina; it would be hard to do it undetected, but he thought the benefits would be worth it. He had gained control over his new power levels, no longer having bursts of magic when he was angry, but he had yet to really explore them. Harry had kept his power masked pretty much constantly; stretching it would probably do his a world of good. Making sure not to act suspicious when he finally went down to lunch, Harry made sure to casually look around and observed the staff. He didnít notice anything off and concluded that his plan had worked. He had gotten away with taking the stone. Thinking back on his conversation with Death, Harry almost wondered if he had imagined it in his sleep deprived state, before he flicked the thought away. ~ Chapter End Notes Shorter than usual but the next one is much longer, Hope you enjoy! ~ Annie Chapter 10: Allies andEnemies Chapter Notes See the end of the chapter for notes Previously Thinking about that, Harry considered ways of training up his magical stamina; it would be hard to do it undetected, but he thought the benefits would be worth it. He had gained control over his new power levels, no longer having bursts of magic when he was angry, but he had yet to really explore them. Harry had kept his power masked pretty much constantly; stretching it would probably do his a world of good. Making sure not to act suspicious when he finally went down to lunch, Harry made sure to casually look around and observed the staff. He didnít notice anything off and concluded that his plan had worked. He had gotten away with taking the stone. Thinking back on his conversation with Death, Harry almost wondered if he had imagined it in his sleep deprived state, before he flicked the thought away. ~ January 1992 Hogwarts When the students returned the next day, Harry was pleased to find himself back in the company of Neville and the girls. ØSo, how was your holiday? Did you do anything interesting?Æ Hannah asked. Harry smirked internally when he thought about all he had accomplished, but outwardly he remained passive. ØNot really, I mainly just tried to ignore Ronald,Æ Harry replied. ØWell, next time you will just have to take us up on our offers to have you over,Æ Susan said. ØIíll think about it, I promise,Æ Harry replied. ØYou had better,Æ she said before smiling. ØOh, and I wanted to thank you again for the hair pins. They really are beautiful.Æ ØItís okay, Sues, I'm glad you like them. The book you got me was amazing as well,Æ Harry said. He had started to read it just that morning and found that it gave clear instructions and suggestions about how to advance in creating mind shields and protections. After that, the group went on to talk about presents and their holidays, as though they hadnít already told each other through owls. Harry didnít mind as he sat silently and observed his friends. ~ Harry had been called up to Flitwickís office just after Christmas to have a chat with him; the meetings were for all first years to see how they were settling. Harry had been nervous about Flitwick as he wasnít sure if his goblin heritage would make him pick up on Harry's abnormalities. However, the diminutive professor hadnít, or, at least, he hadnít made it known he sensed anything different. ØHello, Harry,Æ the Charms professor had greeted jovially. ØGood afternoon, sir,Æ Harry replied. He hadnít really ever interacted with the part-goblin in his last life and was surprised to see how proactive he was in managing his house compared to McGonagall. He always spent the morning of every other Sunday in the common room, making himself available to help his students. ØWhy donít you take a seat for me, Harry?Æ Flitwick suggested. Sitting in the offered chair, Harry looked up and waited. ØWell Harry, from looking at your scores from this past term, I have to say: I am extremely proud of you. You're at the top of all of your classes and you're producing results that are well beyond the normal capabilities of a first year student.Æ Harry contained his scowl. He had tried to bring his work level down, but even with that he outclassed his peers by a considerable margin. ØThank you, sir,Æ he said. He didnít mind being thought of as a prodigy, but he felt bad for his peers competing against him. ØNow, from looking at your results, I can clearly see you're not having any problem with the work. However, I wouldnít be doing my job if I didnít ask; is there any area you feel you need help or support in?Æ ØNo, sir,Æ Harry said honestly. ØThat could obviously change, but at the moment I find the work load and classes to be fine.Æ ØGood, good,Æ Flitwick said before a slightly darker look appeared on his face. ØNow Harry, I know that you and I havenít really had a chance to talk much this year; however, I do want you to know that I will always stand by my students and fight for them,Æ he said seriously. Harry was confused by the sudden turn and let it show in this voice. ØThank you, sir?Æ he said questioningly. ØOver the holidays I received a note from Madam Pomfrey - now, before you get upset, she did not in any way betray your trust. She simply asked for me to keep an eye on you as she had not had a chance to run her check up. Knowing she would not be able to give any insight, I took it upon myself to write to your family asking if they knew about any health concerns as I doubted you would want to talk to me yourself as you hadnít do so. The note I received back shocked me, and I am honestly unsure what to do with the information.Æ Harry was surprised by the manís actions as it showed how dedicated he was to his students. Handing over the piece of paper, Harry read the note with interest. ~ Professor Flittle, If the freak is ill, then itís not our fault! We wanted nothing to do with him from the start! Let the cretin die for all we care! We were promised that if we took him in, your lot would leave us alone. We do not appreciate you and your freakish owls being sent to our NORMAL home. You forced him on us, that old man promised he would be normal if we worked him hard enough! He's your problem now! Leave us alone in the future; we do not care what happens to the boy. Mr. and Mrs. Vernon Dursley. ~ Harry couldnít help but be thankful for his relativesí stupidity. They had just gained him another potential ally. Harry, making sure to turn in on himself, handed the letter back, before letting out a quiet sigh, ØOh.Æ ØNow Harry, the abuse of a minor is a serious matter in our world. Magical children are to be cherished. This letter indicates that your home life is less than ideal,Æ Flitwick said with concern. Shuffling in his seat, as though uncomfortable, Harry spoke, ØWell, my family... they donít like anything they consider abnormal, and magic is just that. They're muggles you see, and they hated it if I ever did accidental magic. They would treat me... worse... when it happened. Theyíve always said that they didnít want me and that they were forced to take me in.Æ Flitwick swore in gobbledegook, much to Harry's hidden amusement. ØHarry, I would like you to be honest with me: do they abuse you?Æ he asked softly. Harry squirmed. ØI wouldnít say that... they just....Æ Harry trailed off as though scared and confused. ØHave they ever hit you?Æ he asked. Harry nodded softly and whispered, ØYes.Æ ØWhat else, Harry...?Æ Flitwick asked, his rage hidden on his face but his eyes dark and stormy. ØThey wouldnít feed me,Æ Harry whispered. ØIs there anything else?Æ Flitwick asked almost fearfully. Harry could image the horrors that some teachers were forced to see and was suddenly glad that his abuse had only been physical. ØNo,Æ Harry said. ØThey would just beat me and lock me up without food sometimes.Æ Nodding, Flitwick let out a long breath. ØMr. Potter, thank you, I know that must have been very hard for you to admit,Æ he said. Harry sat silently, making sure to keep his head down, an idea forming in his mind. ØWho did they mean, professor? In the letter, I mean. They said åthat old maní forced them to take me,Æ Harry asked. Flitwick kept his face carefully blank. ØI'm not sure, Harry. I have my suspicions, but I canít truthfully answer you.Æ He was positive he knew, but he couldnít work out why. What did Dumbledore gain from forcing Harry on people who didnít want him? He couldnít imagine it was because of their family bond, as obviously Harry's family didnít feel it. Filius was confused and concerned as he ran through his list of thoughts. ØWhatís going to happen now?Æ Harry asked. ØWell Harry, I canít promise anything yet, but I promise you this: I will find out what has been going on,Æ he vowed. He would have to make some subtle inquires to check and see if Albus was aware of Harry's home situation. He didnít want to believe that he knew, but he remembered the man talking about Harry growing up. He had said the boy was loved and cared for. What had changed? Harry considered his head of house for a moment and weighed his words and actions against what he knew about the man from the past. Coming to a decision, Harry smiled lightly. ØProfessor, if I told you something, would you keep it to secret? Even from, say, your boss or somebody?Æ Harry asked. Flitwick considered the boyís words. ØWell Harry, I would, depending on the secret. If I thought you would be in danger I would have to tell somebody if it meant helping you,Æ he said honestly, wondering what the boy wanted to say. ØWell then, professor, you should know that with the help of Chief Ragnok and a law wizard, I'm currently in the process of getting Sirius Black freed.Æ Flitwickís eyes had widened at hearing Ragnokís name; with his being part goblin, he knew the importance of having the head goblinís personal help. However, at hearing Siriusí name, he actually spluttered. ØWhy, Harry?!Æ he asked, shocked. ØAs you have probably realised sir, my memory is rather exceptional. I have memories from when I was very young. I remember things about that night and I know Sirius is innocent. When I returned to this world and I realised what had happened to him, I started the process of getting him freed. How he ended up in Azkaban without a trial, I will never know,Æ Harry said. He was, of course, lying about how he knew, but he didnít want go into detail about being the Master of Death. Flitwick sat for a few moments, his face contorting as he processed all the information Harry had just given him. ØHow did you come to know the head of Gringottsí London branch?Æ he finally asked. Having Ragnokís personal help... well, he was blown away. ØWhen I re-entered this world, I was told he was personally overseeing my accounts,Æ Harry said. ØWhy? Is it a big deal?Æ He wanted to come across as naive. ØYes, actually, it is. As the head of Gringotts bank, Ragnok has the power to act on behalf of the goblin nation. By taking in interest in you and supporting you, he is declaring the support of all the goblins in England,Æ Flitwick said, astonished. He had heard whisperings about a new goblin friend being named and he had to wonder: if it was the small boy in front of him, why? ØOh, I didnít know that,Æ Harry said, making sure to seem shocked. ØYou are sure that Sirius Black is innocent?Æ Flitwick asked, going back to Harry's statement. ØYes. I know he didnít betray my parents - it was Peter, and I doubt he would have killed twelve innocent people. He just needs a trial, he never had one, I asked,Æ Harry said earnestly. ØPeter? Peter Pettigrew?Æ Flitwick repeated. ØYes, he brought Voldemort to the house. I remember,Æ Harry lied. ØOh, I see....Æ Flitwick said, his face full of concern at the thought of Harry remembering such tragic events. He had realised that Harry possessed a rare magical ability with his eidetic memory, but he had never considered this possibility. ØI just want Sirius to have a trial. Heís my godfather, you see. If he gets cleared, I will be able to live with him and not have to worry about going back to the Dursleys,Æ Harry explained softly. Flitwick gained an understanding look. ØYou donít have to worry about that, Harry. I will do everything in my power to make sure you never have to return to a home where you're hurt. And, if what you say about Mr. Black is true, then I will do everything I can to support you both,Æ he vowed. Harry smiled. ØThank you, sir, I really didnít want to return to them. I donít know why I was placed there to begin with,Æ Harry said, looking up and watching as the seeds were planted. His work was slow going, but he would have the world questioning Dumbledore; so far, he had Amelia Bones, Madam Pomfrey, and Severus. Adding the goblins and Flitwick, Harry was happy to see his web spreading. The sooner he started to chip away at the old cootís fan base, the sooner he could get his revenge. Flitwick looked pensive as he hummed. ØNo, I donít know the reason either, Harry. But I will look into it,Æ he swore. ØNow, why donít you run along? Oh, and Harry? Do try not to worry.Æ Harry stood and sent him a small smile. ØOkay sir, and thank you,Æ he said. ØNo, Mr. Potter, thank you. You have given me a lot to think about.Æ With that, Harry left, happy with what he had accomplished. ~ The next few weeks seemed to rush by and before Harry knew it, March was turning into April and the older year Ravenclaws seemed to go mental. They wrote out study and exam timetables and Harry was practically hogtied to a chair and forced to sit through Mariaís speech on proper preparation for tests. It had become common knowledge that Harry was gifted and, at first, his fellow Ravenclaws had been hostile about this fact, but they had quickly stopped when they saw he was willing to help them. The rumours that he was a prodigy had Harry wanting to laugh; he was only considered such as he had already done all of the lessons. In truth, during his last life, Harry thought himself to be an average student. Yes, his Defence work had been strong but, other than that, he had been level with the rest of this group. Although, when he thought about it, without the blocks Harry did have a large amount of knowledge to draw from, yet that only helped him so much. He would never be a Potions master; he simply didnít have the intrinsic skill in brewing or the patience for long term brews. Nor would he ever be great at Herbology, he didnít have the touch. The same could be said about Divination, Harry simply lacked the ability. Yes, he could give endless facts and spout obscure knowledge, but he would never master the subjects. Since his talk with Flitwick, the part goblin had made sure to check in on Harry during his biweekly stops in the common room. Harry had noticed how he seemed to now watch the headmaster more, with looks of consideration on his face. Harry barely stopped himself from cackling the first time he had noticed this during dinner. Harry had also finally gotten around to having his checkup with Pomfrey and was happy to have his potions reduced to three times a week for his bones and every other day for his weight. His scar ointment was still being used daily, but they had faded considerably and would probably only need to be used for another three months or so; not all of his scars would fade, but Harry was pleased to note that they would be faint. After being released from the speech about preparations, Harry made his way to lunch. ØAre you prepared for the exams, Harry?Æ asked Terry. He had taken to hanging around Harry and his friends more. ØWhat are you asking about exams for? They're ages away!Æ came Ron's distinctive voice as he appeared. He had been trying to worm his way into their group and had taken to just appearing and inserting himself into conversations. Terry looked aghast. ØThey're not! We only have a few more weeks!Æ ØWhatever. Like Harry needs to worry about stupid exams being who he is,Æ Ron said arrogantly, making Harry wince and the group stare at the redhead. ØWhatís that supposed to mean?Æ piped up Hannah, who had joined them along with Susan. Usually the girls would just ignore Ron, especially as he had taken to going on about the faults of the other houses compared to Gryffindor - they really were proud åPuffs. ØWell, itís obvious: he's the Boy-Who-Lived. Why should he worry about exams? Heroes donít need tests.Æ Harry rolled his eyes at his friends. ØListen Ronald, I do not care about any silly titles. Exams are important,Æ he said slowly. Ron huffed, ØI donít understand why you're being like this, Harry. Over Christmas I thought I had gotten you to understand.Æ ØUnderstand what?Æ ØThat you are destined for greater things. You're a hero!Æ Ron said. Harry snorted. ØI'm no hero,Æ he said decisively. ØBut what about the three headed dog? You were so close to uncovering a mystery - to being a hero!Æ Ron whined. ØWhat three headed dog, Harry?Æ Susan asked. Harry thought for a moment and had a moment of brilliance. ØThere's a Cerberus on the 3rd floor,Æ he said. ØA-a Cerberus?Æ she squeaked. ØReally? They're very rare! An XXXX level beast....Æ Terry, extremely excited by the prospect, began to recite facts about them. ØYou saw it?Æ Susan asked after she had calmed down. ØYeah. During Christmas I accidently wandered to the third floor when I wasnít paying attention; I just walked right into the room with it,Æ Harry said. ØWait. There's no protections or wards stopping people from going near it?Æ Susan asked quickly. Harry, glad that she had picked some traits from her aunt, replied, ØNo, nothing. I was shocked. I wonder what Dumbledore's up to - keeping such a dangerous beast in a school with children? With no protections stopping them from stumbling into it? Itís all very strange.Æ Susanís eyes had narrowed. ØI agree. I think my aunt would as well,Æ she mumbled. ØWhat the hell, Harry?! How dare you question Dumbledore?! He's the greatest wizard since Merlin!Æ Ron shouted. ØI was just wondering what he was thinking not even warding the door against entry,Æ Harry said. ØI canít believe you!Æ Ron screamed. ØYouíre supposed to be a hero! Not questioning Dumbledore like some dark wizard!Æ ØHow does me thinking the headmaster made a mistake make me a dark wizard?Æ Harry asked. ØIf it does, then I must be a dark witch,Æ Susan said, standing next to Harry and glaring at Ron. ØMe too,Æ said Hannah. Ron stood there, face turning red, before he span on his heel and stormed off, knocking into Neville who had just turned the corner and causing the blond boy to fall to the ground. Helping him up, Harry said, ØSorry about that.Æ ØItís not your fault, Harry,Æ Neville said, dusting himself off. ØWhat happened this time?Æ he asked. ØWell, according to Ron, weíre all dark wizards for thinking the headmaster is wrong to keep a level XXXX beast inside the castle without even setting wards around it,Æ Harry said. Neville let out a squeak, ØA-a levelî?Æ ØîYeah, there's a Cerberus on the 3rd floor! Harry saw it,Æ Terry interrupted excitedly. Harry hadnít known he was a creature enthusiast. ØI see,Æ Neville said faintly. Harry was almost worried he would pass out. ØAre you really going to tell your aunt?Æ Harry asked Susan as the group made their way down to the lake. ØYes. I think she would like to know; she's not been happy with Dumbledore since I told her îÆ Susan cut herself off and looked at Harry quickly. ØItís okay if you told her about my family. I should have expected it,Æ Harry said, internally cheering. Slowly, but surely, he would ruin Dumbledore's carefully crafted image. Susan smiled softly. ØI'm sorry, Harry. I didnít mean to betray you or anything, I just couldnít not tell her - she could help. With her job, I thought it would be okay,Æ she explained, rambling in her fear. ØItís okay Susie, I'm not angry,Æ Harry said, soothing her nerves. ØBut I donít understand why your aunt would be mad at Dumbledore for how my family treats me,Æ he said, pretending to be confused. ØWell, when I wrote to her, she apparently went to work and looked up your file. Dumbledore has classified it and it took her some time to get a copy. Apparently, heís your magical guardian and he should have been checking up on you. That he didnít is bad as you're a known wizard, without even mentioning your status in our world,Æ Susan explained. ØShe spoke to me about it over Christmas and told me it was strange. She promised to look into it more.Æ ØThank you,Æ Harry said. ØIt is strange, when you think about it,Æ Neville commented. ØOh?Æ Harry asked, wondering what the blond thought about this. He was hoping to convert them all to him and he worried that, with Neville's history with Death Eaters, he would never accept Harry once he learnt the truth. ØYeah. I mean, I know theyíre your closest family, but usually when a magical child is orphaned, they're kept in our world. Itís just easier on them to adjust. I've never heard of a magical child being given to muggles.Æ ØClosest family? I thought they were my only family,Æ Harry said. ØWell, they're your direct family, but you should know that all pure blooded families are related. Hell, I think me and you share a great aunt or something, so really placing you with a magical family wouldnít have been hard,Æ Neville explained. Harry was slightly surprised. He knew that purebloods all intermarried, but he hadnít thought he had any relation to the Longbottom's. Then again, he had never really seen the Potter family tree. ØI didnít know that,Æ he said honestly before he smiled. ØSo, weíre related then?Æ he asked. ØDistantly,Æ Neville said. A silence fell then before Hannah voiced what they had all been thinking. ØItís also strange how Dumbledore didnít check on you,Æ she said. ØYou would think he would want to keep a close eye on you after everything.Æ ØYeah, well, I guess weíll never know,Æ Harry said. Seeing their doubtful faces, Harry hid his glee and instead distracted them, talking about the up and coming quidditch match. Harry hadnít been bothered about the game since he had come back, but he was grateful for it now as he distracted his friends. ~ Meanwhile, Dumbledore was not pleased. He had thought he had the boy under his control when, over Christmas, he had started going after the clues left to him about the stone. Yet, for the last few months, there was nothing! The boy had gone back to ignoring Ronald and stopped acting how he was supposed to. Albus was not happy; and to make things worse, he was sure that Harry had said something to Filius as the part goblin had been wary around him recently when he had been asking for reports on his pawn. Even Severus had nothing to say when he asked - which would not do! He had spent decades planning this and he would be damned if an eleven year old would destroy all he had worked for! Sitting down to worry at his lemon drop, Albus thought of how he could get Harry back on the correct path. ~ July 9th 1992 Hogwarts It was the day before exams before anything interesting happened to Harry. He had been waiting for Neville by the doors to the great hall when he had accidently overheard a secret conversation between Dumbledore and Hagrid. Their obvious set up was sickening to Harry as he listened. ØNow Hagrid, you must be watchful. With me gone for a few days, the stone will be unguarded.Æ Harry heard Albus say in a grave voice, barely keeping his eyes from rolling. ØI will headmaster, but I doubt anybody would be able to get past Fluffy. Nobody knows that it takes music to put him to sleep,Æ Hagrid said. ØI pray youíre right, my old friend. As you know, I suspect Voldemort is after the stone....Æ Harry wondered if they honestly thought he was that stupid and walked away from them, moving to wait slightly away from the doors. The two continued to have their super secret meeting without their audience; Harry really didnít feel like playing their game. ØYou alright, mate?Æ Neville asked when he arrived and saw the mutinous scowl on Harry's face. ØYes, sorry,Æ Harry said, trying to cover his dark expression. ØJust worried about exams,Æ he lied. Neville laughed. ØWhy? You know that you're going to have the best marks in our year,Æ he said. Harry smiled slightly. ØMaybe, but still, itís normal to be nervous.Æ ØLetís head to breakfast,Æ Neville suggested. Walking in, Harry directed Neville to the Hufflepuff table. He wanted to avoid his fellow ravens as exam fever had hit them hard and he really didnít fancy having Ron pop up. Joining the girls, Harry sat down and made a plate for himself. ØMorning guys,Æ Hannah greeted. ØGood morning,Æ they said, before they fell into conversation about the up coming exams and holidays. ØWhat are you going to do about the holidays?Æ Susan asked, concerned about her friend. ØI'm not sure,Æ Harry said honestly. ØFlitwick wants to meet me after our exams are over.Æ ØYou told him then?Æ Susan asked. ØYeah, after Christmas he found out,Æ Harry told them. ØGood,Æ Neville said. ØYeah, hopefully he will sort it out. My auntís still looking into it, but she said itís hard as your files are locked,Æ Susan told them. ØYou are welcome to spend the summer with me,Æ Neville offered. ØOh, and you should definitely visit us, we live next to each other,Æ Hannah added. ØIíll try to see you all,Æ Harry promised. The owl mail arrived then and Harry saw a familiar small brown owl that he knew belonged to Edgar Dodge. Cutting up his bacon fat, Harry placed it in a small dish and gave it to the owl as he took the letter. ~ Mr. Potter, I believe now would be a good time for you to acquire that piece of evidence. I have managed to secure an emergency trial for the innocent party, which will be taking place in two weeks. I believe with the evidence you supply and his own testimony, you will soon be reunited with your godfather. If you would bring the evidence to Gringotts at your earliest convenience, I will arrange for it to be found by a reliable Auror. Sincerely, Edgar Dodge Jr Law Wizard, Dodge E & Dodge Eír ~ Reading the letter, Harry let out a relieved breath: he wouldnít have to spend the entire summer at the Dursleys. Now, all he had to do was capture that rat. ØGood news?Æ Neville asked, seeing Harry's smile. ØOh yeah, great,Æ Harry said absently as he planned - and he knew just the way to get into Gryffindor tower. Looking up and seeing that the hall was full, Harry decided to collect his evidence now. ØHey guys, I've just remembered Iíve left my Charms work in my room. Iíll meet you all later,Æ he said. ØOkay, Harry,Æ they agreed. Sneaking into the lionís den was ridiculously easy with his cloak. All Harry had to do was wait for somebody to say the password and he slipped in with them undetected. Making his way up to the first year dorms, Harry saw the mess that was Ron's bed. With a grimace of disgust, he approached the cage that Pettigrew was sleeping in. Stunning the already sleeping rat, Harry reached in and grabbed him. Returning to his dorm, Harry transfigured a shoe into a cage and set about warding it. Placing the rat inside, Harry revived him and sent a stinging hex. Seeing the rat suddenly jerk awake, Harry smiled, not realising how sadistic he looked in that moment. ØHello, Peter,Æ he said, enjoying how the rat panicked and let out a pained squeak as he obviously tried to transform and found himself unable. ØNow, now Peter, you wouldnít want to do that. This cage is special. You wonít be able to transform or escape from it,Æ Harry said. Harry flicked his wand so that a small food and water dispenser appeared; it wouldnít be good for the rat to die before he had gotten Sirius free. Placing the cage in his trunk, Harry barely kept from dancing in celebration. Things were working out perfectly. ~ July 15th 1992 Å Hogwarts He should have known that he would jinx himself he thought as he was called up to Dumbledore's office after his final exam later that week. Quirrell had vanished two days ago, most likely with the fake stone, and Dumbledore had been looking grave and resentful whenever Harry had seen him. Entering the office, Harry saw that it was in shambles; the headmaster had obviously lost hold of his temper. ØAh, Harry, my boy, do come in,Æ Dumbledore said, his voice strained as he obviously forced himself to act like a benevolent old man. ØYou wanted to see me, headmaster?Æ Harry said, his voice light and inquisitive. ØYes, my boy, I was just wondering if you had noticed anything strange recently?Æ he asked. ØStrange, sir?Æ Harry replied, sounding confused. ØYes, with your scar?Æ Dumbledore said, his eyes gleaming. ØNo, sir,Æ Harry said. Dumbledore seemed to deflate. ØI see,Æ he said, his voice void of emotion. ØWhy, sir?Æ Harry asked, keeping his voice childlike. ØI have reason to believe Voldemort will likely return,Æ Dumbledore said, his voice suddenly going dramatically grave. Harry made his eyes widen in shock. ØV-Voldemort, sir?! Why would you think that? I thought he was gone,Æ Harry said fearfully. ØOh no, my dear boy, heís out there waiting,Æ Dumbledore said darkly. ØI'm sorry professor, but what has this got to do with my scar?Æ Harry asked, wondering what the old man would say. ØI believe, Harry, that your scar connects the two of you, and I had thought you would feel it if something had happened or if he was close,Æ Dumbledore said, his shrewd eyes scanning Harry. Harry just contained his snort. Yes, the scar connected them, not the fact they were soulmates. ØI'm sorry sir, but I've felt nothing,Æ he said. Dumbledore's eyes narrowed with suspicion, the twinkle disappearing. ØI see. You may go then, Harry,Æ he dismissed, forgetting to act like a kind old man in his anger. As Harry walked to the door, he called out, ØOh, and Harry? Donít mention this to anybody, it wouldnít do to worry them. Perhaps I am wrong about my fears,Æ he said. Harry nodded his head and left the office. He had his meeting with Flitwick as well before the feast, and would have to hurry to get to his office. After Harry left, Albus picked up a paper weight from his desk and launched it at the door. ØDamn him!Æ he screamed. ØTemper, temper, Albus,Æ mocked the portrait of Phineas Black. ØSilence, or so help me, I will find a way to have you removed!Æ Albus seethed. He couldnít work out how Voldemort had taken the stone; he didnít even know how long it had been missing! His protections around the mirror itself had been infallible! He should have felt it the moment the stone vanished but he had felt nothing! And Harry, that damn boy! He refused to take the bait, this was his fault! If he had just gone after the stone, then everything would be alright now! But no, the boy refused to take the bait, refused to be what Albus had planned for. This would not do! ~ Reaching Flitwick's office just in time, Harry knocked and entered, seeing Snape and Pomfrey in there as well. ØHello sirs, maíam,Æ he said. ØHarry, take a seat,Æ Flitwick said, indicating to the chair left free. Sitting, Harry turned to the three adults. ØYou wanted to see me, sir?Æ he asked. ØYes. We all here in this room are aware of your situation at home,Æ Flitwick began. Harry made sure to dart his eyes to Snape, hoping it would be picked up on. ØYes Potter, I am aware,Æ the dark eyed man said. ØI recognised the signs and, based on the potions I have observed you taking and the ointment you require, I pieced the facts together.Æ ØOh,Æ Harry said, trying to act ashamed. ØAnyway, both Madam Pomfrey and Professor Snape have approached me to ask about your summer plans, and I felt it would be best if you answered their questions as I promised the information you told me last time would be secret,Æ Flitwick explained. Harry was surprised at that. Not that Flitwick had kept his promise, but that Snape had worried about him enough to ask. He was also surprised that Madam Pomfrey had gone to Flitwick instead of Dumbledore; he hadnít considered what he would have done if she had gone to the old coot. ØI see. Thank you, professor,Æ Harry said to his head of house. Turning to look at the other two occupants of the room, Harry smiled lightly. ØI am very grateful for your concern professor, Madam Pomfrey, but you shouldnít worry. I will be fine this summer, I've been making alternate arrangements,Æ Harry said. Severus picked up on the vagueness of the answer and narrowed his eyes. It wouldnít be unexpected for an abused child to lie. ØWhat arrangements, exactly?Æ he asked. Harry tried not to scowl at the manís insistence. ØI would rather not say. It has come to my attention that it was Professor Dumbledore who placed me with my relatives and I would hate for him to be aware of my plans,Æ Harry said. ØI see,Æ Severus replied. He was impressed the boy had discovered that much, but his answer also worried him. Why did Potter not trust Dumbledore? ØYou will be safe, dear?Æ Pomfrey asked. ØYes, maíam,Æ Harry replied. ØI donít like this, but I've known Albus for years. He never listens to me when I tell him about children I fear come from bad homes. Itís the reason I go to the head of houses now,Æ she said, sighing. Harry was shocked by this and wondered how Dumbledore maintained his great image if his staff knew he ignored abuse. Severus looked at Filius and saw that the manís eyes were hard. ØYou are aware of Mr. Potterís plans?Æ he asked. ØI'm aware of some of them,Æ Flitwick replied. ØYou trust he will be alright?Æ Severus asked. He respected Filius as the man would always go out of his way for his students. Filius looked at the boy sitting in front of him and considered all he knew about Harry Potter. He was a mystery; he had friends within the goblin race and his abilities were beyond that of his age. He also knew a lot about this world for somebody who had apparently just entered it. However, he trusted the boy. He had seen him spend hours teaching wand movements to his fellow first years, watched as he united the houses. ØI trust Mr. Potter knows what he is doing,Æ Flitwick said. Harry contained his smile. Severus, who had been watching Filius as he thought about his answer, nodded his head. ØVery well, then. However, Mr. Potter, I will expect you to owl me if you find yourself in trouble or in need of assistance,Æ he said, looking at the boy who reminded him so much of Lily. ØYes, sir,Æ Harry said. ~ After that, Harry was allowed to go to the feast. Sitting down with his housemates, Harry smiled at them as they celebrated their first year being complete. Unlike in his last life, when the house cup was awarded, it went to Slytherin. Harry received no unexpected points. Clapping politely for the snake house, Harry returned to his dorm with his roommates and started to pack. Getting on the train the next day, Harry shared his compartment with the same people he had arrived at Hogwarts with. ØYou look happy, Harry,Æ Susan commented, surprised as she thought the boy would be upset about returning to his family. ØI am happy,Æ Harry said. ØItís been a great year.Æ He thought about what he had accomplished. He had gotten his revenge on Hermione, though his gut still churned sometimes at the thought of what happened, started to plant the seeds needed to destroy Dumbledore's reputation, gained the stone, and gotten Sirius a trial. A very good year, indeed. ØIt has been great,Æ Neville agreed. Eventually the journey was underway and they all made plans to meet up, Harry promising to try. He knew he would be busy, though, what with Siriusí trial and everything. Pulling into the station hours later, Harry left his friends, promising to write and visit. Susan had been against letting him leave as she had wanted her aunt to meet his relatives, but Harry had persuaded her to let him go. It wasnít like he was actually going home to Surrey. Finding a secluded spot, Harry shrunk down his trunk and let Hedwig free, telling her to head to the Leaky Cauldron, before he apparated there. Walking out into Diagon Alley, Harry headed straight to Gringotts. He had to see a goblin about a rat. Chapter End Notes Guess who's laptop has decided to commit suicide? The next update should be on Monday... Hope you're enjoying the story!
