Sirius followed him as he entered and watched as Harry and Ragnok greeted each other. ØMr. Black, it is a pleasure to meet you,Æ Ragnok said. Sirius startled slightly as he had never been greeted so politely by a goblin. ØChief Ragnok, the honour is mine,Æ he recited, his mind and body remembering the manners his parents had instilled in him years ago. ØHarry has told me a lot about you,Æ Ragnok continued. ØHe speaks fondly of you also,Æ Sirius said. Turning to Harry, Ragnok spoke, ØHe knows about the compulsions?Æ ØYes, I informed him about everything I told Remus. Sirius is here to be cleansed - and maybe healed, if youíre willing,Æ Harry said. Ragnok nodded, acknowledging the limitations of Siriusí knowledge. ØThe damage for dementor exposure is extensive, but we should be able to help - for a price, of course.Æ ØOf course,Æ Harry acknowledged. ØAnd any compulsions will be removed, along with blocks and potions,Æ Ragnok said. ØAnd the price?Æ Harry asked. Ø90 galleons,Æ Ragnok said. Harry smirked at the higher price. Ragnok had obviously enjoyed their last haggling match. Ø60.Æ Ø75.Æ Ø70,Æ Harry said. ØDeal,Æ Ragnok replied. Sirius watched this in shocked silence; he didnít know people argued money with goblins and had been worried when Harry had started to bargain. Ragnok clicked his fingers, sending for Mauve Harry assumed, before he turned to Sirius. ØMr. Back. Are you aware that your Lordship is currently unclaimed?Æ he asked, not beating around the bush. Sirius was confused. He had thought being a convict would strip him of his title. ØNo,Æ he said honestly. ØBut I thoughtè?Æ ØThe old conviction laws were overturned at the end of the last war. You are still able to claim your title and I would recommend you do so,Æ Ragnok informed him. Sirius nodded his head. ØOkay. I just need to put on the ring and see if it accepts me, right?Æ he asked more than stated. ØCorrect,Æ Ragnok said. ØShould I do it now or after?Æ he asked. Just then Healer Mauve entered. Seeing Harry, she smirked. ØAre you going to be bringing every wizard in Britain to me to be cleansed, Little Lord?Æ she asked. ØMy lady, I shall only bring those who deserve the best,Æ he said, bowing slightly to the female goblin. Mauve let out a low chuckle before she looked at Sirius. ØThe dementors have been having a feast from you,Æ she said. Sirius nodded. ØTen years,Æ he said. Mauve tsked and shook her head. ØI canít make you perfect in one go, but I can make you better. You will follow me,Æ she said. Sirius looked bewildered as the female moved towards the door. Harry smiled. ØShe does that - you should follow her. I will be here waiting when youíre done. Iíll have your ring brought up,Æ Harry said. Sirius nodded and walked to Harry, kissing his head before he darted after the healer. ~ After Sirius had gone, Harry turned to Ragnok. He recited everything that happened in during and after the trial and watched as the goblin took in the information. Ragnok sat in silence as he spoke, digesting the words and thinking about the actions of all those involved. ØDid you actually send a letter?Æ he asked Harry. Harry smirked. ØYes, I sent it after I left Remus here. I thought it would be a good thing to have if I was ever questioned.Æ ~ July 18th 1992, The Leaky Cauldron Harry picked up a piece of parchment and grabbed an ink pot. Placing them on the table, he began to write. ~ Dear Aunt Petunia, I know you asked to never be written to or bothered by the wizarding world again, but I thought I should inform you that I planned on staying in the wizarding world for the summer with friends of my parents and my own school friends. If you are worried, I can be reached by owl if it is addressed to me. Yours, Harry Potter ~ The letter he received back, Harry had opened with glee. He had the letter in his trunk, waiting to be used as evidence. ~ Stop using such a barbaric means of communication! We do not care what you do, just leave us alone. You can get hit by lightning for all we care. Do not contact us again! We are good, normal people. Mr. and Mrs. Vernon and Petunia Dursley ~ July 23rd 1992, Gringotts London ØYou have the letter?Æ Ragnok asked after Harry had told him what it said. ØYes, in my trunk,Æ Harry confirmed. ØExcellent. Now, what are you going to do about the meeting tomorrow?Æ Ragnok asked. Harry considered what to do for a few moments before speaking. ØSirius will be at St. Mungoís, so Iíll take Remus. I want to report my relatives for abuse. Amelia knows about it anyway, so really itís just providing evidence now. She is also looking into Dumbledore for me, according to Susan.Æ ØSpeaking of your young friends, have you spoken to them?Æ Ragnok asked. Harry winced. They would not be pleased when they found out Harry had been hiding Sirius. ØNo, I plan on asking them to meet me in the Alley the day Lockhart has his book signing. I have business that day,Æ Harry said. ØVery good,Æ Ragnok said. ØWhat about your other plans?Æ ØI have ideas, but nothing yet solid. I need to destroy Dumbledoreís fan base - people need to see him for what he isè.Æ As Harry spoke, an idea hit him and his smile turned devious. ØYou have an idea,Æ Ragnok said rather than asked. ØI do. Tell me, what do you know about a reporter named Rita Skeeter?Æ Harry asked. Ragnokís eyes widened at the thought of using the famous gossip journalist. ØHer stories never say what you want them to - sheís unreliable.Æ ØAh, but I know how to make her do exactly what I want,Æ Harry said, smirking. ØBlackmail?Æ Ragnok asked, a smirk curling his own mouth. ØIíd prefer åpersuasive handlingí,Æ Harry said. ØIf you can get her under control, then youíre in business. She has the largest and widest following in the UK. You will just have to be careful; some will discount her because she usually just sprouts gossip.Æ ØBut her gossip is always surprisingly true, if not just twisted,Æ Harry said. ØYou want to use her to spread your web of disheartenment about Dumbledore.Æ ØYes,Æ Harry said. ØWith her, I can gain a control of the media. Not total, but enough to keep what I want in the cycle.Æ Ragnok smiled toothily. ØAnd have you had any other success with your bonded?Æ Harry felt his smile fade slightly. ØNo. I know there is an answer out there, but I have yet to find it,Æ he said. Ragnok nodded his head in a show of support. ØI can ask around in discrete circles,Æ he offered. ØThat would be appreciated. I plan on scouring the Black library once Sirius reclaims his homes,Æ Harry admitted. ØA wise idea,Æ Ragnok said. A knock at the door sounded then and Harry felt an odd, familial pull as Sirius entered. The sensation was completely new to him. Seeing Harryís face, Healer Mauve explained to him, ØYour godfather bond is now open. The bond being blocked on either side makes it impossible for the other to feel it, as itís not an overwhelming bond like, say, a soul bond as it is created, not intrinsic.Æ ØOh,Æ Harry said dumbly. ØWill it always feel like this?Æ ØIt should fade and settle; itís just pronounced now as it had been blocked,Æ Mauve said. Seeing Sirius pacing in the doorway, Harry stood and approached him. Trying to send a sense of calm through the bond, something he had never tried before, but had read about in his mind magic books. ØSiri, calm down. I need you to be calm right now,Æ Harry pleaded, hoping Siriusí need to be there for Harry would get through to him. It worked immediately: Sirius paused and grabbed Harry, pulling the small boy into his arms and hugging him. Harry noticed Sirius looked a lot better, more like the man he remembered. His hair and skin looked healthier and his magic, although agitated, felt stronger. Pulling free from the hug, Harry pulled Sirius down to the chairs and made him sit. Ragnok nodded at them. ØFirst, I believe you should claim your Lordship, Mr. Black, before we discuss your healers report. As a Lord, you have more power and protection,Æ the goblin said. Sirius let out a breath. ØI agree,Æ he said formally, surprising Harry. A formal Sirius was something completely new and a little foreign to the younger man. Ragnok seemed to reach under his desk and pulled out a box Harry recognised from when he claimed his Lordships in his last life. ØIf the magic accepts you, the ring will remain and provide you with protections. If your Lordship doesnít take, it will return to the Black family vault for Heir Black to claim it,Æ Ragnok said. Sirius turned to Harry when Ragnok said Heir Black and Harry nodded, letting him know he was said Heir Black. ØI understand,Æ Sirius said, picking up the ring box and opening it. Harry watched as he placed the ring on his finger and waited to see if it would accept him. His Sirius had never claimed the Lordship once he had escaped. Reaching out with his magic, Harry felt the moment Sirius became Lord Black; the magic seemed to hum in harmony and Harry felt a moment of bliss. ØLord Black,Æ Ragnok said, inclining his head to Sirius, who smirked. ØThank you,Æ Sirius said. ØThe next area of business we have to discuss is Dumbledore. As you saw earlier today, he has taken an unusual amount of interest in Harry,Æ Ragnok said. Harry saw Sirius clench his fists. ØI had compulsions and charms all over me, making me reject my family and hate Slytherins and Dark magic - to act reckless and disregard rules,Æ Sirius said in a controlled voice. ØI also had my bond with you blocked, Harry, and compulsions placed the night your parents died to make me irrational.Æ Harry wasnít surprised and he realised a lot of the adults in his life probably had compulsions of some kind on them. Remus, being a werewolf, Dumbledore would have wanted to control and manipulate and Sirius, being from a well-known Dark family. Harry held Siriusí hand in a show of support. ØHeís my magical guardian; he named himself such when you went to Azkaban. He then placed his blocks on me and left me with the Dursleys to be abused. They have hinted that he told them to abuse me to åmake me normalí. Heís also been stealing from my family vaults,Æ Harry said. Sirius stood up and swore, making Harry wonder if it was a Black family trait. ØHow-how can we stop him?!Æ Sirius asked when he finally calmed enough to sit. Harry shared a look with Ragnok. ØWe first have to get me away from him - not out of school obviously, as I have no desire to go to Beauxbatons or Durmstrang - but no longer his ward. Then we can start to dissolve his image,Æ Harry said. Sirius looked at the innocent looking boy with surprise. ØMaybe you got a few more Black family traits, that sounds practically Slytherin, pup,Æ he said. Harry smirked. ØI do a very good impersonation of a Slytherin. I can also do a disturbingly good Gryffindor if I have to,Æ Harry joked, making Ragnok snort. ØHow do we get custody? I mean, would I even get it?Æ Sirius asked. ØLord Black, we have been preparing for this since Harry told us of his plans to free you,Æ Ragnok said. ØWith your new status, you are one of the richest wizards in Britain. Although the Black family was not a prestigious one in recent generations, your ancestors were not foolish. If you pick a suitable home and prove that you're retaining your former health, then your claim would be without any real concern,Æ Ragnok said. ØWhat properties do I have that would be suitable?Æ Sirius asked. He didnít want to return to Grimmauld Place as he had bad memories tied to each room in the house. Harry knew why he was asking and sent Ragnok a look, hoping he would answer how Harry wanted. The goblin apparently picked up on the look and spoke, ØAlthough the Blacks do own a number of establishments, the most suitable would be the London town house. It is the most recently vacated, therefore in the best condition. It can be cleansed and redecorated quickly for the right price.Æ Sirius nodded. ØThatís fine,Æ he said, hoping the refurbishment would help him bury his anger. ØIf you would like, I could send a team of young goblins not yet suitable for other work over and they can begin the cleaning process now,Æ Ragnok said. ØYes,Æ Sirius immediately agreed. Harry shook his head slightly. ØFor what price?Æ Harry asked ØA galleon each,Æ Ragnok said. ØA galleon for three workers, with each gaining an extra two sickles when the job is done,Æ Harry bargained. ØA galleon for two and three sickles,Æ Ragnok replied. ØAgreed.Æ Ragnok smirked and turned to the new Lord. ØYou should always know what price is being asked before you agree,Æ he warned. Sirius nodded his head. ØI shall try to remember that,Æ he acknowledged. ØAnyway, Harry, have you thought more on how much youíre willing to use the abuse in order to win the custody case?Æ Ragnok asked. Harry knew he already had the answer, so he was guessing the goblin wanted Sirius to know ØDodge can use it all. Iím officially reporting them tomorrow with Remus, so it will all come out anyway. Therefore, I donít mind,Æ Harry said. Sirius squeezed Harryís hand. ØPup, you donít have to do that,Æ he offered. ØI donít mind, Siri, Iím not ashamed. I think I could also help us if we did an interview tomorrow afternoon. When weíre both free,Æ Harry said, shocking Sirius as he hadnít realised how much Harry had planned or how mature and independent the boy was. ØAn interview?Æ he asked. Harry nodded. ØI have a contact: Rita Skeeter. Sheíll write whatever we tell her,Æ Harry said. Sirius snorted and spoke with disdain, ØEven I know that she writes whatever she wants and Iíve been in jail for ten years.Æ Harry smirked. ØTrust me, she will do what I want,Æ he promised, slightly darkly. Sirius looked at that smirk and shook his head. ØSometimes I see so much of your mother in you and sometimes itís your father - but that right there? That was pure Black family.Æ Harry smirked even wider. ØWhat can I say? I like variety.Æ ~ July 23rd 1992, The Leaky Cauldron. After leaving the bank, Sirius and Harry returned to the pub and went upstairs. Remus was waiting anxiously for them to return and practically pounced when they closed the door. ØHow did it go?Æ he asked. ØYou're looking at the new Lord Black,Æ Sirius said, holding out his arms and spinning on the spot so that his robes billowed slightly. Harry shook his head at his antics but smiled. ØIt was as we expected. Dumbledore had him dosed, but theyíve been removed. By becoming Lord Black, Sirius is protected - we should look at getting you something for that,Æ he said to Remus. ØThe goblins also think I should be able to get custody of Harry easily, I just have to move back to Grimmauld,Æ Sirius said. ØAnd get treatment,Æ Harry reminded the animagus. Remus smiled at their easy dynamic. ØYou look better, Pads,Æ he said. Sirius smiled. ØIím not, but I will be,Æ he said. Harry sighed. ØRight, tomorrow we will have a busy day. Sirius, you need to go to your appointment at St. Mungoís; I donít really want you going alone, but Remus and I are needed at the DMLE. Afterwards, we should meet back here for the interview Iím about to set up. Itís best if we get out ahead of this and tell the world the truth in our own words,Æ Harry said to the men. Remus chuckled at Siriusí bewildered face. ØYeah, he does that,Æ he told his friend. ØHeís not a normal child, is he?Æ the animagus asked the wolf. ØNo, not at all,Æ the wolf whispered back, Øbut heís Harry.Æ ØHe can also hear you,Æ Harry said back to the men. ØI donít think Iím missing anything, so does anybody have anything to add?Æ ØWhoís interviewing you?Æ Remus asked, making Harry groan as he had had to explain the situation twice. ~ After they had all made their solid plans, Harry went about writing several letters. First to Rita Skeeter, getting her to come tomorrow. Harry knew he would have to entice her. ~ Miss Skeeter, My name is Harrison Potter, I am writing to you as I believe I have a story that you would be perfect for. Iím offering you the exclusive as well as a chance to interview myself and another influential member of our society. If youíre interested, please be at the Leaky Cauldron at 3 oíclock tomorrow. Yours, Harrison J Potter Heir Potter ~ Next, Harry had to write to his friends; he started with Susan, who he knew was probably writing to him already. ~ Dear Susan, Now, before you plan where to bury my body, please know the only reason I didnít tell you guys was because I was worried and didnít even know if Sirius would get a trial. So, please, donít be mad. I didnít want to involve you if Dumbledore got involved and made things difficult. Ask your aunt if you donít believe me - Dumbledore has a very unhealthy interest in me. However, I am seeing her tomorrow about my relatives, so you donít have to worry about them anymore. I promise I will explain everything properly when I see you! Which will be soon! I want us all to meet up in Diagon Alley on the 1st. We can do our school shopping as our school list should be here later this week. Please forgive me, Susie! Your loving and very worthy of forgiveness friend, Harry ~ Harryís letter to Hannah was slightly less pleading, but still followed a similar pattern as he knew the girls were neighbours and what one knew, the other was bound to find out. ~ Dear Hannah, Iím sure youíve been told but, if not, here goes: I helped get Sirius Black freed from Azkaban. Now, before you freak out, heís innocent. He never had a trial. The only reason I didnít tell you guys was because I didnít want you all to worry and get involved, especially if Dumbledore got involved. Iím sorry! I promise I will explain properly when I see you! Which will be soon! I want us all to meet up in Diagon Alley on the 1st. We can do our school shopping as our school list should be here later this week. Your loving and very worthy of forgiveness friend, Harry ~ Nevilleís letter was less begging as Harry felt his relationship with the other boy was, not necessarily stronger, but Neville seemed to always understand why Harry did things. However, he didnít want to risk it so he did ask for forgiveness. ~ Nev, You probably wonít find out until tomorrowís papers, but I thought you should hear it from me first. I helped get Sirius Black freed from Azkaban. Now, before you freak out, heís innocent. He never had a trial. Heís my godfather and I wanted to help him. The only reason I didnít tell you guys was because I didnít want you all to worry and get involved, especially if Dumbledore got involved. Iím sorry! The girls will probably already know because Susieís aunt Amelia oversaw the trial, but I wanted you to know, too. I promise I will explain properly when I see you! Which will be soon! I want us all to meet up in Diagon Alley on the 1st. We can do our school shopping as our school list should be here later this week. Your loving and very worthy of forgiveness friend, Harry ~ With the letters written, Harry attached them to Hedwig, making sure to tell her to deliver Ritaís letter last as he wouldnít put it past her to look through the rest. The white bird was excited for her task and jumped on Harryís shoulder for a moment to nuzzle his hair, before she took flight. ØSheís a sweet bird,Æ Sirius commented. ØHedwig is great - she was my first friend,Æ Harry said. Although he was not as close with this version of her, Harry still held a lot of love for his snowy owl. ØI need to look at getting an owl and a wand,Æ Sirius said, coming to sit by Harry. ØWe can look to see if there are any family wands in the Black vault,Æ Harry said. ØI tried that when I was younger; they didnít work,Æ Sirius said sadly. ØGetting a new wand made could also work, if Ollivanders doesnít have what you need,Æ Harry said. Sirius frowned. ØBut Ollivander doesnít make wands for people, he just makes them and you find a match,Æ Sirius said. ØThereís a wand show in Knockturn Alley which makes wands; you pick the wood and core that you react best with,Æ Harry said. Sirius frowned. ØHow do you know whatís in Knockturn?Æ he asked, worried about the thought of the small boy wandering the streets of Knockturn without an adult - even with an adult it was dangerous. Harry shrugged. ØI like to investigate,Æ he hedged. Sirius sighed. ØI know youíve never had an adult you could depend on, or who worried, but please, for me, donít go to Knockturn Alley,Æ he said, then seeing Harryís frown, he added, Øor at least, donít go without telling me so I can come with you.Æ ØI think I can agree to that - for now,Æ Harry said. ØSo, this wand shop - you think I will be able to get a match?Æ Sirius asked. He would no longer deny something just for being Dark and, if Harry trusted the shop, Sirius would consider it. ØYou should be able to. Itís where people go to get second wands made as the wands created will always work, just maybe not as well as original wands,Æ Harry warned with a wince. Sirius had, in his last life, bemoaned the loss of his wand when it had been snapped while he was in Azkaban. Sirius held back the pang he felt and Harry sympathised. Although his holly wand was his, he had loved having the elder wand. That wand had made his magic sing and surge. He couldnít wait to obtain it again. ØThink we will have time to go tomorrow?Æ Sirius asked, anxious to get a wand back. ØWe should be able to fit it in before the interview. If not, the shops in Knockturn stay open later,Æ Harry said, making Sirius realise just how much time the boy must have spent down the darker Alley. His family had always been Dark and, growing up, he had tried to distance himself from that. As soon as he got his Hogwarts letter he had fought to be everything they werenít: Light, Gryffindor, and good. Having had his blood inheritance results and being cleansed, Sirius realised now that most of his behaviour was influenced by his Hogwarts letter and the compulsions placed on it; not that his family wasnít awful, but they were not awful because of their magical classification. They were just awful people in general - well, most of them. Sirius though, he had denied his own magic and forced himself to become something he was not. He was a Dark wizard. Not a bad person or a sadist, but his magic was naturally Dark. Forcing himself to only do Light magic and become a Light wizard was unnatural for him; it had made him weaker. He would be dammed if he would force Harry to be anything other than who he was and, in the short time he had been with the boy, he suspected the boy was Dark. How Dark, he didnít know, but he would support his godson no matter what. Harry had given Sirius back his freedom, both from Azkaban and from Dumbledore. He would do anything for him now. ~ Chapter End Notes Faster update then expected, next one in a few days. Comments & Kudos ~ Annie Chapter 14:Let thedisillusionmentbegin Chapter Notes See the end of the chapter for notes Previously Having had his blood inheritance results and being cleansed, Sirius realised now that most of his behaviour was influenced by his Hogwarts letter and the compulsions placed on it; not that his family wasnít awful, but they were not awful because of their magical classification. They were just awful people in general - well, most of them. Sirius though, he had denied his own magic and forced himself to become something he was not. He was a Dark wizard. Not a bad person or a sadist, but his magic was naturally Dark. Forcing himself to only do Light magic and become a Light wizard was unnatural for him; it had made him weaker. He would be dammed if he would force Harry to be anything other than who he was and, in the short time he had been with the boy, he suspected the boy was Dark. How Dark, he didnít know, but he would support his godson no matter what. Harry had given Sirius back his freedom, both from Azkaban and from Dumbledore. He would do anything for him now. ~ The next morning, Harry and the two men had breakfast together before Sirius left for his appointment at St. Mungo's. Harryís appointment wasnít until noon, so he and Remus had some time. Harry was waiting for Hedwig to return with his letters and the daily newspaper, anxious to see how the paper reported Siriusí release and trial. Setting aside his replies, Harry gave Hedwig some treats and unrolled the paper. ~ Ministry Blunders - Who Else is Innocent? By Rita Skeeter Yesterday in a shock trial, which had members of the Wizengamot unaware until the trial itself, revealed shocking ministry injustices. Sirius Black, renowned for being a mass murderer and supporter of You-Know-Who, was found to have been innocent of all crimes! The man, now 31, was imprisoned without a trial in Azkaban ten years ago after being accused of the murders of Peter Pettigrew - who has been found by aurors in the last few days - and twelve muggles. In a great twist, it has been revealed that it was Pettigrew who betrayed the Potter family to You-Know-Who, resulting in their murder and it was Pettigrew who killed the twelve muggles. The man is an unregistered rat animagus and was found just days before the trial of Mr. Black by Auror Kingsley Shacklebolt. What happens next? Who knows, but it begs the question: if Mr. Black is innocent, how many more are? This reporter aims to find out. ~ Harry smirked as he read the article before handing the paper to Remus. Picking up his letters, Harry was slightly relieved that Susan hadnít sent a howler. Reading her letter first, Harry opened it. ~ Harry, Harrison James Potter, you donít know how lucky you are that this letter isnít a howler. My aunt refused to do the charm for me - so you should thank her later! How could you keep this from us?! You know we would have helped you! Screw Dumbledore - yes, I swore - weíre your friends, Harry! We will always stand by you and help to support you. You had better not keep anything from us again without a great reason! When we meet up on the 1st, you had better be prepared to answer all my questions mister, or I will not be happy. I hope your summer has been okay regardless. I was getting ready to send my aunt after you because I hadnít yet heard! So, donít be a stranger and write! I want to hear all about it! Love, Susie, Your still frustrated but loyal friend ~ Hannahís letter was more reserved than Susanís, but Harry was still a bit apprehensive for seeing the two girls together. ~ Harry, How could you not tell us? Donít you know how worried weíve been?! Next time you had better keep us in the loop. You had better hope that Amelia doesnít do the howler spell for Susan! I do understand why you were worried, but next time, let us be involved; weíre your friends for a reason, Harry - we want to help you! I canít wait to see you on the 1st and I hope you're prepared to talk, mister! Either way, I hope your summerís been good! If you need anything or just want to talk, remember, Iím always here! Love, Hannah ~ Nevilleís letter was like Harry expected: reserved, but understanding. He really was glad he had befriended the blond boy. ~ Harry, I canít say that Iím too impressed to hear you did this alone, but I understand why. Youíve always been alone before you had us, right? You need to remember that weíre here for you, Harry. Next time I expect to be told straight away! So, how is Sirius? If heís innocent, I bet that heís grateful for having you! Remember to keep writing! I want to hear from you! And if you're free on the 30th, I would like for you to visit. Itís my birthday and gran usually has the elves make a cake. Iíll ask the girls as well, so maybe you can warm them up if you write. Neville ~ Harry replied to them all and asked Remus if they were free on the 30th so that he could go to Nevilleís. Getting an affirmative from the werewolf, Harry was happy to write back, saying he would go. He hadnít yet bought the boy a present and wanted to get him something nice to show his appreciation. He had been lucky so far in that he hadnít been found in the Leaky Cauldron, and he wanted it to remain this way as he didnít want to be surrounded by people looking to catch a glimpse of the famous Boy-Who-Lived. As they still had a few hours before they had to go to the ministry to meet Madam Bones, Harry decided to look through his books. He had spent the last few days focusing on Sirius and now he wanted to spend a moment on Tom. He knew the easiest horcruxes to get would be the diadem and the locket. Hopefully the book as well if Lucius acts as he did last time; if not, it wouldnít be impossible to get the book - all Harry would have to do was get an invite to Malfoy manor. Although he needed the ring, as it was also a Hallow, Harry was wary of going after it; the protections surrounding it would take time and magical energy, which Harry knew he couldnít spare presently. He had to collect the pieces of Tomís soul; even without knowing how, he would save Tom. Harry knew he couldnít let his soul fragments to remain out there unguarded. Picking up a book, Harry got out a blank notepad and started the translation spell. The book was an old Russian book he had bought in Knockturn Alley a few days earlier and had looked promising. By doing the spell on a blank notepad, Harry could let the spell run even without him present and he would eventually have an English copy. ØWhat are you reading?Æ asked Remus, walking closer to the boy. Harry looked up. ØJust something in Russian - Iím translating it,Æ Harry said, not wanting Remus to become too interested and, therefore, not being obviously vague. Remus hummed. ØDo you need any help?Æ he offered. Harry smiled and replied, ØIím okay, thank you. I like to keep busy.Æ He didnít want Remus to notice his use of magic and question it. He had only been using wandless magic sparingly since Remus and Sirius had been around him, unable to use his holly wand as it held the ministry trace. He didnít want to answer their questions. ØIf you're sure,Æ the wolf said, walking away and back over to whatever he had been doing before. ~ Eventually, it was time for Harry and Remus to go to the ministry. Dressing in smart clothing, they apparated in. Harry, stumbling slightly as side-along apparition was worse than normal apparition, grumbled as they left the apparition zone. ØWelcome to the ministry, you need to register your wands in order to obtain a visitorís pass,Æ a young man said at the visitor's desk. Harry pulled out his holly wand and held it out. The guard took it without looking and waited for the results. ØThank you, Mr.îÆ the guard paused then and quickly turned to Harry, Øîoh, Merlin, I'm so sorry! You're Harry Potter! Welcome to the ministry!Æ Harry sighed and turned to Remus with an exasperated expression. ØThank you. Could I possibly have my wand back now and my pass?Æ he asked. The guard seemed to startle and fumbled with passing Harry his wand. ØIf thereís anything you need, anything at allîÆ Harry sighed and cut him off, ØI think I will be fine, thank you.Æ Remus huffed a slight chuckle at Harryís exasperation before he handed his wand over. The guard blanched when he read Remusí results. ØErm, Mr. Lupin, you will need a special pass due to your status as a we-werewolf,Æ he stuttered out the last word. ØI know,Æ Remus said pleasantly, used to the fear and prejudice. The guard watched him warily, as though he would suddenly turn and attack. He practically threw Remus his wand and pass. Walking to the room towards the lift, Harry watched as the ministry workers eyed Remus warily as soon as they noted his pass. Entering the lift, a young woman even stepped out when she saw the silver pass. Harry let out a low growl when he saw it happen and Remus stepped closer and rested a hand on his shoulder. ØItís okay,Æ he whispered into Harryís ear. Harry turned towards him. ØItís not, but one day - one day it will be,Æ Harry whispered back. Remus smiled at the boyís words. He didnít mind the treatment anymore, he had long since grown used to it. Reaching the DMLE floor, they stepped out of the lift and approached the bustling centre. Seeing the words ØBONES, AMELIA. HEAD AUROR.Æ on the centermost door, the two approached the door and knocked. The door opened quickly and Amelia ushered them in. Inside, there was another woman around the same age, an older man, and Dumbledore. Harry sent Remus a look when he spotted the old coot and carefully blanked his face. ØMr. Potter, Mr. Lupin. Thank you for coming today, wonít you please take a seat,Æ Amelia said, smiling at Harry. ØNow, we are here today as there have been allegations made about your home life, Harry.Æ Harry nodded, ØYes, I understand.Æ ØWith me are Joanne Mirwood, head of the Childrenís Protection Offices, and Westley Lane from the Muggles Relations Office, as well as Professor Albus Dumbledore, who you obviously know as your headmaster, but he is also your magical guardian.Æ Harry nodded to them. ØGood afternoon, maíam, sirs,Æ he said, Remus doing the same. Ms. Mirwood looked at Harry with a light smile. ØWhy donít you start, Harry? I think itís important if you tell us in your own words how your relatives treated you before we make any decisions.Æ Harry internally smirked but outwardly showed a child unsure; he fidgeted slightly in his seat and looked at Dumbledore with a wary look before turning to the rest of the adults. ØIf you think thatís best,Æ he eventually said, before shuffling once more. ØMy memory has always been good, I remember things from my life before I went to live with my aunt and uncle. I remember my mum singing and my dadís laugh....Æ Joanne smiled. ØWhy donít you tell us your earliest memories with your aunt and uncle?Æ she asked. ØI remember being hungry and crying and I kept asking for my mum. I couldnít understand why she wasnít there; I was cold and scared and usually she would come and sing to me. I can remember that night, the flash of green, but at the time I didnít understand it. In the beginning, they would only tend to me when it became obvious that they had to. I wasnít ever cared for. However, one of my clearest memories was when I had turned three; my aunt had slapped me and told me: åYour whore mother had gotten herself killed in a car crash with your drunk fatherí,Æ Harry said, making Remus growl and the women gasp. Dumbledore's eyes tightened and Harry saw him clench his fist. Joanne recovered her composure. ØDid you get hit often?Æ she asked. Harry shrugged and looked down. ØWhenever I did something bad - like magic or burning the breakfast or doing better than my cousin in school. Things like that,Æ he mumbled. Amelia looked livid and Remus was obviously struggling. Dumbledore though, he had stiffened and looked ready to jump across the room and strangle Harry. His blue eyes were piercing into him and Harry could feel the signs of legilimency, however, with his rings and his own occlumency, it wasnít working. ØYou were punished for accidental magic growing up?Æ she asked, shocked. Magic in children was cherished and celebrated. Usually a childís first big accidental magic was cause for a celebration or party. ØYes, they didnít like it when I did freakish things,Æ Harry said, flinching slightly on purpose when he said the word freak. ØHow did they punish you, other than hitting you?Æ she asked softly. ØI would be locked in my cupboard without food for a few days sometimesè,Æ Harry said. At this point he had made sure to curl in on himself as he spoke. ØWhat cupboard, Harry?Æ the child officer asked. ØThatís where I lived. The cupboard under the stairs,Æ Harry said. ØI see,Æ Joanne said, sharing concerned looks with the adults. ØCome now, Harry, I'm sure you're exaggerating,Æ Dumbledore said jovially. Harry flinched, making Remus and the other adults turn to the old man and glare. ØHarry, you mentioned burning the breakfast; are you telling me it was your job to cook?Æ Amelia asked, joining in the questioning as she had, in her opinion, cause for a criminal case. ØYes. As soon as I was tall enough to reach the stove, my aunt had me cooking all the food,Æ Harry said. Amelia looked confused for a second before she realised what the child meant. ØYou mean to tell me she had you doing all of the cooking from a young age?Æ ØI was four when I started, itís how I got the burn on my arm. I wasnít good at seeing the pans as they were too high. After that, though, I got really good at judging how long things needed to be cooked for,Æ Harry said, making sure to sound slightly proud of this achievement. Internally he was dancing with glee - the looks on the adults faces were priceless. They were all eating out of the palm of his hand and he had yet to lie. Only Dumbledore looked murderous, making the moment even sweeter for Harry. ØYou were cooking unsupervised and using flames at the age of four,Æ Amelia stated. ØYes,Æ Harry said. ØI see,Æ she said, turning to share a look with Joanne. They seemed to share an entire conversation in a look, making Harry assume that as they were both parents, or at least parental figures; it was a look of motherly concern. Westley joined in then, ØYou mentioned a cupboard. Were you living there, as you claim, because there was no space room for you to have a bedroom?Æ Dumbledore looked victorious for a moment as he thought he had an ally. ØNo sir, there are four bedrooms. My aunt and uncleís room, Dudleyís bedroom and his 2nd bedroom, and the spare one used by aunt Marge when she comes and visits,Æ Harry said. Westley looked shocked. ØSo there was adequate space for you to have a room to yourself?Æ he asked. ØI think so, sir,Æ Harry mumbled. Dumbledore had gone back to looking mutinous. ØCome now, my boy, I'm sure your relatives did their best for you. They are your family, after all,Æ he said. Harry could feel a slight compulsion pushing against him. Amelia seemed to be pulled out of her shock at that and sent the Professor a dark look. ØIt is my opinion, based on these accusations, that Harryís aunt and uncle are not fit to care for a magical child,Æ Amelia said. ØI'm sure you agree, Ms. Mirwood.Æ ØI do,Æ Joanne said. ØCome now, they are the boyís family and he is a child. Surely you know how a child can exaggerate,Æ Dumbledore said, making Harry want to hex him as he was basically calling him a liar. Amelia looked angry but turned to Harry. ØHarry, do you have any proof of what you say? Any evidence at all?Æ she asked. ØWell, I saw a private healer, Jordan Theon, last summer, who recorded all of my injuries. He prescribed potions to combat my malnutrition and to help heal my bones as they had not been set correctly and were weak from breaks. He also gave me an ointment for my scars. Kosti-Recovery, Helgaís Fortune, and Apolloís Tears,Æ Harry said. ØVery good. We will ask for him to provide the note of his scans. Is there anybody else who you can think of who could provide evidence?Æ ØWell, Madam Pomfrey did a scan while I was at school, so she would probably help. And Professor Snape brewed my potions and ointment. My head of house, Professor Flitwick, also knows; over Christmas, he wrote to my family and became concerned as they wrote about how they didnít care what happened to me if I was sick,Æ Harry said in an embarrassed voice. ØThree teachers at Hogwarts are aware of your home life?Æ Amelia said, sending Dumbledore a dark look. The old man looked livid, probably with how the situation had played out. However, Harry honestly couldnít see how he thought this would go - maybe the legilimency was supposed to make him say his family was great? ØThatís everybody, I thinkè,Æ Harry mumbled. ØCome now, Harry. I really must insist you tell the truth; your family loves you. They were very worried when you didnít return to them from school,Æ Dumbledore insisted. Harry shrunk back. ØI didnít want to go back. I wrote to them and they said they didnít care,Æ Harry said. ØDo you have this letter?Æ Amelia butted in when she saw Dumbledore open his mouth. Harry nodded and reached into his robe, pulling out the letter Vernon had written him back. Handing it over, Harry watched as Amelia read it. ØThank you, Harry. Would it be okay if I kept this and made you a copy? This will be used as evidence,Æ she explained. ØThatís fine,Æ Harry said. Joanne spoke up then, ØI do understand your fear of returning to your relatives, Harry, but it was very irresponsible of you. You should have gone to a friendís house. Being out there alone isnít an option for a child, especially for you, with your status,Æ she explained. ØPrecisely. He needs to return to his family,Æ Dumbledore said, making the adults and Harry look at him incredulously. ØI didnít mean that, Harry,Æ the woman said. ØWhat I meant was that you cannot live alone and look after yourself. It has come to my attention that you are no longer alone as you have Mr. Lupin, but before then what were you planning on doing? You said to Madam Bones yesterday that you are staying at the Leaky Cauldron. That isnít the best place for a young boy to grow up.Æ Harry was happy this conversation was happening now that Sirius was free. ØI'm sorry,Æ he said, Øbut like you said, I'm not alone now. Remy and Siri are looking after me. And yesterday I went with Siri after he was freed to a healerís appointment and then the bank; heís having his house in London redecorated and cleaned for us already.Æ ØSiri as in Sirius Black?Æ Amelia questioned. Seeing the looks on the faces of the two adults, she added, ØHe was found innocent of all crimes yesterday. Peter Pettigrew was the real murderer and was found alive last week - he had set Black up.Æ ØHeís my godfather. He said he would be filing for custody today to become my guardian in both the muggle and magical worlds,Æ Harry said. Dumbledore must have had enough at this point as he sat forward and frowned. ØNow see here, I am your magical guardian,Æ he said. Amelia, looking exasperated, turned to Dumbledore. ØYes, you are, and do not think I will not be questioning you about your lack of involvement in Harryís life growing up. As his magical guardian, you had a duty of care - which you failed!Æ she said, shutting the man up. Harry barely restrained himself from kissing the woman as she pointed out Dumbledore's failings. Maybe he could send an anonymous gift basket? ØI see. Well, in light of this, I will grant Mr. Lupin temporary guardianship,Æ Ms. Mirwood said, pulling out some documents from Merlin knows where. Remus looked startled. ØBut I'm a werewolf,Æ he said quietly. Amelia looked pained by Remusí quiet admission. ØI am aware,Æ Joanne said. ØHowever, it is clear you have provided care and support for Mr. Potter. Until a real custody trial can be held, I am entrusting Mr. Potter into your care.Æ Dumbledore stood at this point. ØIf the boy cannot return to his relatives, like is best, then surely you should give me custody as I am his magical guardian,Æ he said, his voice tight. ØMr. DumbledoreîÆ ØProfessor Dumbledore,Æ Dumbledore interrupted contemptuously. ØProfessor Dumbledore,Æ the head of the Child Protection Office started, refusing to be intimidated, ØI believe that Harry would be better off staying with Mr. Lupin in his current place as he is clearly happy, well-loved, and looked after. Until the custody trial, which I will book for the 3rd of August, he shall remain in his care as disrupting him anymore would not be beneficial for Harry, who is the most important person in this situation.Æ Dumbledore straightened his back and looked down at the woman with thinly veiled contempt. ØVery well,Æ he said, before turning and walking out of the door. Amelia looked annoyed, but turned to Harry. ØI will consult the people you spoke of and have them provide evidence for your custody trial, but rest assured: you shall not have to return to your relatives if I have any say in the situation,Æ she promised. Mr. Lane stood then. ØI havenít had much to say in this meeting, but it has been a pleasure to meet you, Harry,Æ he said, offering his hand. Harry shook his hand and returned the sentiment. Eventually, just the women and Remus remained. ØI shall send you a letter about the trial details, but if Mr. Black files for it today and, in light of the evidence, I do not see a reason why he should not gain full custody,Æ Ms. Mirwood said. ØAnd, can I just say, Harry, I am very sorry you had to grow up in such an awful environment. I can promise you this: the wizarding world never knew.Æ Harry smiled. ØItís okay, I survived,Æ Harry said. ØYes, you did,Æ Joanne said before leaving. After she had gone, Harry turned to Amelia. ØThank you, Madam Bones,Æ he said. ØCall me Amelia when weíre alone, Harry,Æ she said, sitting back with a sigh. ØThank you, then, Amelia. Also, thank you for not charming Susieís letter.Æ Amelia laughed. ØItís okay, but next time you may not be so lucky,Æ she warned. ØI also want to thank you for looking out for me. Susan mentioned you started looking into my file at Christmas, that you wanted to help me,Æ Harry said. Amelia nodded tiredly. ØI did and I do; I donít know what Dumbledore is up to. He has your file secured - but, no matter. I donít want you worrying about a thing; weíll get to the bottom of this,Æ she promised. ØThank you, and please mention to Susan how sorry I am for not telling her. I didnít want her or any of our friends to be worried about me being alone or about the trial,Æ Harry said. ØWhy did you choose to go alone, Harry? I know Susan has offered you a place in our home if you ever need it - and I donít doubt Hannah and Neville have as well,Æ Amelia asked out of curiosity. ØHonestly?Æ Harry asked. ØI didnít want to burden any of you or for you to feel sorry for me. I knew the trial would work, it had to. So, I knew I wouldnít be alone for long - well, I hoped I wouldnít be alone for long.Æ ØI can see why you would think that, but Harry, you are only eleven. I know you are mature for your age, but it really isnít safe. Please promise me that you will never do something like this again,Æ Amelia said, more as a concerned parental figure than an auror. Harry nodded. ØI promise,Æ he said. Remus interrupted then, ØI'm sorry, but I think we should get going. We have a lot to do today.Æ ØIt's fine, I have mountains of paperwork,Æ Amelia said, waving off his concern. ØRemember Harry: write to Susan. If you donít, sheíll be wearing holes in the carpet,Æ she warned. Harry rubbed the back of his neck and muttered about mother hens. ØI will,Æ he promised. ~ Returning to the Leaky Cauldron after their meeting, Harry wrote to his friends explaining what happened and what he hoped would happen next. He also wrote a more detailed version for Ragnok and Mr. Dodge, warning them about Dumbledore and making sure they were prepared. He wanted to go to Gringotts, but he feared he wouldnít have time or be able to slip away unnoticed. He also sent a note to Dodge, asking him to file Siriusí custody claim immediately. Sirius could sign it tomorrow, but Harry wanted it notarized, even if it wasn't completely above board. Once Sirius returned, they had around an hour before Skeeter was due to arrive, so he and Harry picked up cloaks with hoods and disappeared into the summer crowds. That was the one thing he liked about the busy streets; with the amount of people around, it was easy to remain anonymous. Taking the lead as he knew the dark alley better, Harry lead Sirius through the back streets to Morgauseís Wands and More. When he opened the door, Harry felt a ward wash over him; and knew that if he meant ill will towards the owner, he would be in serious pain right now. ØThis is the shop?Æ Sirius asked, looking around. It was nothing like Ollivanders; there were no wand boxes or supplies. Instead, the small shop was surprisingly light for the alley, and only had a desk and a few paintings of trees. A small bookcase sat to one side with an old sofa and coffee table. ØYes,Æ Harry said. He had never been inside, but he had heard about it during his trips to the alley. He had gotten good at going unseen and had heard many interesting things. ØGreetings, Lord Black, Heir Potter. What can I do for you today?Æ came the voice of an old woman. Looking up, Harry saw her appear from the back room. She was ancient and probably only stood a few inches taller than Harryís own miniscule size. ØI need a new wand, mine was snapped when I went to Azkaban,Æ Sirius said, looking at the woman. ØVery well, Lord Black. My name is Lorelei, please follow me into the back,Æ she instructed. Harry followed Sirius as he was interested in the process. He watched as the old woman flicked her wand and a box appeared and grew on a large table. ØPlease reach out and feel each piece of wood with your magic. You will know when you feel the right one. If you cannot feel with your magic, then you will have to pick up each wood and see which reacts to you best,Æ Lorelei instructed. Sirius closed his eyes and centred himself. His magic was still weak, but it had stabilized and was getting stronger every hour. Harry, interested in the procedure, focused his magic on Sirius' and helped him to guide his, causing the man to open his eyes in shock as he felt Harryís strength. Harry felt the moment Sirius' magic bonded with the wood and watched as Sirius approached and picked up a dark brown, almost black, branch. ØEbony wood. An apt choice for you, Lord Black,Æ Lorelei commented. Harry searched his memories and remembered what he had read about ebony wands. Ebony wands, Harry remembered, were best suited for those who favoured combative magic and transfiguration. Ebony wands are happiest in the hand of those with the courage to be themselves - wielders were frequently non-conformist individuals or comfortable with the status of an outsider. Sirius smiled. ØMy first wand was oak.Æ The old woman nodded and took the piece of wood from Sirius, placing it on the side. She then waved her wand and the contents of the box changed, no longer wood, but an array of magical cores now sat inside. Some of which Harry knew were rare and hardly used. ØYou will repeat the process to find your core. Sometimes, in rare cases, multiple cores are chosen,Æ she said. Sirius nodded his understanding and, once again, with Harryís help, directed his magic to the box. This time, when his magic reacted, Harry wasn't as shocked as he recognised the sensation. Sirius reached in and pulled out a jar of dark hair. ØRougarou hair. Not often found in British wands as the beast is native to America,Æ Lorelei said. Harry once again searched his memories and remembered the monster; it was native to the southern states like Louisiana. The dog monster has the head of a wolf and the body of a man. Although sentient, they were a reclusive and volatile race. Sirius nodded. ØHow long until the wand is ready?Æ he asked. ØYou can collect it tonight. Be back at seven,Æ the old woman instructed. ØThank you,Æ Sirius said, pleased he would not be so helpless for long. He was capable of some basic wandless magic, but not much. Probably none in his current state. Leaving the shop, the two quickly returned to the Leaky Cauldron. They had around 15 minutes before Rita was due to arrive and they had to freshen up. Seeing the time, Harry had to convince Remus and Sirius to let him go downstairs alone. ØI promise I will be fine, I just want to talk to her alone first,Æ Harry said. ØBut HarryîÆ Remus said, wanting to point out, again, that he was acting as his guardian. ØîI will be fine; the pub is a public place. I will literally be five minutes,Æ Harry interrupted. ØLet him go, Remy,Æ Sirius said before he turned to Harry. ØBut you have three minutes.Æ Harry contained his eye roll - he hated looking like a preteen. ØFine,Æ he said, rushing out of the door. Harry only had to wait for a few seconds once he got downstairs. Rita appeared on the dot, her photographer Bozo trailing after her. Approaching the vibrantly dressed woman, Harry withheld his sneer. ØMs. Skeeter,Æ he said, offering his hand, which she took. Kissing the air above herís, Harry smiled. ØItís a pleasure, maíam. My name is Harry potter.Æ ØMy, Mr. Potter, what lovely manners,Æ Skeeter purred, making Harry want to wipe his lips. ØNow, before we begin, I just have one thing to say to you,Æ Harry said, leaning in close. ØOh?Æ Skeeter said, her eyes wide as she thought of the gossip she would get, the prestige. ØItís a five year sentence to Azkaban for being an unregistered animagus, isnít it? Even for such a small, little bug,Æ Harry whispered. Seeing her panic, he continued quickly, ØNow, donít worry, nobody else knows. But it would be such a shame if my favourite reporter got caught. So, perhaps we should take our conversation upstairs. I do want you to be able to hear me properly; it would be a shame if I was misquoted.Æ Harry was proud of how quickly the woman understood his silent threat - he had feared she would miss it. ØI quite agree, Mr. Potter,Æ she said, her voice losing its sickening purr and gaining an ounce of fear. ØNone of that now, Rita, call me Harry. We are friends, after all,Æ Harry said, leading the way for her upstairs. Entering the room with Rita and Bozo, Harry saw that Remus and Sirius had laid out tea on the table and were sitting, obviously anxious. ØTake a seat, Rita, we will explain everything,Æ Harry said. Rita moved quickly, eyes widening when she saw Sirius Black. ØLord Black, itís an honour,Æ she said, the purr returning but vanishing before she finished when Harry cleared his throat. Sirius, noticing this, sent Harry an inquisitive glance but got a faux innocent look in return. Joining them at the table, Harry sat with Remus and Sirius on either side, opposite Rita. ØNow, Rita, I'm sure you're aware that Sirius here has recently been found innocent of all of the terrible crimes he was accused?Æ Harry asked. ØYes, such a shameful misjustice,Æ Rita agreed. ØWell, what is less known is that he is also my godfather,Æ Harry said. ØIn a few days, he will be going to trial to prove he is a fit guardian for me.Æ Ritaís eyes lit up as she realised how important this information was. ØAnd you would like for me to help his claim, Harry?Æ she asked. ØVery good, Rita,Æ Harry said. ØWhat is not known is that the muggles I grew up with were the worst sort. Physically and verbally abusive. They starved me and hated my magic. Sirius, however, is a perfect guardian; he cherishes me and in the short time I have known him, I feel like I have gained a real family. Remus is, as well. They are the best possible people to raise me, to help me recover from the muggles, and to become the wizard the world needs me to be,Æ Harry said, looking Rita in the eye to get the message across. Ritaís eyes had grown huge. ØI see, I think I know exactly how to help. Perhaps a picture of the two of you though, to help sell the article,Æ she suggested, her eyes calculating. Harry knew she wouldnít betray him; working with him, she not only got to keep her secret safe, but also she gained exclusive rights to stories about him - as long as she painted him in the light he wanted, of course. ~ After the pictures, Rita disappeared, promising Harry the article would be printed how he wanted. Harry had also told her that, if he liked what she did, she would always be the first person he contacted in the press - thus making sure she was firmly in his pocket. ØSo, how did you do it?Æ Sirius asked when they were alone in Harryís room. Harry turned innocent once again. ØWhatever do you mean?Æ he asked. ØI know you said you had a contact - but that right there? That was almost scary. So, what do you have on her?Æ Sirius asked curiously. Although he said it was scary, he wasn't afraid of Harry; if anything, he was proud of the boy. Yes, the tactics where Slytherin, but he was proving he could survive, that he would survive. ØDid you know itís a five year prison sentence for not registering as an animagus after you finish school?Æ Harry asked, smirk widening as he saw Sirius make the connection. ØSo, sheísè?Æ he trailed off. ØYes. People always did say they thought she bugged them to get her information,Æ Harry said, hinting widely. ØBugged?Æ Sirius asked. Harry laughed. ØItís a muggle thing,Æ he explained. ØBut either way, sheís an unregistered beetle animagus. I just pointed out the consequences if the wrong people found out and the benefits of our ongoing professional relationship.Æ Sirius was silent for a moment before he barked out a dog like laugh. Eventually, he calmed down. ØYou're scary sometimes, but I have never been so proud,Æ he said, making Harry smile. ~ Chapter End Notes So i'm ridiculously sick- summer flu is apparently a real thing -..There will be an update in a few days, shouldn't be any longer than a week.
