He had taken out a blank book and was planning his days before the trial: He needed to go out a get Neville a birthday present, and would be busy on the 30th seeing his friends. His own birthday was the 31st, so he should probably expect Sirius and Remus to do something, and the Malfoyís had also mentioned dinner, so that would most likely occur then. On the 1st of August he needed to be in the alley to meet his friends, but more importantly he needed the diary. He hoped Lucius planned on sabotaging Arthur again, he had heard about the raids so he knew the Weasley patriarch was acting as he had in his last life. The custody trial was on the 3rd, so they had to be prepared for that. Thinking about all of this made Harry want to groan and he bemoaned his fate; he half.heartedly considered if it was worth maintaining his friendships, but he knew that he would regret breaking them off. Not only would they be useful in the future, but all the kids he had befriended were genuinely nice - and Neville, well, Harry couldnít do that to the blond boy. He enjoyed their company, so he knew he wouldnít deliberately end any of his friendships. ØWhat are you writing now?Æ Remus asked, not surprised anymore to find Harry with a book; he was always either reading or writing. He couldnít believe anybody who spent more than five minutes with Harry would doubt the small boy belonged in Ravenclaw. ØJust a plan for my next few days. I need to go shopping for Nevís birthday present,Æ he said. ØThis is Neville Longbottom?Æ Remus asked. ØYeah, Nevilleís my best friend,Æ Harry told the wolf. ØWhatís he like? I knew his parents. They were great people,Æ Remus said, his voice sad as he thought about the couple's fate. Harry spared him a sad smile. ØNeville is great. Heís in Gryffindor - when we first met he didnít think he was brave enough for the lion house, but almost immediately he stood up for me against some busybody muggleborn girl. Heís not a loud boy, more brave when it counts. He also loves Herbology and cannot even look at a potion without it failing somehow,Æ Harry said. Remus smiled, ØHe sounds like a nice boy.Æ ØHe really is. I donít think he had many friends growing up, he seemed kind of lonely. He also doesnít care that I'm Harry Potter, the Boy-Who-Lived. To him, I'm just Harry. He doesnít expect me to be anything or anybody I'm not,Æ Harry said. Remus frowned as he remembered how the clerk at the ministry had acted. ØDoes that happen a lot - people acting weird around you?Æ he asked. He hadnít realised Harry would dislike the fame, but thinking about it, he thought it obvious. Harry shrugged. ØSometimes thereís this awful boy, Ron - Ronald Weasley, who I think is actually stalking me. He insists I'm a traitor because I'm in Ravenclaw and not Gryffindor. He says I'm supposed to be a hero and a Light wizard who hates everything Dark and Slytherin,Æ Harry said with a sneer. Remus frowned. He knew the Weasleys, he had gotten along very well with Arthur during the last war. He hadnít seen Molly as much as she had been home with her young children, but he didnít like the idea of their son making Harryís life miserable. ØWell, you know to ignore him right? You are exactly who you are supposed to be, Harry,Æ Remus said. Harry smiled. ØThanks, Remy.Æ Sirius returned then and smiled when he took in the relaxed atmosphere. ØTomorrow I want to visit Grimmauld Place; I want to see how the goblins have been getting on. Not that I havenít enjoyed the Leaky Cauldron, but I think itís time I started proving I can look after you,Æ he said. Harry internally cheered when he thought about the Black library - finally, he would be able to get back to looking. He had been scouring his books, but he just had a feeling about the library; he knew the answer was in there. There was also the added bonus of getting hold of the locket as well - he would just have to persuade Kreacher. ØIt would be good to actually have a house,Æ Harry said. Sirius smiled. ØI wouldnít get your hopes up yet, pup. The house I grew up in wasn't the best and it might be a few days before we can move in.Æ ØI donít mind. I need to go shopping for birthday presents anyway,Æ Harry said. ØSpeaking of birthdays, what do you want to do for yours?Æ Remus asked. Harry smirked. ØWell, I guess cursing Dumbledore bald doesnít count,Æ he said. ØHonestly, I donít know; Iíve never celebrated it before. Canít we just have cake and stuff?Æ The two men exchanged dark looks, realising all Harry had missed out on. ØWeíll think of something,Æ they promised. ~ Entering Grimmauld Place the next day, Harry saw the goblins had done great work; they could probably move in that day. The walls were spotless, with fresh plaster and paint. The floors had been fixed and the wards Harry felt actually intimidated him - they truly were forces to be reckoned with. Sirius looked just as shocked. ØWowè,Æ the dog animagus said. Even the portrait of Walburga was silent, the curtains closed. ØYour goblin friends sure do work fast,Æ Remus said, walking around the house. Entering the kitchen, Harry was shocked to see the improvement; it had been modernized slightly and actually sparkled in places. The drawing room, which held the family tree, had been almost remade. The family tree itself was remastered: Harry saw that Sirius was no longer blasted off and a branch now came from him with Harrison James Potter-Black engraved into the weave. ØSo, I think we can move in,Æ Sirius said slowly. The furniture had obviously either been transfigured or replaced as everything was new. There were still a lot of the original items (coffee tables, ornaments, book shelves, etc.), but there were new elements mixed in, making the home unrecognizable. Even Harry, who hadnít been gone that long in comparison as he had lived in the remains of the home in his last life before returning, couldnít recognize the house they now stood in. Re-entering the kitchen, Remus and Sirius startled when Kreacher appeared with a pop. Harry felt a tug on his magic when he saw the elf, startling him. The elf looked between Harry and Sirius in confusion. ØWhich one is Master? Both boy and stain on family have bond - oh, what to do - Mistress would have known,Æ the elf muttered, distress clear. Sirius looked shocked and took a step towards the elf. Harry, seeing this, ran and put himself between the two. He knew their relationship was terrible but he had truly bonded with the elf in his last life. Sirius, startled by Harryís sudden movement, stopped. ØKreacher, how are you still alive?Æ he asked The elf, who was staring at Harry with shocked eyes, turned back to Sirius. ØKreacher lives .oh, how he lives. Great Master Regulus is gone, even Mistress is gone, but still Kreacher lives,Æ the elf wailed. ØKreacher felt it when the stain became Lord Black. Kreacher never thought he would serve such a man. Sending nasty goblins in to clean and bringing with him a wolf and a boy is also Master.Æ Sirius looked angry again, but before he could lash out, Harry intervened. ØHello, Kreacher, my name is Harry. I suspect you feel the bond because I'm the Black family Heir,Æ he explained. Kreacher looked startled and pulled his ear. ØHalfblood Heirs, wolves, what is next for poor Kreacher?Æ he muttered. ØYou're right, I am a halfblood and I was even raised by awful muggles. Muggles who hurt me,Æ Harry said. Kreacher stilled then. ØMuggles hurt little Heir?Æ he asked. ØYes, thatís why Sirius became your new Lord. We are going to live here from now on and he is going to help me to get better,Æ Harry said, looking at Sirius when he went to interrupt Harryís lies. Kreacher looked indecisive before he nodded. He could feel the boyís power as it called to him; he was strong and Dark. The power was intoxicating. If this boy was the Heir to the Black family, Kreacher would be happy to help him and their magic was already bonded, anyway. Kreacher was confused - his boy was his Lord but not his Lord. ØKreacher is happy to serve new Lord Black if he is helping Little Master,Æ Kreacher said. ØLittle Master should have never been with nasty muggles. Bad things. No, Little Master should be here with Kreacher. Kreacher will look after him, help him. Yes, Kreacher will serve,Æ the elf muttered. Harry smiled and sent Sirius a smug look. Sirius was floored - the elf never listened to anybody other than his mother and Regulus, who he adored. He had always hated Sirius, even when he was a child. ØHow?Æ he asked Harry when the elf popped away. Harry had used their shock to ask for the locket, promising to destroy it when he could. Harry shrugged. ØI'm just that good,Æ he deadpanned. ØNo, seriously. Kreacher hates me,Æ Sirius said, still bewildered, Øand here he is, willing to serve me just because of you. Heís just met you.Æ ØMaybe he liked the feel of me through the bond?Æ Harry suggested. Sirius shrugged and Remus took the moment to intervene. ØThis is a good thing, anyway. It means Kreacher will help us and he obviously likes Harry so he will look out for him.Æ Sirius nodded, shocked silent. ØMaybe Iíll even work on your mother,Æ Harry mused. ØYou said her portrait was behind the curtains.Æ He had never really tried to win over Walburga last time and wondered if he could. Sirius seemed to freeze before he grabbed Harry and physically lifted him. ØNo, I donít care. You are not meeting that woman for as long as I can manage it.Æ ØShe canít be that bad,Æ Harry said, knowing she was but still wanting to try. ØSheís worse!Æ Sirius cried dramatically, Remus nodding his head. Harry laughed. ØSo are we moving in today?Æ he asked. Sirius looked around. ØThereís no reason not to. Letís check upstairs first, but if itís like down here we should be good,Æ Sirius agreed reluctantly. Checking out the remaining three floors, they found them all cleaned and refurbished. Even the library, much to Harryís pleasure, had been refreshed. Seeing the look of glee on Harryís face, Sirius sighed. ØAm I going to have to put a ban on the door, locking you out, if you start spending all your time in here?Æ he asked. Harry stilled and looked at him in betrayal. ØYou wouldnít?!Æ he gasped. Sirius and Remus laughed. ØPup, you look like I just told you Santa wasn't real.Æ Pausing, Harry turned. ØWait, he isnít?Æ Harry asked in a shocked voice, making sure to look sad. The men froze but seeing Harry smirk after a few seconds, they breathed out a sigh of relief before laughing. ØYou had us,Æ Remus admitted. ØI think I just had a heart attack,Æ Sirius said. ØI mean, this parenting thing will be harder than I thought.Æ A look passed over his face and he froze suddenly, horror filled eyes going to Harry. ØMerlin! I'm going to have to give you the Talk,Æ he cried. Remus paused and shared a horror filled look with Sirius. Harry stilled also. No - just no. ØSirius, I've already had the Talk; they do that in muggle primary schools. Also, I go to Hogwarts - I share a dorm with boys. Trust me, I know all I will ever want or need to know. You will never, and I mean ever, have to give me the Talk,Æ Harry said. Sirius looked so relieved it was almost comical. Remus, however, always practical, took a deep breath. ØI know you think that, Harry, but the practicals are different in real lifeîØ Harry was panicking. He really didnít need this! He was 18, almost 19, for Merlinís sake! ØRemus, I promise, I swear on my wand, if I ever have any question I will come to you or Siri, but please, no talk,Æ Harry begged. Remus looked conflicted but nodded, relieved. ~ After their traumatic discussion about the Talk, Harry managed to ditch the men by asking to go exploring and pick out his room. In reality, he wanted to find a private spot to summon Kreacher. Calling out, Kreacher appeared with a pop. ØLittle Master,Æ Kreacher said. ØYou can call me Harrison or Harry if you like, Kreacher,Æ Harry offered. He doubted the elf would, but he had to try. The elf just shook his head, mumbling about proper rules and propriety. ØCan I have the locket now, Kreacher?Æ Harry asked. Kreacher stilled and reached into his dirty pillow case. Pulling out the locket, Harry reached for it and felt a fluttering in his scar. Putting it on, he felt his magic surge slightly before it settled and he felt inexplicably more peaceful - like he had been incomplete without knowing it. Once he had calmed, Harry looked at Kreacher, who was staring wide eyed, and smiled. ØThank you, Kreacher,Æ he said honestly. ØIf I can, I promise to destroy this locket.Æ The elf nodded. He had felt the Little Masterís magic just then and it felt like home. Harry, seeing the dirty pillowcase, held in his wince. ØKreacher, I would like for you go and rest. Have something warm to eat and bathe. When you are rested and full, please find something clean to wear,Æ Harry instructed. The elf was shocked by the orders and popped away after bowing. Such a great new Master. Rejoining the men, Harry told them he had picked a room; he was planning on using Regulusí old room. It was light and airy and had its own bathroom. They returned to the Leaky Cauldron to collect their things and, after a few hours, they had officially moved in. Kreacher had taken to following Harry around and would answer Siriusí call, but begrudgingly. ØReally, did you bribe him or something?Æ Sirius asked after the elf had finished cleaning their dinner plates up. He had cooked Harryís favourites. Harry just laughed. ØNo. I think the fact I was nice to him straight away and that he doesnít like muggles helped,Æ Harry lied. He didnít want to explain about the horcrux or Tom yet, he wanted to build a relationship first. Sirius just shook his head. ØMaybe you would be able to talk to my mother,Æ he mumbled to himself. ~ Going into Diagon Alley the next day with Sirius in order to get Nevilleís birthday present was manic. The papers had circulated the news, meaning Sirius was able to go out, but the crowds were horrendous. It reminded Harry of his first time in Diagon Alley with Hagrid all those years ago. Strangers were coming up to them, shaking Sirius' hand and saying how they had never believed it. ØSuch a shame!Æ ØOutrageous!Æ Some people though, Harry noticed, turned the other way. He could hear whispering about cover-ups and guilt. Harry turned dark eyes on them, and barely stopped himself from hexing the crowd. Sirius took it all in stride though, making sure to smile and ask for privacy while he shopped. Hearing the negative whispers didnít haze him, he just shrugged them off. Eventually, the crowds seemed to thin out; the sycophants got used to their presence and left them alone. ØSo, do you have any idea what you want to buy for your friend?Æ Sirius asked. Harry frowned. He had been thinking about it all night but he wasn't sure. He knew Neville loved plants, but everybody knew that - he wanted to get the boy something special. ØNo,Æ he admitted. ØI donít want to get him something obvious. I want Neville to see that I really do care.Æ Sirius nodded and looked at Harry with thought. ØHeís just a friend? This Neville?Æ he asked. Harry actually blanched at the thought of having romantic inclinations towards Neville. ØEw,Æ he said with a shudder, making Sirius laugh. The man was probably thinking Harry was still too young, when in reality Harry saw his classmates as children. As his body was physically eleven, almost twelve, Harry wasn't yet suffering from hormones thankfully. He was not looking forward to going through puberty again; at least this time he knew what to expect and wouldnít have to stumble blindly. ØYou know I wouldnít care if you like boys or girls or both, right?Æ Sirius said in a moment of parental brilliance. Harry smiled. He had suspected Sirius was bisexual in his last life - surely his animosity towards Snape couldnít be anything but pent up sexual tension. ØI'm gay,Æ Harry said plainly, shocking Sirius with his easy exclamation. Harry had never actually kissed a man, but as his soulmate was male he was pretty sure he was gay. It only added to the fact the two kisses he had shared with girls, Cho and Ginny, had been ratherè lacking. Sirius coughed slightly. He wasnít bothered about Harry preferences; homosexuality was accepted in the wizarding world. Nobody cared as the use of potions made it so family lines could continue. However, he was shocked at how easily Harry admitted his sexuality. Most young boys wouldnít openly say it - especially a boy who was raised by muggles. ØThatís good - fine, I mean, itís good that you know,Æ Sirius stuttered out. ØBeing gay in the wizarding world doesnít matter, well not really - there's are always some idiot who wonít accept it, but there are people like that everywhere, people who look down on family and blood status. I just wanted you to know, that nobody will judge you for being gay - if you were worried.Æ Harry smiled. He had never had a problem with peopleís sexuality, but the issue had never come up. ØThanks, Siri,Æ Harry said, spotting a small obscure knick-knack shop that looked promising. Entering the shop, Harry noticed that the wards must have been keeping the magical signature blocked as the shop practically hummed with latent magic. Sirius followed Harry. ØSo when did you notice that you liked boys?Æ he asked, interested in getting to know more about Harry. Harry shrugged. He didnít want to bring up Tom, so instead he turned and arched an eyebrow. ØHow did you know you liked both?Æ he asked instead, getting a roguish grin from Sirius. ØA bottle of firewhiskey and lack of propriety. However, thatís a story for when you're older,Æ he said with a wink. Harry chuckled under his breath at the answer. ØI've never liked girls - not like that, at least. I just didnít realise that wasn't ånormalí,Æ Harry said, his fingers curling into air quotes when he spoke. Sirius frowned. ØItís perfectly normal,Æ he insisted, understanding what Harry meant but wanting to reassure the boy. Harry smiled and noted something in the corner of his eye that caught his attention. There were two glass orbs and Harry could feel the impressive magic coming from them. Picking one up, he examined it. ØAre you interested in the message orbs?Æ asked a bored assistant, who materialized as soon as Harry picked up the orb. Hearing that, something in his mind clicked and Harry realised what the ball was. ØI think so. How do they work?Æ he asked. The shop assistant looked at Harry properly then and did an almost comical double take when he realised exactly who he was talking to. ØThey work a bit like muggle telephones. Theyíre connected, you simply speak a message into your orb and the message can be played back in the other,Æ the assistant said, staring reverently at Harryís forehead, where his scar was hidden by his fringe. Harry hummed. ØHow much are they?Æ he asked. They would be the perfect present. ØTwelve galleons.Æ Harry considered them again. They were a bit expensive, not that he couldnít afford them, however he knew that he wanted them. ØIíll take them, please,Æ Harry said. Sirius nodded. ØCan you gift wrap one of them?Æ he addressed the shopkeeper. Turning to Harry, he asked, ØI'm assuming that you're planning on giving one to Neville?Æ The shopkeeper let out an actual squeak when he spotted Sirius and, with shaking hands, took the orbs and ran to the back room after frantically nodding his head. ~ Once they were done with the gift shopping, Sirius and Harry wandered around the stores with their cloaks up, their faces partially hidden. Sirius had insisted on paying, claiming it was his job to take care of Harry. There was a large crowd of children outside of Quality Quidditch Supplies, making Harry remember that Nimbus had just released the 2001. Seeing the direction of Harryís gaze, Sirius grinned and asked, ØDo you like quidditch, Harry?Æ Harry frowned. He had loved it, but he honestly hadnít played in so long and never in this life. He settled with an, ØItís okay.Æ ØJust okay? Blasphemy!Æ Sirius said dramatically. Harry rolled his eyes at his antics. ØI was considering trying out for the team,Æ he told the dog animagus. Siriusís eye lit up with glee. ØThatís great, Harry! I loved quidditch when I was at school; me and your father were on the team. I was a beater and your dad was a chaser. He was captain as well in our 6th year,Æ Sirius said, excitedly taking Harryís hand and pulling him through the crowd and into the shop. The shop was not that busy inside, however there was a familiar family standing by the counter. The door had opened with a bell, meaning that when Harry had observed the shopís occupants, he had met the eyes of Lucius Malfoy, who had looked up at the sound. Harry remembered how Draco had bought the entire Slytherin team new brooms and wondered if the boy would do so again. The boy didnít really need to; he was a fantastic seeker, he didnít need to resort to bribery. Sirius saw the Malfoys and frowned, probably conflicted by his lack of compulsions warring with his own memories and possible regrets. Harry gave his hand a tight squeeze before he pulled free. Lucius, with Narcissa on his arm, approached the pair, Draco trailing behind them. ØHarrison,Æ the blond said to Harry, giving the boy a nod, before he turned to Sirius. ØMight I offer my congratulations on claiming your title, Lord Black,Æ he said, offering his hand. The gesture was not lost on either Harry or Sirius: Lucius was offering his friendship, or more likely, a political alliance. Harry wasn't sure if Sirius would take it; he knew Sirius had probably fought the man during the last war and he wouldnít hold it against him if he held onto his resentment. Sirius sent Harry a look that said he would be explaining things later before he took Lucius hand and gave it one firm shake. ØI welcome them, Lord Malfoy, but please call me Sirius. We are family, after all,Æ he said, giving Narcissa a smile. Narcissa was looking at her cousin with calculating eyes, taking in every detail. Eventually she smiled lightly and Harry saw her eyes soften. ØCousin Sirius,Æ she greeted. Sirius stepped forward and pulled the aristocratic woman into a light hug, shocking Harry. He had never even heard his old Sirius mention the blonde womanís name in his last life. ØItís good to see you, Cissy,Æ Sirius whispered. He had clearly decided to ignore his resentment at the former Death Eaters. The woman smiled and pulled out of the hug, before she lightly swatted Siriusí arm. ØNone of that, you're making a scene,Æ she lightly scolded. Sirius just grinned and reached over to peck her cheek. Narcissa smiled again before she turned to look at Harry. Her expression didnít change as she looked at the boy. She remembered reading Dracoís letter and had spoken about him with Lucius after their meeting at the trial. She understood now what her husband had meant. This child feltè alive. He seemed to vibrate magic, but she felt no overwhelming power from him, and his eyes - they looked like they held all the knowledge in the world. They were both old and young, full of secrets and radiating life. Holding out a hand, Narcissa smiled lightly when Harry bent and kissed the air. ØLady Malfoy,Æ Harry greeted. ØYou may call me Narcissa, Heir Potter. After all, like my cousin has said, we are practically family,Æ Narcissa told the small boy. Harry smiled. ØThen you must call me Harrison or Harry.Æ Narcissa turned to Sirius then and the two lost themselves talking about family and memories. Narcissa told Sirius of plans for having them over to Malfoy manor for dinner. Draco was watching with interest Harry noted, so he turned to him with a smile. ØHello, Draco,Æ he said, making sure his voice was friendly. Draco smiled slightly. ØHello, Harry,Æ Draco replied. ØAre you getting a broom for school?Æ Harry asked. ØYes. I'm trying to persuade father to buy the entire team new brooms. Have you seen the school ones? Theyíre just terrible!Æ Draco said. Harry smiled lightly. ØThey are, but still, it's best to have terrible brooms for the beginners. Imagine the muggleborns having to ride the latest Nimbus model the first time they got on a broom,Æ Harry said. Draco had stilled when Harry had said muggleborn, but smirked as he obviously pictured it. ØIt would be a disaster,Æ he said with slight glee. ØOr, heaven forgive, Neville. Heís my best friend, but give him a broom and he is likely to cause permanent damage.Æ Draco laughed. ØOr a potion,Æ he added before suddenly looking unsure as he had insulted Harryís friend, though not maliciously. Harry nodded. ØTrue, but watch him in Herbology and youíll see how amazing he can be. Heís also surprisingly adept at pranks, as Ron Weasley can attest to.Æ Harry did want to try with Draco, but he wouldnít allow him to think Neville was a fool. Draco's eyes widened. ØLongbottom was the one to turned the weasel green?Æ he asked. It had been a running bet in the Slytherin common room. Harry nodded. ØHe didnít take kindly to Ronís Gryffindor Light warrior speeches, which usually feature me and how I'm evil incarnate for being in Ravenclaw.Æ ØI swear that entire family is mental. Their dad is currently having our manor raided hoping to find Dark artefacts, like we would keep them lying around,Æ Draco scoffed then stilled when he realised what he had said. Harry didnít hold any resentment towards Arthur Weasley. It wasn't his fault his wife and children were morally corrupt twats. However, the man was a true believer in Dumbledore and his Light regime - not in on his plans, but a supporter. Harry didnít doubt he targeted the Darker families purposely and with Dumbledore's support, honestly thinking he was doing the right thing. ØWhat do you expect? I bet theyíre all Gryffindors,Æ Harry said. Draco smirked. ØAll the weasels are Gryffindors.Æ ØTo be fair, I do rather enjoy the twins. They have rather Slytherin cunning at times,Æ Harry said, making Draco frown. ØTheyíre menaces,Æ he said, offended at the thought of Weasleys in Slytherin. Harry just smirked. ØBut I can see their potential.Æ Draco frowned when he saw the calculating look in Harryís eye. He had observed the raven haired boy this year. He had wanted to offer his hand in friendship, but had been unsure how to approach him. Harry Potter was nothing like he had expected him to be: he had grown up with the stories, hearing the rumours. Yet, the boy who turned up to Hogwarts was not the boy he had spent his life picturing, and he found he rather liked that. Harry noticed Lucius was observing them and turned to include him in their discussion. ØDid you ever play quidditch at school?Æ he asked. Lucius had observed the boy as he spoke and had to admit Harry was skilful in how he spoke and manipulated people. He didnít get the sense the boy was being malicious, he was just very calculating. ØI did. I was a chaser during my school years.Æ Sirius jumped into the conversation then, almost relieved to be escaping Narcissaís talks of family and available women. ØHe was quite good, too. Couldnít beat us lions, though.Æ Lucius frowned at that. ØDo you wish to play when you return back to school, Harrison?Æ he asked instead of responding to Sirius' claim. ØI think I will try out for the Ravenclaw team. I want to be a seeker,Æ Harry said. ØYou have the build for it,Æ Draco said, though he wasn't sure he wanted Harry to try out. He didnít like losing and Harry really did the perfect build for the position, and if the rumours were true, he was a natural on a broom. Harry smiled. ØI still havenít decided. What position do you want, Draco?Æ he asked. ØI want to be a seeker, but I wouldnít mind being a chaser,Æ he said. ØI'm sure you would do well at either,Æ Harry said. ØWell, if you're considering trying out, you will need a broom,Æ Sirius said, Øand everything else that a broom requires.Æ ØI donít need you to get me a broom, Siri,Æ Harry said. He didnít like having money spent on him. Even now knowing he could afford it, Harry still always got a twinge when he had people buy him things. ØNonsense! Itís your birthday in a few days anyway,Æ Sirius answered, brushing off Harryís concern. ØItís your birthday?Æ Draco asked, forgetting that fact from the books and stories. ØYes, on the 31st,Æ Harry replied. ØIf you would like, I'm meeting some friends on the 1st in Diagon Alley to celebrate and get our school supplies. You're welcome to join us - you can bring a friend as well if you're wary of the company.Æ Draco had a considering look on his face. ØNo, itís fine. I shall have to check, but I think that it would be okay.Æ ØIt is fine, dear,Æ Narcissa said, having overheard. ØI shall have to send you an owl, but I will have you, Remus, and Sirius over for dinner in a week and we can celebrate your birthday properly.Æ ØWould you mind if I joined you? I have business in the alley and was planning going on that day,Æ Lucius said, making the hairs on Harryís arm stand on end. It seemed fate really was trying to recreate certain things. ØNot at all, Lucius. In fact, I'm sure that Sirius and Remus would welcome your company as being responsible for five children is a daunting task.Æ Sirius had returned at this point, his arms laden with supplies ranging from goalie gloves to wood treatment kits. Seeing him struggle, Harry rolled his eyes and regretted having to pretend to be magicless. åStupid ministry trace rules,í Harry thought. Instead, he asked, exasperated, ØYou do know you're a wizard, right?Æ Sirius looked over his pile. ØWhat has that got to do with anything?Æ he asked. ØMagic, Siri. You could have shrunk those things and put them in a charmed bag - or any other countless option instead of struggling,Æ Harry explained. Sirius grinned and dropped his pile on the counter. ØItís more fun this way,Æ he told Harry. Harry shook his head and smiled when he heard Draco muttering about Gryffindors. ~ After getting their supplies and brooms - a Nimbus 2001, a broom Harry had neither owned or ridden in his last life - the group split up. Harry promised to write to Draco about the meeting on the 1st and Narcissa promised a family meal for Harryís birthday in return. Seeing the time, the two decide to return home. Harry spent his time grumbling when he had to apparate side-along. Leaving the drawing room, Harry allowed Sirius to follow him into the library. ØI didnít know that you had a cousin,Æ Harry lied, taking a seat in his favourite chair. He had been pleased to find the goblins hadn't changed the chair in the refurbishment. Sirius winced. ØI have three cousins: Cissy, Andromeda, and Bellatrix. When we were kids, we were quite close; well, I was close with Andy and Cissa. Bella was always a bitè,Æ Sirius cut off. ØWhen I went off to Hogwarts, I stopped caring about them. Bella was a 5th year, Andy was a 3rd year, and Cissy was a year younger. I was sorted into Gryffindor, which Blacks just donít do, and, with the compulsions, I became the person I was. I see now that my behaviour probably caused them some pain; I was always a strong headed kid, standing up for what I believed in. There's a strong chance I would have gone Gryffindor anyway, but wellè.Æ ØYou canít change the past, Siri,Æ Harry said, the irony not lost on him. ØI know and I would love to get to know Cissa again, even Lucius. I didnít like him in school, he was always too Slytherin for me. Then, when the war was starting up, he was already a tattooed member of the other side,Æ Sirius admitted before he turned to look at Harry. ØHow do you know Lucius and why do you call him Lucius?Æ Harry sighed and explained, ØI met him the day of your trial, we spoke before it started. He asked for me to call him his given name and I consented for him to do the same.Æ Sirius nodded. ØYou do know he was a real Death Eater, right? He was never under the Imperius.Æ Harry nodded. ØI know, but the war is over and now heís just a man. Granted, an influential man who holds a lot of power, but he only ever had loyalty to the Dark cause anyway, not Voldemort.Æ Harry added that information, remembering how Lucius had abandoned Voldemort. He was a true believer in the Dark cause and had joined hoping to build a better world. Sirius looked startled. ØHow could you possibly know that?Æ he asked. Harry shrugged. ØTrust me,Æ he answered without answering. Sirius shook his head and looked around the room. ØYou know, you spend too much time in here,Æ he said. He wanted to question the boy further but he didnít want to risk pushing for too much too fast. Harry snorted. ØMost people would like the fact that their child enjoys spending time in the library,Æ he commented. Sirius grinned. ØHave you seen the duelling room?Æ he asked, twitching in his seat. Harry stilled. ØNo,Æ he said truthfully and barely resisted hitting himself - he was a fool. He had been using and stretching his magic as much as he could a lot more since the houseís wards prevented the ministry trace from working. He had noticed his magical stamina increasing and using the duelling room would be massively beneficial. He had totally forgotten about it as, in his last life, the room had been warded off and closed for their safety -because practicing duelling during a war where people kept cursing at him was so dangerous. Sirius grinned and looked triumphant. He had started to randomly pop up whenever Harry had been spending, by Siriusí own words, too much time in the library, and tried to drag him out. ØCome on, then,Æ he said. Harry trailed after the man. The duelling room looked like it had been upgraded. The practice dummies looked new and the wards around the area where much more complex. ØYouíve got your wand on you, right?Æ Sirius asked. Harry rolled his eyes. As if he would leave his wand behind; true, he was proficient with wandless magic, but Sirius didnít know that. Entering the room after Sirius, Harry looked around and saw how much the room had been upgraded; he had only entered it once in his last life. Now, the room had a row of practice dummies and a power gauge, meaning they could have the difficulty altered depending on who was using them. There was also a raised platform and a small selection of daggers and other physical weapons. Sirius darted into the room and spun around. ØItís brilliant in here, isnít it?Æ he asked Harry. Harry smiled at his excitement and nodded. ØWas it like this when you were younger?Æ he asked. ØBack then it was more basic. Your goblin friends really did a great job.Æ Harry nodded and went to inspect the power gauge. Seeing Harryís attention waver, Sirius came over. ØYou can use them but I would prefer it if you waited for either me or Remus to be around,Æ he told the small looking boy. Harry nodded. He didnít plan on doing that as it would be difficult to explain his prowess, but what they didnít know wouldnít harm them. Sirius grinned and set up a single dummy for Harry to use, putting it on the lowest setting. Harry resigned himself to fighting a child dummy and flicked his wrist so his wand was out. Racking his brain, Harry thought of a simple spell chain a child should be able to do: a simple blasting and stunning combination. Sirius took a step back when he felt Harryís magic. His small godson was deceptive. He had such control over his magical core that he could go undetected unless he unleashed his power. The control and speed in which he could wield his magic was advanced; his finesse was like somebody already of age, not a nearly twelve-year-old. ØWell, you make me look like a novice,Æ he joked. Harry sent him a bland look. ØHardly, I just find that magic comes much more easily to me, especially since I had my blocks removed last summer. I can sense magic, feel it, and with my memory I donít really need to practice a spell more than once before Iíve got it for good.Æ Sirius hummed. The explanation was true, but it felt like there was something missing. ØWell, I'm probably at the same stage as you right now. My healer recommended me coming in and practicing for half an hour a day, but my stamina is shot,Æ he admitted. Harry made sure not to look sympathetic. ØGive yourself some time - you know youíll get there,Æ he said with conviction. Sirius smiled. ØWell, maybe you could join me. You can show me what else you know - donít think I was fooled by those firstie spells.Æ Harry nodded. ØI suppose I could practice with you.Æ Harry would just have to stick to 4th year and below. ~ Chapter End Notes I've tried lemon and honey & putting 'vicks' on my chest nothing works :'( also is it summer or spring? My healer recommended me coming in and practicing for half an hour a day, but my stamina is shot,Æ he admitted. Harry made sure not to look sympathetic. ØI suppose I could practice with you.Æ Harry would just have to stick to 4th year and below. ~ 30th July 1992 By the time Neville's birthday came around, Harry had developed a minor routine with Sirius and Remus. In the few days they had been together, all three men had sorted out their own lives around each other. Harry would wake up and immediately head to the library (he was so close to finding an answer he could practically feel it; he hoped it wasn't just wishful thinking, but his magic seemed to be humming, making Harry believe he was getting close.) After that, Harry would have lunch when Sirius arrived and then go with him to practice his magic. He had slowly upped his skill level, nothing drastic, when he noted that Sirius wasn't going to say anything. He knew he needed to sit both men down and explain a few things to them soon, but he hoped he would be able to wait until after the custody trial had passed .and maybe after he had a way to return Tom to sanity. It would definitely make it easier to sell if his soulmate was sane. For Sirius, he would go to his healerís appointments in the mornings, have lunch, and then spend half an hour with Harry in the duelling room. By that time he would disappear, usually to have a short nap. Sirius was also working through his familyís journals, looking up Wizengamot rules and rulings. He had told Harry and Remus during dinner that he would use all the power he could to ensure he won his custody suit. The temporary grant of custody to Remus was a bonus they could and would use, but Sirius appearing interested in re-entering the world as a proper pureblood Lord would be beneficial. Remus, as opposed to his more active family members, seemed to putter in and around all day. He would spend time with Harry while Sirius was at his healerís appointments, letting Harry read and research in the library, while he himself flicked through books. Then he would go out and run errands in the afternoons. There had been the first real argument the other day when Remus spoke about the full moon; it would occur on the 5th, meaning Remus would not have to worry the custody trial, but he was refusing to allow Harry or Sirius to get him the Wolfsbane potion. Remus had told them he was wanting to become less dependent on them anymore than he already was. Harry had pointed out he knew a reliable Potions Master, which had led to an even greater argument as Sirius was reminded that Harry not only knew Severus, but also liked and respected the man greatly. 28th June 1992 ØI donít understand why you're being so difficult about this, Moony. I have more money than I could ever spend. Even if I lived to be 1000 and penniless, I want to do this for you!Æ Sirius shouted, standing up from the table and pacing. Remus remained sitting, but his face was tight. ØI have survived many times without the potion and besides, I simply canít afford it for a few months. I will be fine without it like I was before,Æ he said, his voice not raised but strained. ØBut you donít have to!Æ Sirius said, turning to look at the wolf. ØI can help you! Why wonít you let me help you?!Æ Remus sighed. ØSirius, youíre already doing too muchîÆ ØîNo, I'm not!Æ ØI'm living here without paying rentîÆ ØîSo? Iíd be living here with Harry anyway! You're helping me! Let me help you!Æ ØIf you're concerned, I can always ask Severus to brew the potion. He promised to help me over the summer and to contact him if I needed anything. I'm sure he would brew the potion for you,Æ Harry said. ØSeeîÆ Sirius said before cutting himself off. ØWait. Severus? As in Snape? You know Snivellus?Æ he asked. Harry and Remus both sighed simultaneously. ØSirius, you canít call him that,Æ Remus said. ØButî!Æ Harry interrupted him this time, ØI know you didnít get on when you were in school, but Professor Snape really helped me this year. Not only did he brew my ointments and potions, but he looked out for me. He even followed up on his concerns about my family and made sure I would be okay this summer. Heís changed from the boy you knew.Æ Seeing Siriusí look, Harry continued, ØAnd trust me, I know everything about his past and how he and Lucius have matching tattoos. However, I donít hold it against him; heís paid for his past mistakes. Heís also not the bad guy in our lives. And Remus, remember how you felt when you found out about the Dursleyís? When you held me and promised to be there for me? Well, you canít do that if you keep doing this. Take the potion, please.Æ Both men looked shocked, Sirius also looking slightly ashamed. Reminding them about Dumbledore worked exactly how Harry had wanted it to and, although he was emotionally blackmailing them, it was for the greater good - a phrase that, even thinking it, made Harry cringe. ØHe really helped you?Æ Sirius asked after a few moments of silence. ØYes, he really helped me,Æ Harry confirmed. Sirius and Remus seemed to share a look, before Remus spoke, ØIf you could contact him and ask him to brew it, I would be very grateful. Make sure to mention he will get paid market price.Æ Remus had smelt Harryís emotions and he knew that the boy cared for him and was genuinely worried. Harry nodded. ØThank you.Æ Sirius sat back down and Harry could hear him grumbling about Severus under his breath. ØI know you hated each other, but Siri, please, if you're willing to give Lucius a chance, why not Professor Snape?Æ Harry argued. Sirius opened his mouth but didnít speak. Closing it, he ran his hand over his face. ØI'm sorry,Æ he said eventually. ØItís hard for me - sorting out my feelings and emotions towards certain people.Æ Harry and Remus nodded. They too had found it difficult to control their emotions towards people after they had been magically cleansed. 30th July 1992 Like Narcissa had mentioned, an owl had arrived later in the week, inviting the three of them to dinner at Malfoy manor on Harryís birthday to celebrate. With that in mind, the two men were planning on going out and buying everything they needed to properly celebrate whilst Harry was at Nevilleís. Flooing over with Sirius, as Remus was busy in planning mode, Harry was greeted by a stern faced Augusta Longbottom and a slightly embarrassed Neville. Harry had rarely met the Longbottom matriarch, however he knew of her fearsome reputation in and out of the Wizengamot. The woman was surprisingly tall and her grey hair was up in an elaborate bun. Her robes when dark purple and expensive without being over the top. Looking around the room, Harry noted that was the general theme - it was clear the Longbottom family had money, but they didnít advertise the fact as sharply as other families, such as the Blacks (once upon a time) or the Malfoys. Seeing as he was the adult with the greatest title in their party, Sirius stepped forward and bowed slightly, taking Augustaís hand and kissing the air above it. ØThank you for having Harry today, Dowager Longbottom,Æ he said formally. Augustaís stern expression seemed to melt slightly as she released Siriusí hand. ØNone of that, now; you will call me Augusta. I've known you since you were a boy, for Merlinís sake! I never did believe you were guilty; I even wrote to the former minister about it, but got written off as a grieving mother - for shame! And as for this young lad, well, heís made my Neville very happy, really brought him out of his shell. I'm happy to have him.Æ Sirius looked shocked and pleased; knowing he had genuine support was heartening. Seeing as Sirius looked slightly choked up, Harry took a step forward and took the dowager Ladyís hand, ØThank you for having me, Dowager Longbottom. I'm very pleased to meet you - and happy birthday, Neville,Æ Harry greeted, kissing the air above her hand before sending Neville a large smile. ØI'm very pleased to meet you too, young man, but you should call me Augusta or gran. I expect Iíll be seeing a lot of you, so we shall forgo the formalities,Æ the elderly lady said. Harry nodded and sent the woman a smile. Neville shook his head lovingly and turned to his best friend. ØThanks, Harry. The girls arenít here yet, but we can go through and wait for them. My gran already knows Amelia, whoís bringing them both.Æ ~ After Sirius left and the girls arrived, Dowager Longbottom waved them off with Neville, saying she would send an elf when it was time for tea. Neville lead them through the house and out into the garden. Seeing the manor for the first time from the outside, Harry saw it was a large Tudor manor. The gardens were extremely well maintained and Harry guessed that Neville had spent most of his time since returning from school out here. Finding a quiet spot by a large pond, the four friends all sat down. As soon as they were settled, three sets of eyes turned to Harry and he gulped slightly. ØSoè,Æ he said, trailing off. Susan huffed and reached over to lightly clip the back of his head. ØYou had better start talking or I will hex you. There's no aunt Amelia to watch me and I know Nevilleís wards will block the wand trace,Æ she threatened. Harry jokingly raised his arms. ØAlright, alright, no need to resort to such violence,Æ he said. He then explained what had happened, going into more detail than his letter had. Once he had finished his friends looked contemplative. ØMy gran doesnít mention the war much. Apparently, Dumbledore's been kicking up a fuss since your meeting, trying to get you back to your aunt and uncle. Heís being sneaky about it but she knows itís him.Æ ØHe really needs to get over his obsession with me,Æ Harry said, before adding in a sly voice, ØI mean I know I'm fabulous, but I like my men a little less decrepit.Æ Neville choked and looked green while the girls giggled. ØPlease donít talk about him like that! Heís ancient, he even taught my gran,Æ the blond boy wailed. Susan, who was still giggling, calmed slightly. ØSo how do you like your men?Æ ØTall, dark haired, and pale eyed,Æ Harry said, remembering a sixteen-year-old Tom Riddle. The three looked slightly shocked, but they didnít react negatively to Harryís confirmation about his sexuality. Hannah instead tilted her head and said, ØReally, I prefer blonds myself.Æ She dramatically looked over at Neville, who blushed and threw grass at her. The group laughed again and Harry found himself relieved about their acceptance. The next half an hour was spent talking about their summer plans. Neville was going away with his gran and his uncle Algie for the last week of the summer to visit distant family in the Netherlands, which he was excited about as they had a large botanical shop there which sold a variety of exotic plants and herbs. Susan was staying home as her aunt was too busy, but she had plans to visit the beach a few times for the day, and Hannah was leaving in a week to spend the remainder of the holiday until the last week in the USA visiting her cousins. Eventually the house elf promised by Augusta popped up and they made their way into the informal dining room. Harry saw that Augusta was with an elderly man and Harry assumed that this was Nevilleís famous uncle Algie. Looking at the table, Harry saw that a large arrangement of party nibbles had been set up with plates and the children were told to help themselves buffet style. Harry was happy to see such a large selection and picked a few different sandwiches to try, as well as salad and cakes. After the food was cleared, the presents were passed out. Neville opened Susanís present first and saw that she had bought him a book on medicinal uses of Herbology and a childís medi.wizard kit. Although slightly practical, Harry could see that Neville loved the book; he had remembered in his last life how the boy had at one point bemoaned how he didnít have any skills apart from Herbology, which is wrongly considered useless by many. Harryís present came next. Opening in the gift-wrapped box, Neville looked confused before he read the card Harry had written and added, ØWow, Harry, these are great!Æ He looked at the ball with renewed wonder now that he knew what it was. Harry smiled. ØWell, this way we can talk all the time, even at Hogwarts,Æ he said. The girls looked at the note and oohíd and ahhíd once they understood what the crystal orb was. Neville moved on and opened Hannahís gift; she had bought the box a rare hybrid plant, a miniature pineapple-apple tree. Once grown, it would never be over four feet tall, and the fruits would be the size of golf ball at largest. ØThank you,Æ Neville said, looking slightly overwhelmed by the sheer fact he had not only friends, but friends who knew him and what he liked. ØItís okay Neville, thatís what friends are for,Æ Harry said, pulling Neville into a one-armed hug. ØYeah Nev, I'm just happy you liked my present. I thought it was too practical,Æ Susan said, smiling. ØHonestly, itís great,Æ Neville said, stroking the cover of the book. Augusta, who had been watching, smiled at the children. ØThank you all for making the day special,Æ she said. ØNow, I do believe itís time for birthday cake.Æ Singing happy birthday to Neville, Harry was surprised that the day had passed so quickly. He had an hour before Sirius or Remus would arrive to collect him. Spending the remainder of the time playing a few games of exploding snap, Harry was happy to have seen his friends. He had missed them, even though they were so young in comparison to him - at least mentally - Harry found he still liked them and enjoyed their company. ~ Meanwhile at Grimmauld Place ØHave you noticed anything off with Harry?Æ Remus asked, thinking about the boyís complex emotions. Sirius hummed. He had forgiven Remus for his part in his incarceration and loved the man like a brother, but if it came down to it, he would always pick Harry. The boy was his son for all intents and purposes. When he blood-adopted him as part of an old magic godfather ritual James and Lily had chosen, he had developed a deep bond and love for the child. Even after being separated for so long, as soon as his magic was cleansed, that bond had flared back to life. Sure, they were still getting to know one another, but Sirius knew, no matter what, he would pick Harry. ØLike what?Æ he asked casually, trying to keep his emotions in check as he knew, especially since being cleansed, that Remus could smell emotions on a person if he tried or if they were strong enough. Remus caught on to how wary Sirius was. ØHeís the most amazing child I have ever met,Æ Remus said, making Sirius relax slightly, Øbut, I donít know, I sometimes think he knows more that he should, even with his memory.Æ Sirius considered this. ØI think that with everything he has been through, that maybe he has secrets heís not yet willing to share.Æ Remus smiled at how well Sirius had taken over parenting - he was instinctual. ØYou think he will tell us?Æ he asked. ØI think he wants to tell us, but heís waiting until heís ready,Æ Sirius said, thinking about the time he had caught Harry looking at him before he realised himself and pulled away. ØYou're probably right,Æ he said, before he smirked. ØYou know, I saw him do a sixth-year spell perfectly the other day. Wordlessly.Æ Sirius snorted. ØPlease, I caught him wandlessly floating a book to himself. I donít think he even realised he had done it. It just zoomed to his outstretched hand when he was reading. He checked it then sent it back with a flick of his wrist.Æ Remus felt his eyes go wide. ØI'm guessing he didnít notice you?Æ ØNo. I think he forgets sometimes or gets distracted by whatever heís searching for.Æ ØSearching for?Æ Remus asked. ØAll of his books are similar: theyíre all Dark, but they all relate to the same branches of magic. He must be searching for something - nobody reads that much or that obsessively without a reason,Æ Sirius said. He didnít mention the branch of Dark magic, soul magic, as he didnít want to worry Remus. He was going to have a talk with the boy soon if it continued; he may have decided to be more open with Dark magic, but soul magic was dangerous. ØHow advanced do you think he really is?Æ Remus asked in wonder - controlled wandless magic was extremely rare. It had been in the Potter family before, but to have it you had to have a large magical core as magical abilities like wandless magic take a lot of core magic to manifest, let alone master. If Harry had done so before the age of twelve, then Remus was more than impressed, he was flabbergasted. ØHonestly, I have no idea. And I know for a fact heís trying to hide it from us, so I canít even begin to guess.Æ ØIf we keep showing him we love him no matter what, I donít think it will take him long to come to us,Æ Remus said. ØI'm just glad it wasn't just me thinking he was hiding something.Æ Sirius smiled, glad he still had his friend. ØHeíll talk when heís ready.Æ ~ 31st July 1992 For the first time in his life, Harry didnít force himself to stay awake and wish himself a happy birthday. After he had been picked up yesterday, he had been surprisingly tired and had gone to bed early to make sure he would be awake and ready for today, his first proper birthday. His friends had given him his gifts as they all left and instructed him to open them today, so Harry knew that he would definitely have presents, real presents, for the first time. In his last life, everything he had received, he realised now, probably came from his own money. He didnít doubt Remus and Sirius had gone over the top as well, so he was looking forward to a day of celebration without any strings or manipulations. Waking up, Harry pulled on a pair of light grey denim jeans and a navy shirt. ØIt was a Marauderís tradition when we were in Hogwarts. Birthday boys got pranked in the morning.Æ Harry smiled. He was no longer doing any harm and did maintain the house for them and cooked without complaint. Harry shrugged. It was only really Remus who ever sat and relaxed in the room. Harry saw the rather large pile of presents and rolled his eyes. I'm making up for ten years of no presents!Æ he argued before picking up a present and giving it a slight shake. Harry shared an amused look with Remus before moving to sit with Sirius. ØFine, but I want you to promise: next time, no extra presents. That includes Yule, Sirius.Æ Sirius pouted and opened his mouth to argue but Remus cleared his throat. ØFine,Æ he said, slightly petulantly. Harry rolled his eyes and picked up a present. Reading the tag, he saw it was from Remus. Inside was a beautiful leather bound book, the cover engraved with a basilisk and a unicorn. Looking at the back, he saw a phoenix and a thestral. ØItís beautiful,Æ he told the wolf, genuinely entranced. ØI know how you like to take notes, so I thought you would like something attractive to take them in. Itís charmed to be never ending and, if you mark it with your magical signature, only you and those you allow will be able to read it,Æ Remus explained. Harry smiled and opened the blank book. ØThank you,Æ he said honestly. There was also a large box of chocolates from the wolf, which Harry noted was of the dark variety - his favourite. Sirius smiled at their interaction before his excitement got the better of him. Picking up a random present, he practically threw it at Harry. ØOpen this one next!Æ Harry smiled at his eagerness and opened the present after seeing it was from Neville. Inside was a black and silver bracelet with a small raven charm. Although maybe slightly effeminate, Harry was still very pleased with the gift. He was especially intrigued when he read the note explaining his friends had worked together to each buy combined presents. The raven was supposed to represent Harry. With this in mind, Harry found Hannahís present next. Opening the small box, he found a potion charm and a badger charm. The note explained the vial was supposed to go extremely cold when in the presence of deadly potions and the badger was purely for decoration and to remind Harry of his friends in Hufflepuff. Susan following the same theme and bought Harry two charms as well: a lion and an eye. The lion was to remind Harry of his own bravery and of Neville. The eye would apparently help him to hone his occlumency and other mind-magics. He didnít really need that charm as he had mastered the art in his year at Hogwarts; well, as much as he was able to without a legilimens. Even if he hadnít, his heir rings protected his mind - still, he appreciated the gift and the thought they had put into it. Next, Harry started in on the pile of broom related things from Sirius. Harry amusedly asked the animagus if it really was necessary for him to buy Harry everything, even items like keeper gloves when the boy never played keeper. ØYes,Æ was Siriusí response, though he looked slightly abashed when both Harry and Remus laughed. Harry laughed and noted he still had a few presents. Picking up a present wrapped in silver, Harry saw it was from the Malfoys and was surprised: he assumed they would wait and give it to him later if they brought him anything. Inside the box was a written note. Considering the more effeminate writing, Harry thought the note was by Narcissa. ~ Happy birthday Harrison, Weíre very happy that you accepted our invitation for dinner tonight and cannot wait to see you. As I know how Draco usually loves to tear into his presents straight away, I thought it was best to send you yours now so you didnít have to wait. I do hope you like them. Best wishes, The Malfoy family ~ Looking inside the box, Harry saw that they had gotten him two subscriptions to Quidditch Weekly and Young Minds, a monthly magazine that talks about politics and important discoveries in the world of academia. There was also a large crystal wizarding chess set, which Harry found to be beautiful, and a box of Honeydukes selections. Harry also received a card from Dumbledore; he opened it and could feel the magic coming from the card. The magic was very slight but he had been developing his magic sensitivity ever since he had it unlocked in his last life; his ability to feel magic was now well honed. Scowling at the familiar scrawl, Harry opened the card. ~ Harry, My dear boy, I do hope you have a wonderful birthday and do consider visiting and returning to your family. If not them, then perhaps a familiar wizarding family. I'm sure I know the perfect family who would love to take you in. It really would be for the best, donít you agree? Kind regards and salutations, Professor Albus Percival Wulfric Brian Dumbledore ~ Harry snorted as he read the note and passed it to Sirius, who read it with a scowl. The animagus barely resisted his urge to rip the letter up before Remus took it and read it himself. ØHe really doesnít give up, does he?Æ Sirius said, standing and pacing slightly. He hadn't said anything but Harry could see he was worried; most likely about the trial and the influence Dumbledore had. ØCan you feel the magic on the paper?Æ Harry asked. Sirius stilled and closed his eyes - Harry recognised it as him connecting to his own core. Unlike Harry, who could do it instinctively, Sirius had to concentrate - and even then such sensitivity didnít always work. Remus, he noted, didnít attempt to sense it at all; Harry assumed the wolf knew he wouldnít have a chance at detecting the very subtle compulsion. Although a strong wizard, Harry would acknowledge that Sirius did have more latent magic to draw from in his core compared to Remus. Remus did, however, have an advantage with his wolf senses and stamina. Sirius took a few seconds before he let out a slight growl, sounding suspiciously like he did in his dog form. ØItís got a compulsion on it. I can just about feel it when I'm concentrating my magic.Æ Harry nodded. ØYes, I felt it as soon as I noted the envelope. Itís subtle, but rather strong.Æ Remus placed the note down hesitantly. ØDo you have any idea what the compulsion was for and if Iíll be affected? I donít have rings like you two and I didnít even note the charm.Æ Harry double check the envelope with his magic, feeling the charm. ØItís okay, it was aimed at only me. It was to make me agree with him, most likely with the suggestion about visiting my family and trusting him again.Æ Remus let out a relieved breath. That was his new fear - the fact he was susceptible to Dumbledore's compulsions and could therefore be used against Harry and Sirius was more terrifying than any full moon. Even his occlumency training, although not advanced, didnít help and his wolf, which usually hated being manipulated, didnít seem to help at all. ØWhy?Æ Sirius asked. ØWhatís his plan, his goal? Why doesnít he just leave you alone?Æ Harry sighed. ØI canít be sure.Æ Sirius swore, making Remus glare. ØPads, not now. Itís Harryís birthday and we are going to make it special.Æ Sirius nodded and let out a long breath. ØI would just love to know what he's up to.Æ ØWe all wouîÆ Remus was cut off when Sirius suddenly jumped up and made a muffled noise, sounding distinctly like a curse word. ØWhat are you doing now?Æ the wolf asked in exasperation. ØI have an idea! I'm such an idiot - I had completely forgotten about him!Æ Sirius shouted, sounding like he was halfway up the stairs. Harry and Remus shared a look and followed the sounds of Siriusí footsteps. They found him on the 3rd floor, outside an office, talking to a portrait Harry had never really noticed. The family portraits had been frozen by the goblins and left that way. However, Harry soon realised why Sirius was so excited. In front of them was a portrait Harry recognised well: it was Phineas Nigellus Black. ØItís about bloody time, boy!Æ Phineas berated Sirius. ØI've been trying to get your attention all week!Æ Sirius mumbled an apology while Harry wondered how he had never noticed the painting - in this life or the last. He suspected Dumbledore had the paintings removed as most of them were thrown out. ØHello again, sir,Æ Harry said, drawing the attention to himself. Phineasí painting turned and let out a huff of air. ØYou! I donít know whether to be proud of you or worried. Youíve got that fool of a man Dumbledore acting rather erratically,Æ he said. Remus seemed to suddenly recognise the painting and took a step forward, placing a hand on Harryís shoulder. ØWhatís he doing?Æ Sirius asked. ØThe coot is obsessed with the boy, always going on about him and his plans. He doesnít go into detail around us - I suspect he knows we hold no loyalty to him,Æ Phineas said. ØYouíve heard nothing concrete?Æ Remus asked. Phineas started at Remus with pursed lips before answering anyway, ØNo, not as such. He has mentioned a petition to have more Dark homes searched and getting Grimmauld on the list. Something about making sure something is found. And he has mumbled a few times about suitable Light families, such as the Weasleys.Æ Sirius and Harry both growled. The house, being as old as it was, did have a few Darker artefacts - and the books, well, Harry refused to be parted with them. He knew a lot of them were banned and even illegal, but magic shouldnít be sanctioned. ØI see, thank you,Æ Sirius said. ØIf you keep my portrait unblocked I will be happy to pass along anything I see or hear. Although he is paranoid, he does forget, and sometimes he seems to get caught up,Æ Phineas said. ØIsnít your loyalty keyed to Hogwarts, though?Æ Remus asked, remembering something he had once read. ØPartly yes, that is true, but Dumbledore isnít Hogwarts. The school has my loyalty as well as my family. Sirius and young Harrison here are my family, my Heirs.Æ ØThank you, sir,Æ Harry said. The portrait stared at Harry for a moment. ØHave we met before? Or have you ever been alone in Dumbledore's office?Æ he asked. Harry shook his head; he had never spoken to the portrait in this life. ØNo, sir. I have been to his office a few times over this year, though. He is rather persistent in trying to get me up there.Æ Phineas hummed as observed the boy. ØStrange, you seem very familiar.Æ Sirius sighed and ran a hand through his hair. Fortunately, it had regained a lot of its former glory with the help of his healers and the goblins who he had visited once again. ØIf you would pass along anything, we would be grateful. However, as itís Harryís birthday, I think we should get going. We have the day planned before dinner tonight with the Malfoy's,Æ he told the painting. ØOh, finally living up to the familyís standards, are we?Æ asked the painting snidely. Sirius had never gotten on well with the painting growing up and after he had entered Hogwarts it had been even worse. However, he knew that Phineasí loyalty was to the Blacks and he would help. Sirius glared. ØI'm finally free of Dumbledore's manipulations and giving people a second and last chance,Æ he said. Phineas hummed and looked pleased. ØGood. You have to potential to bring our family back to itís former glory.Æ Sirius nodded and walked away from the painting, grabbing Harryís hand and pulling him along when the boy seemed to stop and stare at the painting. Harry wondered if Phineas was sensing things about him from his other life encounters. ~ The rest of the day passed quickly. The two men had planned it so that Harry could experience as many things as possible. They had apparated him to muggle London and taken him to a matinee performance of a west end show. They had had lunch in Italy - really, Sirius had paid for an international floo to take them all to Rome to have lunch in a private wizarding area of the Colosseum, and then they had explored the city for a few hours, seeing the main sights easily with the help of magic. Harry had also bought a load of souvenirs for his friends, even getting Draco something. By the time they had returned home, Harry was beyond exhilarated; it had been one of the best days of his life. ØWe have an hour before weíre due at the Malfoy's,Æ Remus said after casting a tempus charm. ØI'm going to take a quick bath,Æ Harry said. Even with cooling charms, which he had been casting wandlessly to not gain attention, he still felt the need to shower before dinner. ØGood idea,Æ Sirius said, dramatically sniffing Harry, making the boy hit him in the stomach. ~ Dressing slightly more formally than he would for dinner at home, Harry selected to wear black trousers and a silver shirt with an open emerald green robe, a few shades darker than his eyes. He also had on his new charm bracelet and his hair tied back with a silver ribbon he had bought in Rome. Looking in the mirror, Harry noted the differences between this twelve-year-old boy and his last. Although still not tall, he was probably an inch taller than last time. His hair was now smoother and fell to his shoulders in more controlled waves. He no longer wore glasses and, although his face was softer, it wasn't as pronounced as it was when he had first had the glamour removed - perhaps as he was younger and still had baby fat. He was slightly androgynous as an adult he supposed, but at this age it wasn't as noticeable. His eyes still dominated his face, the green actually glowing slightly when Harry allowed his magic to flair. Walking downstairs, he saw that Remus and Sirius had done the same as him and were dressed much more formally than normal. Flooing into the Malfoy's entrance room, Harry noted the luxury and grace of the place. It was graceful and luxurious but lacked a certain home-like feeling. ØWelcome to our home,Æ Lucius greeted after Harry had straightened himself out. He still stumbled slightly when exiting the floo and he doubted he would ever master the art. ØThank you for having us,Æ Sirius responded as the highest-ranking member of their party. Narcissa entered then with Draco. ØI thought I felt the wards let you in. Welcome,Æ she greeted warmly, stepping up to Sirius and kissing his cheek. ØHappy birthday, Harry,Æ Draco said, smiling hesitantly, making Harry realise that his pompousness and arrogance had yet to fully manifest. ØThank you for having us and thank you for the birthday gifts,Æ Harry said, sending Draco a smile before Narcissa leant down and kissed his cheek also. Lucius shook hands with Sirius and even Remus, and offered his hand to Harry once he was free of Narcissa, who had started to flutter about him. Harry took the hand and shook it, before he felt Luciusí magic suddenly prod at his. Raising an eyebrow in question, Harry felt it as Luciusí magic seemed to dance around his. Seeing he had been noted, Lucius straightened. ØI apologise, it seems my magic reacts strongly to yours,Æ he said, making Harry wonder. Sirius, who heard the exchange, turned and looked at Harry with concern. Seeing this, Harry waved him off before turning back to Lucius. ØItís fine, Lucius. I keep my magic masked slightly, perhaps that is the reason,Æ Harry said, knowing that Lucius was really trying to gauge his magical core with his prodding. Lucius nodded, showing he understood Harry had caught him, but he wasn't bothered. ØI see. Yes, that must have been it.Æ Draco took a hesitant step forward then and offered his own hand. Harry shook it without concern. They all moved into the family sitting room then and had drinks whilst waiting for dinner. ØSo, did you get anything good for your birthday?Æ the blond asked, sitting with Harry near the large windows overlooking immaculate gardens. Harry had even caught sight of a white peacock roaming the grounds. Harry nodded and explained his presents, once again thanking Draco for his gift. ØYour friends seem nice, like they really care,Æ Draco said, looking at Harryís bracelet with a slight frown, making Harry think about Draco's own friends. He had his two goons, Crabbe and Goyle, but Harry wasn't sure if they were friends. In his later years he had hung around Zabini, Nott, and Parkinson a lot, but Harry couldnít be sure who his actual friends were. Realising this, Harry felt bad for the boy and nodded. ØYes, theyíre actually pretty great. I never had real friends before I met them,Æ he admitted. ØThey wonít mind me coming along tomorrow?Æ Draco asked. ØNo. As long as you donít call them names or spout off rubbish about them like Ronald does, then I canít see them having any problems. I told them yesterday when I saw them that you would be joining us,Æ Harry said. Draco snorted. ØLike I could ever act like the weasel,Æ he said arrogantly. Harry smiled. It seemed that without Harry there to be his rival, Draco had promoted Ron into that place. Soon enough an elf appeared - Harry was actually pleased to see it wasn't Dobby - and announced dinner was ready. Chapter End Notes I'm actually beginning to feel a bit better!
