Comments & kudos, ~ Annie Chapter 20: Fourout of five Chapter Notes See the end of the chapter for notes Previously ØI'm okay now,Æ Harry said, his voice soft. Sirius ran a hand through his hair. ØWell, if I ever had any doubts about your story, theyíre certainly gone now,Æ he joked. Harry looked up and smiled, though it was a sad, little thing. ØI want him dead, for everything he's done to me, to us, to everybody. The lives he's ruined and manipulated,Æ he whispered. Sirius let out a breath. ØI know, pup, and I will stand by you. Hell, I will even cast the curse if you need me to,Æ he vowed. After a few more minutes, the pair regained their composure and Sirius felt safe to dispel the ward. Turning their attention back to the trial, neither noticed a pair of eyes staring at them in both shock and awe. ~ 6th August 1992, Grimmauld Place, London After Harry's outburst, Sirius had been distracted through the entire trial. He had only half heartedly listened as Peter - the snivelling rat - was sentenced to five years in Azkaban after which he would be given the Kiss. Harry, however, had paid attention with a look of rapture; he wanted the rat to pay. He was the most pathetic excuse for a wizard Harry knew, a spineless coward who stood for nothing and would sell his own mother if he thought it would keep him alive. Five years and then the Kiss was lenient in Harry's mindîthe rat deserved a much harsher treatment. ~ As it was the first day of his summer that he didnít have anything he needed to do, Harry wandered the house aimlessly. He had never had a chance to do nothing - in his past life, he was always either with the Dursleys being worked worse than a house-elf or with the Weasleys, recovering and constantly being harried. The rest of his time was spent at school trying not to fail his classes while putting up with Voldemortís yearly schemes. Then, to top it all off, he had been on the run for ten months, living like a vagabond, constantly worried he would either die from exposure to the elements, of being cursed to death, or from going insane. However, the point being was that he had always had something to do and now he honestly felt slightly awkward. He had freed Sirius, found his cure for Tom, and even gotten Peter punished, but he was honestly feeling slightly lost. With a sigh of frustration he turned and went to the library, a thought finally crossing his mind and giving him a purpose. He still didnít have all of the horcruxes and, thinking about it, was Nagini even a horcrux yet? Harry thought about it and realised Voldemort had used the death of Bertha Jorkins to create the horcrux with his familiar, therefore he actually only had six horcruxes: the locket, the diary, the ring, the diadem, the goblet, and Harry himself. With this in mind, Harry thought about the ritual. He wanted to be there for the final ritual as that would force Tom to appear and he wanted to be there to calm his soulmate and explain what he had done - hopefully Tom would understand. According to the log in Corvus Blackís book, the ritual worked quite suddenly, meaning Tom would not feel his soul rejoin until the last part was performed on the final holiday - the final ritual used a different incantation to bring all the soul pieces together and force them to reenter the person. This also meant that Harry would be able to perform the final ritual without returning all of Tomís soul, keeping himself as a horcrux as long as he performed the ritual five times and used the final wording on the 5th. Thinking about Tomís horcruxes made Harry realise he had yet to tell Sirius and Remus about where they are. He was hoping, with Sirius being Lord Black, that he would be able to enter the LeStrange Vault; although not technically legal, Harry hoped his friendship with the goblins would ease the way. However, Harry did understand that it would be difficult for Ragnok - the reputation of Gringotts was infallible and if it ever got out they allowed somebody to just enter anotherís Vault, the ramifications would be immense. Getting the ring would also be difficult and Harry felt that it should be him who goes after it just to be on the safe side. The curse should not affect him and, if it did, then he wouldnít die being the Master of Death. The diadem would be the easiest to recover hopefully; it was just sitting, waiting in the Room of Requirement. Harry could recover it just before coming home for the Yule break and then they could start the ritual. If they performed the first ritual on Yule, they would finish on Midsummer and Harry would be present for Tomís resurrection. The realisation that he only had a year to wait for his soulmateís return made Harry giddy. He had worked so hard for this. ~ Persuading Remus and Sirius was the only thing standing in Harry's way to getting another horcrux. ØThe persuasions on the ring are deadly,Æ Harry told them. Heíd asked Kreacher to gather the men together; he wanted to tell them about the horcruxes and his plans for the ritual. Remus frowned. ØPrecisely why we need to come with you,Æ he told the small preteen. Even knowing all he did, he was still having difficulty seeing Harry as an adult, somebody who didnít need constant care. Harry sighed. ØI know I look like a child, Remus, but I have fought and won a war. Itís safer for me to go and get the ring as I will most likely not be overwhelmed by the compulsions. I already know theyíre there and, if for some reason I am overwhelmed, I canít die.Æ Sirius frowned. He hated the reminder of all Harry had been through and his blunt way of speaking about it didnít help - but, then again, he also hated the idea of Harry going alone. ØLet me come with you. Not into the shack to get the ring but to Little Hangleton.Æ That way he would be able to help if something did go wrong. Harry tilted his head as he considered it. ØOkay, but promise me you wonít follow me in. I donít want anything happening to you.Æ ØI promise.Æ ~ Meanwhile Malfoy Manor, Wiltshire Lucius sat in his office and considered all he knew about the mystery that was Harrison James Potter-Black. The boy was truly an enigma; how he managed to keep such power contained and hidden he would never know. It had been pure chance that he had looked up and seen Harrisonís magical outburst before Sirius had shielded them. Had he not, he would have never realised the power the small and delicate looking boy could wield. Thinking back, Lucius recalled how Harrisonís emerald eyes had actually glowed, illuminated by his power, and how his hair had been stirred by the magic currents he had been giving off. Such a display should have been impossible for a child. Lucius had wondered when Harrison had admitted to masking some of his magic, but he had never thought it would be such a shocking amount. The boy was truly impressive. Lucius hadnít felt power like that since the glory days of the Dark and even then his Lordís power was tinged with weakness, brought on by his own insanity. How anybody could believe that Harrison was the saviour of the Light he wouldnít know; even from a distance Lucius had recognised the Darkness in his magic. The boy had the makings of a Dark Lord. He would have to watch him, be there for him. Lucius was suddenly very grateful to his son. When he had first read Dracoís letters, he had been tornîhis son had expressed a desire to befriend the Boy-Who-Lived, a boy who Lucius had assumed incorrectly would be a Light pawn. Now, instead, his son was friends with a great potential ally. ~ Hogwarts School of Witchcraft and Wizardry, Scotland Albus paced his office as he waited for Severus to join him. How dare his spy give evidence to support that little brat! The man is supposed to hate him, hate him for being the child of his school bully, of living when Lily died! ØStupid child, ruining my plans,Æ Albus muttered to himself, not noticing how one portrait in particular was actually paying attention. Hearing his wards beep, letting him know Severus was on his way up, Albus quickly gripped his wand and straightened out his office. It wouldnít do for Severus to see how out of place everything was. Sitting behind his desk, Albus carefully constructed his facial expression right before Severus strode inside. ØSeverus, my dear boy, thank you for joining me,Æ he said, before holding out a bag of familiar yellow candies. ØLemon drop?Æ Severus sneered at the offered sweet and took a seat. ØYou asked to see me, Headmaster?Æ he said more than asked in his usual drawl. He suspected it was because the old coot had found out about his involvement in the Potter boyís life. ØYes, yes, if youíre quite sure,Æ Albus said, putting the sweets down but not before putting one in his mouth. ØI've just come from Harry Potterís custody trial.Æ Severus allowed an eyebrow to rise. ØOh?Æ he inquired. ØYes, I'm surprised you yourself were not there, considering the evidence you gave. Evidence which, might I just say, not only allowed for myself to be removed as Harry's magical guardian, but also weakens your own status as a spy for when the Dark Lord returns.Æ Severus withheld a sneerîhow dare Albus blame him. He had helped the boy, his best friendís only child, to escape from his abusive family and to be reunited with people he loved and would look after him - albeit people Severus hated. ØI gave records of my actions. I'm surprised you felt the need to challenge the court; surely the boy is better off with the mutt and monster then where he was. However, had I known of your involvement, I would have warned you,Æ Severus lied, he had known and kept silent. He didnít know what the old man had planned, but he doubted he would like it. Albus was far too interested in the boy and his actions so far - nevermind what he had planned never had been in Harry's interest. He had lied about him and his life growing up and it seemed that he wanted Harry to continue living in an abusive home. Severus couldnít work out his reasoning yet, but he would be damned if he let this old fool harm Lilyís child anymore. Albus, thankfully ignorant to Severusí thoughts, was relieved - at least Severus was still in his pocket. He had feared he had lost the man when he heard he had given evidence to support Harry. ØYes, I wished to remain Harry's guardian. Your evidence, however, made it look like the boy was abused. Sure, he may have had a firm hand growing up, but all young boys need that,Æ Albus said. He needed Snape - he knew Tom would be back, he had made sure of it. The words made Severus want to lash out; his own father had been an abusive drunk and the fact Albus was making light of it and making excuses sickened him. ØIndeed,Æ he said. ØYou will have to be careful this coming year, last year you were almost nice to the boy. If this continues when the Dark Lord returns you will be unable to rejoin him.Æ ØI shall have to try and rectify the situation.Æ ØIndeed, my dear boy - I would hate for you to be in danger. It really shouldnít be so hard; I'm surprised, really. I mean, Harry really is just like James was as a child.Æ Severus was thankful for his mastery of occlumencyîhow the old man could say such a thing with a straight face he didnít know. If it wasnít for the fact he knew Lily would never cheat on her husband, Severus wouldnít have been surprised if Harry had been somebody elseís son with how opposite he was from a young James Potter. James had been arrogant, cruel, a bully while Harry was quiet, soft spoken, intelligent. ØIf the Dark Lord returns Iím sure I shall be able to regain any position.Æ ØGood man,Æ Dumbledore said, eyes twinkling. Now he just had to find a way to get Filius and Poppy on his side. His plans for Harry would be so much simpler if he was in Gryffindorè. August 9th 1992, Gaunt Shack, Little Hangleton Harry apparated himself and Sirius to Little Hangleton, as the animagus had never been before and was therefore unable to apparate. It was a novel experience for Harry as he had not apparated in over a year as being a child had made it next to impossible to get away with. Landing, Harry closed his eyes and took a breath. Although he hated magical transport, he preferred to be the one in control as it didnít leave him feeling quite nauseous. ØItís weird seeing you do magic like that so easily. You look like a kid,Æ Sirius said after straightening himself. Harry shrugged. ØItís weird being in such a young body, but Iíve got used to it.Æ Looking around, Harry spotted the path which lead to the rundown shack and made his way towards it. ØSo youíve still got all you powers from when you were an adult?Æ ØYes, however Iím still limited in what I can do. Iíve been working my magical stamina but still, this body is that of a childís. I find I tire more easily than that of my adult body. It also doesnít help that when I'm at school I have to revert to only using age appropriate magic, so any progress I make while at home is usually null and void,Æ Harry said. ØThereís nowhere you can practice?Æ Sirius asked, just as the shack came into view. ØI've got a few places that I will check out this year,Æ Harry said, thinking about the Room of Requirement and even the Chamber of Secrets. Harry stopped when they reached the shack. ØStay outside,Æ he said, turning to Sirius and making sure he saw how serious he was. Sirius nodded. ØI will, but if you need me, call me,Æ he said. Harry nodded his understanding and entered the shack. He could practically taste the malicious magic in the air. The whole place vibrated with it. Remembering what he had been shown by Dumbledore, Harry approached the kitchen where he knew the ring was .underneath the floorboards. Walking slowly with all of his senses open, Harry could feel the magic reaching out to him; however, when it reached him, it seemed to stop. Scanning him, it seemed to recognise Tomís soul in him and the locket. Tensing as the magic continued to push against him, Harry was about to push back when the magic suddenly retreated. Letting out a breath, Harry crouched down and loosened the floorboards. Seeing the ring, Harry didnít have the compulsion to put it on. Instead, he levitated it and placed it into a small velvet bag he had brought for this purpose. Tucking the ring away ,Harry let out a breath. He had done it. ØCongratulations Master, youíve regained one of my Hallows,Æ said a deep voice, causing Harry to spin. Standing, well, more like floating, behind him was Death. Harry took a second to get his heart rate back under control and sent Death a glare. ØAre you trying to kill me?Æ he asked the entity. Death chuckled, the sounds coming out scratchy. ØYou are unable to die, Master, so no. I do not have any plans to kill you.Æ ØWell, that's something I suppose,Æ Harry grumbled sarcastically. ØYou have done well,Æ Death said, Ønow all you need is the wand.Æ ØLike that will be easy. Did you forget whoís got the wand? I doubt Dumbles is going to happily hand it over to me.Æ ØThe wand will know its true Master. You will be surprised at how well it knows. I shall be going now,Æ Death said before He slowly disappeared without even waiting for Harry to speak. ØWell that wasnít cryptic at all,Æ Harry muttered under his breath as he made his way back to Sirius. ~ Sirius had been pacing outside and it was only through his sheer force of will that he hadnít gone after Harry. 100 different scenarios passed through his head as he paced: what if something had gone wrong? Shouldnít Harry be finished by now? Seeing Sirius pacing, Harry felt bad; he knew both he and Remus worried, but honestly there was no need. ØSirius, stop, youíre going to wear a hole through to China,Æ Harry joked, gaining Siriusís attention. Hearing Harry's voice, Sirius spun and looked him up and down, searching for any damage or injury. Seeing this, Harry huffed, ØIím fine Siri, and I've got the ring.Æ Sirius let out a breath. Although he still had his reservations about the resurrection, he would always stand by Harry. ØThatís good,Æ he said. ØDo you want to go to Gringotts now?Æ Harry considered it and nodded. ØPlease.Æ Sirius had been shocked when Harry had told him the location of the goblet horcrux, but then again Bella always was very devoted to the Dark Lord. Apparating to the alley, Harry and Sirius made their way to the bank. Harry had missed seeing his friend and had only spoken to him through owl in the last week. Entering the bank, Sirius followed Harry as he made his way through the back passages, no longer shocked by the respect shown to his godson. Seeing Harry stop and wait to enter the office he recognised as Ragnokís, Sirius hoped the goblin would be able to help. ~ 9th August 1992, Gringotts Bank, London Branch Entering Ragnokís office once he was called, Harry was happy to see his old friend. ØRagnok,Æ he greeted, taking a seat. ØHarrison. Lord Black,Æ the goblin chief said in return. ØI trust business is well,Æ Harry said. Ragnok smiled toothily. ØOur gold continues to increase,Æ he said. ØExcellent,Æ Harry said. Ragnok looked at him then and decided to forgo anymore pleasantries. ØAlthough I do enjoy your presence Harrison, I would rather you tell me what it is that has brought you here. Our business with Dodge is firmly settled for now, I believe.Æ Harry smirked. ØYou never have been one for small talk,Æ he said. ØVery well. As you know, my soulmate was foolish enough to create multiple horcruxes. In his bid to protect them he gave one to his follower, Bellatrix LeStrange nee Black. Bellatrix, not knowing what it was but knowing it was important, placed the horcrux, which is within Helga Hufflepuffís cup, inside her Gringotts Vault.Æ Ragnok nodded then realised why the young wizard was here. ØYou wish to enter her Vault,Æ he stated. ØAs Lord Black, I believe Sirius is able to enter the Vault of a family member,Æ Harry said, Øespecially one who is so indisposed.Æ Ragnok sighed. If it got out that he had allowed such an action then the reputation of Gringotts could be ruined. However, he trusted Harrison to keep the act secret. ØI see. Was the cup given to her when she was still a Black?Æ he asked, finding a stretch in loopholes. Harry wasnít actually sure, but seeing as he knew his chances would be better, he lied, ØYes.Æ Ragnok knew Harry wasnít being truthful, but allowed it. ØVery well - I shall accompany you. You will remove nothing but the cup, Lord Black,Æ he said. ~ Entering the LeStrange Vault, Harry watched as Ragnok nullified the Flagrante and Gemino curses, making it so the treasures within wouldnít burn and multiply. ØYou may collect the cup,Æ Ragnok said, watching with sharp eyes as Sirius walked into the Vault. Sirius didnít dally and practically ran to the cup; he wanted this to be over with as quickly as possible. Picking up the cup, Sirius barely withheld his shudder as the Dark magic washed over him. As he was no longer renouncing his magical affinity, Sirius found the presence of powerful Dark magic almost intoxicating. It wasnít an exactly pleasant experience, but it was overwhelming. Seeing Sirius struggle, Harry held out his hands as he rejoined him and Ragnok at the doors to the Vault. ØLet me have it, Sirius, its magic doesnít affect me,Æ Harry told his godfather. Taking a breath, Sirius handed the cup over. As he was no longer touching it, its presence wasnít as bad - he could still feel it, but it was diluted. Placing the cup into his pocket after wandlessly shrinking it, Harry turned to Ragnok. ØThank you, my friend. I understand that doing this was difficult for you, but know I appreciate it and all you have done for me. May your Vaults never empty and your enemyís blood run cold,Æ Harry said. Ragnok inclined his head. ØI thank you, and may your gold overflow. However, we shall never speak of this again,Æ he said with finality. Harry sent Sirius a look. ØNever,Æ he promised. Instead of returning to Ragnokís office, Harry and Sirius said their goodbye and returned to Grimmauld Place. Having the extra two horcruxes on him had made Harry feel almost giddy, their presence soothing him. Going to his room, Harry carefully dispelled the charms and hexes around the box containing the diary and placed the ring and cup inside. Looking at the diary he had a moment of intense longing - he could talk to Tomè. He quickly buried the desire; the soul piece in the diary wasnít really his soulmate he had to remind himselfîand it was also a soul leech. If Harry used the diary he would have to be careful - the spells on it would easily drain him of both his magic and his life. ~ After collecting the ring, the rest of the summer weeks flew by for Harry. This was the first summer that he was completely free to do whatever he wanted and although the lack of purpose was frustrating, Harry soon found ways to amuse himself. Spending most of his time in the library, Harry soon found books which drew and captured his attention, most notably a book on Parselmagic. Why the Black family library had such a book when there was no known Black family Speaker, Harry didnít know, but he was grateful. Although the book was small, Harry found the spells inside quite interesting. They were not necessarily Dark, but Harry doubted they would be legal in this pro-Light government. Most of the spells in the book were basic; all they required was the user to speak Parseltongueîthey were mostly charms and a few minor hexes. However, there were some much Darker spells that Harry vaguely recognised from the last war - spells Harry had assumed Voldemort had created. There were spells which left the victim without blood as theirs boiled from within, spells which trapped a person in their nightmares unable to escape, spells which caused hair to grow inwards. These spells Harry did teach himself - he had no real plans on using them but one never knew. This system of lazily learning spells continued throughout the summer, broken only by trips out with Sirius and Remus and visits to his friends. Never had Harry had such a peaceful break. ~ August 20th 1992, Malfoy Manor, Wiltshire When Harry had received Dracoís invitation to visit him for the day, he had accepted without concern. He had really come to like this Draco; sure, he was still slightly pompous and his views on muggleborns and muggles were outdated and needing change, but he liked the boy. He had a dry wit that Harry appreciated, he was intelligent, and he wasnít obsessed with Harry's fame. Oh, Harry didnít doubt that it had initially been Harry's status as the Boy-Who.Wouldnít-Die that drew Draco in, but getting to know him, they had actually become true friends. Shockingly enough, Neville and Draco got on ridiculously well too - Draco's more outgoing sarcasm mixing well with Neville's sly cunning. They had been writing to each other and swapping ideas about how to get back at Ron this year. Arriving at the manor via Floo, Harry stumbled out and, after straightening himself, saw that it was slightly less perfect than it had been last time. Draco, who had been waiting, saw Harry's look and snarled, ØThe bloody ministry fools came again and searched the manor.Æ Realisation hit Harry and he remembered the threat of having Grimmauld searched. ØI gather they found nothing?Æ ØNo, but itís the second time this summer. Itís Dumbledore and his pet weasels - I swear that man is getting worse.Æ ØHopefully the old coot will stop once weíre back at school; he has enough problems being Headmaster without meddling in our familiesí affairs,Æ Harry placated. Soon enough Harry had distracted Draco into forgetting about the old cootís persecution against Dark families and the two boys spent the morning flying and chatting about the up and coming school year. ØFather has been trying to find out who the new Defence teacher will be, but apparently Dumbledore hasnít officially announced it yet,Æ Draco grumbled as they sat in the gardens, drinking some iced tea an elf had gotten for them. Once again, Harry was surprised he hadnít seen Dobby. ØIf theyíre awful, Iíll just do what I did last year and teach myself,Æ Harry said. Seeing Draco's look of intrigue, Harry expanded on his sentence. ØI made a study group last year. We all taught ourselves and helped each other. You're welcome to join us, we usually meet in the library after classes.Æ Draco smiled slightly. ØI would like that. Perhaps I could bring a few of my Slytherin friends,Æ he said cautiously. Harry didnít allow his face to change, but inside he was wary. He didnít really know any of the other Slytherins; even as enemies, like he had been with Draco, he didnít know any of them. However, he didnít want to exclude anyone just become of their House so he nodded. ØSure, bring them along. I donít really know that many Slytherins to be honest.Æ Draco nodded his head once. ØWell, there's Vincent Crabbe and Gregory Goyle - you've got to have noticed them, they're the brainless lugs that tend to follow me around. They're alright I suppose, but you canít have any form of conversation with themîat least not one when you gain any reply. Then there's Theodore Nott - heís alright but he doesnít really speak to anybody and keeps to himself. I canít really say much for him, he doesnít even sit around in the common room much. Finally for the boys thereís Blaise Zabini; heís my best friend in Slytherin, youíd like him. Heís half Italian and from a Neutral family. Heís the boy you've probably noticed daydreaming in classes, but heís probably one of the smartest people I know. Heís also the gossip king of Hogwarts - anything you want or need to know, Zabini can find out. Even I donít know how he gets some of his information. Don't worry though, heís a great friend, very loyal.Æ Harry smiled at Dracoís descriptions. He had never really spoken to Zabini - a few threats and grumbled words here and there, however he had seen how in later years he and Draco had become close, especially after the Dark Lordís return. Nott he had never even noticed until the war, the boy really was a quiet wallflower and the other twoè well, Harry honestly had no desire to get to know. ØFor the girls in Slytherin, there's Bulstrode, she's the rather... well... sheís the larger girl.Æ Draco's hesitance on his description of Millicent left Harry fighting laughter. True, Millicent wasnít blessed with great physical beauty, but to hear Draco scramble to say so made Harry smile. ØSheís alright though, great sense of humour for a girl. Then thereís Tracy Davis, she's a half-blood but you wouldnít notice - her and Daphne Greengrass are always together, theyíre annoying to be honest, always whispering and giggling. Finally, there's Pansy Parkinson, sheís actually my other best friend. She pretends to be a total girly-girl in classes and around school, but sheís actually ridiculously smart and very practical. She says she acts like that so people underestimate her, which is true as I know everybody thinks she's a fool. Itís like me .I know everybody thinks I'm a cold and heartless Slytherin who relies on my family name, which is fine as then they will underestimate me too.Æ Harry was surprised by Pansy, he had honestly believed the mask she portrayed, but then again he had believed Dracoís in his last life as well. ØI think I will get on with your friends, but why do you have to pretend to be underestimated?Æ ØEverybody expects Slytherins to be evil, to automatically be Death Eaters. The other Houses exclude us and bully us, we have to act this way to protect ourselves. Even the teachers to an extent treat us differently and sure some of them try not to, but itís still there. Weíre automatically viewed as being Dark, so we act to be underestimated; that way, if we ever are bullied or attacked, then we can surprise them. Iím not going to lie: we are sneaky and cunning, we cheat and manipulate, but weíre not evil,Æ Draco explained. Harry nodded. ØThe hat was torn between Ravenclaw and Slytherin for me. I picked Ravenclaw because I knew Dumbledore wouldnít leave me alone if I went green.Æ Draco's eyes went wide. ØSo the Lightís golden boy was almost a snake?Æ ØYes, the hat thought me very cunning.Æ Draco laughed. ØYou would have been good in Slytherin, but I suppose Ravenclaw is okay.Æ He knew not to dismiss Gryffindor and Hufflepuff now and, having met the rest of Harry's, and now his, friends, he could see the benefits of the other houses - even Gryffindor. Harry was pleased Draco was learning and no longer thought the other Houses were automatically jokes. Now he just had to work on his muggle viewsè. ØItís stupid - what people think about Slytherins. Youíre not all bad. Judging you just because the Dark Lord was in Slytherin and a lot of his Death Eaters came from there is ridiculous. I mean, come on! Look at that rat Pettigrew, he was in Gryffindor.Æ ØI know, but everybody just sees a Slytherin and thinks evil. Dumbledore doesnít help; I mean, he goes on about the greater good and so called fairness but he never does anything to actually help,Æ Draco ranted. Harry was surprised the blond had noticed - or perhaps he had been listening to Lucius rant. ØHopefully Dumbledore will not be around for much longer or, at least, his influence on the world will start to crumble,Æ Harry said, hoping that everything he had brought to light and was planning on bringing to light would be enough to destroy the old man. Draco saw Harry's dark look and hid his shudder. His friend could be scary sometimes. Eventually the topic was dropped and the two went back to playing catch the snitch. A game which Harry won eight out of ten times, losing only because he started to lose interest. He still loved flying but his passion for it was not the same anymore. ~ Harry eventually persuaded Draco to stop flying and he instead was lead to the boyís room, which was more like a suite than anything else: It had a bathroom, dressing room, and small sitting room attached. ØNice,Æ Harry said, looking around and actually finding he liked the style. The room had white walls with one wall painted a midnight blue and a pale blue fluffy carpet. The furnishings all matched and were a dark mahogany wood. The bed spread was dark blue and pale green and the throw pillows were green. It was slightly mature for a twelve year old Harry supposed, but it was very nice. It was also quite relaxed with slouchy looking sofas and knickknacks around. Harry even spotted a few soft toys from when Draco was younger hidden among the rest. ØItís the Heir suite,Æ Draco said as an explanation for the size. Harry nodded and went to the bookshelf to see what types of books Draco had. There were the usual ones you would expect to find in a magical childís room: fairytales, encyclopaedias, and quidditch books. However, what drew Harryís attention was the number of magical animal books and even more potion guides. Seeing the number of potion books, Harry smiled. ØYou actually enjoy Potions, donít you?Æ Draco shrugged and looked slightly self-conscious. ØTheyíre alright,Æ he mumbled, shocking Harry as he had never seen the Malfoy heir mumble. ØI think thatís cool. I'm okay at the subject but for some reason I just canít feel the passion, you know?Æ Draco suddenly looked much happier. ØReally? I think that they're fascinating; I mean, you can do almost anything with potions.Æ Harry smiled at Draco's enthusiasm. ØI remember - Snapeís speech from the start of year said the same.Æ ØSeverus is right. I mean, I doubt I could do half of those things but he could. Heís a Potions genius,Æ Draco said, the familiarity and happiness in which he said Severusí name reminding him that they were close. ØYou know Professor Snape from before school?Æ he asked. ØHeís my godfather. He helped me last year; he brewed potions for me and he even helped out at my trial by giving evidence. Heís a good man, even if he does scare the magic out of Neville and make people cry,Æ Harry joked before he acknowledged the rest of Draco's sentence. ØAlso, Dray, you should know I donít care about the last war. I mean, I do, but I donít hold it against people. Itís time to move on; most people did what they thought best for themselves and their families, for the Wizarding world.Æ ØWell said, Harrison.Æ Harry turned and saw Lucius standing in the doorway. The aristocratís mask was firmly in place, revealing nothing of what he felt about what he had heard. ØFather, what are you doing here?Æ Draco asked. ØItís almost time for lunch and as I was passing by, I thought I should collect you myself instead of sending an elf. I believe your mother wants to eat outside on the terrace,Æ Lucius said. Harry nodded. ØIt is a lovely day.Æ ØIndeed. Come along then, boys,Æ Lucius said. Following Draco, Harry felt more than saw Lucius staring at him and wondered what he had done this time to garner his attention. ØDo you really feel that way, Harrison?Æ Lucius asked softly as they walked. ØYes, I tend not to say things I donít mean,Æ Harry replied. ØItís a rare few who are willing to move on so easily. Many hold grudges; it would not be shocking if you yourself did, as you lost a lot in the war.Æ ØYes, but holding onto the grudges would only make it impossible for me to move forward. I also do not believe in hating those who have done nothing to me personally; many people may have fought in the war, either directly or indirectly, and for many different reasons - who am I to judge them? I only hold grudges against those who stand against me and my plans, who try to do me or my loved ones harm.Æ It was the best way Harry could reveal his feelings without saying too much. It also explained his willingness to forgive Lucius and other Death Eaters, but not people like Ron and Dumbledore. Most Death Eaters were simply following orders and their beliefs, few actually enjoyed their actions. They were soldiers fighting for what they believed in. However, there were a few who Harry did hate and held grudges against: Pettigrew was an example - he was a traitor and he was weak. He didnít believe in anything other than saving himself. Bellatrix was another, although he could perhaps sympathise more since Sirius said she was honestly mentally ill. Still, she was a sadist who enjoyed hurting people, even children. Then there was the pink toad, Umbridge; her vindictive spreading of hate and prejudice was sickening. Then there was Hermione, who was a conundrum. Harry still didnít forgive her - sure, she was innocent in this life, but she had manipulated him in his last, made him believe he had friends and then plotted to steal from him and kill him. She and Ron had utterly destroyed his heart in his last life, they had betrayed him along with Molly and Ginny. However, the one person he knew he would never forgive was Dumbledore. That man had destroyed Harry's life, he had plotted and schemed and manipulated until Harry had nothing - and why? For what purpose? Harry still didnít know for certain. ØYou have goals, then?Æ Lucius asked, bringing Harry back to the present. Harry tilted his head. ØI think everybody has plans, Lucius.Æ Lucius smirked at the answer. How very Slytherin. ØIndeed,Æ he agreed. ~ After lunch Harry and Draco played a few games of chess and exploding snap while they talked idly. Draco was a better player than Harry, but Harry could hold his own. Seeing an opportunity to ask something that had been bothering him for a while, Harry spoke, ØDraco, I was wondering, do you happen to have a house-elf named Dobby?Æ Harry saw Draco's face scrunch in confusion before he answered, ØYes, but heís not here at the moment, heís still in training. However did you know about him?Æ ØI'm not sure - perhaps you or somebody mentioned him?Æ Harry dismissed quickly. ØAnyway, what training?Æ Draco didnít look sure, but shrugged slightly before he continued, ØWell, heís a young house-elf and, to be honest, heís slightly batty. Anyway, heís been sent off to train under the older elves so he can find his designation; to see if he would be best in the garden or looking after children, for example.Æ Harry nodded - that did make sense. ØWhere do they do this training?Æ he asked, a suspicion growing in his mind. ØHogwarts, of course. Hogwarts has the most house-elves anywhere in Britain.Æ ØI see. So is he a Hogwarts elf or a Malfoy elf while heís training?Æ Harry asked. ØWell, heís officially a Malfoy elf, but he has to follow Hogwarts orders until heís finished.Æ ØAh.Æ Harry thought about the events that transpired thanks to Dobby and honestly? He wasnít surprised that Dumbledore had manipulated them. However, it did leave the question of how much control the old man had on Lucius. If he simply manipulated Dobby that was one thing, but to manipulate the head of the Malfoy family was another. Perhaps Harry needed to bring in the elder Malfoy on a few of his secrets. If he was under charms Harry wanted him free, if only to strengthen their newly formed alliance. Harry had also genuinely liked Dobby, the little elf had died rescuing Harry and the rest of them. But if the elf had been manipulated at first then he wasnít sure how much he could rely on his old friend. ØWhy are you asking about elves anyway, Harry?Æ ØNo reason - just interested, I guess,Æ Harry lied. Since Harry had become close to the Malfoys, he had noticed that their elves were actually treated rather well. They wore clean uniforms at leastîsure, Lucius treated them like lowly servants but he wasnít excessively violent or cruel and Narcissa seemed to simply ignore them while Draco was a mixture of both. He hadnít seen anything like the abuse he had witnessed towards Dobby in his second year. 20th August 1992, Evening, Grimmauld Place, London Returning home, Harry Flooíd into the parlour only to be met with the sight of garish periwinkle and orange robes. It seemed that Dumbledore had somehow talked his way into Grimmauld. Looking around, Harry saw that Sirius had a look of anger on his face and Remus looked no better. Steeling himself, Harry plastered a fake smile on his face. ØHello Headmaster, what brings you here today?Æ he asked. Dumbledore turned and looked at the boy. He hated how at ease Harry looked wearing wizarding robes, he should be like a muggleborn. ØHello my dear boy, I'm actually here to see you and Sirius. Itís rather good timing on your part, I myself have only just arrived a few moments ago.Æ Harry sent Sirius a discreet look before he nodded. Remus, seeing this, stood and gave the Headmaster a strained smile. He would never forget his words during the trial and how the man who had allowed him an education had thrown him under the bus. ØI see. Well then, why donít you go into the parlour?Æ Sirius nodded stiffly. ØYes, why donít you follow me Albus,Æ he said, with barely concealed anger. Harry followed behind and sent Remus a look to control himself. Entering the parlour, Harry joined Sirius on the sofa and sat opposite to Dumbledore. He wondered how he had gotten in as the wards were very impressive and decided that Sirius must have let him in. ØWhy are you here, Albus?Æ Sirius asked, his voice coming out frostier than before. Albus withheld his snarl at the tone - who was he to speak like that at him? ØI thought it would be a good idea to come and clear the air, so to speak, before young Harry comes back to school next week.Æ ØThere is no need Headmaster,Æ Harry said. Albus smiled, thinking he still had some control over the boy. ØYes, very well then,Æ he said. ØHowever, there was another reason I came.Æ Sirius barely stopped his eye roll. Of course there was. ØOh?Æ Harry inquired. ØYes, I thought that after such a hectic summer you may wish to be re-Sorted when you return to school.Æ Harry just about held back his snort of derision - that was an exceptionally weak excuse. He wondered if the old man was really becoming that desperate. ØThank you, Headmaster,Æ Harry said, allowing him for a moment to think he had won. ØHowever, I will have to decline. I love being in Ravenclaw.Æ Albus felt his elation crash and narrowed his eyes. ØI see, you're certain?Æ ØYes,Æ Harry said smiling. ØVery well then, I shall be going. Do enjoy the rest of your holiday,Æ Albus said, his voice clipped as he thought about how the boy refused to do as he was told. Walking him back to the parlour, Harry sent Sirius a look which had the animagus biting his lip to stop himself from laughing. He did so love winding the old fool up. Chapter End Notes Big thank you to my Beta reader Miisticalwrites Hopefully you're all enjoying the story! He did so love winding the old fool up. ~ After Dumbledoreís visit the rest of the summer flew by - the only thing to break it up being the promised surprise visit from the Department of Childrenís Protection. It was Joanne Mirwood herself who came, much to the relief of Remus and Harry. August 25th 1992, Grimmauld Place, London ØYouíre looking well, Mr. Potter,Æ Joanne said, following them into the kitchen. Her quill was taking notes as she walked, probably on the house and its suitability for a family. ØThank you, Ms. Mirwood,Æ Harry said, sending her a sweet smile. He had liked Ms. Mirwood when he had met her in Madam Bones office and hoped she would continue to be in his good books. ØSo Harry - is it okay if I call you Harry?Æ she asked. ØYes, maíam,Æ Harry said. ØCall me Joanne,Æ she said, smiling. Unlike most adults, her smile wasnít patronising and Harry could see how such a young woman had become the head of such an important department. ØAnyway, as I was saying, Harry, would you mind if I asked you a few questions about your life now that you've been living with your blood father?Æ ØCertainly,Æ Harry agreed easily. ØWell then, letís start with the basics: are you happy here?Æ ØYes, Sirius and Remus are great guardians,Æ Harry said. Joanne smiled at the small boy in front of her; she had been present during the trial and had been disgusted by what had come to light. She was one of the many who had wanted Dumbledore charged with neglect and she had, along with the Muggle Relations Department, already sent their findings off to the muggle authorities. The Dursleys would be in a lot of trouble soon and there would be no way for them to worm their way out of it if she had anything to say about it. ØThatís good, Harry. Now could you tell me what has been the best thing youíve done together so far?Æ Harry tilted his head when he thought about it. ØMy birthday,Æ he said. ØOh?Æ ØWhen I lived with the Dursleys, I never got to celebrate my birthday. When I was younger I didnít even know I had one. This year though, I got to celebrate it. Sirius and Remus took me to muggle London and we saw Cats - itís a muggle West End show - and it was so good. Then we went to Rome for lunchîdid you know the Coliseum has a wizard only part with a restaurant? Then we went to the Sistine Chapel, the Pantheon, the Trevi fountain. It was great!Æ Harry had sure to act excited, which wasnít hard as he really had had a fantastic time in Rome. ØThen to finish it off we had dinner with the Malfoys. Did you know aunt Cissa is Siriusí cousin? That means me and Draco are sort of related, which is great because Dracoís one of my best friends.Æ ØThatís sounds great,Æ Joanne said, smiling at how obviously happy the boy was. ØWhat about punishments? Have Sirius or Remus had to punish you since youíve been living with them?Æ ØNo. Well, at least not that I've realised - theyíre not like the Dursleys. They donít shout at me or hurt me,Æ Harry said. ØSirius did once speak to me about being too independent and knowing I could come to him and he made me promise not to wander off on my own, but other than that I donít think I've done anything wrong.Æ ØThatís really good, Harry. So you're not worried about being punished here?Æ ØNo, I know Remy and Siri wouldnít hurt me.Æ ØThatís great! Now, what about food? What's your favourite thing to eat?Æ ØTreacle tart,Æ Harry said immediately, an answer that hadnít changed since his last life. Joanne smiled again at the answer. ØThat's one of my favourites too. How about dinners though?Æ Harry considered this for a minute. ØI like it when Kreacher makes roast dinners,Æ he said. It was true, he liked having roast dinners on Sundays with Sirius and Remus. ØKreacher?Æ ØHeís our house-elf. Heís a bit old and slightly batty, but heís great. He likes me the best for some reason,Æ Harry said, blushing slightly. ØHe obviously has good taste,Æ Joanne said. The quill was still moving behind her and noting everything that was being said and anything else that was relevant. ØNow, what have you been doing for fun since you came here?Æ ØI spend a lot of time in the library. Itís great! There are so many books, itís like Hogwarts library. I've also been to see my friends a few times. Neville like plants so I've helped him in the gardenîwe even got to harvest his pineapple-apple tree. I've also spent a lot of time with Draco; we both love flying so I've done quite a lot of it, I'm even thinking about joining the Ravenclaw team. I've even seen Susan a few times. We went to the beach with her aunt once, which was fun as I've never seen the sea before, but I'm not the best swimmer so I didnít go too deep.Æ All of this was said with excitement, Harry making sure to portray a happy child, not that it was too difficult a task. ØIt sounds like youíve had a great summer,Æ Joanne said. ØI think that's everything I need to know. Why donít you show me around? By then I think Iíll be done, I just need to have a quick word with Sirius.Æ She had no concerns about the boy and was glad to see him so happy. Harry smiled and went to give her the tour, making sure to happily show off his room and the library. Once that was done he left Sirius to have his conversation. ØHello, Lord Black,Æ Joanne said. ØPlease call me Sirius, Ms. Mirwood,Æ Sirius said, offering her some tea. ØThen call me Joanne.Æ Accepting the tea, Joanne looked at the young Lord Black and noted he looked healthier. ØNow I've just got a few questions, then Iím done.Æ ØThatís fine.Æ ØHave you noticed anything wrong with Harry since you've had him? Things such as nightmares or behavioural problems? They're not uncommon in children from bad homes.Æ ØNo, nothing like that, heís been great. I'm surprised, really; I swear he handles some of the things better I do.Æ Joanne nodded. ØHe certainly is a remarkable young man. Iíll be honest when I say I can see no cause for concern. Harry appears to be well adjusted and heís settled with you; however, I wouldnít be doing my job if I didnít ask. Harry mentioned being independent, would you explain that for me?Æ ØI think itís from him never having an adult to rely on. He sometimes doesnít realise heís not alone - that he can ask for help. Itís not a problem really, and since living with us heís got a lot better. I think heís realised that weíre here for him and that we wonít abandon him,Æ Sirius said. ØThatís good. Have you any idea about his school work? I heard that's heís in Ravenclaw, so I'm guessing heís smart?Æ ØHeís top of his class. I was thinking about writing to Professor Flitwick about getting him some advanced tutoring if heís still so ahead by Yule. I know he gets advanced work in Potions already.Æ ØThat is remarkable,Æ Joanne said, surprised at just how advanced Harry was. ØIím guessing that he got Lilyís brains. Me and James were more interested in having fun when we were at school, Lilís was always the smart one.Æ ØHave you spoken to him about his parents and his past? Not just the abuse, but everything? With his history and his fame, itís important he has a good foundation and knows he can talk to you about these things.Æ Sirius nodded. She had no idea just how much of his past and technically future Harry had shared. ØAt the start Harry was hesitant to talk but since then heís opened up. I think heís told us pretty much everything and he knows he can come to me and Remus. Heís got pictures of his parents and Iíve explained about the last war and what it involved, not the gory details, but the basics so he would understand.Æ ØHe took it well?Æ ØHe said that if the world wants to move on and get better then we had to forgive and forget. He doesnít like to dwell on the bad stuff, he says it hold people back.Æ ØHe really is a special young man,Æ Joanne said. ØI think that's everything, unless you have any questions for me?Æ Sirius thought for a moment. ØI was wondering about Dumbledore, actually. I mean, he was Harry's guardian yet he allowed for the abuse to go on for so long. Is there anything we can do?Æ Joanne hesitated. ØI was present during the trial, you know. I canít say much as the voting is bound by secrecy vows, but I can say this: Dumbledore's recent actions have brought quite a few suspicions to light and many people are starting to question him.Æ ØThank you,Æ Sirius said, hiding his glee. He knew Harry would love this. ØNow, if that's everything, I shall be going. Thank you for your time and hospitality, Lord Black.Æ Walking her out, Sirius practically ran to Harry, who was sitting in the library, to let him know about Dumbledore's growing troubles. ~ August 31st 1992, Grimmauld Place, London Harry watched as Remus fretted and Sirius paced. He had never had such mother hens in his lifeîeven Mrs. Weasley wasnít as bad. Perhaps having to go through the process so many times with all of the older Weasleys had mellowed the manipulating witch. ØYouíve definitely packed everything?Æ the wolf said, looking between Harry's trunk and him like he would suddenly say no. ØYes Remus, Kreacher and I packed everything.Æ ØAnd you're sure you want to go to Hogwarts? I mean, I hear Beauxbatons has lovely gardens,Æ Sirius said. In the last few days the animagus had become worried about Dumbledore's influence and what he might do to Harry while at school. ØYes, I'm sure they're very beautiful, but still I want to go to Hogwarts,Æ Harry said, amusement clear in his voice. Harry was actually looking forward to the year. Sure, he knew Dumbles - the manipulative old coot - would try something, but honestly, as far as he knew, this year would actually be totally event free. There would be no opening of the Chamber as he had the diary safely locked up here in Grimmauld. So, as far as Harry could tell, he had nothing to really worry about. Instead, he would focus on stretching his magic in either the Chamber or the Room of Requirement and simply prepare for Tomís return. He would just have to be careful about the basilisk, but Harry was sure that if he spoke to her, then he would be able to get her on his side. He didnít want to release her or get her to harm muggleborns, he just wanted her not to harm him if he does decide to investigate the Chamber. Yes, he would have to deal with the pompous peacock Lockhart for a teacher, but if he really couldnít cope he had already devised a plan for him. He didnít like cheats, let alone liars who made their fortunes off others, and he had planned to make it known that Lockhart was nothing more than a fraud. Lost in his thoughts, Harry didnít realise he was smirking until Sirius spoke. ØYou know, when you look like that I expect you to suddenly start cackling or talk about world domination.Æ Harry rolled his eyes playfully at his father figure. ØI'm not into being a dominatrix, Siri,Æ Harry deadpanned, making the animagus choke on air and Remus stumble. ØLittle boys should not even know that word,Æ Sirius said after eventually getting his breath back. ØI'm 19,Æ Harry said, shrugging. ØThatís more information than I ever wanted or needed to know,Æ Remus said, shaking his head but his eyes were twinkling with mirth. Sharing another look, Harry could no longer contain his laughter and suddenly burst out with it, his chuckles sending him, and eventually Sirius, to the floor. ØI'm going to miss you, pup,Æ Sirius said after finally calming down. ØIíll be back for Yule and I promise I will write.Æ ØDaily.Æ Harry rolled his eyes. ØNo, but at least weekly.Æ Casting a wandless tempus, Harry saw the time and announced he was going to bed. He would be up early in the morning as he knew he would end up having to calm both Sirius and Remus down before actually getting to the station. He and his friends had arranged to meet at ten in order to find a good compartment and hopefully not get caught in the main rush which usually arrived around 10:30. ~ 1st September 1992, Kingís Cross Station, London As he had suspected, Harry had spent the better part of his morning calming down both Sirius and Remus, who both decided to have nervous breakdown at the thought of Harry being so far away and in Dumbledore's grasp. ØIíll be fine. I have allies in the castle, both Filius and Snape are trustworthy and, hell, Lucius is on the Board of Directors. Nothing bad will happen,Æ Harry said as he forced the two men towards the parlour and, more importantly, the main Floo system. It was almost ten and he didnít want to keep his friends waiting. ØFine, fine, no need to push,Æ Sirius said, letting Remus go through the Floo first. ØYou know, I think the Blacks have a family seat on the Board of Governors,Æ Harry said, thinking of a book he had read. ØWe do,Æ Sirius confirmed before taking a handful of powder and disappearing. Harry followed after him, stumbling as he landed at the station. ØWill you take it up?Æ ØMaybe. Although there is a seat, I would still need to be recommended by a current member.Æ Sirius was becoming more active in politics since Harry had freed him; although he didnít have a passion for it, he knew that making allies would benefit him and Harry. However, as he had never bothered to pay much attention to his fatherís lessons, and the fact he had run away before they had gotten to the important political strategies, Sirius was pretty much playing blind. His name and natural charisma, and the fact he and Lucius had a rather strong alliance, were the biggest draws he currently had. He was lucky there hadnít been a formal full Wizengamot session yet. While all members were invited to vote in trials, full sessions were called only four times a year with the next in autumn. He had already had many different proposed bills sent to him as he was now an active member and he honestly didnít know how people could find any joy out of this. All the reading and the scheming was boring to him, however, for Harry, he would do it. ØI'm sure Lucius would gladly recommend you, if just to have an ally with him,Æ Harry said, cautiously looking around before flicking his wrist and making all of the powder currently on his robes disappear before his robes and hair straightened out. He had finally had his hair cut and it was now back to being shoulder length; still longer than in his old life, but Harry had found he liked it. It separated him from the boy he was - he still had things in common with the åold Harryí but he was slowly becoming more Harrison James Potter-Black than Harry James Potter. Seeing Lucius alongside Narcissa and Draco, Harry made his way other to his friends. Narcissa, seeing them approach, sent them a dazzling smile. ØAre you ready for your second year, Harrison?Æ she asked after leaning down and giving him a soft hug and kiss on the cheek. As there was nobody else really on the platform she allowed her pureblood mask to fall. ØYes, aunt Cissa.Æ She had insisted he call her aunt after the second time he had been over to the manor. ØExcellent! Now, I expect at least one letter this week from both of you. I want to hear all about your lessons and classmates,Æ she said in mock sternness. Harry and Draco exchanged looks before replying in sync, ØYes, maíam.Æ Lucius gave the boys a soft look. ØNow boys, I donít want to hear youíve been caught getting up to any trouble.Æ Sirius smiled. ØAnd by that he means cause as much as you can, but donít get caught.Æ Lucius sent the man a look. ØObviously,Æ he said. Harry smiled at the interaction. Although Lucius wasnít as obvious as Narcissa, and much more calculating, he really felt like he had a family in these people. Neville was still his best friend, but Draco was sort of like an annoying little cousin who you couldnít help but like. He was spoilt and arrogant but also caring, sweet, and, underneath all of his masks, he was a loyal friend. Soon enough Madam Bones appeared with both Hannah and Susan; they usually arrived to everything together with either Hannahís parents or Susan's aunt. Madam Bones didnít stay long, trusting the girls to get on the train, however she did stop to briefly talk to Sirius and Lucius and give both of the boys hugs. Harry hadnít seen Hannah since getting their school supplies as she had been on holiday and greeted both girls with hugs. Neville soon arrived and Harry was happy to see the slight changes in the boy. No longer did he walk with his head down and eyes averted but instead he walked with confidence. Seeing this, Harry smiled. He would never regret being friends with Neville. Once they were all together the adults quickly spelled their trunks and owl cages onto the train. Harry, however, didnít need this as he kept his shrunk in his pocket. There was another round of goodbyes and promises to write and the friends soon found themselves on the train in a compartment near the back. ~ The train Halfway through the journey, Harry found himself wondering if perhaps the Weasleys would actually be better off if their youngest male member was to mysteriously disappear. His journey had started off great: he and his friends had chatted about their holidays, eaten sweets, and played a few games of exploding snap. However, Harry should have known it wouldnít last. It was while they were daring each other to eat different Bertie Bott's Every Flavour Beans that the door to their compartment had opened quite forcefully. ØHarry mate, howís your summer been?Æ Ronald asked. Harry sent his friends incredulous looks before he sighed and stood, facing the redhead. Although taller than in his last life, Harry was still shorter than Ron by a few good inches. ØAs I said when I saw you in Diagon Alley, I do not wish to speak to you again. If I had my way I wouldnít even see you again. Unless you're here to apologise, which I very much doubt, I think you should leave,Æ Harry said. The rest of the compartment watched as Ron's face slowly went red and started to match his hair. ØBut-but - I was only saying the truth. You were with Malfoy, he's a dirty Death Eater!Æ Harry sighed again and put up a hand when he saw Draco rise, most likely to defend his father. ØLord Malfoy is practically my uncle, so I would recommend you stop saying such slanderous things. Now, as I said before, leave.Æ ØYou know what? I will. You're just a dirty Death Eater sympathizer. You're worse than them, actually - you're supposed to be a hero! But you're nothing! Iíll show you. Iíll be a great hero one day and you'll regret ever picking them over me!Æ Harry watched with cold eyes as Ron stormed out of their compartment. Although he knew the words were meaningless, he momentarily got lost in his memories. Flashes of Petunia and Vernon beating him, calling him a worthless freak, quickly crossed his mind before he was able to stop them. Shaking his head to rid himself of his past, Harry turned and faced his friends. ØI hope you two have finalised your plans for that menace,Æ he said to Neville and Draco. The two boys shared sly looks. ØWe may have.Æ ØGood.Æ ~ The rest of the journey went by quickly and before long they were reaching Hogwarts. Seeing a few Slytherins, Harry nudged Draco and told him to go get Blaise and Pansy so they could all share a carriage. ØAre you sure?Æ ØYes, itís fine. If they're your friends, then I want to meet them.Æ Draco smiled and quickly pulled a reluctant looking pair of Slytherins back with them. ØBlaise, this is Harrison Potter. Heís my friend and I think my second cousin or something. Harry, this is my best friend Blaise Zabini, heir to the Zabini family.Æ Harry smiled and held out his hand. ØNice to meet you, you can call me either Harrison or Harry. I'm not really bothered which,Æ he said, sending the dark skinned boy a smile. Blaise looked at his hand and his smile with calculating eyes before nodding and smiling back. ØPleasure,Æ he said, his posture relaxing. Draco looked between them and nodded happily. ØNow Harry, this lovely young lady is Pansy Parkinson. Pans, this is Harrison Potter, Boy-Who-Lived andîÆ he managed to say before he got cut off by Harry smacking him in the stomach. Harry smiled at Pansy and kissed her hand. ØIgnore Draco, apparently his manners have left him. I shall have to tell aunt Cissa. Anyway, itís a pleasure Ms. Parkinson,Æ he said. Pansy blushed slightly before straightening out. ØThe pleasureís all mine, Heir Potter.Æ ØPlease, call me either Harry or Harrison.Æ ØThen you must call me Pansy,Æ she said. Harry nodded and motioned for them to get into a carriage. All eight could fit comfortable as they were designed for up to ten. ØSo, how did you and Draco become so close?Æ Blaise asked. The rest of the group had done introductions in the carriage and now the girls were all sitting together, giggling about Gilderoy Lockhartís latest interview while Draco and Neville were plotting Ron's fate. ØWe met through my guardian, Sirius Black. He and aunt Cissa are cousins and, after his release, they rekindled their relationship,Æ Harry said, remembering what Draco had said about Blaise being a gossip king. ØI see, that's good,Æ Blaise said. ØItís outrageous - what happened to Lord Black.Æ ØI know. I doubt if he had been from a Light or even Neutral family such an injustice would have occurred.Æ ØUndoubtedly,Æ Blaise agreed. With his opinion now out there, Harry moved on to safer topics and soon the carriages pulled up to the castle. Promising to meet up after breakfast, Harry and his friends soon split up and went to their individual tables. ~ The Sorting, Harry realised, didnít really change, but Harry was happy when a tiny Luna Lovegood was placed in Ravenclaw. He had grown extremely fond of the airy blonde in his last life; her peculiar outlook on life and bubbly personality always making him smile, even during the worst of the war. At times Harry had even wondered if the little blonde was a Seer of some sort as she always knew just what to say to cheer him up. Sitting at the end of his yearmates in the hope the little blonde would join him, Harry soon relaxed as Luna danced towards him and took a seat. ØHello, Lord Potter-Black-Gryffindor-Peverell-Slytherin.Æ Harry blinked when Luna trailed off all of the titles he had gained in his last life. åWell,í he thought, åit would appear she was a Seer of some kind.í ØHello, Luna. Itís actually just Heir Potter-Black at the moment,Æ Harry said, smiling at the small girl. Luna looked up at him with large, unfocused blue eyes. ØOh, did you lick a moon frog and loose them?Æ Harry chuckled. ØNo, no, moon frogs this time, but I did come back with the help of somebody, so I no longer have them.Æ Harry watched as Lunaís eyes gained focus for a moment. ØYouíll be a great man, Harrison James Potter-Black.Æ Harry smiled. ØHopefully, but only if I can count you as one of my friends.Æ Luna stilled again and a beautiful smile appeared on her face. ØYouíll keep the nargles away?Æ she asked. ØIíll even disinfect the mistletoe,Æ Harry promised. ØThen I canít wait to join your followers,Æ Luna said with a smile, making Harry wonder for a moment before he shrugged and smiled back. After that, Harry tuned to watch the end of the Sorting. It all passed as he remembered up until it was time for Ginny to be Sorted. Harry watched as the hat was placed on her head and, for a while, all was silent. Unlike her brothers, the hat didnít immediately shout Gryffindor. Harry wondered momentarily if perhaps she would go into Slytherin - it wouldnít surprise him as he knew she was a manipulative swine. Eventually the hat shouted out, ØGRYFFINDOR!Æ Harry watched as Ginny sat up and shouted, ØBut I donít want to go to Gryffindor!Æ She then proceeded to put the hat back on. The whole hall was silent; the few who had started to clap stopped to stare. Even the teachers were sharing incredulous looks and, much to his amusement, Snape looked like he was attempting to set the redheaded fool on fire with his glare. Minerva was shocked to say the least. ØMs. Weasley, you have been Sorted. Now stop this foolishness at once and go and sit with your housemates!Æ she said. ØNo! I want to be in Ravenclaw with Harry. This stupid hat will put me there!Æ Ginny whined. Although most of the hall couldnít hear, some did - including Harry, who was paying special attention. Minerva shook her head and flicked her wand, summoning the hat to her. ØGo and join your housemates and be prepared for a serious conversation, young lady.Æ Ginny stood up and stomped to the Gryffindor table but not before sending Harry a look of longing that make him want to take a bath. ØI've never seen so many Blibbering Humdingers in one personís head before,Æ Luna said, staring at Ginny with a look of disgust, shocking Harry as he had thought they were friends. ØI'm surprised, I thought her head empty,Æ Harry grumbled back. Luna snorted softly. ØDonít worry, Harry, she wonít get you. Her plans are childish and immature.Æ ~ At the end of the feast everybody was still gossiping about Ginny's outburst and Harry could see the even the twins were looking at their little sister with slight incomprehension. He hoped that their obvious reaction showed they were not in on the plan to get him, and would be willing to join him. The other shock for the students was Gilderoy Lockhart. Harry watched with amusement as the students found out and Lockhart made his grand entrance. The hall was spilt in their reactions; half obviously excited and the rest clearly disgusted. Harry was happy that 90% of the Ravenclaws looked exceedingly putout and upset with the news. They had obviously read his book and realised what a load of rubbish the man spouted. Harry actually felt bad for the 5th and 7th years, they would have to work exceedingly hard to pass their OWLs and NEWTs. Once all of the news was done and Dumbledore had passed on some words of åwisdomí, all of the students were dismissed. ØActually, a lot in the house of intelligence and wit seem to have a nargles problem.Æ Harry tilted his head and thought about what he had learnt about Luna and her creatures before he realised she was worried about bullies. He remembered how much of a tough time she had had in her earlier year and vowed that he wouldnít let it happen. He was surprised when he thought about it, as last year they had been almost threatened to not bully their own, but he supposed there was always an exception. ØIf any of the nargles-infested do or say anything, you let me know, Little Moon,Æ Harry said. ØI wonít let them upset you.Æ ØThank you, my Lord,Æ Luna said, her usual soft and dreamy voice once again making Harry wonder about what she could See. Eventually the new 7th year prefects came forward and gave more or less the same speech as last year, once again outlining what Ravenclaw stood for, their unofficial rules, and their study policy with the notice boards and Flitwick. After their speech was done, Harry left Luna to get sorted and meet her roommates while he went to sort out his own things. Although he had initially been close to Terry last year, he hadnít really spoken much to the boy over the summer and was feeling bad about that now. So he spent the night talking to him and asking about his summer. ~ In the morning Harry once again resorted to sending tickling charms at his roommates to get them up; he had done so throughout the year last year and was happy to keep the trend going. ØIíll see you guys down there,Æ Harry said to them once they were all up. He didnít want to miss Luna coming down; he wanted to know about her night and if she had had any problems. Sitting in one of the midnight blue loveseats, Harry waited for Luna and was happy when she appeared looking excited instead of forlorn. ØYou had a good night then, Luna?Æ Harry asked, offering her his arm to walk to breakfast. However, they are all interested in you, mighty Dark wizard slayer.Æ ØWell then, they obviously donít realise what they're missing.Æ Getting to the hall with the rest of their House, who, like last year, all turned up together. Harry sat down with Luna and enjoyed his breakfast. Out of nowhere Luna spoke, ØThe manipulator is watching you, my Lord. He has many plans.Æ Harry looked up discreetly and saw that Dumbledore was indeed watching him. However, he didnít know how Luna would have noticed as she had been daydreaming while eating her grapes. The thought solidified his theory about her being a Seer of some sort. ØThank you, I will make sure to be on my guard.Æ When Flitwick gave out their timetables, the half goblin gave Harry a smile and asked if he would like to join him for tea in the evening. ØCertainly, professor,Æ Harry agreed. Before looking at his timetable, he had Herbology first, which was good as he would be with all of his friends. Glancing over at Lunaís timetable, he saw she would have to suffer Lockhart straight away. ØI'm sorry, Little Moon, but if it helps he shouldnít be here all year. I have plans for him,Æ Harry said, seeing her forlorn expression. ØOh, are you going to feed Aithusia?Æ Harry thought for a moment. ØIf that's her name, then perhaps,Æ Harry agreed. He hadnít known the basiliskís name yet, but he was going to go out on a limb at guess it was Aithusia. ØShe would like that, she gets very lonely.Æ ØThen Iíll try to visit her,Æ Harry promised, making Luna once again smile brightly. Chapter End Notes Sorry about the delay, i've been rushed off my feet and literally not had a free minute. Looking up, Harry noticed how jumpy the redhead looked and how his already pale skin seemed even whiter, the dark bags under his eyes standing out. ØWhat did you do to him?Æ Harry asked, watching in amusement as the redheadís eyes seemed to dart around. ØWell, last year I found out he hated spiders,Æ Neville said. ØHe would actually scream like a girlîÆ Neville was cut off when Susan hit him. ØNot all girls are afraid of spiders,Æ she said. ØI know, sorry. It was genius - evil, but genius. Walking towards the greenhouses, Harry was actually looking forward to the lesson as he would be with all of his friends; sure, they would most likely be potting mandrakes again like last time, but at least he had a chance to talk to all of his friends. However, before he reached his destination, he was accosted by none other than Colin Creevy. He had forgotten about the boyís almost manic hero worship - he had grown out of it or at least mellowed before his death during the final battle. The blinding flash made Harry growl as he fought his reaction to obliterate the camera, his war-honed instincts fighting to come out. ØYouíre Harry Potter! You're my hero! I read all about you - I'm Colin, Colin Creevy. I tried to get put in Ravenclaw like you, but the hatîÆ ØEnough,Æ Harry growled out, holding up a hand. I'm just a regular student like everybody else.Æ ØBut youíre the Boy-Who-Liî!Æ ØNo, and another thing: I hate that title. I lost my parent that night, all it does is remind me of that fact. I'm nothing special and I would ask you to please not take my picture again without my permission. Actually, donít take anybodyís picture without permission; most people will find it incredible rude.Æ Colin looked both awestruck and devastated. ØO-okay,Æ he squeaked. Feeling bad, Harry sighed. ØItís okay to have heroes and look up to people, Colin, even looking up to me. But you should only do it to people who deserve it, whose actions actually merit praise. Most people will probably be happy for their picture to be taken.Æ The boy seemed to perk up. ØThanks, Harry,Æ he said, his awestruck expression taking over his devastation. Harry rubbed the back of his neck before he nodded and walked away. He hoped that he had nipped Colinís excessive hero worship in the bud; he really didnít want the excitable first year trailing after him like a lost puppy. ~ At the end of his first day, Harry was happy to be free of classes. He had forgotten how exhausting it was to play down his abilities, especially since coming clean to Sirius and Remus meant he didnít have to hide at home anymore. Making his way up to Flitwickís office, Harry knocked and was soon called in. ØCome in, Harry,Æ the half goblin said, smiling and offering Harry a chair. ØGood evening, professor,Æ Harry said, sending the diminutive man a genuine smile. ØYouíre looking very well,Æ the professor observed. ØI trust your summer was good?Æ ØAs you're probably aware from the papers, if you've been reading them, I had a hectic summer to say the least,Æ Harry said. ØBut yes, it was surprisingly good. Throughout the first week, Harry watched as Ron slowly seemed to lose his mind. He would jump up and scream at random intervals and constantly twitch. This left Gryffindor in the minus when it came to House points, Severus taking points almost gleefully. However, the charms soon started to fade and the boys gleefully informed Harry that they were preparing for phase two. What phase two was, Harry had no idea, but he was looking forward to their ingenuousness. It wasnít until the next day that Harry saw exactly what it was that the two boys had done. He had been sitting with Luna, who was excitedly telling him about her plans to find a Crumple-Horned Snorkack and prove their existence whilst he was braiding her hair. He hadn't braided hair before and honestly he found the experience quite therapeutic. Ron had stormed in, much like he had a year ago with green skin, this time however his hair was also bright Hufflepuff yellow and his robes Ravenclaw blue. Seeing this, Harry burst into laughter along with the rest of the hall. When the teachers tried to spell him back, Harry only laughed harder as the colours shifted: his skin turning yellow, hair blue, and robes green. ØOh my! Even the nargles are laughing at him,Æ Luna said, giggling her bell-like laugh as she stared at Ron. Harry snorted and tied off the braid with a piece of bark blue ribbon. It wasn't anything fancy but both Harry and Luna took pleasure in the activity. Luna because it was something her mother used to do, and Harry because it was something new and had no association with anybody else. Luna hummed when she pulled the braid around and saw the ribbon. Øthank you,Æ she said happily running her dainty hands over it. Harry smiled and stood. Øcare to join me on finding out more about this fine example of magic?Æ he said, offering his arm. Luna took it and skipped next to Harry who was making his way over to the Slytherin table. Luna, who he had introduced to his friends and who had easily found her place among them smiled at the snakes as they made their way over. ØHow long will it last?Æ Harry asked once Draco was in hearing range. ØIt should last for a few days and hopefully they wonít find a counter. Harry had lent him some of his less age appropriate books over the summer when Draco was complaining about a lack of inspiration. He knew the boy wouldnít judge him and as they were not actually Dark arts books, Harry didnít fear lending them to his sort of cousin. Harry smirked. ØI'm glad I could help.Æ ~ As Neville and Draco's pranking vendetta continued, Harry wasnít surprised when he was suddenly pulled into a hidden alcove. ØWeíll be watching you, baby bird.Æ Harry nodded before walking away, careful to keep an eye out for any pranks they might send at him. He didnít know what to think about the twins; he didnít want to get his hopes up, but he wished more than anything to have them back in his life. ~ The only other interesting thing to happen in Harryís first week was his first Defence Against the Dark Arts lesson. ØAll we could ever learn from this buffoon is how to write a fantasy book.Æ ØAnd not a good one,Æ Blaise added from the front, sending Harry a smirk. Harry wasnít surprised that the Slytherins held his disdain for the man - they could probably see through his lies easier than most. ØBut he's a member of the Defence Association,Æ Hannah argued. ØHave you read his books? I didnít get rid of the Bandon Banshee by smiling at her!Æ At the end he turned and looked expectantly at the class. Harry was thankful that nobody laughed. Even Hannah and Susan seemed put-off by the man after his jab at Harry's fame. Couldnít answer the questions?Æ the man asked. I simply refuse to take part and waste ink on a quiz that holds no educational value at all.Æ Lockhart stood with his mouth open and gaped for a few minutes, reminding Harry of a fish, while he struggled to find words. ØDetention with me tonight and ten points from Ravenclaw, Mr. Potter!Æ he shouted. Harry shrugged. ØSo be it.Æ The class watched in fascination as Harry continued to daydream and refuse to answer any question Lockhart asked him, resulting in a further ten points being lost by the end of class. The Ravenclaws looked horrified but the Slytherins were almost losing their pureblood masks with the amount of delight they found themselves in. By the time they were dismissed, Draco was actually shaking with his suppressed laughter. Even the Gryffindors, who had followed Ron's example and had not really taken to Harry, were laughing. ØNow, if you will excuse me Ronald, I want to get to lunch. Dealing with idiots makes me hungry.Æ Harry turned to leave, but, when he felt an oncoming hex, he span around and flicked his wand. The hex rebounded off of his shield and hit Ron, who immediately started breaking out in boils. ØAttack me when my back is turned again and I will do much worse than rebound your pathetic little hex,Æ Harry threatened, not realising how terrifying he looked. His friends, who had drawn their wands, and even the Slytherins all sent Ron dark looks. The picture they presented was making him shiver in fear and the pain from the boils intensified. Straightening out, Harry shook himself and flicked his wrist so his wand went back into his holster. ØCome on guys, I donít want to be late to lunch,Æ he said, walking ahead of them. When they neared the hall, Luna appeared and draped herself against Harry. ØThe dingle sprites told me you were angry, my Lord.Æ ØI'm not angry Little Moon, I just donít seem to be able to suffer fools for as long as I could in the past,Æ Harry said. ØI see.
