She still hadnít forgiven him for making Harry laugh at her weeks ago. Harry rolled his eyes at the irony. ØThey're my friends. Now, I think itís best if you left, Ms. Weasley.Æ Ginny gaped. ØButîÆ ØGoodbye, Ginny,Æ Luna said, sending the redhead a sweet smile and wave. Ginny let out a small snarl and turned with a huff. If she didnít get Harry to start liking her back soon, then she was done for. Dumbledore was already angry enough about the Lockhart situation. She didnít understand how he could resist herîshe was perfect! She was smart, funny, beautiful - she even liked quidditch! What was she doing wrong? ~ November in Hogwarts always seemed to drag for Harry. Without Voldemortís yearly Halloween session and the fallout which would always follow, the month had no draw for Harry. In this life, October at least held Samhain and the ritual which solidified his friendships. December had Yule - which, this year, held the greatest importance for Harry as he would be starting the ritual to bring Tom back; it would also be his first proper Yule celebration with family and friends who wanted him only for himself, not his money or his name. ØYou know, I think that somebody has your birthday wrong,Æ Neville said, looking at the package Harry had received just moments ago. Harry looked up and frowned. The parcel was wrapped in cheap, easily available brown paper and, although it was neat, it didnít have the finery he associated with either the Malfoys or Sirius. Even Ragnok tended to use high quality paper. ØI doubt itís a birthday present,Æ Harry said. ØOpen it,Æ Susan said, peering up from her Daily Prophet. Harry looked at the parcel and frowned once again. Opening his senses, he allowed his magic to wash over the package and found it clean. Picking it up, he used his wand to break the wrapping with a small severing charm. Inside was a box of chocolates. Harry, remembering his last life, knew not to eat them and instead picked up the note. ~ Always yours, XOX ~ Scowling, Harry picked up the box and placed it in his bag. Ginny had apparently upped her game if she was resorting to potions this quickly. Although he didnít have definitive proof, he recognised the childish handwriting. He had to wonder if Dumbledore had put her up to it; he knew the old man recognised his Heir ring - perhaps the girl and her mother were acting alone? Or at least without the council of the old coot. ØNot going to share, Potter?Æ came Ronís voice. He had been watching enviously from down the table. Harry frowned before a thought hit him, the smirk on his face making him look truly sinister. ØBy all meansè,Æ Harry said, taking out the box and offering it to the redhead. Ron looked surprised for a minute, the shock making him gape. ØOh-wha-? O-okay,Æ he said greedily, his sense of entitlement overruling the little doubt he had. Harry noticed his friendsí confused looks and hoped they wouldnít question him. Seeing Ron eat the chocolate, Harry watched in fascination as his eyes clouded over for a second before clearing. Taking the chocolates back, Harry hurried out of the hall. ØWhat was that all about, Harry?Æ Draco asked, recognising the smirk on Harryís face. It usually came before he destroyed somebody or made a fool out of them. Harry had been wearing it a lot recentlyîmostly around the youngest weasels and the peacock, as Harry had dubbed Lockhart. ØI think these have been doped with a love potion,Æ Harry said conversationally, as though he was talking about the weather. Susan and Hannah gasped, the Slytherins frowned before smirking, and Neville looked confused. Luna, however, looked slightly green. They had each realised now why Harry had let Ron eat one and, although none of them objected, their own personalities still came into play through their reactions. Neville took the longest to catch on but he soon joined the Slytherins with a matching smirk. ØYou let Ron eat one?Æ Susan asked. Harry shrugged. ØHe wanted one and I donít know for certain,Æ he said. Susan frowned before nodding. ØWell, thatís true, and anyway itís not your fault somebody wants to poison you.Æ ØThatís right. I wonder who would do such a thing?Æ Hannah said, frowning. Draco laughed then. ØMerlin! Imagine if itís the weaslette.Æ Luna just nodded. ØPlease donít, itís not a pretty picture.Æ Harry just smirked again. ØThatís why people shouldnít play around with love potions,Æ he said, practically biting his tongue to hold back the manic giggle that wanted to escape. ØLove potions?Æ The rest of the group looked up at the repeated phrase and all took a step back when they saw Severus appear, even the Slytherins. ØYes, sir,Æ Harry said, taking a step forward. He had been leading the group towards Severusí office to have the man check for love potions. ØWhatís this about love potions? Surely you're not brewing them, Mr. Potter. Although I doubt even Amortentia is beyond your ability, I had thought you had slightly more common sense. Perhaps I was mistaken?Æ Harry smirked at the backhanded compliment. ØLike always sir, you're correct. I've not been brewing love potions; however, I think somebody has. I was sent these and I noticed something was, well, off about them. Could you perhaps check them for me?Æ Harry asked, offering the box. Severus took the box and noticed the missing chocolate. Raising an eyebrow in question, he held back his snort when he got a smirk back. He was coming to recognise that expression as being Harryís face for whenever he had done something devious and slightly amoral. ØDid you notice this... off feeling after consuming one yourself?Æ Severus asked just to check if the boy had been stupid enough to do such a thing. ØNo, before,Æ Harry said. ØOh?Æ ØWell, Ronald Weasley thought I was being unfair to put away an entire box of chocolates. He clearly wanted one and, as I didnít know if my feeling would be correct as I am not a Potions Master, I had no reason to deny him,Æ Harry said. Severus, who like many of the teachers, had noticed the youngest Weasleyís obsession with the Potter-Black Heir and immediately realised the potential backlash. It was only his years of training that made it so his face remained impassive when he realised the situation. ØI see,Æ he said, making sure to sound monotone. Harry and the Slytherins, however, recognised the tone and all shared smirks. ØI will let you know the outcome of the tests. If a love potion is found, then I will of course notify your Head of House and guardians. Love potions are highly illegal,Æ he said. Harry nodded. ØThank you, professor,Æ he said. Severus nodded and turned, taking the box with him. It was only when he closed the portrait behind himself that he allowed his laughter free, snickering until his eyes watered and sides ached. He hoped the female Weasley had been stupid enough to attempt to use a love potion .the pure horror on her face and that of her overly sanctimonious mother would make his year. 16th November 1992, the Great Hall, Hogwarts It was a few days later, when Harry saw Flitwick walking towards him looking furious, that Harry found out the reaction to the chocolates. It was dinner time and Harry and Luna were sitting at the Ravenclaw table alone for once. The rest of the group hadn't made it to dinner yet. ØHarry, I know you have not eaten yet, however an urgent matter has come to my attention and our presence is required in the headmasterís office,Æ the part-goblin said. Harry nodded and spared Luna a glance. ØWill you be okay here alone, Little Moon?Æ he asked. Luna nodded. ØYes. The nargles are not as bothersome this time around, my Lord,Æ she said. Harry nodded and stood, picking up his bag. ØIíll see you later then, Luna,Æ he said. Following his Head of House out of the Great Hall, Harry saw how agitated he was. ØIs everything alright, sir?Æ he asked. Filius sighed. He was furious, angry, confused, and concerned. Ever since last year he had been questioning the people and things around him and, although he didnít blame Harry in any way, he did recognise that it all started when he re-entered the magical world. ØNo, Harry,Æ he said. ØIt seems that the chocolates that you received really were doped with a love potion.Æ Harry nodded sagely and used his occlumency shields to hide his mirth. ØI see.Æ ØThis is obviously a serious matter as love potions are illegal. However, the use of them are not altogether unheard of; we do sometimes get a few cases of young witches and wizards brewing them, usually unsuccessfully. However, that is not the reason for this meeting. Your situation is more complex.Æ Harry sent the man a look. ØIsnít it always?Æ he asked. Filius shared a sad smile. ØIt would appear that the potion was keyed to the youngest Weasley child and, when her brother ate one of the chocolates, the artificial feelings attached themselves to him.Æ Harry was happy for his success at mastering occlumency as he hid his reaction. ØThatís awful,Æ he said, making sure to sound both shocked and disgusted. Filius nodded. ØYes,Æ he agreed. ØNow, letís not speak more about it until we reach the headmasterís office. He wants the entire situation sorted out.Æ Filius thought the situation, although bad, was clear. He had seen Ginevraís obsession with Harrison and didnít doubt she had been the one to send him the potion but he didnít know where she would have gotten it. Although not dumb like her year older brother, Ginevra didnít seem the type to have the ability to brew such a complex potion. The situation itself was awful: young Ronald had thrown himself at Ginevra and kissed her quite forcefully. Thankfully thatís all he had done, but the damage was done. Still, Filius didnít see why Dumbledore was insisting on a meeting between the Weasleys and Harry - it just added to his belief that the old man was abnormally invested in his young studentís life. Harry had done nothing! Thankfully, Filius had seen to it that Sirius would be present as well as Severus, who had done the initial testing. Harry followed his Head of House and wondered what Ron had doneîprobably an overly soppy love poem or song like he had written for Lavender in his last life. Reaching the gargoyle, Harry watched as Filius gave the password. ØPumpkin pastries.Æ The Headmasterís Office Entering the room, Harry was surprised to see both Sirius and Lucius sitting together with Severus standing behind them like a shadow. Both men were dressed in expensive robes, looking like the Lords of two Ancient and Noble Houses that they were. They contrasted drastically to Molly Weasley, who Harry was seeing properly for the first time in this life. The matriarch of the Weasley clan was dressed like she had always done: she wore a loose-fitting dress with a knitted shawl over it. Seeing his guardian, Harry approached and gave him a smile before Sirius pulled him into a hug. Dumbledore was sitting behind his desk, looking grandfatherly in yellow robes with silver suns. Seeing Harry enter, he picked up his lemon drops. ØExcellent, itís good that we are all here,Æ he said before holding the bowl out to the young boy who had become the biggest pain in his side since Tom Riddle. ØLemon drop?Æ he offered. Harry rolled his eyes at Sirius before looking at Dumbledore. ØNo, thank you. I've yet to have dinner.Æ Dumbledore frowned, the expression only becoming more pronounced when Ron immediately reached forward and snatched a handful of the sour sweets. ØVery well then, letís get straight to business,Æ he said. ØI'm sure you all know why we are here.Æ Lucius took that moment to intervene. ØActually, professor, I for one do not understand why we are here.Æ Dumbledore frowned. He hadn't expected Filius to contact Sirius so quickly or for the animagus to arrive with Lucius in tow. However, he should have realised their partnership .the two of them had been spending a lot of time together recently. Both of them most likely working together to undo all of his hard work and decades of progress. ØYes, wellîÆ ØWhy is he here?Æ Molly Weasley demanded, pointing a finger at Lucius and looking at him like he was a bug under her shoe. ØHe was invited to come with me as both a member of our extended family and the Head of the school governors,Æ Sirius said, managing to sound snobbish, something Harry knew he struggled with. Molly looked like she had sucked on a lemon before she harrumphed. Dumbledore sent her a sharp look before turning back to Harry. ØAs I was saying, we are here about the situation about some chocolates,Æ he said, playing down the seriousness of the matter. Harry narrowed his eyes. ØI see.Æ Lucius, however, wasn't done. ØI do not understand why you have called this meeting. The situation is quite clear. Why havenít you contacted the DMLE?Æ Molly let out a small shriek. ØThe DMLE?!Æ Dumbledore hushed her. ØWhat I'm sure Mrs. Weasley meant was that contacting the aurors isnít necessary.Æ Sirius growled. ØNot necessary?Æ he repeated. ØYes. Surely you agree, Sirius? What with your past pranks.Æ ØThatís Lord Black to you,Æ Sirius snarled. Molly turned. ØAfter all he's done for you, you no goodîÆ Sirius, looking like he was about to lose control, went to stand before Harry placed a hand on his arm. ØHeadmaster, I too do not understand why this meeting is taking place without law enforcement. The use of love potions is illegal.Æ Dumbledore bit his tongue before taking a deep breath. ØYes, howeverîÆ ØThere is no alternative here, professor. The law is the law,Æ Harry said. ØSurely you do not think yourself above it?Æ Harry made sure to ask the question as innocently as possible and watched as all of those standing in his corner smirk, even Flitwick. Dumbledore frowned. ØSurely you wouldnít want to take this further, Harry? You would be potentially running a young girlís life - and over what? A prank and school girl crush?Æ Lucius stepped back in then. ØIf it was just a crush we wouldnít be here, nor would Severus. As it stands the potion used is not only illegal but exceptionally potent, as I'm sure Mr. Weasley the youngest can attest to.Æ He sneered the last bit and Harry watched in fascination as both Ginny and Ron turned white while Molly turned red. ØSurely they have suffered enough?Æ Dumbledore continued, sending Harry hopeful looks. Harry tilted his head. ØSuffered enough? What about what I would have suffered? Do you know that line theft usually has a lifetime sentence to Azkaban?Æ Dumbledore choked for a moment, Molly let out another shrill sound, while Ginny whimpered and Ron just looked confused. ØNow see here, nobody is talking about line theft. Itís just a schoolgirl crush,Æ Albus quickly defended. Harry sent him a dark look. ØItís a schoolgirl crush now, but then what? Would she have just stopped one day, hoping I would have fallen in love with her?Æ Ginny picked that moment to speak up, ØI would have! You will love me one day!Æ Harry sat back, satisfied with the events, while Lucius shared a silent conversation with Severus. He saw that his friend was also surprised at how well Harry had played the room. Sirius turned and gave the Weasleys the darkest look he could; the look the girl was sending Harry gave him the creeps. ØI very much doubt that,Æ he said. Ron, thinking they were being insulted, forgot his fear and puffed up his chest. ØYeah? And whyís that? You too good for us? Too rich and fancy?Æ Harry snorted. ØNo, you fool. I'm gay.Æ Ron recoiled as though struck while Ginny shrieked, impressively sounding very much like her mother. The Slytherins were shocked but hid it well. Lucius thought for a moment about the implications of this, but he, unlike some traditionalists, didnít mind same-sex relationships. The use of potions made it so heirs were possible, therefore he was all for the celebration of love in all forms. Severus too didnít mind, he was just shocked at the delivery of the news - it reminded him of Lily and how she would have announced something. Albus, however, was gapingîthis wouldnít do. He needed his Light warrior to be perfect, to be the ideal that people aspired to be. How was Harry gay? Surely his muggle relatives had beaten any freakishness out of him? Yes, Dumbledore could understand the weakness of flesh, him more than anybody, but Harry should be above it. He should aim to be normal, not happily spouting that he's gay! ØYou're a freak,Æ Ron said after a few seconds. Harry responded by raising an eyebrow. ØYes, because being gay is so outrageous.Æ ØYou canít be gay! Weíre going to get married!Æ Ginny protested. Harry sent the adults on his side a look as if to say, åSee what I have to deal with?í He turned back and sighed. ØI will only marry for love and who I love will be a man.Æ Filius shook his head. ØNot that this isnít important, but we have gone off topic,Æ he said, speaking up for the first time. ØI agree,Æ Severus said, reminding the room of his presence. ØIndeed. Now, headmaster, I demand that you contact the DMLE as both Harryís father and a member of the Board. And do not think I will be keeping this from them,Æ Sirius said, using the title father for the first time and making Harry smile. Albus floundered; how had he lost control? He was sure that Harry would be swayed once he realised the trouble Ginny would get in. ØThat was not a request,Æ Lucius said, managing to sound threatening without actually doing so. Molly sent Dumbledore a panicked look. ØNow see here, surely you're overreacting! Ginny is a good girl.Æ ØA good girl who attempted to use highly illegal potions to poison another student,Æ Severus said, happy to add to the womanís panic. He had never liked her and, out of her seven children, she only had four children he could tolerate. Well, perhaps just the oldest two. The twins, although gifted potioneers, were menaces. ØPoison? It was a love potion, for Merlinís sake!Æ ØA potion which alters the senses and free will of the victim. It is a poison, Mrs. Weasley,Æ Severus said. Sirius nodded and sent Snape a grateful look. It was the first time he had seen his schoolhood enemy and he was grateful to the man; not only for the help he had provided Harry, but for his support now. Severus saw the look and nodded his head once, remembering Harryís letter about Black being a new man. Albus stood and glared at the men in betrayal before sending Harry a disappointed look. ØIf this is what you really want, Harry,Æ he said one final time. ØIt is.Æ Making sure to sigh dramatically, Dumbledore stood and went to the fireplace. ØAlbus!Æ Molly shrieked in betrayal. Albus turned to her. ØMy hands are tied,Æ he said, before throwing in the green floo powder. ØMadam Bones office, Department of Magical Law Enforcement, British Ministry of Magic.Æ Soon Harry heard Amelia through the Floo. ØDumbledore, is there a problem?Æ ØYes, Amelia. Perhaps you and one of your aurors could step through? Weíve had a slight issue with a love potion.Æ Amelia sighed when she heard the words. They would get one of these every few years and each time she would end up with a headache. ØGive me two minutes,Æ she said before leaving her office and pulling the first auror she saw along with her. ØKingsley, with me,Æ she said, seeing the tall black auror. She trusted and respected the man enough for this. ØThereís been use of a love potion at Hogwarts,Æ she said as explanation to the manís look. Kingsley cringed - he hated love potions. Stepping through the Floo, Ameliaís gaze automatically locked with the emerald eyes of Harry Potter and she found herself looking up and praying to Merlin for peace. ØI should have known you would somehow be involved, Harry,Æ she said to the boy, who was smiling sheepishly. ØAmeliaîsorry, Madam Bones, itís lovely to see you again,Æ he said making sure to act charmingly. Amelia sighed and smiled at the boy. ØHeir Potter-Black, this is my associate Senior Auror Kingsley Shacklebolt.Æ Harry nodded and looked at Kingsley - he hadn't changed much from the man he once knew. ØItís nice to meet you, Auror Shacklebolt,Æ Harry said. The rest of the introductions were quickly given and Madam Bones was swiftly brought up to speed. Once all of the information was given, she sighed and turned to look at Harry and Sirius. ØLord Black, as your Heir is the victim here, it is up to you on how we proceed. Although itís not enough to convict Ms. Weasley with line theft, you still have the right to claim for the attempt of poisoning and the use of an illegal substance,Æ she said. Molly, who was listening, let out another harsh sound and brought Ginny closer to her while sending Albus looks of betrayal. Sirius nodded and rubbed his neck. ØMay I speak to Harry in private, please?Æ he asked. Harry couldnít believe his luck and once Amelia agreed, he turned to face Sirius along with Severus, Lucius, and Filius. ØWhat do you intend to do?Æ Lucius asked. Harry smirked. ØI have an idea.Æ Once he explained what he had in mind, he watched as the two teachers nodded. ØThat should work,Æ Flitwick said. Severus nodded as well, smirking. ØI have made a decision,Æ Harry said, Øthat my father agrees with.Æ Amelia nodded. ØVery well,Æ he said. ØI do not want Miss Weasley to be kicked out of Hogwarts. She deserves a chance at getting an education and snapping her wand would just mean that there is another person out there more likely to expose us to muggles or cause damage to herself and others. Instead, I think she should serve detention every other night for the remainder of the year and every weekend bar one a month. She should also receive no special privileges. I know that the Weasley family is not well off it terms of monetary means, therefore I do not want them fined. Why should the entire family suffer for the actions of one? Instead, I think that it should be placed on her permanent record that she is guilty of trying to poison somebody with a love potion, so that the world knows what she has done.Æ Amelia nodded. ØVery well. If that is all, then we can leave it here. This is now a school matter and not a matter for my department.Æ Albus nodded, glad she would be going. ØDonít you think thatís a bit harsh, Harry?Æ he asked. Amelia, who had been about to leave, turned then and shot the old man an incredulous look. ØHeadmaster, you should know that Heir Potter-Black is being incredibly lenient. He could have demanded conviction and, being who he is, he may have gotten it. He could have even demanded excessive fines as compensation. He could have had Ms. Weasley kicked out of school. I, for one, think he is being more than lenient and should be commended on his compassion.Æ ØCompassion? Itís going to be on her record!Æ Molly screeched. ØThen she shouldnít have attempted to do something illegal! You should be grateful Heir Potter-Black is not pressing charges. If I had had to investigate, I wonder what I would have found concerning where the potion came from?Æ Amelia asked. Molly immediately shut up and sneered. ØA whole year?Æ Ginny whined. ØHush now, dear,Æ Molly said to her daughter, still sending Amelia irritated looks. November 16th 1992, the Ministry of Magic, Head of the DMLE Office After taking her leave, Amelia sent a confused looking Kingsley on his way before taking out her wand and unlocking her desk. Picking up the folder she had hidden, she sat down and looked through it briefly. ~ Albus Percival Wulfric Brian Dumbledore Born: August 17th 1881 Mould-on-the-Wold, England, Great Britain Blood Status: Half-Blood Marital Status: Single è ~ Not reading the entire file, Amelia instead wrote about their recent meeting and the implications of it. She knew there was something more going on between Dumbledore and the Potter boy, but she didnít know what. Dumbledore's recent behaviour was inexcusable and the information being brought to light about him made Amelia wonder about his past actions. His famous defeat of Gellert Grindelwald had made it so nobody questioned him too harshlyîhe was given untold amount of freedoms to do as he pleased and Amelia wondered what it was he had actually done. The unknown answers frustrated her. Meanwhile Headmasterís Office, Hogwarts ØI believe we are done here,Æ Sirius said, his tone of voice leaving no room for question. ØThank you.Æ The others watched as the elfís eyes grew wide and it squeaked before popping away. ØSo, itís not just Kreacher who loves you?Æ Sirius asked. ØNope,Æ Harry said, popping the åpí. Sirius shook his head. ØFrom what I have learnt in the past year, Dumbledore has done nothing for Harrison other than try to control his life. This doesnít even take his obsessive observations into account. What I want to know is why? Why is he after my student?Æ Sirius shot Harry a look. Harry looked around the room and considered his options. Looking at the occupants, Harry made a decision. Flicking his wrist, he gripped his wand and set the strongest privacy and secrecy wards he knew. The ease at which he put them up shocked three of the four occupants. Sirius looked at Harry surprised, knowing Harry was about to let more people in. Flitwick reached for his wand and tested the wards. ØThese are professional grade, Mr. Potter, and yet you didnít say a word,Æ he said. Harry raised an eyebrow. ØMagic doesn't need words. Words are just props; like wands, they allow easier access, but they're not necessary with the right level of power, skill, and control. Magic is about intent - itís alive, after all,Æ Harry said, lifting his hand and focusing so a controlled ball of fire lit up and twisted around his fingers. He also allowed his shields to drop, exposing his magical potential and core. Severus gaped at the boy in front of him. The power and control he was showing was beyond anything he had felt. Lucius, however, was conflicted. The blond man was both excited and smug; he knew the boy had power and potential and now he was going to get some answers. Harry sent Sirius a look, hoping that the animagus would agree with whatever he was about to say. ØIt all started with a prophecy,Æ Harry said, startling Severus. Taking a deep breath, Harry recited the words that clawed his life away from him. "The one with the power to vanquish the Dark Lord approaches... born to those who have thrice defied him, born as the seventh month dies... and the Dark Lord will mark him as his equal, but he will have power the Dark Lord knows not... and either must die at the hand of the other for neither can live while the other survives... the one with the power to vanquish the Dark Lord will be born as the seventh month dies...." In the quiet that followed the prophecy, Harry continued, ØThis prophecy was told by Sybil Trelawney to Albus Dumbledore at the Hog's Head Pub in 1980 during her interview for the Divination teaching position, which had just opened up. It referred to a boy born at the end of July who has the power to defeat Lord Voldemort. The timing fit both me and Neville Longbottom, so our parents, who had been fighting him both, went into hiding, not knowing which one of us was this child. Yet, by coming after me, Voldemort made it so I was the marked child. However, more recent events make me question the prophecy.Æ Severus sat back and stared at the boy. ØYou know?Æ he asked. Memories of his role flashed before him and the guilt made his knees weak. Harry spared him a look. ØAbout your part in the prophecy? Yes. However, itís not important right now. You hold no blame or responsibility. You were young and manipulated,Æ Harry said. Harry watched as Severus seemed to fold into himself. The rest of the room looked confused while Sirius, who knew, spared Snape a look - not of pity, but concern. ØAlthough this does explain his interest, it doesn't explain you,Æ Filius pointed out, indicating the flame that Harry still had dancing in his hand idly. Harry nodded. ØAs I told you before sir, my memory is rather exceptional. I have memories from when I was very young. I remember a few things about my life before my parents died; not a lot, but brief flashes. However, growing up, my memories left me confused. I was beaten for any instances of ØfreakishnessÆ, which I now know were moments of accidental magic, so my memories and their abuse conflicted. Why would I be punished for something that I couldnít control if they knew? I convinced myself my memories were just dreams up until I received my Hogwarts letter. When I returned to our world, I realised my memories were true. ØThe first thing I did was look myself up. My memories lead me to believe I had magical parents, so imagine my surprise when I found out I was right and famous. This discovery lead me to the goblins. I did an inheritance blood test when I went after they realised I knew nothing of our world. The results were fascinatingîI had a blood glamour placed on me, as well as many magical blocks and a core block. There were also some compulsions on me; however, my Heir rings nullified them. Still, not all things can be nullified by rings, so the goblins removed these blocks and compulsions for a price. During this process I gained their respect and they, mine. ØIt was also then discovered that Dumbledore had been stealing from me; he was granted access to my family Vaults as my magical guardian. As I said before, I remember things about my past - which includes the night my parents died. Realising Dumbledore was my guardian made me think Sirius was dead as well as I knew he was innocent. However, I soon found out he was not dead but wrongly in Azkaban. I worked on freeing him and I did so. Dumbledore is the reason Sirius was sent without trial to Azkaban; he was the bonder for the ritual, he knew who the Secret Keeper was. ØI returned home after the removal but my family didnít take kindly to my presence. Their actions against me lead me to returning and staying at the Leaky Cauldron for the remainder of the summer where I studied all of the books I could get my hands on. With my eidetic memory, I was quickly able to learn and, from watching the people, I soon worked out how to blend. The removal of my blocks left me able to feel my magic and sense it on a deeper level - honestly, magic comes easily to me. Even without a wand I can manipulate my magic. Itís a part of me. I can read about a spell once and get it right performing it after a few tries before I move on to the next. I can recall entire books, spells, and even rituals. ØAfter a few days of studying, another thought occurred to me and I started to see things clearly. Dumbledore had been sending money to my relatives, relatives who beat and starved me and, although I cannot prove it, I would bet he knew how I was treated. Itís then when I went to see my healer and had the abuse treated. Why he has done these things I donít know, but he did them. He manipulated my life from the very moment he heard that prophecy. He wanted me to become his prophesized little Light warrior, somebody he could mould and manipulate.Æ Harry sat back then and allowed the flame to diminish, allowing the men to fully digest his entire story. ØThat is quite a tale,Æ Filius said after a long pause. Harry nodded his head to acknowledge him. ØYet you believe me.Æ ØI do,Æ the part goblin admitted. It was a far-fetched tale, but he did believe it. ØWhy you? You clearly donít think the prophecy has much credit. So why you?Æ Lucius said. Harry knew the answer to this probably laid with his soulmate, but he didnít want them knowing about that, so instead he smirked darkly. ØI was born under a lucky moon. A cursed star - maybe I kicked puppies in my last life?Æ He said sarcastically before adding in a normal tone, ØI honestly donít know - yet.Æ ØYou think he set everything up. The prophecy and all that followed?Æ Severus asked, his voice sounding rough. That night he had lost his best friend and he had blamed himself. He had lived for years with that crushing guilt weighing him down. ØI find it odd that he would hold an interview in a pub during a period of civil unrest. A pub which is notorious for its reputation,Æ Harry said. Lucius nodded and Severus looked shocked as he too realised the facts made little sense. How he hadn't noticed before he didnít know. ØYou suspect more foul play,Æ Filius stated. ØI do. Either the entire prophecy is fake or just the delivery.Æ Harryís words made the entire room silent again. ØSo youíve never trusted him? The entire time youíve been in our world?Æ Filius asked eventually. ØNo, and I never will. I am not the little Light warrior he wanted. I will find out what he has planned for me and I will have my revenge.Æ ØYou're Dark,Æ Lucius said. ØMy magic is Dark, but I am not evil. I am not, nor will I ever be, a Dark Lord. I would never murder innocents nor do I care about blood status, creatures, or other frivolous things like that.Æ ØNot a Lord?Æ Sirius asked, surprised Harry felt the need to say that. ØLuna keeps bringing it up,Æ Harry grumbled. ØYou're building a circle,Æ Severus pointed out. ØNo, I have friends.Æ ØFriends who would follow you, trust you, fight for you.Æ ØI would never ask that of them.Æ ØYou wonít have to, they will do it anyway,Æ Lucius said, agreeing with Severus. Harry frowned. ØHow did we get onto this? I have no plans on being the next Dark Lord. I honestly just wanted you to know why Dumbledore is obsessed with me.Æ Filius, who had taken a step back as he listened, laughed slightly. ØSometimes you donít have a choice.Æ ØI trust you will all keep this information to yourselves?Æ Harry asked. ØYes,Æ they all agreed. ØI will also watch out for you around Dumbledore. I have been doing so more diligently this year but now that I know why, I will try harder,Æ Filius said. ØAs will I,Æ Severus said. Harry nodded his head. His privacy wards would make it so they kept their words, but he liked to hear them vow their silence. He trusted them. Chapter End Notes I'm so sorry for the slow update- mt laptop & external hard drive were stolen during a fire drill along with half of my classes. He wasn't lying when he spoke about being able to learn a spell in a few minutes - I've seen him do it. I've never seen somebody so in tune with their own magic; itís as easy as breathing for him.Æ ØHow advanced is he?Æ Filius asked. He knew Harry was beyond his year group and, as a teacher, he knew the boy would be bored in classes. ØHe could probably take his OWLs and get Outstandings, however his knowledge is mainly based on whatever he finds interesting. As a part-goblin, he had been Neutral in the last war; he hadn't liked or believed the values of the Death Eaters, but his nature didnít allow him to take part. He was still confident and cocky, but he could see he had grown up, though maybe his stay in Azkaban had had an influence on him. ØHe really is a remarkable child.Æ ØThe best,Æ Sirius agreed happily, proud of how much Harry had achieved already. ØDo you think he means it when he says he wonít be a Dark Lord?Æ Severus asked. Sirius thought about all he knew. ØI donít know,Æ he said. Severus watched Sirius and noticed how he tensed slightly before he spoke. ØHopefully time will tell,Æ Severus said. He desperately wanted to know what was going on. He knew Severus and Lucius had gotten on when they were at Hogwarts, but he had wrongly had assumed the older man had taken Severus under his wing out of pity rather than anything else. ØYou heard him, heís a freak,Æ he grumbled. I'm sure Harry is just confused and needs a guiding hand to see reason,Æ Albus said. He had worked too long and too hard to let the boy destroy all of his plans. ØHe will soon see the error of his ways.Æ The two youngest Weasleys shared a nod. Ron began gleefully daydreaming about all the things he would one day be able to buy once he had access to the Potter fortune while Ginny imagined all of the attention she would get - every other girl in the wizarding world would envy her, every boy would want her. ØDo you have a plan, Albus?Æ Molly asked. ØWe need to tread carefully. First, we need to get him away from Sirius, we need to destroy their relationship and cut him off from his support. When he's alone, I can appear and be his saviour. He will be indebted to me and easily follow my instructions. We also need to find out just how strong his ringís protections are and how to get around them. Well, all bar one Phineas Nigellus Black who looked almost gleeful. He couldnít wait to tell his Heir this news and see what revenge they take. ~ Meanwhile After leaving the office, Harry wandered aimlessly. However, he soon stopped when he realised just where his subconscious had taken him. Looking around, Harry cast a silencing charm on himself before he disillusioned his body. Stepping through the bathroom door, Harry glanced briefly around and noticed Myrtle wasn't about but he knew that the ghost girl was probably lurking somewhere hoping to jump out or catch a free show. Really, the dead girl was a menace! She had practically stalked Harry after watching him bathe in the prefectís bathroom after his fourth year - she took being a peeping Tom to the next level. Flicking his wrist, Harry placed a repulsion ward around the first floor bathroom doors. The obscure ward was one he had read about over the summer and acted rather like a muggle repelling ward. It wouldnít stop people from entering if they really wanted to, but it would make them not want to come in and instead find another bathroom. Flicking his wrist so his wand was back in his holster, Harry approached the sinks and focused on his Parseltongue. ØOpen,Æ he hissed, looking at the snake carving in the sink. Harry took a step back and watched as the Chamber of Secrets opened. Swinging his legs into the slide, Harry pushed himself off the floor and allowed gravity to work, casting a wandless cushioning charm just before he reached the bottom. Standing up properly once he had landed, Harry looked around as he cleaned his robes. The place was much like he remembered, but this time there wasn't a giant sheddingîmost likely because Aithusia was still locked behind the giant statue of Slytherin. Walking down the hallway he remembered lead to the main area, the place Ginny Weasley had almost died, Harry cast a few Incendios to light the torches around the room. Once the room was bright enough for him to see, Harry looked around in fascination. The stone carvings in the walls and the overall rustic beauty of the Chamber greatly appealed to him; it needed cleaning, but Harry liked the open space. Walking into the middle of the room, Harry closed his eyes and opened his mouth as he tried recalling what the apparition of Tom Riddle had said in his second year. ØSpeak to me, Slytherin, greatest of the Hogwarts four.Æ With his eyes closed, Harry heard the stone creak before he heard movement of soft scales dragging against stone. ØWho calls for me?Æ With his eyes closed, Harry could feel something approaching him. ØHello Great One, Queen of Serpents.Æ ØWho are you, Speaker? You are not the same boy who came last time. Your scent is similar, but different.Æ ØMy name is Harrison Potter-Black.Æ Harry felt something flicker against his face and fought the urge to open his eyes. ØWhere is Tom? Tom used to visit me often - he would bring me food and warm my tunnels with his magic.Æ ØTom is dead,Æ Harry admitted but instantly regretted it when he felt the snakeís body suddenly surround him. ØWho killed him?! I will destroy them. Kill them,Æ Aithusia hissed angrily. ØItís okay, Great Lady, I'm working on bringing him back.Æ The snake seemed to still and Harry felt something flicker against his face again. ØWhy?Æ ØHe is my soulmate.Æ Silence fell and Harry decided to speak, ØIs it possible for me to look at you without harm?Æ ØYes.Æ Harry took a deep breath and opened his eyes. He knew he couldnít die, but he didnít want to be petrified and trapped down here for millennia, just waiting for another Parselmouth to find the secret chamber. As soon as his eyes opened, Harry jumped and let out a breathless squeal before he could stop himself. Aithusia was directly in front of him, the serpentís massive head just inches from his own face, her yellow eyes boring into him. ØYou are a strange child,Æ the snake said, flicking her tongue against Harryís face. She was scenting him, he realised. Harry gulped. ØMay I ask your name, Great One?Æ ØI am Aithusia.Æ ØA beautiful name. May I ask how is it I am looking at you? It is thought that the gaze of a basilisk is deadly.Æ ØIf you thought it deadly why do you risk yourself to look at me?Æ the snake asked. ØI had a suspicion,Æ Harry said honestly. ØItís true the gaze of my kind is deadly, but only when we want it to be so. We have a second set of eyelids which we use to cover the deadly gaze.Æ Harry nodded. It made sense as he doubted Salazar Slytherin interacted with his familiar with his eyes closed, let alone Tom doing so. ØWhy are you here, Little One?Æ the snake asked. ØI wanted to find the Chamber.Æ ØWhy?Æ ØI want a place that I can come and practice my magic without being caught in the school wards. There is another room, but in this one I can be completely alone - I can be myself.Æ ØI see.Æ ØWould you be able to tolerate my presence?Æ Harry asked. ØIf you warm my tunnel and allow me to wander and bring food, I shall allow you to build your nest here.Æ Harry nodded before a thought hit him. ØYou wonít leave the Chamber, right? I donít want to risk harming any students or staffîor, at least, not without my permission,Æ he said. There were one or two people he wouldnít mind harming, but not yet. ØNo, I have never wanted to harm any students. I will do as you instruct me, Little Speaker.Æ ØThen what happened to Myrtle?Æ ØIs that the girl who died last time? Itís my fault. I thought I was alone and lowered my eyelids as I was returning to the Chamber. She was hiding and, when she saw me, she screamed, drawing my attention. There was nothing to do, she was dead before she hit the ground. I didnít see Tom after that.Æ Harry nodded. ØI'm sure he didnít blame you and missed you. And I have no problem with allowing you to roam as long as you stay clear of the school.Æ ØThen please, feel free to join me in the Chamber.Æ ØThank you. I will remember next time to bring you some food.Æ ØI liked it when Tom would bring rabbits. Lots of rabbits.Æ Harry nodded and fought the smile which threatened his face when he thought about the greatest Dark Lord of the century bringing bunnies down for his pet snake. ØI will get the House-Elves to fetch some rabbits,Æ he promised. The snake hissed in pleasure and slowly drew away from Harry. Looking at her, Harry saw she was around 50 feet long and her scales ranged from green to black, the lights making them seem to change colour. She was quite beautiful in a terrifying way and he felt a slither of guilt of having killed her in his last life. ØAithusia, do you perhaps know of a way out? The slide isnít exactly practical,Æ he asked, realising he didnít actually have a way out. While he knew some levitation spells, he had a feeling that Slytherin would have built other exits. ØMy master would just ask for stairs,Æ the snake said. Harry nodded and berated his stupidity. ØThank you, Great One. I will go now, but I promise to return in a few days with food.Æ ~ Leaving the Chamber, Harry realised it was almost curfew. Walking quickly back towards Ravenclaw Tower, he felt approaching magical signatures and quickly found a corner in which to stand and wait. He didnít want a teacher, or worse, Filch, finding them. ØWell then gentlemen, itís been a pleasure,Æ he said before turning. However, before he could get far, they called him back. ØWait,Æ Fred said. He and George had quickly shared a conversation. ØYeah,Æ George agreed. ØHere,Æ they handed the map over to Harry. Harry took it and looked at the twins. They looked happy but he could see the slight disappointment in their eyes. ØIt should belong to you, really.Æ ØYour dad worked on it and, well, if it was us, we would want something he had made.Æ Harry smiled and tucked the map into his bag. ØIíll ask Sirius and Remus about how they made it, maybe we could duplicate it. If not, I donít mind lending it back to you,Æ he promised. The twins smiled and looked relieved. ØLittle bird, I do believe this is the start of a beautiful friendship.Æ Harry was happy to hear the words. ØI do believe you are correct, my good sirs.Æ Realising the time, Harry quickly said his goodbyes and managed to reach Ravenclaw Tower before curfew. Harry smirked when he thought about all of the adventures the twins could get up to with Draco and Neville. Entering the tower, Harry made his way over to Luna, who was sitting alone reading a star chart. ØHello, Little Moon,Æ he said, sitting down next to her. ØHello, my Lord,Æ she said. ØDid you have a productive meeting with the goat and his minions?Æ Harry snorted at her dismissive tone when it came to Dumbledore. ØIt was certainly interesting. I also decided to trust a few more people.Æ Luna hummed and looked thoughtful. ØI think youíve made the right choice,Æ she said before pulling on her hair and looking at Harry with big doe eyes. Harry nodded, relieved. ØI'm glad. You will also be happy to hear that Aithusia will no longer be without company,Æ he said before picking up her long pale hair and running his fingers through it. Slowly, he started to braid small sections before pulling them together in a knot at the back of her head. Looking around, he saw a few eyes on him, so he knew he couldnít use wandless magic. As though sensing his problem, Lunaís hand appeared and in it was a beautiful silver hair pin. Smiling, Harry picked it up and used it to secure the braided knot. While this was going on, their conversation continued to flow. ØYou found her?Æ Luna asked, happy that such a magnificent creature would be allowed some freedom. ØYes and she is beautiful,Æ Harry said smiling. ØWill she be willing to help with our peacock problem?Æ Luna asked, smiling deceptively. Harry laughed lowly. ØHonestly? I forgot to ask, but I think she would be willing.Æ ØGood. Maybe you could write to a certain bug and get her to spread a web. I'm sure she could dig up the truth with the help of your family,Æ Luna said. Harry grinned. He hadn't thought to use Rita, but could see the logic behind the idea. ØLittle Moon, you are a genius.Æ Luna giggled. ØWell, this is the House of wit and intelligence.Æ ØIndeed it is.Æ Harry opened his bag then and pulled out a piece of parchment and a quill. Moving so he could use the desk, Harry thought about what he wanted Rita to write. She really had been doing a fantastic job demeaning Dumbledore - her stories all told the truth but in such a way they really engaged the readers. ~ Rita, I must say I am very impressed with your work so far and commend you for it. You truly are a remarkable reporter. I'm sure you will be pleased to hear that Dumbledore now fears to read the papersîyour articles really are causing him quite some bother. Therefore, I need you to use your talents to spread the truth once again. Gilderoy Lockhart is a fake. I know this to be a fact, but I need you to do a little investigating and produce them for me. I want the world to know the type of man he is: a liar, cheat, and fraud. You will find all his claimed acts of heroics are those of others who he has Obliviated. The man has not done a single thing he has claimed. I'm sure you, like I, feel the public must be made aware of such outrageous news. It might also benefit us both if you were to share your findings with my guardian, Lord Sirius Black. I'm sure he would be very pleased with them and happily share them with both the Wizengamot and the Hogwarts School Board, of which he is a member. Yours, Harrison Potter-Black ~ Smiling, Harry tucked the note into his bag and vowed he would take it to Hedwig first thing the morning. Everyday Harry would wait with baited breath to see if Rita had decided to publish her article on Gilderoy. Her response to his letter had left him giddy for days when he had read it. ~ Heir Potter-Black, I must say, I am pleased to hear you have found my work to be satisfactory. You would not believe the uproar my articles have caused - itís been very entertaining to say the least. Do believe the old man has even tried to have me fired! Therefore, I must thank you for your support in me keeping my job. I never knew the Potters owned such a large share in the Daily Prophet. I would be beyond happy to go and look into Mr. Lockhart for you, I'm sure I will be able to dig up all of the juicy tidbits surrounding his stories. Once I have them, I will gladly share them with the world. It is like you said after all - they deserve to know that such outrageous lies have been fed to them. And I quite agree! I'm sure Lord Black would be very pleased to hear from me. Rita Skeeter ~ December 1992, Hogwarts School of Witchcraft and Wizardry ØWhat has you so excited, Harry? Youíve been like this all week,Æ Neville finally questioned his friend. Harry smirked. ØI just canít wait for the morning owls to arrive.Æ ØOh? Why?Æ Susan asked, looking up from her breakfast. ØBecauîÆ ØI bet you're waiting for a love letter from your boyfriend,Æ spat Ron Weasley. Harry rolled his eyes. The redhead had taken to bombarding him every breakfast, especially by making snide digs at Harryís sexuality ever since he had learnt of it. ØI donít have a boyfriend, Ronald, but with the way you keep bringing one up, I might begin to suspect you want to be mine.Æ Harryís friends sniggered as they watched Ron turn red with anger, his cheeks blotchy with the colour. ØNever! Youíre just a freak!Æ Harryís eyes narrowed. He hated that word, even after all this time. It reminded him of how weak he had once been, how easily he had been controlled. However, before he could voice his fury and, better yet, curse the sad excuse of a wizard, the twins appeared. ØNow, now, Ronnykins, there's no need to blush.Æ ØYeah. I mean, we do understand - little Harrison here is rather lovely.Æ ØQuite a catch.Æ ØApparently the most eligible bachelor under 16!Æ ØBut, like we said, there's no reason to blush around him. I'm sure if you just declared your love, he would be willing to consider you.Æ The twins stopped then and dramatically looked Ron over before exclaiming, ØOr maybe not.Æ ØI do believe we got all the looks in our family, my good sir,Æ Fred simpered to his twin. ØYou might be right, you handsome chap.Æ Harry smirked and nodded at the twins while his friends looked fascinated by the exchange. ØNow boys, both William and Charles are rather pleasant as well,Æ Harry said. His statement made Ron turn even redder. ØShut up!Æ Harry raised an eyebrow. ØTemper, temper, Ronald.Æ The twins laughed and picked Ron up, making his legs flail. ØWeíll just go get rid of the trash, little bird,Æ George said. ØYeah, it seems the House-Elves forgot,Æ Fred agreed. As the older boys left, Ron struggling in their arms, Harry let out a small chuckle. ØWhat was that?Æ Draco demanded, recovering the quickest. ØI do believe the twins are taking the rubbish out,Æ Luna said, making Harry snort louder. ØBut-whatîwhy?Æ the blonde aristocrat spluttered. ØI told you, the twins are friends,Æ Harry said. ØThey are funny,Æ Neville agreed. He had spoken to them quite a bit that year in the common room. They had started to share a few of their ideas about pranks with him, which he had been sharing with Draco. ØBut they're Weasleys!Æ Draco all but wailed. ØEnough, Dray. They are my friends and I think, if you got over your silly little prideful vendetta, you would find yourself liking them as well.Æ Draco looked shocked and a little hurt, making Harry feel bad momentarily. But, before the blond could retaliate, they were interrupted. ØLook at this, Gred!Æ ØI know, Forge. ØThe little bird is defending us!Æ ØHeís such a heroîÆ Øîso dreamy!Æ ØNo wonder Ginny is so in love with him.Æ Harry turned to see the twins standing there and smirked at them. ØI take it the rubbish is gone?Æ ØOh, yes,Æ they said together. ØFancy joining us? Breakfast is far from over,Æ Harry offered. The twins shared a look before jumping to sit down. ØSo, how long have you guys been friends?Æ Blaise asked, reminding Harry that he loved to gossip like an old lady. Harry watched as the twins quickly became accepted, Neville being the easiest because he had been talking to them in the common room all year. Even Draco, after initially sitting there pouting like a toddler, soon jumped into the conversation as they started to exchange notes and ideas on pranks. After breakfast was finished, Harry and his yearmates made their way to Potions. ØSee, they're not so bad,Æ Harry said, poking Draco in the ribs. Draco grumbled what sounded like an agreement before he straighten his uniform. Harry smirked. ØWhat was that? I didnít quite catch it.Æ Draco grumbled again. ØSorry, you will have to speak clearly. I would hate to have to write to aunt Cissa and say her only son has turned into an urchin who cannot speak.Æ ØI said you were right!Æ Draco snapped, making the group snigger. ØSee! That wasn't so hard, was it Dracie?Æ Harry said Draco looked aghast at the nickname and turned pleading eyes on Harry, ØPlease never, ever, call me that again.Æ Harry smirked. ØWhy ever not, Dracie?Æ ØI beg of you, have mercy.Æ The group continued to laugh as they made their way into class, making Harry glad he had decided to risk having friends again. ~ Meanwhile Grimmauld Place, London ØYouíve got an owl, Sirius,Æ Remus said, looking up from the letter to see the name. Sirius looked up and frowned when he didnít recognise the bird. It wasn't Hedwig or one of the Malfoy's eagle owls - it wasn't even a ministry carrier. Flicking his wand, Sirius checked for curses and compulsions and found it to be clean. Even with how tightly warded the house was, he wouldnít become complacent; he had vowed that to himself when he had finally been cleared by the healers. Picking up the letter, he broke the wax seal and quickly read the entire thing. ~ Lord Black, I am writing to you on the suggestion of your son and Heir, Harrison Potter-Black. He suggested I contact you and give you a copy of my findings into the actions of one Mr. Gilderoy Lockhart. You will see I have enclosed them all, so you may view them and share the results among your colleagues. Sincerely, Rita Skeeter ~ Sirius smirked and read through the files, grinning like mad when he got to the last one. Remus, who was watching him, looked amused. ØI take it youíve had some good news?Æ Sirius grinned. ØOh, yes.Æ ØCare to share?Æ ØYou know how weíve been having trouble finding proof that Lockhart is a fraud?Æ Sirius asked. ØYes?Æ ØWell, it seems Harry asked Ms. Skeeter to look into it and sheís found the evidence we need.Æ ØReally? I thought that Dumbledore and his supporters in the ministry were making it hard for the investigators.Æ Sirius grumbled, ØHe was - why he wants the fraud to keep his job, I donít know.Æ ØHe probably fears what the repercussions will be if the public finds out he hired such a fool,Æ Remus said. ØIt shouldnít matter, he has a duty of care and heís failing,Æ Sirius said happily. Remus chuckled. ØMost people would look sad by that.Æ ØI'm not most people.Æ ØAre you going to let Harry or Lucius know?Æ ØYes, Iíll write to them both,Æ Sirius promised. Oh, what a good day this was turning out to be. Chapter End Notes Sorry about the serious delay, hope you're all well! enjoy this chapter as the next wont come for around 2 weeks as I'm going on holiday without my laptop!
