Oh, what a good day this was turning out to be. ~ Hogwarts School of Witchcraft and Wizardry, Late November 1992 Harry was counting down the days until the 21st of December; he wanted to see what would come from Lunaís insight. He knew it would be spectacular thanks to the build up provided by Sirius and Lucius. Looking back, Harry was glad that the two had never teamed up in his last life - together they were a force to be reckoned with. It had started up immediately after what Harry called his coming outîafter he had set Rita onto Lockhart. Harry had waited with bated breath each morning, reading the subtle hints she would drop. It had started with an article on the illegality of memory charms. ~ Ministry Blunders: Memory Charms Not So Charming By Rita Skeeter In a recent investigation, I, Rita Skeeter, journalist extraordinaire and seeker of truth, went undercover to discover the secrets of the Ministryís lack of control when it comes to one of the most delicate branches of mind magicè ~ The article had brought to light how easily mind magic, like the Obliviate charm, can be used and abused and how the ministry had no procedures set in place for the control of the branch. After reading it, Harry had watched in fascination as Lockhart held his composure. The man really was a good actor when the situation called for it - the only sign he was bothered by the article was a slight tightening of his eyes and a subtle stiffening of his back. Next came an article on Azkaban, the conditions, and a proposal for a tightening of sentences. ~ Azkaban: The Truth - Dementors or Demons? By Rita Skeeter Many of us know the name, but how many can say they know the truth about Azkaban? I, Rita Skeeter, journalist extraordinaire and seeker of truth, decided it was time to really expose the deep dark secrets of the hellish islandè ~ That article had made the man blanche and Harry snort. True, the prison was nothing to joke about and thinking of Sirius there made him shiver, but the manís reaction was gold. Severus also quickly caught on to the ongoing situation after a particularly entertaining article and cornered Harry after a Potions lesson. ~ Truth and Lies By Rita Skeeter In a recent top secret investigation, I, Rita Skeeter, journalist extraordinaire and seeker of truth, have discovered something so shocking that I tremble as I write. But, before I say more, I must wait and verify more factsînever let it be said that I spread lies! However, I can say that my recent investigation involves none other than our beloved hero and winner of the Witches Weekly most charming smile award: Gilderoy Lockhartè ~ ØMr. Potter, if you could please stay behind.Æ Harry paused from packing up his things and nodded. ØYes, professor.Æ Turning to see Hannah and Susan, he sent them both a smile. ØI will catch up to you in Charms,Æ he promised. After they left, Harry resumed tidying his things. ØIs everything okay, professor?Æ Severus snorted at the innocent tone. ØIndeed. I simply wished to inquire as to what you have on Rita Skeeter - the woman is notorious for her articles, yet she seems to be firmly in your pocket.Æ Harry smirked and stopped what he was doing. ØI havenít a clue what you mean.Æ Severus allowed a single eyebrow to rise. ØReally?Æ ØWell, I may have found out about a... small condition she has.Æ Severus smirked. ØI see. I had wondered why she decided to target Dumbledore and now that fool Lockhart.Æ ØSheís a very morally conscious woman,Æ Harry said, nodding solemnly before smirking. The innocence of his expression was quickly turning into mischief. ØI have no doubt,Æ Severus deadpanned. ØWas that everything, sir?Æ ØNot quite. I would like to know what it is Lockhart is hidingîI know you know.Æ Harry tilted his head as he considered the man in front of him. ØGilderoy Lockhart is a fool, but one you donít need to worry about for much longer.Æ He didnít want to spoil Ritaís or Lucius and Siriusí fun too early by revealing the truth. Severus hid his frustration at the vague answer and instead asked, ØHow did you manage to avoid my snake pit? You're sneakier and more cunning than anybody I have ever met - and I served the Dark Lord once.Æ Harry felt his smirk grow. ØCould you imagine it? The famed Boy-Who-Lived a Slytherin? No, the hat agreed with me, I'm better off in Ravenclaw.Æ Severus considered the boy in front of him. ØYou made the hat put you there, didnít you?Æ Harry smiled brightly. ØI can neither confirm nor deny.Æ Severus snorted, flicking his wand so a note appeared. He passed it to Harry. ØGet out of here, brat,Æ he said fondly. Harry smirked as he took the note. Snapping his fingers, the remainders of his potions supplies were instantly packed up. Severus shook his head at the casual display of power and control. How he had missed it to begin with he would never know. The next morning was a Saturday and Harry finally got the confirmation he needed when Siriusí familiar brown owl landed in front of him during breakfast. ~ Pup, It would seem that your relationship with that bug Skeeter has really paid off. She somehow managed to get around all of the roadblocks Dumbledore and his flock set up and has found quite a bit of evidence condemning Lockhart. I must say, I am very impressed with her. I've sent a copy of her files to both Lucius and Amelia Bones, so hopefully the DMLE will be able to investigate and verify Skeeterís findings. With any luck, you wonít have to suffer with the fool for much longer. Me and Lucius, along with Severus, are having lunch tomorrow, so we will play our attack. Remember, donít do anything to draw the old cootís attention and look after yourself! Love, Pads ~ Satisfied that Lockhart would be dealt with soon, Harry turned his mind to his magi. Ever since opening the Chamber, he had visited Aithusia only twice; getting free time was hard for him, so he wasn't stretching and using a lot of magic. Ever since the block had been removed in his last life, Harry had found his magic was easier to react and call upon. However, at times it appeared almost sentientîit would react to simple things, passing thoughts, or ideas. Keeping his power under wraps was definitely becoming harder - his classes required little in the ways of mental stimulation or magical power, so he had a lot of power built up and masked behind his occlumency shields. ØYou know my Lord, I'm sure Professor Flitwick would be happy to give you some extra Charms work,Æ Luna said seemingly out of nowhere. Harry tilted his head in consideration. Even the extra work wouldnít do much for his magic build up. ØThank you Little Moon, but I think I will be okay.Æ ØYou should consider it my Lord, it could be like your remedial potions.Æ ØI see,Æ he said, surprised she had Seen something so specific about his old life. Usually Luna was vague in the visions she Saw and mostly relied more on feelings and intuition. Harry was still secretly shocked at how accepting Luna was being. He hadn't mentioned Aithusia to his friends yet as he hadn't yet worked with them enough to introduce his darker ideas and wanted to build closer and stronger relationships before he started revealing more truths, but Luna seemed to be the exception. He recognised that his friends were children and, although more mature than Ron and Hermione had been at that age, they were still young and nave. Even his Slytherin friends, who would undoubtedly support his Darker tendencies, were still only children. Therefore, using Charms lessons would be a great excuse to go and visit the great snake and work his magic. With this in mind, Harry excused himself and went to find the part goblin. After knocking on his office door, Harry was quickly called in. ØGood morning, Harrison,Æ Flitwick greeted happily. He felt like he saw the boy much more clearly ever since the meeting in his office. ØGood morning, sir.Æ ØWhat can I do for you today?Æ ØI was wondering, sir, if you wouldnít mind being my excuse with my friends? I need time and space to work on using up and stretching my magic. I have somewhere to go where I am safe and can be alone, but I simply donít have the time or reason to leave my friends. I donít what them involved yet - they're still children, still innocent,Æ Harry said. The words about the innocence of children made Filius both profoundly sad and simultaneously angry. The fact that Harry couldnít count himself among them made him want to hex Dumbledore. ØI donít see why not. What were you thinking?Æ ØI was planning on saying I had Advanced Charms; it shouldnít be hard to pull off.Æ He shrugged. Filius smirked. Harry was once again at the top of his year by a large margin. ØIf they ask, I will back your story up.Æ Harry smiled. ØThank you, sir.Æ ØHow have you been anyway, Harrison?Æ ØI'm good, thank you. Sirius wrote to me today saying they had found some evidence of Lockhart being a fraud, so hopefully we wonít have to deal with him for much longer. And the headmaster has left me alone since our meeting.Æ ØThatís great. Dumbledore has been steering clear of me this week, he no longer asks for near daily updates.Æ Harry nodded and after a few more minutes of idle chat left his Head of House, happy he now had an excuse for disappearing. ~ Malfoy Manor Wiltshire, Sunday November 30th, 1992 Sirius practically bounded through the floo connection to Malfoy Manor on Sunday. He had owled the files over to Lucius when he had received them and he wanted to know what the blond thought of them. Barely holding his composure, Remus followed at a more sedate pace, arriving just in time to watch Sirius bound off through the doors in search of Lucius. He was glad at times like this that Harry was already mentally an adult as Sirius really could be a child. Using his nose, Remus tracked Sirius through the manor into the informal sitting room. Entering he saw Lucius and Severus sitting in large lounge chairs while Sirius was bouncing on the spot in front of them. ØDid you inject him with sugar?Æ Severus said, eyeing Sirius with caution as if he was contagious or would explode at any moment. Remus chuckled. ØNo, he's been like this since I woke up.Æ Sirius pouted for a moment before sitting down on a lounger. ØHave you read them?Æ Severus looked intrigued and turned to the blond aristocrat and noted his smug expression. ØYes, I have and I must say I never knew Skeeter could be so useful.Æ ØWhat has she done - other than her articles, I assume?Æ Severus asked. ØHarry put her on the task of investigating Lockhartís success,Æ Sirius said, grinning madly. ØOh?Æ Severus said. He knew the man was a fraud but he had no evidence other than his obvious incompetence and lack of magical and mental skill. ØShe sent her files to me a few days ago and to say she found gold would be putting it lightly.Æ Severus snorted. ØSo the fool will be gone soon?Æ ØHopefully. I doubt Dumbledore can keep this hidden. I sent a copy of the files to Amelia Bones as well.Æ Severus smirked at Sirius. ØSmart, Black. I heard rumours he was trying to stop the ministry investigation?Æ ØIndeed. However, now itís been done for them; all they have to do is check the facts,Æ Lucius said, his smooth voice injected with amusement. ØIt shouldnít take them more than a few weeks to verify them - what I read was pretty condemning,Æ Remus said. ØWhat she has printed so far hints as much,Æ Severus agreed. ØOh yes, her articles have been great!Æ Sirius said, ØThe one about Azkaban was pretty accurate.Æ ØHopefully that buffoon will find out for himself,Æ Severus said, not wanting to linger on the subject as he wasn't sure if Sirius would be comfortable. ØI must admit I'm disappointed in myself for never seeing her full potential. Harrison really did find a great resource in her,Æ Lucius said. ØIndeed,Æ Severus agreed. ØHas the ministry started their investigations?Æ Remus asked. ØMy sources in the DMLE confirmed they had sent agents out to look into the claims this morning.Æ Sirius snorted and grinned. ØSomehow I'm not surprised you have sources in the DMLE.Æ Lucius allowed one perfectly shaped eyebrow to rise. ØI have people everywhere,Æ he said before continuing. ØMadame Bones is overseeing the investigation personally because of all the roadblocks Dumbledore put in place.Æ Severus smirked. ØSo his meddling has drawn her eye.Æ ØWell, it is rather obvious if you know where to look. I gathered from our meeting in the headmasterís office that she is no longer enamoured with the beloved leader of the Light.Æ ØHarry is close with her niece, Susan. He spent a few days with them over the holidays - they even went to the beach. She likes Harry and sheís always been more skeptical of Dumbledore from what I remember.Æ ØSuch powerful and influential friends he has... it really does make you wonder if he planned it,Æ Lucius said, more to himself than anyone in particular. What he saw of the boy reminded him of the stories his father would tell him about their Lordís early days. Sirius, however, just smiled. He knew Harry didnít pick his friends for that reason, but he did sometimes wonder if he subconsciously allowed what he knew of their families to influence his decision when befriending them. Neville was influential because of his family and status, Susan had her aunt, Hannahís family, although not as well known, were still respected among the Light sects, Lunaís father owned the Quibbler, Draco was a Malfoy, Pansyís family was almost as respected by the Dark families as the Malfoy's, and the Zabini family was not only rich but a recognised and respected Neutral familyîHarry had even recently mentioned befriending the Weasley twins. After that the group slowly fell into idle chitchat as Narcissa joined them and lunch was served. Before leaving, Lucius arranged to meet up with Sirius later in the week to discuss the Lockhart situation in detail; they wanted to work together with the DMLE and the Hogwarts Board to bring the man, and hopefully Dumbledore, down. ~ 21th December 1992 The latest article by Rita had really made an impact on Lockhart and Harry enjoyed watching him as the man struggled to respond to the rising number of questions being raised. He feared the man would flee before his plan had a chance to take effect - he knew in his last life the man had tried to run before he and Ron had forced him into the Chamber to rescue Ginny. Harry knew that now practically nobody attended Defence Against the Dark Arts lessons. Instead, most were choosing to self-study and several students had also opted to do the same for History of Magic. Yet, it hadn't been until Lucius and Sirius had come into school that Lockhart had really panicked. ~ Earlier in The Week Harry and his friends were sitting at the Gryffindor table for breakfast - they had slowly started to include the lion House in their daily rotation ever since the twins had come on board and often joined them at meal times to plan pranks with Neville and Draco. This new development between the four made Harry smirk internally as the foursome honestly thrived together. The twins were better at pulling off the pranks, having had years of experience, whereas Neville was better as their informant as he was always overlooked or underestimated and Draco was the mastermind behind most of them. Somehow, he had hidden this talent in Harryís last life, but now he was letting it flourish. He was full of ideas, it just took the help of the twins to bring them to life. They reminded Harry of the Marauders but, unlike his father and his friends, they were not malicious. Sure, they did tend to target Ron and Ginny, but they never skirted close to bullying. Sitting together at the table gave Harry the perfect view when Sirius walked in, flanked by Lucius and who he guessed were the other members of the School Board. Harry noticed how the room quickly silenced as the imposing looking group entered. All of the members were dressed in robes - not formal, but certainly high quality. Sirius quickly scanned the crowd of students and spotted Harry and his friends. Catching his eye, he winked at him and smiled when he saw Harry shake his head in amusement. ØWhat is the meaning of this?Æ Dumbledore's voice boomed out, tearing almost everybodyís attention from the intruding witches and wizards. He stood tall from his chair in the centre of the room and stared at the group. Harry, however, focused not on his words, but his magic. It was swirling unnoticed by all but him, angry and malicious. ØHeadmaster, as you seem to have not received any of our numerous owls and appear to never be in your office when we attempt to floo you, we, the school governors, felt it necessary to come in person,Æ Lucius said, coming to the front of the group and letting his cool aristocratic voice ring over the crowd, hushing the growing whispers. ØYou have no business forcing these witches and wizards to follow you, Mr. Malfoy,Æ Dumbledore said, the rude tone showing his displeasure. Lucius didnít respond. Instead, an older looking woman stepped forward next to the Lord. Harry didnít recognise her from either this life or his last, but he recognised some of her features and wondered if she was related to Justin Smithís family. ØActually Albus, it was Lord Black who brought our attention to the matter we wish to discuss with you. From this it was our decision as a body to hold a full meeting. The fact you would accuse us of being so weak willed that we could be manipulated is offensive, and to do it so publicly not only insults Lord Malfoy but also us.Æ Harry wanted to laugh as he knew just how easily they could be manipulated but instead nodded his head. He could have clapped when he saw a few students looking disgruntledly towards the headmaster and guessed they were related to the members Harry didnít know. ØI apologise. That was not my intention and, like you say, this is not the place for such a conversation,Æ the headmaster said, his voice now soft and genial, the tone not matching the anger his magic was pulsating with. After they left the Great Hall broke out in whispers and Harry held back a smirk when he noticed most eyes were on him and Draco. ØLittle Bird, you wouldnít happen to know what's going on would you?Æ Fred asked, seeing Harryís expression. ØYes, you have a look which just screams hidden knowledgeîÆ ØAnd we should know, we have often had the same expression.Æ Harry allowed himself to smirk then and allowed his eyes to fall on Lockhart, who, as though sensing his gaze, looked up and allowed his blue eyes to lock with Harryís own. ØI may have an idea,Æ he said. The twins followed his gaze and nodded. ØWe see,Æ they said. They knew that Harry was behind the articles by Rita Skeeter and they had caught on quickly. ØMy Lord, I do believe that rabbit and peacock go very well together,Æ Luna said, staring at Lockhart with large eyes. Harry tilted his head. ØAre you sure? That is a rather gruesome end result,Æ Harry asked. He knew Lockhart needed to pay, but he was having doubts about giving him to Aithusia - being eaten by a basilisk was a bit extreme and the man hadn't targeted Harry or his friends personally. ØItís not the 21st yet,Æ Luna said as though it explained everythingîthen again, Harry supposed it did. Harry hummed and wondered what the man was going to do to make Harry feed him to Aithusia. He wasn't too worried as he knew Luna wouldnít let him or somebody he loved to get hurt, but he was curious as to what the man would try. ØDoes anybody have any idea what theyíre talking about?Æ Pansy asked, looking between the two with open concern. ØNot a clue,Æ Draco said. ØBarmy, the pair of them,Æ the twins added, Øbut we love them anyway.Æ Luna smiled at them and nodded, happily going back to her breakfast. ~ Meanwhile The Headmasterís Office ØNow, what can I do for you?Æ the headmaster asked, looking around the room and smiling. Sirius held back his snort - the tone was so humble and deceiving. ØHeadmaster, have you not been reading the owls we have sent you?Æ Lucius asked straightaway. Albus bit back his snarl at the tone and hoped to look benign sitting behind his desk. ØI'm sorry to say that sometimes things get on top of me. Your owls must have built up in the pile of mail I have yet to get through. As I'm sure you're aware, I've recently been under some strain.Æ Sirius once again struggled to remain composed. ØI'm sure. I gather you simply missed our floo calls as well then?Æ ØYes, my boy. Being headmaster I am often out of my office dealing with the children and other issues.Æ Sirius shook his head. ØUndoubtedly, however, as headmaster, itís your duty to run the school and to provide your students with a happy, safe environment and, most importantly, a quality education. You have staff and, as deputy, it is Professor McGonagallís duty to deal with students and any behavioural concerns, as well as their Head of Houses. This makes me wonder how seriously you're taking your duty.Æ Albus bristled at the words. How dare Sirius Black of all people tell him how to run his school. ØA simple oversight, my boy.Æ ØIndeed,Æ Lucius said. ØNow, what is the matter that is so important that you felt the need to intrude on our breakfast?Æ ØIntrude? You make it sound like weíre not welcome,Æ Lilian Freemont said. Sirius remembered her family from one of Luciusí speeches - they were Light-Neutral. He vaguely recognised her as being from Ravenclaw and a few years older than him. ØA simple slip of the tongue, my dear,Æ Albus said, exasperated. Never before would they have questioned him and it was leaving him flustered. ØI'm sure youíve read about the investigations into your current Defence teacher?Æ Lucius asked. Albus wanted to growl. ØYes, well, if we believed everything written then we wouldnít know who to trust,Æ he said. He had tried to bury the foolís incompetence, but even he could only do so much. If it wasn't for the fact he owed the man for saving his reputation he would have left him to hang. It was five years ago when a busy body reporter had found out about his connection to Gellert and their relationship; he would have been ruined until Lockhart had literally stumbled in on their meeting. When he saw what was happening, Lockhart had Obliviated the man and, from that point on, he had Albus in his backpocket. As such a public figure, Albus hadn't wanted to risk getting rid of him, but now he was questioning his actions. ØI'm sure,Æ Lucius said, Øhowever, you undoubtedly also know that the ministry has looked into Mr. Lockhartís claimed feats of magic and heroism and they have found nothing. He is a fraud and the ministry is, as we speak, finalising the order for his arrest. It will be done in a matter of days, if not sooner.Æ Albus bristled internally. ØI see. As awful as this is, I am sensing that some of you hold me responsible for this?Æ he asked, making sure to sound tired and sad. ØYou have refused our input when picking teachers. It is the Boardís job to decide who is hired; however, since becoming the headmaster you have yet to hold onto a teacher for more than a year.Æ ØYes, wellîÆ ØAnd what's more, the man is clearly incompetent. Why, a second year has been more successful tutoring students in Defence these last few months,Æ Lucius finished with. Albus stared at the man for a moment, barely holding back his contempt. ØAs the headmaster, I feel that I should hire my staff. I know the school and the students the best; however, I will admit that perhaps I have made a mistake with Mr. Lockhart.Æ Sirius bit his lip to stop himself laughing. Perhaps? There was no perhaps about the situation. ØAs a matter of precaution, I have a signed letter of approval from Madam Bones and the Minister himself for you to lock the Hogwarts wards, stopping Mr. Lockhart from leaving the school grounds,Æ Lucius said, pulling out the mentioned letter and passing it over to Albus. Quickly reading the letter, Albus hid his anger and carefully concealed how his hands shook with rage. ØI see. However, I donít agree that such drastic action needs to be taken.Æ He hoped his grave voice would sway the crowd and he saw a few members falter slightly. Sirius, however, wasn't moved and quickly spoke up, ØItís just a precaution and as a staff member, he should not be leaving before the investigation is completed anyway. If the investigation turns up with nothing then he will be apologised to and released. Why, with how worried you look, itís almost as though you believe he is guilty.Æ Closing his eyes briefly to hide his anger, Albus bit back another snarl. ØI have the utmost faith in my staff,Æ he said eventually. ØThen letís get to it, some of us have important things to be doing,Æ Atticus Vane said. Although Neutral, the man was a former Slytherin and throughout the meeting he had observed the headmaster, and he had not liked what he had seen. Knowing he was unable to get out of it, Albus stood and gathered the ward book for the school. As headmaster, he had primary control of the wards; however, McGonagall could take the reigns as deputy and, in the cases of emergency, the Heads of Houses could make a few edits. Pushing his magic into the book, Albus allowed the Board to witness him binding Gilderoy. After it was done the group left, many not even bothering with polite farewells. Their dismissal of him had Albus trembling in rage; he would have to carefully plan how to get the man out now - he didnít trust him not to spill all he knew in a desperate effort to save himself. Lucius and Sirius, however, detoured back to the Great Hall in the hopes of seeing their sons and heirs. ~ Meanwhile The Great Hall, Hogwarts School of Witchcraft and Wizardry Harry, feeling the schoolís wards flicker, allowed a smirk to fall into place. When Sirius had written to him about the plans to bind Lockhart until he was arrested, his final plans had fallen into place. ØYou know, you look rather terrifying when you do that,Æ Blaise said, observing Harryís face. ØTerrifying? Me? I'm an angel,Æ Harry said, allowing his eyes to widen and an expression of innocence to take over. The group all paused to look at him. ØMerlin, you're good at that,Æ Draco said. ØYeah, do you teach lessons?Æ the twins asked. ØBecause, if you do sign us up,Æ Fred continued. ØYeah, Minnie would never punish us if we could look like that!Æ George finished. Harry snorted and allowed his face to go back to his normal expression. ØItís a gift,Æ he deadpanned. Hearing the doors open, Harry looked up to see Sirius and Lucius enter. Smiling, Harry stood and walked to his father figure. ØConstructive meeting?Æ he asked. ØVery,Æ Sirius said, pulling Harry into a sideways hug. Smiling at the action, Harry allowed the contact. ØI felt the wards change. I take it the peacockís bound?Æ he whispered. Sirius nodded and was slightly disturbed by the look that passed over Harryís face. ØAm I going to need an alibi again?Æ he asked, only half joking. Harry snorted. ØPlease, I'm not an amateur.Æ Lucius, who had caught the last of their conversation, looked intrigued. Seeing this, Harry sent the man a nod. ØOver Christmas I think we could do with meeting up, there are things you need to know.Æ Lucius nodded. ØI look forward to it.Æ The rest of Harryís friends took that moment to join them. Sirius was happy to meet the twins and share what he remembered about making the map. As the adults left, Harry wasn't the only one barely holding back laughter. The awestruck expressions on the two Weasleys faces had drawn a crowd which was shaking to remain emotionless. ØHarry, you're related to the second coming of Merlin,Æ Fred said with awe on his face. Nodding vehemently, George stood next to his twin with a matching expression. With that Harry lost it, laughing until his eyes watered. ~ Meanwhile Gilderoy Lockhart was panicking. He was caught, trapped, and that senile old man could do nothing to help him. How it had come to this he didnít know. He was beloved by all, a hero, cherished and adored. Actually, that was a lie, he knew exactly who to blame. He knew whoís fault this was and, if he had his way, he would get his revenge. Harry Potter would rue the day he challenged the great Gilderoy Lockhart. Chapter End Notes Please not this chapter is NOT Beta'd but I felt bad for keeping you all waiting for so long! So there are mistakes, I don't think there are any drastic ones but still just a warning to you all. Updated 21/07/17 it's now been beta'd so big Thank you Miisticalwrites, the next chapter should be up on Sunday ~ Annie Chapter 27: Foolish plans of apeacock Chapter Notes See the end of the chapter for notes Previously Gilderoy Lockhart was panicking. He was caught, trapped, and that senile old man could do nothing to help him. How it had come to this he didnít know. He was beloved by all, a hero, cherished and adored. Actually, that was a lie, he knew exactly who to blame. He knew whoís fault this was and, if he had his way, he would get his revenge. Harry Potter would rue the day he challenged the great Gilderoy Lockhart. ~ 21th December 1992 Harry could practically feel the excitement pouring off him; it wasnít just the actual event, but the knowing why that made him giddy. He hated Lockhart and, yes, he was a fraud, but even with his new, albeit looser, morals he just couldnít see why he would actually feed the man to Aithusia. He had enjoyed the manís obvious distress brought on by being bound in the wards for the last few daysîthat added to his reactions to Ritaís articles had actually made his repeated second year slightly enjoyable. How he was going to cope with another five years he wasn't sure - perhaps he should consider skipping a few? ØWhat's got you so happy, Harry?Æ Neville asked. They were in Herbology and Harry had paired with the blond Gryffindor for the day. ØI'm not that happy.Æ Harry tried to play it off. The fact he wasn't occluding his emotions was showing just how comfortable he was with the young lion. ØOh, I thought it was because we only have today until we break up for Yule.Æ Harry blinked in shock when he realised Neville was correct; he had honestly forgotten about that in his build-up to today. He had owl ordered his gifts weeks ago but his mind had been completely focused on just how and why Gilderoy Lockhart would earn his wrath. ØWell, Yule is always something to be excited about,Æ Harry said. This Yule would bring him the best gift: he would be one step closer to having Tom back. ØIt will be your first one with Sirius and Remus,Æ Neville commented. Harry nodded and hoped that with everything going on the pair hadn't forgotten to get everything he would require for Tomís ritual. ØI know. It will be my first proper holiday with them - my birthday doesn't count.Æ Neville smiled. ØI'm glad that you're happy, Harry. You deserve it.Æ Harry sent the Gryffindor a look. ØSo do you, Nev. Howís your gran been treating you? And your uncle Algie?Æ Neville shrugged a shoulder. ØThey're nicer to me now that Iím here and have friends. I swear gran almost cried when she saw my grades last year - not a single T, even in Potions.Æ ØThatís good. You know you could always come and spend a few days with me over the holidays, I'm sure Sirius and Remus would love having you. They could even teach you a few things!Æ Nevilleís smile widened. ØI could write to gran and ask before we go to the feast.Æ Harry nodded. ØDo it.Æ ØThanks Harry, you really are the best friend I could have ever had. Without you I would probably be a friendless Hufflepuff.Æ Harry shook his head. ØI donít think there is such a thing as a friendless Hufflepuff but even without me you would have been a lion. You're brave Neville and you will be a great wizard. You donít need me for that.Æ Neville smiled bashfully making Harry smirk. ØNow enough of this, show me how I'm supposed to make these things stop crying and trying to bite me,Æ Harry said, indicating to the baby mandrakes. Neville nodded and fell into his instructor role, clearly showing his passion and skill with plants. ~ As the two second years approached the leaving feast, having detoured so Neville could send his letter, Harry was surprised when Luna didnít attach herself to them. The small blonde was always waiting for them and would usually be outside their classes (how she managed to get to theirs from her own he didnít know), but on the rare occasion she didnít she would always find them by the time they reached the Great Hall. Sitting at the Ravenclaw table, Harry looked around before pulling out the map. He had shown it to the group when the twins had given it to him so he didnít feel the need to hide it as they covered him from the eyes of the rest of the hall. ØI solemnly swear that I'm up to no good.Æ As the map came alive Harry scanned it until his eyes fell onto the name he was searching for. ØWhat's she doing on her own on the seventh floor?Æ Draco asked, looking over Harryís shoulder to see the map. Harry felt his eyes narrow. ØI donít know. Why donít you guys wait here for us though? Iíll be back,Æ he said, standing to leave. ØWeíll come with you,Æ Hannah offered. Harry, however, knew that whatever he found would be the reason he fed Lockhart to Aithusia and knew they werenít ready for that. ØNo, I donít want you guys to miss the feast. Save us something good, though!Æ The group shared a look but eventually conceded. Picking up the map, Harry wasn't surprised when he saw Lockhart not far from Luna. With a growl he closed the map and made his way to them. ØMischief managed.Æ ~ Entering the room that he had seen Luna in, Harry felt his magic strain to lash out. Sitting bound to a chair with bleary eyes was his little moon. A hiss escaped him, whether it was English or Parseltongue he didnít know. ØLuna!Æ Her unfocused gaze strayed towards him and a half smile appeared on her face. ØHello, my Lord.Æ Harry walked forward and, with a flick of his wrist, unbound her from the chair. He couldnít see anything wrong with her, but he knew with magic sometimes the effects were hard to find. ØAre you okay, little moon?Æ ØI feel a bit dizzy, my Lord. Did you know that you did that without a wand? Also my head hurts, itís very dark in here.Æ Harry felt his anger rise. ØI know, Luna. Lumos,Æ he said. Lifting his wand, he allowed the spell to light up the large, empty room. ØHarry Potter, I knew you would come if I took your little girlfriend,Æ came a voice from behind him. Harry allowed his occlumency shields to rise and killed off any emotion he might have felt. ØLockhart,Æ he greeted tonelessly. Gilderoy felt a shiver race down his spine at the voice of the child in front of him, it held no emotion whatsoever. That alongside the glowing green eyes and unnatural stillness of the boy made him question himself for a moment. However, he quickly shook off his fears - he was Gilderoy Lockhart after all. ØYou should have never chosen to go against me, you mere child!Æ Harry felt his magic crackle and wondered if the fool was smart enough to back down, but he didnít hold much faith in the thought. ØI may not have done all of the things I have claimed but I am still a wizard of great skill, skills you could never dream of reaching. You're just a school boy with a pretty face and fame you donít deserve. You're nothing compared to me, Harry Potter. Nothing.Æ Gilderoy pulled out his wand in a flourish and pointed it at the boy. Harry took a moment to check his shields before answering, his voice conversational but emotionless, like he was talking about the weather. ØIf you tell me exactly what you wished to accomplish here I promise you will not find a painful end. An end yes, but it will be painless.Æ Lockhart felt his mouth go dry at the boyís reaction. There was no fear, no pleading, it was like he was void of all humanity. He hadn't even glanced at the wand pointed at him. ØYou think you can threaten me? Admit it Harry, youíve lost!Æ Harry tilted his head for a moment. ØLost?Æ he asked. Gilderoy felt a moment of satisfaction, thinking he had won, before it quietly died. Harry allowed his magic to slowly fill the room, the darkness seeping and growing. ØYou're a foolish man. You should have targeted me - yes, I would have retaliated, but I would have been swift. However, instead you chose to target somebody I love.Æ Harry heard Luna let out a small giggle and turned to her to find that her eyes were now on him and she was smiling. She wasn't bothered by the darkness he was radiating nor the power that was now making the air around him vibrate. Nodding to her, he turned back to face Lockhart who now stood dumb in the middle of the room, his wand limp in his hand and eyes starting to dart around in panic. ØMy friends, my familyè you donít get to touch them. Nobody gets to touch them,Æ Harry said, his voice quiet and chilling yet somehow managing to fill the room. In his now growing panic Lockhart sent a stunner at Harry, but the spell died before it reached him, sizzling out with his lack of power and overwhelming fear. ØS-s-stupify!Æ ØI shall give you one last chance. Tell me what you had planned!Æ Harry demanded, his voice filled with power. Realising he had made a mistake, Gilderoy turned and tried to run. But before he had taken a step, he was frozen. ØI asked you a question, professor. It would be rude of you to run away without answering me.Æ Harry turned his palm and watched as the frozen man mimicked his movements until he was on his knees in front of him, their eyes almost level. ØIf you donít wish to tell me professor, I think I shall find the information for myself.Æ Harry knew he wasn't the best legilimens around. He had studied occlumency almost religiously but his study of breaking into peopleís minds was limited. However he had read all of the theories behind it and, in the present circumstances, he didnít fear leaving lasting damage like he would have had he been attempting the mind magic on somebody else. ØLegilimens.Æ Harry met a surprisingly strong resistance before his magic flared and crushed the barrier, bringing a shocked gasp from the man bound before him. Suddenly images swarmed him: pieces of broken conversations, dreams of a child, actions of a man, lost thoughts and hidden desires. Overwhelmed, Harry felt his body tremble slightly as he focused on slowing the information, on viewing it and searching through it for what he wanted until something finally caught his attention. ~ The Hagís Lair, Knockturn Alley, ~5 years ago Harry looked around the memory, viewing it from Lockhartís perspective. The pub, which he recognised from his own wanderings in the dark alley, was hardly visible in the torrential rain, the few street lamps blown out by the harsh winds. Walking in, Harry noticed Lockhart was afraid of the sparse occupants of the old pub and soon found himself settled into a corner with a warm mulled cider for comfort while he waited out the storm. Harry, from the memories he had stolen from the manís mind, knew Lockhart needed to pick up a book from a nearby shop - that was the reason he was seeking shelter and waiting. Soon, however, the reason why Harry had his interest piqued walked in. Harry felt the memory of Lockhart look up, shocked when he recognised the old man under the dark cloak. The rest of the rooms occupants didnít look up, used to looking the other way, so Dumbledore's entrance was only noted by Lockhart. Dumbledore was dressed in a way Harry had never seen before. Gone were the bright and garish robes and in their place was a dark cloak, the colour of which he couldnít make out in the poor lighting. Harry watched as the professor looked around with shrewd eyes before walking towards the stairs with purpose. Harry felt Lockhartís interest spike as he rose to follow the man; he didnít fear being found as everybody knew the headmaster was the beacon of Light in the magic world. Following the old man, Harry noted how Lockhart's excitement grew, the fool thinking he would find out some juicy gossip or perhaps some important secrets. Letting Dumbledore enter the room, Harry noticed how it took Lockhart four fumbled attempts to cast an eavesdropper charm and overhear the conversation going on inside. ØThe world needs to know what you did, Albus! Who you were!Æ ØThey were the mistakes of a child. I am a different man now, a better man.Æ ØA man who has never paid for his crimes!Æ ØWould you really destroy all I have worked for?Æ ØWhen it is built on lies and deceit? On the suffering of others? Yes!Æ Lockhart had heard enough and with a flourish, he opened the door, his wand raised. ØObliviate!Æ he shouted, pointing the wand at the surprisingly young woman. Harry was fascinated as he watched the memories of her discoveries disappear as they were removed with the spell and her eyes go glassy. He didnít recognise her from this life or his last, but he studied her features and vowed to himself he would find her and find out what it was she knew. After a while he turned his attention to the headmaster, who had turned and drew his wand when the door opened. ØWhat is the meaning of this?Æ he asked Lockhart. ØA debt, headmaster,Æ he said almost gleefully. Harry picked up on his thoughts of a mutually beneficial agreement. Harry watched as the headmaster looked between the woman and Lockhart. ØA debt?Æ he asked. The memory faded then as Harry lost interest in the terms. However, the two came up with an Unbreakable Vow and agreed to help one another. Pulling out of the memory, Harry allowed himself to filter through the manís thoughts once again, only catching glimpses of them but not delving in fully like he had just previously. Another thought quickly caught his eye, but unlike before, it wasn't such a mystery as he was in it. After a few moments Harry felt his smile become almost feral as he realised he had found what he was looking for. ~ The Great Hall, Hogwarts School of Witchcraft and Wizardry Harry, once again, found himself viewing the memory from Lockhart's perspective. This time, however, he knew exactly where he was and even when he was as he was watching himself. Viewing yourself was quite a disconcerting experience; he was actually rather fascinated seeing how he walked and interacted. Looking at the memory of himself, Harry realised just how different he was from the old Harry Potterînot just his looks, but his mannerisms and his confidence: Gone was the lost little boy. However, Harry soon focused again on why he was here and allowed Lockhart's thoughts to filter in. Gilderoy had watched the boy for days; he and his little friends didnít understand just who they were messing with. He was the Gilderoy Lockhart: Order of Merlin Third Class, Honorary Member of the Dark Force Defence League, and five-time winner of Witch Weekly's Most Charming Smile award! He was what mere wizards aspired to be yet Harry Potter thought he could take him on and win? On no, he would make the boy pay for humiliating him. He knew he was done for - even Albus couldnít get him out now. The DMLE was coming for him thanks to the brat. How he had known, Gilderoy didnít know. He was careful when covering his tracks, yet Harry bloody Potter hadn't bought his act from day one. He had set his murderous godfather on him and that pompous Death Eater then that evil wrench Skeeter. He knew he would be caught, but he refused to go down without taking that brat with him! Looking back at the group, he clenched his fist. The boy had everything Gilderoy wished for: money, fame, friends, looks, charmîyet he didnít even try! He didnít deserve any of it. Looking at them, he noted how the small blonde girl seemed to gravitate to Potter and how the boy allowed her to drape over him with no concern. He had taught her a few times - she was a Ravenclaw like Potter. Linda? No, Lucy? Not quite... Luna. Yes, Luna Lovegood. Staring at the pair, Lockhart started to plot. What better way to destroy Potter than to go through the person he clearly loved the most? The line of thought had Harry snarling silently before he calmed himself down and let Lockhartís thoughts filter back in. åPotter thought he was untouchable as the Heir to a Noble House. Well, Iíll show him.í Harry watched as the plans were built. Lockhart targeted Luna because of how close they were, though the thought made Harry tilt his head. While Luna was probably the closest of his friends simply because she knew so much, he loved them all equally. He had given Luna detention and forced her to come to his office where he had stunned her and moved her to the classroom. He had planned on using a blood ritual, on sacrificing her and her magic, and blaming it on Harry. The thought made Harry scoff slightly as he doubted Lockhart would be able to actually draw the correct runes required for a successful ritual, especially for something that complicated. Runes was an extremely complex concept, after all. The next part of his plan was that he was going to give Harry a detention, stun him, and force him to watch as she died before planting false memories in his mind. He had planned to make Harry believe he had done the ritual. Lockhart knew he would still be caught for being a fraud, but this way he would drag the Potter brat down with him! The memories would make the child think he was guilty and, even if he tried to hide it or cover up the crime, he would be caught, blamed, and thrown away just like he was about to be. It was perfectînot only would the brat suffer seeing his little girlfriend die, but he would end up in Azkaban as well. With the plans found, Harry violently pulled out of the manís mind, hoping to cause him the maximum amount of pain when doing so. ~ Harry felt his magic straining to release once he pulled out of the manís mind. How dare he?! Wordlessly, he thrust his hand out and knocked the man out, but not before locking him in a nightmare. Lockhart would be trapped in his version of hell until Harry woke him up. ØI think I understand why rabbit and peacock go together so well, little moon,Æ he said after taking a deep breath. Luna giggled. ØI knew you would.Æ Harry turned to the girl then and saw she was looking a lot better. ØDid you know he would target you?Æ he asked, concerned with how she allowed herself to be hurt. Luna tilted her head. ØI didnít not know,Æ she said softly, knowing Harry would be upset. Harry prayed to Merlin for strength. ØLunaè,Æ he said, drawing her name out. ØI knew he would try something but it wasn't clear until today! And I knew you would come for me, my Lord. I knew I would be fine.Æ Harry sighed. ØI will always come for you Luna, but promise me that you wonít put yourself at risk next time.Æ Luna smiled a blindingly. ØI canít promise that. If putting myself at risk helps you then I will do it. I think we would all do it for you, my Lord.Æ Harry tutted and walked towards the smaller girl. ØI would never ask that from you or anybody else.Æ ØI know! Thatís why you're so special, Harry.Æ Pulling her into a hug, Harry turned and observed the slightly twitching form of their current Defence Against the Dark Arts teacher. ØHow do you suppose weíre going to get him to the chamber?Æ he asked. Luna giggled. ØThe map, silly.Æ ~ Levitating and disillusioning Lockhartís body from the 7th floor to the 1st floor girlsí bathroom was actually easier than Harry had first anticipated. Luna held the map and directed them so they didnít encounter a single person or ghost on the way. Yet their luck didnít hold out as they were greeted by Myrtle. The ghost girl stared at them before narrowing her eyes at Harry. ØWhat are you doing in here? This is a girlís bathroom! Have you come to make fun of me, to bully me for my glasses?Æ she cried, not pausing for breath between her accusations. åThen again,í Harry thought, åshe doesnít need to breathe.í Before he could respond, Luna spoke up with a bright smile and a tilt of her head. ØHello again, Myrtle.Æ ØWhy have you brought a boy in here?Æ she asked Luna, her tone on the word boy making it sound like an accusation. ØThis is the boy I was telling you about, Myrtle. This is Harry.Æ Harry watched their interaction with fascination. He knew the old Luna got on with most of the ghosts, but he hadn't known she also spoke to Myrtle. Harry had been lucky so far in all of his visits to the Chamber he had yet to meet the dead girl. ØOh, you're Harry,Æ Myrtle said, getting closer to him. A lot closer than he was comfortable with, actually. Hiding his discomfort, he smiled. ØHello Myrtle, itís nice to meet you.Æ The ghost giggled and blushed. ØHello,Æ she said. ØWould you mind standing guard for us, Myrtle? We have something that needs disposing of,Æ Luna asked. The ghost nodded, still giggling whenever she looked at Harry. ØOf course, Luna! Anything for you and Harry.Æ Harry held back his groan. Even in this life he was doomed to be stalked by the ghost. ØThank you,Æ he said and watched as she disappeared with another giggle. Sighing, he looked at Luna, who was also silently giggling. ØItís not funny, you know,Æ he said. With obvious effort Luna managed to straighten her face and curb her laughter. ØOf course not, my Lord.Æ Harry shook his head before muttering about stalker ghosts and evil Seers. Approaching the sinks, Harry opened his mouth and hissed, ØOpen.Æ Luna let out a slight squeal at the used of Parseltongueînot one of fear, but one of delight. Turning to look at her, she looked completely unashamed and beamed at him. ØStairs,Æ he said and watched at the slide transformed. Harry turned to Luna and said, ØFollow me.Æ Luna nodded and followed Harry down the stairs into the Chamber, keeping at a slight distance as the disillusioned body of Lockhart was floating behind him. Just before entering the main chamber, Harry flicked his wand and lit the torches. ØStay here for a moment while I tell Aithusia about you, I donít want her to accidently harm you.Æ Luna nodded and bounced on the spot. ØOkay,Æ she said, her excitement obvious. Harry shook his head. Most people would have a small amount of fear meeting a legendary Dark creature like a basilisk yet Luna looked like all of her dreams were about to come true. ØAithusia,Æ he called out. ØHello, youngling,Æ the snake said, appearing from one of the antechambers. ØI've brought you a present but first, I have a friend with me. Sheís waiting outside, would it be okay if I called her so she could meet you?Æ ØYes little one, bring in your friend. I will judge to see if they are worthy for being a speakerís friend.Æ Harry nodded and called out to Luna to join him. Turning back to the snake he saw that her yellow eyes were focused on the still disillusioned Lockhart. With a wave of his hand the body became solid once again and Aithusia hissed. ØIs this my present, youngling?Æ she asked, the tongue scenting the man. ØYes.Æ Luna appeared by his side, her blue eyes wide. ØSheís beautiful, my Lord,Æ she said, any hint of dreaminess in her voice hidden by her sheer awe. Aithusia hissed in pleasure. ØA smart friend, youngling, clearly she has great taste. Youíve picked well.Æ Luna looked at Harry. ØWhat did she say, my Lord?Æ ØShe says you're a good friend, little moon,Æ he said, finding it funny that it took a simple compliment to win over a 1000 year old basilisk. Then again, she could probably smell Lunaís emotions and knew she was pure-spirited. ØLittle one, why have you brought me a man as a present?Æ Harry turned his attention back to the great snake who was still staring at Lockhart with unblinking fascination. Quickly telling the snake what the man had done, Harry had to take a step back as she began hissing in fury; some of the words even he couldnít make out. It was the first time the snake sounded anything less than human. ØStupid man, eat him, crush him, poison him! Thinks he can hurt my youngling? My speaker child?Æ ØI take it she doesn't like peacock?Æ Luna asked from her position hidden behind him. ØShall I awaken him for you, Aithusia?Æ ØYes!Æ Harry almost felt sorry for the man. Almost. Flicking his hand again, Harry watched as the man turned and grumbled about being bald and fat, making Harry snort as that must have been his nightmare. The man quickly gathered his bearings and he shot up after a few seconds. His blue eyes first focusing on Harry and then Luna, who was poking her head around him to get a better look at what was about to happen. ØPotter!Æ he growled before a loud hissing filled the air. Turning, he wasn't prepared for the sight that met him. Harry watched as the man turned around and blanched. He had never seen somebody go so white so quickly and feared the man would pass out. Gilderoy looked at the giant snake in front of him and felt his bladder give, before blackness took over. Harry wrinkled his nose at the scene and sent a Rennervate at the man. ØPathetic creature,Æ Aithusia hissed. Lockhart blinked twice before letting out a high pitched shriek and scrambling back. Reaching for where he kept his wand and panicking when he came up empty, he continued to scramble away. Seeing this, Harry whistled to gain his attention and pulled out he manís wand. Lockhart locked eyes on his wand and screamed, ØPotter! Save me! Kill it!Æ Harry laughed lowly. ØWhy would I kill her? Sheís my friend.Æ ØItís a bloody monster! Kill it, boy! Now!Æ Harryís eyes narrowed. ØI donít like it when people call me boy and she is not a monster. She is a beautiful basilisk, the familiar of Salazar Slytherin himself. Aithusia, donít listen to the fool, you're not a monster.Æ Lockhart stared incomprehensibly. ØY-you're a parselmouth?Æ he whispered, horrified by the discovery. ØYes,Æ Harry said plainly. ØYou're a freak! A Dark monster!Æ Harry felt his magic strain to let out. ØAithusia, I'm tired of his words; enjoy your meal and have a great Yule. I've asked some house-elves to make sure youíre brought some rabbits and other things to eat while I'm away,Æ Harry said, his voice strained even though he was talking in Parseltongue. ØYoungling, his words are meaningless. You are not a freak! You are a wizard beyond the dreams of many and he is nothing. Donít let him affect you.Æ Harry nodded. ØThank you. Enjoy your peacock.Æ Luna seemed to understand what was going on even though she didnít know the words and hugged Harry. ØMy Lord, the peacock is a fool. Like the old goat, his mind is full of nifflers.Æ Harry laughed. ØI know, little moon. Come on, letís go, I think the rest will have probably sent out a search party by now.Æ Luna hummed and skipped away. ØBye, Aithusia, have a great Yule and I hope you enjoy the peacock!Æ Lockhart seemed to realise they were leaving him and tried to stand. ØYou canít leave!Æ Harry turned and sent him a cold look. ØWatch me.Æ The last thing he saw was Aithusiaís lunge before he led Luna out of the chamber. ØI still think ginger would have been a great starter for her,Æ Luna said, twirling around next to Harry, not bothered that she had just witnessed a manís murder. ØPerhaps that shall be the summer feast?Æ Harry said eventually, barely holding back his laughter at her innocent viciousness. ~ By the time they reached the hall dessert was being served. ØAnd where in Morganaís name have you been?Æ Draco demanded when they sat down. Harry and Luna shared a look. ØDid you know snakes like peacocks?Æ Luna asked. Everybody paused and sent her confused looks. ØWhat?Æ Neville eventually asked. Harry just laughed. ØSorry we took so long. It took us slightly longer to get back, but at least we made dessert.Æ The rest still looked confused but nodded. ØWell, at least thatís something, but pudding isnít a proper meal,Æ Hannah said, showing them two plates with actual food from the feast. Sending the Hufflepuff a grin, Harry picked one up and tucked in. ØYou're a star, Hannah Abbott,Æ he said seriously. ØOi, we helped!Æ the twins said in mock outrage. Harry smiled and looked at his mismatched group of friends. I've only just gotten Blaise to stop.Æ Hearing his name, the Italian looked up. ØStop what?Æ ØCalling our dear friend here Drakie.Æ Harry laughed. After the feast, Harry returned to Ravenclaw tower with his roommates. All of them gathered their things and made sure their trunks were more or less packed; nobody wanted to rush in the morning before the train. Getting done quickly, Harry pulled out his cloak and the map. He had one final thing he needed to do before going home for Yule. ~ 22nd December 1992, Hogwarts School of Witchcraft and Wizardry The next day the group all met at the station. The man couldnít spell his way out of a locked room, let alone a fully warded ancient castle, yet every spell Albus tried was coming up empty. Gilderoy Lockhart was no longer in Hogwarts. The news would have initially caused him great relief if it hadn't been for Sirius Black and Lucius Malfoy getting involved and forcing his hand. Now the wards were up and if Lockhart was caught, he would be ruined. That was, of course, a lie. She was very much looking forward to seeing Susan and having her back for Yule, but before she could go and pick up her niece she was going to have to go and deal with the headmaster yet again. The current situation was ridiculous. The investigation had taken weeks as they had been stalled and mislead at every turn, no matter that the results were clear. Gilderoy Lockhart, formerly in possession of an Order of Merlin 3rd class, was a liar and a fraud. Amelia knew his books were ridiculous, the claims in them laughable, and even a child under 11 would recognise them as fake. In one he claimed to have punched some vampireís fangs out, making him only able to eat mushed up carrots for the rest of eternity. Ridiculous! Firstly, vampire teeth are nearly impenetrable to force and secondly, while a vampire can consume other substances other than blood most chose not to as they gain little from it. That didnít even touch upon the fact that even fewer use their fangs to drink instead of using donated blood poured in glasses. The claims in most of the books were all as ridiculous. However, prior to the investigation Lockhart got away with it as some of the claimed feats did happen, such as a slain mountain troll that saved a village in Peru. It did happen and Lockhart was there - though now it is clear that his claim to have been the one to do the feat was a lie and he modified the memories of those around him to get away with it. Amelia, like everybody else, had simply taken his other claimed victories such as the vampire as fiction to make his books more interesting, not realising he was manipulating and mentally damaging hundreds of people. Feeling her alarm go off, she stood and let out a sigh. His eyes, although still older than they should be, lacked the horror and pain they once held. His body was no longer gaunt and frail, instead he stood tall and proud, lightly muscled and tanned. He is a Dark wizard and a supporter of Tomís after all, as well as being in close contact with us both.Æ Sirius surprised Harry when he didnít outright object - but Harry hadn't seen them when they were alone together out of the public eye, when they didnít have to maintain a certain level of decorum, so he didnít know just how close their friendship had become. ØI think youíre right,Æ he said eventually. Harry nodded. ØMaybe Severus as well.Æ Sirius smiled at the mention of the Potion Masterís name, making Harry consider him for a second. ØThey will probably be like you and Remus.Æ ØWhat?Æ ØI donít think that Dumbledore will have limited himself on who he dosed.Æ Sirius looked confused for a moment before s look of understanding and anger passed over his face. ØThere are probably hundreds of witches and wizardsè.Æ Harry nodded. ØI know,Æ he said, not concealing his disgust. An image of a young Tom Riddle filled his mind; he wasn't sure, but he would bet his Gringotts vault that Albus Dumbledore was the reason behind Tomís eventual unravelling and psychosis. ~ 23rd December 1992, Grimmauld Place, London Lucius was pleasantly surprised when he entered Grimmauld Place. The last time he had been inside, the townhouse was practically a cesspit; it had been dark, dank, and poorly kept. Narcissa had visited her aunt shortly before she had died, the old woman insisting on seeing the remains of her family. Now however, the house actually felt like a home, the furnishings clearly new and well-kept. ØWelcome, Lord Malfoy,Æ a slightly gravelly voice said, drawing his eye to an elderly house-elf. Before he could speak another figure emerged from the green flames. ØLucius,Æ Severus greeted after flicking his wrist to rid himself of any dust the floo had left behind. ØAh, Severus. I didnít know you were also invited.Æ ØIndeed. Have you any idea what this is about?Æ The Potions Master asked. ØNo, though I hope it shanít take too long. I hate to leave Narcissa and Draco alone for long on such a holiday - Yule is a time to be with family.Æ Severus rolled his eyes at the jab. The blond had been trying to get him to join them for such celebrations for years. The opening of a door drew their attention and both looked up when Remus entered. The wolf looked much healthier than he had in years and Severus was reluctantly pleased about this. ØGentlemen, thank you for coming,Æ Remus said. ØItís no bother,Æ Severus said quietly. He was still slightly uncomfortable around the man. ØIf you would follow me, I believe Harry and Sirius are waiting in the family parlour for us.Æ ~ Harry watched as Sirius paced. He loved the man dearly and he found his nervous anxiety humorous, but it was slightly annoying. ØYou said you trusted them,Æ the preteen reminded. ØI didîI do, butè.Æ ØCalm down Siri, it will be fine.Æ ØI know, butîÆ Before he could worry himself further, the door opened and Remus walked in followed by Severus and Lucius. Seeing how worked up his friend was, Remus shook his head and asked, ØHas he been pacing since I left?Æ Harry snorted lightly. ØYes.Æ Sirius pouted slightly and threw himself into an armchair, sprawling out yet somehow managing to look elegant. ØPlease take a seat gentleman,Æ Harry said, indicating to the other chairs. Once everybody was seated, Harry smiled. ØCan I offer anybody a drink?Æ he asked, proper manners refusing to be ignored. Lucius and Severus shared a quick look. ØI believe we are fine for now, Harrison. Perhaps you should tell us instead why you asked us to be here?Æ Harry nodded and quickly filtered through what he planned on saying before throwing caution to the wind. ØSo, tell me, what do you know about horcruxes?Æ ~ Both men sat in shocked silence trying to take in everything they had just been told. Neither was surprised that their Lord had been less than sane in the end if he had really created so many of the abdominal things. Harry watched as they both seemed to lose themselves in their thoughts. He had not told them about being Master of Death or returning and re-living his life - instead he spoke about the compulsions and binds Dumbledore had put on him, his soul bond, and how his soulmate had tried to become immortal through the use of horcruxes. He told them that they should go to the goblins and see about having themselves checked over for compulsions and how tonight he planned on starting the process of bringing Tom, their Lord, back. ØYou really want to bring him back?Æ Severus finally asked, shocked that Harry of all people would want to bring Voldemort back. ØHe wonít be the same man you knew,Æ Harry said, hoping to have their support. Lucius still looked shocked but Severus was clearly torn. ØBut he killed your parents. Murdered Lily!Æ Harry sighed and shut his eyes for a moment. ØEven when insane he offered her a chance to move aside and live. She refused and sacrificed herself, saving my life.Æ Severus sat back. ØThen how can you consider bringing him back?!Æ ØI need to! Without him I will die. Heís my soulmate,Æ Harry said, stressing the words. ØDo you have all of his horcruxes?Æ Lucius said, finally forcing himself out of his panic. Harry looked at him and smirked. ØYes, even a certain diary.Æ Lucius gulped and paled considerably. ØHowè?Æ ØI have my ways,Æ Harry said, confusing the other occupants of the room. ØDonít worry, I can keep how I managed to gather that particular one secret.Æ Lucius stared at the young man before him and felt a wave of relief. He still remembered his Lordís instability and how curse happy he had been by the end. ØThank you,Æ he said honestly before continuing. ØThis ritual, it will return to our Lord both his sanity and his life?Æ ØYes, he will be as he was supposed to be.Æ Lucius nodded. He remembered the stories his father had told him about their Lord and his goals and, for the first time in a long time, he felt hope. He had honestly resented having the Dark Mark by the end of the last war; he had taken it as a teen, thinking it was a great honour, but the Lord his father had praised had been gone by that point and in his place was a mad man. If this ritual could restore the man his father had idolised, whose dreams and ideals he had grown up hearing about, then he would be honoured to help Harry. ØThen I will help you with whatever you need,Æ the blond vowed, bowing his head to Harry in a sign of respect. ØThank you,Æ Harry said. ØI honestly donít need help with the ritual tonight, but your willingness to aid me is appreciated.Æ Severus was still hesitant. ØHow can you forgive him?Æ he asked the teen. ØHe wasn't in his right mind. The Tom who I will be bringing back will not be Voldemort.Æ Severus sighed and nodded his head in slight defeat. ØI will stand by you in this,Æ he vowed. He had sworn to protect him and if Harry could forgive his Lord, then he would try. ØThank you. Both of you,Æ he said. ØYou really should try and see the goblins sooner rather than later as well,Æ Remus said, reminding the room of that. ØThese compulsions and binds, how bad were they?Æ Severus asked. ØWell, mine made me hate anything remotely Slytherin and Dark in magic. They also made me reckless, careless, and oppose to authority. I was also made to hate certain people, members of my own family and evenîÆ he cut off, looking at Severus, before continuing. ØNow donít get me wrong, I was always rather free thinking when it came to magic, but I was a Dark wizard and accepting of that until I went to Hogwarts. Then suddenly the slight resentment I held towards my parents grew to me hating everything Dark, my harmless pranks grew to me becoming a bully. My godfather bond to Harry was also blocked.Æ The two men nodded. ØI see,Æ Severus said, shocked at the emotion he saw in the animagusí eyes. Lucius looked contemplative. ØYou were the Black Heir, didnít your ring protect you?Æ Sirius blinked. ØI didnít have the ring when I got my Hogwarts letter. The Blacks, like most families, donít usually give the Heir ring until the Heirís 13th birthday. Harry is the most recent exception as he needed the protection and he had already claimed it along with his other titles while I wasè indisposed. I suspect that after I had it, I didnít gain any new compulsions, at least until I ran away. I had left the ring here as I didnít want to be associated with my family.Æ ØI see,Æ Lucius said, going silent as he thought about his own behaviour after he entered Hogwarts. This time Remus spoke up. ØMine were similar in nature. I was made to be ashamed of myself, submissive, loyal, and grateful to Dumbledore. I also had a poison in my blood, making me weaker. It would have eventually killed me, probably within the next few years.Æ That shocked the two men, leaving them completely baffled. Severus was disgusted at how far Dumbledore was willing to go, and confused as to why. ØYou're fine now, though?Æ Lucius asked eventually. ØYes, I was healed by the goblins.Æ ØMy own compulsions were not as deadly. I was placed under a blood glamour, making me look like my father, and I had a magic binding on my core,Æ Harry added. That caused the two men to react violently; both knew the danger of putting such bindings on children. They could cause irreparable damage to both their magical and physical development. ØHe what?Æ Severus asked, his voice going cold and causing shivers amongst the other occupants. ØHe bound my magical core and attempted to bind my other inherited magics. I also had a slew of compulsions similar to Sirius' which would have made me a weak willed and feeble-minded Gryffindor, besotted with Dumbledore and easy to manipulate. I had them all removed when I re-entered this world, but the fact he tried sickens me. There is also the theft from my vaults which I immediately stopped.Æ ØHe stole from you?Æ Lucius asked. ØA few thousand galleons, pocket change in the large scheme, but still.Æ Severus was startled by this, though he hid his derision at the casual dismissal of such large sums of money in favour of letting his anger at the situation prevail. ØWhy are you telling us all of this now? You have said that you donít need our help, so I question your motives.Æ Harry smirked at his question. ØTrue, I donít need your help but Tom is still your Lord and I wanted to test your loyalties as well as warn you of Dumbledore's manipulations.Æ Harry focused on Severus for the last part, emerald green eyes boring into black. Severus felt like his very soul was being searched before he nodded. ØI see.Æ ØIf that is everything, I think itís best if we retire for the evening. You have your ritual to prepare for and Narcissa and Draco are waiting for me,Æ Lucius said after a beat of silence. Harry nodded. ØI agree. Good evening, gentlemen. Oh, and happy Yule.Æ Chapter End Notes Sorry that its taken me so long to update, life is so manic at the moment! However I promise that this story has not and will not be abandoned!
