Looking up he saw that everybody in the hall was reading the article, whispering about the still absent Head teacher. /// Albus paced up and down his office, ignoring the mournful song Fawkes was letting out, as he tried to figure out how he had missed Toms return. The bird had become even more melancholy recently and he was fed up of it, if it wasn't for the fact he needed the hopeless thing to maintain his image he would simply get rid of it. However Albus Dumbledore was famous for having a phoenix as a familiar, therefore the damned bird had to stay. ØHow?Æ he repeated, muttering the word over and over again under his breath, he simply couldnít understand it. Severus hadn't said anything, his actions recently hadn't changed nor his attitude. Albus refused to believe his spy had turned traitor, so that left the question of just how was Tom back and how had he hidden it. Albus was not prepared for Toms return yet, his control over Harry wasn't firm nor were his plans going according to schedule. Sitting, he drafted letters to his order members, they would have to move quickly if they wanted to stop the Dark Lord. /// Severus fought to maintain his blank facade as he watched the clearly insane man before him pace around the room. Albus didn't seem to realise that he was muttering to himself, twitchy blue eyes darting around the room as he went. The old man had hardly acknowledged his presence, seemingly lost in his own mind and ramblings. Clearing his throat once again, Severus watched as the old man spun to face him rather nimbly for somebody of his advanced age. ØAah, Severus, my boy.Æ Fighting the grimace the words involuntarily caused, Severus nodded his head in greeting, ØHeadmaster.Æ ØSeverus. Dark times are upon us..,Æ Dumbledore started, face grave but eyes shining madly, ØI must know, your mark, have you felt anything from it?Æ Severus knew this was coming, had know of his Lords plan to rejoin society so he was prepared, Øno, I would have told you immediately.Æ Albus narrowed his eyes, he didn't want to doubt Severus but how could he not. ØYour mark... Let me see it.Æ With a sneer Severus unbuttoned his shirt sleeve and rolled it up, allowing pale skin to show, a faded dark mark marring the white flesh. His Lord had tweaked his mark in preparation for this very moment. Albus slumped when he saw the mark was the same as it had been the last time he saw it. ØWhat about your contacts? You're close with Lucius, he must have told you something!?Æ Severus allowed an eyebrow to rise at the manís near hysteria, ØLucius has said nothing to me, which isnít shocking... Many do not trust me. I donít doubt that if and when the Dark Lord does call for us I will be treated with suspicion as most, if not all of, my old colleges view me a traitor.Æ Albus snarled, ØYou must find out everything you can from them my dear boy. You must. It will be dangerous yes, but I trust in your ability to regain Toms favour. I must know how he has returned.Æ Severus fought to maintain his stoic facade, as it was Albus didn't know that Voldemort was now sane, so demanding his return to the dark side was beyond belief... well not really, Severus knew Albus cared for nothing beyond himself and his own power. Still, if his Lord had returned in his previous mindset, then Albus would be sending him to his death; that is if he was lucky and Voldemort feeling generous. Traitors very rarely got quick and painless deaths. ØAs you wish,Æ the dark-haired man replied, hiding his disgust. /// Harry looked around the Ravenclaw common room with a sense of satisfaction. The first part of this year had gone by quickly, after Dumbledore's disgrace at the first Wizengamot meeting, the old man had been targeted by all members of the press, not just Rita. This sudden plummet in his popularity had caused the old manís focus to seemingly shift and he had for the most part left Harry alone. Doge however had been a nightmare, not only was the man useless but he followed Harry around like a lost puppy. It was only because Harry had promised to try and avoid confrontation with Dumbledore after the compulsion attempt that he even attended the manís lessons and didnít once again stage a boycott. Therefore returning home for Yule was a blessing, not only was he getting away from his new shadow but he would get too see Tom again. Their separation was harder on him than he realised, he missed his bond mate like crazy and even writing near daily letters didnít lessen his misery. ØHave you any clue how Dumbledore almost trapped me?Æ was the first thing Harry said after getting their greetings out of the way. Marvolo scowled, he had missed his little raven and the reminder of the danger he was constantly in wasn't something he enjoyed. ØNo,Æ he admitted, Øthere are too many options and unknown variables.Æ Harry hummed, ØHave you asked Phineas if he saw anything?Æ ØPhineas?Æ Harry paused and turned to look at a suddenly sheepish looking Sirius, Øyou forgot that Phineas has a portrait in the headmasters office didnít you?Æ Marvolo took a breath and forced himself not to curse Black. How could the man fail to mention such a monumentally important thing? He could understand Harry, his little raven had to be vague in his letters recently, with Dumbledore's growing paranoia he had limited his correspondence. Sirius fidgeted as he was glared at by not just Harry but the Dark Lord... ØIn my defence the portraits were silenced a few months ago...Æ he trailed off as the glares intensified. /// ØBloody fool of a man!Æ Harry heard the portrait before he saw it. Ø...Been shouting for you for weeks! Weeks I tell you! And do you hear me? No. Because surely you know best!Æ Harry and Marvolo turned the corner and saw Sirius standing, or rather, slumping before the portrait of the former Black headmaster. Harry could feel Marvolo's magic hum with amusement and sent him a quelling look. ØHeadmaster,Æ he said sending the portrait a small smile, Ødo forgive him, he is at heart, a Gryffindor.Æ Sirius sent Harry a betrayed look hearing the words. Phineas however huffed, Øitís good to see that the headmasters charm failed. Nasty works that, dark shamanic blood ritual.Æ Harry felt Marvolo physically freeze while his magic wiped out, striking around like a rabid viper. Thinking for a moment Harry allowed his mind to wander, however he drew up a blank on that particular variation of blood magic. ØI see,Æ Harry murmured, Øhas he prepared anything else since his first attempt failed?Æ Phineas looked at the young heir with speculation; unlike Sirius this boy had true potential. ØHeís been focusing his efforts into rebuilding his order and gathering information on how Marvolo here returned.Æ Harry nodded; he had seen previous members of the order around the castle, going to and from Dumbledore's office, so the news didn't shock him. Still he could help but wonder who would be in this new order. Sirius and Remus were obviously out, he doubted Kingsley would join, the man had clearly seen through the headmasterís facade during the term teaching. Harry also doubted that Tonks would be making an appearance; the young metamorphmagus had only just graduated and was scarcely starting her auror training from what Harry knew. Yet they were the only members he doubted; the other members, that he knew of, he was almost certain would rejoin the order one Dumbledore's claims. /// Unlike the previous year, this year Harry's Yule was being spent at Malfoy manor, something he was quite looking forward to. He, Sirius, Remus and even Severus would be joining the blonde family from the morning of the 21st right through to the Malfoyís Yule ball on the 24th and then into the new year before his return to school; Severus only managing to get away from the castle by manipulating Dumbledore and playing on the old fools fears that the Dark Lord had returned. The potions master had promised to use the time away to try and weasel information of from his darker contacts, something the headmaster had wholeheartedly agreed with. Stepping from the floo Harry's eyes automatically sought out Marvolo, just seeing his mate caused a knot he hadn't know was there to loosen within him. After forcing himself to look away Harry greeted his hosts, pleased to see Draco looked relaxed even with Marvolo in the room. However the longer he looked at his mate the more he saw; Marvolo, whose magic and strength had continued to grow since his return, looked tired. It wasn't obvious, he doubted anybody else would even notice, but Harry considered himself an expert on his mate and he would see past the front Marvolo was exuding. After getting the traditional greeting out of the way, Harry quickly made his way over to the older man in order to assess him more thoroughly. ØYou look drained. What have you been up to?Æ he asked, allowing himself to slip into parseltongue so that the others wouldnít be privy to their conversation. Although the questions were direct Harry allowed his genuine concern to bleed through their bond. Instead of the indignation that he knew he would have felt had anybody else questioned him; Marvolo allowed himself a moment of enjoyment, at seeing and feeling his mateís genuine concern. ØIíve come up with a solution to our Dumbledore problem.Æ Harry wasn't surprised that Marvolo had managed to create a solution, more he was amazed that he had managed to do so in such a short amount of time, then again Marvolo had always been an overachiever... ØStillè you're okay?Æ ØThe ritual took more from me than expected, but I will be fine after some rest.Æ Nodding, Harry allowed himself to relax slightly, Øthen let us rest for a few hours.Æ Marvolo was again taken back by Harrison's attitude; he knew the boy loved his family, which he knew now included Severus and the Malfoy's. Yet here he was, willing to leave them and spend time with him simply because Marvolo was slightly magically drained. Never had he had somebody willing to do anything for him and expect nothing in return, and every time Harry did it caused a swell of emotion to build within him. Harry seeing the shock in Marvolo's eyes, or rather feeling it, allowed his magic out to caress Marvolo's. He had missed his family yes, but seeing his mate and spending time with him would always be one of his main priorities, especially after such a long separation. Turing he noted that the rest of his family was now relaxed and simply enjoying each otherís company. Remus and Draco had pulled out a chess board and were engrossed in trying to bet one another. Lucius and Narcissa were sitting together in front of fire, enjoying what looked like a tender moment and Sirius and Severus, well Harry didn't want to read too much into their interaction, but the look in Siriusís eye said more than enough. Harry didn't know if he should be shocked by the development of their relationship or not, as it was well known that the line between love and hate was very thin. Seeing that they wouldnít be missed Harry simply took Marvolo's hand and lead him from the room, pausing only to ask where the manís rooms were. /// Harry felt truly content as he lay across Marvolo, his head resting in the older wizards lap as he ran an idle hand through his hair. They had been sitting like this for around an hour, Marvolo resting with his eyes closed, awake enough to run his hand through Harry's hair but only just, while Harry read a book on household charms. Not the most interesting subject but Harry had realised he needed to expand his spell repertoire beyond combat and obscure magics. Feeling the rhythm of the hand in his hair shift, Harry idly flicked his wrist and sent the book towards the table. ØFeeling better?Æ he asked. Marvolo hummed and opened his eyes, smiling at the sight that greeted him. His mate was truly wondrous; such power hidden behind such a delicate facade was truly breathtaking. He had felt Harry's magic surround him and adding to his as soon as they had settled in his rooms, his own magic surging and replenishing faster once surrounded by his mates. ØIndeed.Æ ØSo what was the solution you found?Æ Harry asked, reluctantly sitting up and instead turning to face Marvolo on the couch. Holding his hand up Marvolo summoned a small black box, ØI was going to wait and give you this as your Yule gift but I would rather you have it now as you never know when the man will try something again.Æ Taking the box once offered Harry opened it and saw a small delicate ring with a small black gem, looking closely he could see minuscule marking engraved into the metal surrounding the gem, it was beyond beautiful. Yet the truly breathtaking thing was not the ring itself but the power it extruded, the ring practically sang with Marvolo's magic. Picking the ring up Harry hesitated momentarily as to what finger to place it on. Seeing the hesitation Marvolo eased the ring from Harrison and gently lifted his left hand, ØI know I can not openly court you yet, but think of this ring as a promise. Not only will it protect you from harm but it will also show others that you're mine.Æ Harry looked at the ring and felt himself break into a tender smile, the reaction almost subconscious. For all he knew that Marvolo still struggled with expressing his emotions, the man was certainly getting better at causing Harry's to run wild. He didn't need to ask about the specific charms infused in the ring, he trusted Marvolo knew what he has doing, and judging by the magical aura it gave off, there were a great many enhancements made to the piece of jewellery now resting on his ring finger. ØThank you,Æ he said, the words infused with gratitude and love. Marvolo allowed a small smile to show, Øthink nothing of it, I would do anything for you, my little raven.Æ /// Dumbledore looked around his office and couldnít help but disappointed with the turnout; he needed stronger followers, people with power and prestige. Still he was determined not to let Tom win. He knew that as soon as people saw the Dark Lord had returned they would come flocking to him, their saviour. The Weasley family were here, the oldest son looking confused and slightly belligerent. Young William had a good job; he was in his final year of his curse breaking apprentice at Gringottes. Yes William was somebody Albus could utilize; his connections with the goblins were sorely needed especially now he had been cut off from the bratís vaults. Arthur was useless, his position at the ministry a joke, and molly, while loyal to a fault brought nothing to the table in the way of influence. Dodge was here looking red faced and eager. His dear old friend was a faithfully follower, always willing to let Albus steer him towards the right goal, willing to offer up his money and familyís vote in the Wizengamot for Albus and the light. Mad-eye stood stiffly, artificial eye wildly spinning as he checked out the other occupants of the room. The old auror had taken a lot of convincing, but eventually relented to come and listen, refusing to offer his support just yet. Alastor was sceptical of Albus, but by being here Albus was sure he could steer his old friend back on the right path. Hestia Jones was here alongside her brother, neither had needed much persuading, which Albus was grateful for. Both member of the Jones family were handy with a wand, Hestia a dueller and Michael an auror, which would come in handy in the coming war Albus was sure. Mundungus Fletcher had also appeared, the thief scuttling in and situating himself as far away from Alastor as he could get. His twitchy form and eyes not settling, Albus could hardly contain his sneer, as he took note of the man, robes more like rags and no remarkable qualities beyond his merger knowledge of the comings and goings in Knockturn alley... no Albus was not impressed with the turnout. ØAs we all know, I've call you here today as our world is once more in grave danger. Lord Voldemort has yet again risen and will soon resume his reign of terror.Æ Albus enjoyed the way the room silenced t his words, how the majority of people paled and looked frightened. It proved that he still had power and that they listened to him. ØWhere is your proof Albus?Æ came a rough voice as Alastor stepped forward. His face marred by scars twisted even more by his scowl. ØI've seen him.Æ ØLord Gaunt, right? I saw what you did during the Wizengamot session.Æ Albus sneered at the mention of the session and the subsequent newspaper articles that followed. Clearly Skeeter was a dark witch who supported Tom, as she had been continuous in her smear campaign against him, none of his threats or warnings had deterred her or even put a dent in her incessant reports. ØI know what the papers have been saying but I assure you, Lord Gaunt is Voldemort.Æ The announcement met silence before chaos ensued; shouted whispers and arguments broke out, however Albus focused on Moody. The old auror wasn't arguing but rather observing the commotion, artificial eye spinning. Not long after the meeting broke down, the members all promising to go out and attempt to find out more about Tomís return, leaving Albus alone. ØYou need to take care, old friend.Æ The gruff voice made Albus spin, wand trained on the space. Moody eyed the wand before seemingly dismissing it and inelegantly slumping into one of the ornate chairs in front of the headmasterís desk. ØI thought you had left with the others.Æ ØI will leave soon, but first I think you need to listen to me... Albus I think you need to take a step back.Æ Albus felt his eyes narrow, how dare he? He was Albus Dumbledore, the greatest wizard alive, he didn't need to step back, he needed his pawns to come back to the board and do what he wanted, he needed them to listen to him. ØI never expected you to be wilfully blind, old friend,Æ Albus spat back, making sure to use the same words but coating them with disappointment, hoping to guilt the man. Moody stiffened, ØI witness horrors every day, from work and in my dreams, I am not blind. I see what is happening, what I donít see is how or why you believe You-Know-Who is back.Æ ØYou where there, you saw him!Æ ØI saw a young lord, who thanks to you is more popular and powerful than ever, whose ideas and goals seem to line up with the purebloods and traditionals. If you suspected him of foul play, you should have gone about trying to expose him in a better way. You're a laughing stock Albus!Æ Albus had to fight the urge to curse the man, how dare he? He was Albus Dumbledore, the most powerful wizard since Merlin himself, the defeater of Grindelwald, champion of the light. He was not a laughing stock! ØI know Voldemort better than anybody; I know Lord Gaunt is just a mask.Æ ØWhere is your proof, anything Albus, just a single piece of evidence?Æ ØI never thought you would doubt me.Æ ØI donít want to Albus, but you're not making believing you an easy task. These last few years, well, they havenít been your best.Æ Albus internally snarled, fighting the urge to lash out as his magic whipped up in response to his inner frenzy. This was all the brats fault, his reputation was in tatters thanks to him. If he had just done what he was supposed to and fought the dark lord then nothing like this would be happening. Still he couldnít show Moody how his words angered him and instead settled for looking pained and disappointed yet again. ØBad press is fleeting; I had hoped that my past actions would speak for themselves. I have always fought for the light and for the betterment of our world.Æ Moodyís lips thinned, ØI know you have Albus. I just think that perhaps you should take a step back and revaluate things, maybe take a break.Æ The words cut like daggers... a break? The world would fall into chaos if he want there to guide it. Still he didn't want to cause the old auror a reason to fully abandon the cause; he needed people like Moody, so he bit his lip and instead smiled wanly. He would slowly draw Moody back in, make him realise what a mistake doubting him was... ØPerhaps you're right.Æ After that, the old auror made his exit, seemingly reassured. Albus however wasn't reassured as he needed his followers to trust him; his words as law, their gospel. He needed to take back control, starting with Potter. He hadn't made any moves against the boy since his failed attempt with the blood talisman; he only had a drop of his blood left, extracted when the boy was a baby, he doubted obtaining more would be plausible. He had focused on, and needed to find out more about Toms return, the brat had fallen into the background, but now he realised he needed the brat more than ever, he was the key player in Albusís plans. With this in mind, he sat and allowed himself to plot, he would win this game. Molly had been brewing for him over the summer, he just needed to perfect a plan in order to get the boy under control, and he already had the perfect minion in mind to help him. /// Harry watched as the candles started to dim, the Yule log had been burning for 24 hours and the final ritual was soon to be done in order to renew their magic. He could feel in the air everybodyís magic brimming, preparing for the rite and he couldnít wait. Last year had been amazing but he knew it wouldnít be able to compare to this year; this year he had Marvolo with him, completing him. Lucius stood, as the head of the family and lord of the manor; he was performing the final act, burning a drop of blood from all those present. As the blood burnt, Harry felt his magic build in a crescendo before it broke free of him, leaving him feeling rejuvenated and alive. Unlike last year Harry knew what to expect, still the whole process left speechless. Looking around the room he could see that he wasn't alone in his awe, even Lucius had allowed his masks to drop. Once the magic had dispersed Harry made his way over to Marvolo, enjoying the peaceful look on his face. ØHow was your first Yule?Æ he asked. ØDoes this count as my first Yule?Æ ØItís your first Yule since your return, so yes.Æ Marvolo hummed quietly, ØI canít remember the last time my magic felt so peaceful...Æ he said softly, so that only Harry could hear him. Harry smiled at the words, Marvoloís progress and recovery where astonishing to him, the manís strength and power mind-blowing. /// Waking up to dog slobber was something Harry was sadly familiar with, waking up to prank spells and even tackle hugs were also common occurrences he has grown used to. What he had sadly, or rather happily, grown unaccustomed to was waking up to hushed whispers and curses. Therefore waking up to Sirius and Lucius arguing outside his bedroom room wasn't something Harry hadn't expected. Sitting up he decided to find out what was happened, so he made his way toward the door, their voices becoming clearer as he approached. ØHe deserves to know!Æ ØOur lord will tell him when the time is rightÆ Lucius replied. ØYour Lord, not mine!Æ Sirius hissed, Ø...and I donít care, he's my son and he should know what that crazy son of a bit-Æ Having heard enough Harry opened the door, cutting Sirius off. ØYes?Æ he asked, having his sleep interrupted leaving him less then pleased. ØHarrison, I apologise if we woke y.Ø-shut up! Harry I have something to say,Æ Sirius interrupted. Rolling his eyes Harry waved his hand silencing the two men. ØThis is about Marvolo?Æ he asked. The men nodded, Lucius with clear hesitation and reluctance. ØThen Iíll go and ask him myself,Æ he decided, ignoring the look of satisfaction in Sirius's eyes and panic in Luciusís. Harry wondered what Marvolo had done to cause such a stir. Since his return he had been working on getting accepted into society and gaining a solid persona. He hadn't mentioned anything to Harry about plans, therefore he was intrigued. Finding Marvolo wasn't hard, his magic almost acting as a guide. Entering after knocking he couldnít help the smile that formed seeing his mate looking healthy, Marvolo's improvement since Yule had been remarkable. ØHarrison, I'm surprised to see you so early.Æ ØI was woken up by Sirius and Lucius, they seemed to think youíd finalised some plans?Æ Marvolo's face didnít give away the slight annoyance he felt, but Harry could feel it swirling in his magic. ØI've been reviewing things my spies have brought me and making plans.Æ Harry hummed, Øwhat are your thoughts?Æ ØI want to remove my most faithful from Azkaban.Æ Harry had known this was coming but still, he could not help the slight look of distaste that passed over his face quickly before he managed to mask it. ØI know you have reservations my raven, but I am their lord. they're in there for refusing to denounce me, I can hardly repay their loyalty by letting them rot in that hell hole.Æ Harry knew this, but he could not help but picture Sirius as he fell through the veil, could not help but hear Bellatrix as she laughed, could not help but remember Neville's face. ØI know, but you're not the lord they worshiped. You're not that man anymore Marvolo.Æ Marvolo nodded his head slightly, he couldnít deny his raven that truth, but he still felt oath bound to help those who swore them themselves to him. ØBe that as it may I am still their Lord, they still carry my mark.Æ ØThey're all mad,Æ Harry spat out, for once losing his calm as visions of Sirius swam before his eyes ØI do not doubt that those whoíve withered away in Azkaban all of these years have suffered for it but.ØNo buts Marvolo, they were mad before they entered now, they're worse. Vicious dogs without reason.Æ Harry felt Marvolo's surprise and his annoyance, it had never been aimed at him before, yet he couldnít bring himself to care. ØI need to do this for them.Æ ØNo, doing this will just cause more problems. Dumbledore is already suspicious of you, if your followers break out itís just going to adds fuel to his fire!Æ ØThen what do you suggest? That I just leave them rotting?Æ ØYes, you donít need them!Æ ØPerhaps not but I wonít leave them!Æ Harry fought the urge to storm out, his emotions swirling madly as he was unable to occlude them while so worked up. ØI donít understand why this is such an issue for you, my raven, I would have thought you would want me to help themè you helped me after all,Æ Marvolo said, looking at Harry with burgundy eyes full of confused emotion. Harry knew he was being irrational and hypocritical, but he didnít care, ØBellatrix killed Sirius before my very eyes,Æ he said, sending Marvolo a look when it looked like the man was going to interrupt, ØI know you killed my parents and countless others and I've forgiven you, but sheè I never knew them not really, Sirius, he was my first taste at a family that loved me. When he died, I lost it, and she just laughed, she gloated and took such pleasure in it. She got off on my pain, on all the pain she causes. Unlike you she doesnít need a reason, she doesnít need a goal or purpose she just wants to hurt and destroy.Æ Marvolo had forgotten that part of his mates past. He could just imagine what such a loss would do to his raven. He would admit he wasnít overly fond of the loud and boisterous Lord Black but he could see how much he meant to his Harrison, how much they loved each other, his loss would without doubt cause his mate harm, something he wouldnít allow. He had enjoyed Bellatrix's madness last time, it had played well with his own, but he knew she wouldnít do well within his newly established ranks. There wasnít a place for such insanity anymore, still he wouldnít, leave her in Azkaban. No, if he must, he would release her even if it was just to put her out of her misery, she was his creation after all. ØI admit those like the Lestranges are not perhaps the best examples, however there are followers inside who I would like out and if I must, I will not hesitate to put an end to any who cause you harm or jeopardise what weíre buildingè but they're still mine, I canít leave them.Æ ØYou're jeopardising it yourself. How are you planning on getting them out without Dumbledore finding out about it, about you?Æ ØI've been consulting with Severus-Æ Ø-So, Severus knows?Æ Marvolo sent Harry a sharp look at the harsh interruption, Øyes. There are potions that allow the effects of Polyjuice to last for a month, its very hard to brew and the use of it is heavily restricted, but for one as skilled as Severus it wouldnít be too difficult to brew and have ready for me.Æ Øyou plan on what? Replacing your followers with people under this potion, surely the guards will notice when Bellatrix goes from raving mad to sobbing?Æ ØI have a plan for that, charms and compulsions, as well as releasing a deadly strain of wizarding flu. All of the imposters will succumb, and if a few others do as well then its no massive loss. The ministry wonít look into it as they will be too busy celebrating the death of such monsters.Æ Harry took a deep breath and counted to ten slowly in his head, Marvolo had clearly been think about this for a while if he already had everything planned out, a thought which hurt him as he thought they had agreed to tell each other everything. ØYouíve clearly though about this for a long time.Æ ØI have.Æ ØI wonít stand in your way, but I wont support you in this, not fully. I canít,Æ Harry could see the pain is words caused the older man, but he refused to stay and listen, turning he quickly walked out of the room. Not even noticing how his eyes filled with tears, too focused on the magic of his mate that seemed to cry out with longing as the distance between them grew with each hurried step. Chapter End Notes So... it's been a while. I hope you'll forgive me but a muse is such a fickle thing and i've all but lost mine in recent years, I'm hoping that i'll find it again soon considering I managed to drag this chapter out after over a year but no promises other than I do plan on finishing this fic. Blame her for what she has done, she isnít innocent, nor do I think she's sane, but she's not guilty of everything you hate her for.Æ Harry couldnít believe that out of everyone it was Sirius who was able to speak so rationally, Øyou think she deserves freedom?Æ Sirius bit his lip, since finding out everything from Harry's previous life, to Dumbledore's manipulations, he had been revaluating a lot of things, ØI donít know. I think that she deserves to suffer for her crimes, but I can safely say she has done so in Azkaban, that place, that place is hell Harry, being thereè being there for so long is punishment enough.Æ ØHe says itís his duty, that they pledged themselves to him so he must save them.Æ ØHe's not wrong pup. Their actions, although atrocious, were done in his name. He deserves to free them, even if its to punish them, for they are reflections of him. Their actions are his, just as their lives belong to him.Æ Again, Harry felt unmoored at the wise words, wondering where his dog-father was as this man was too serious. ØI know, but he's changed. He's not the Lord they swore to, they offer us nothing but potential problems, itís too risky.Æ Sirius grinned, although it was weak, Øwhatís a little risk?Æ Huffing Harry pulled away, ØI donít understand why he canít see why this is such a bad idea, we donít need people like Bellatrix.Æ ØPerhaps not, but he is their lord therefore he has a duty to them. A duty that you will come to understand yourself one day from what I've seen.Æ /// Marvolo felt the bond between himself and his little raven quiver with emotion he couldnít quite name, but suspected was heartbreak. The sight of emerald green eyes filling with tears played on repeat in his head, tearing away at him. He had known his little mate wouldnít be happy with the decision he had made, but he hadn't realised the true extent until he had seen the tears in his eyes. A hesitant knock echoed through the room. ØMy Lord,Æ Lucius called out, his voice drawing Marvolo's attention. ØEnter.Æ The blonde lord entered the room with Severus following close behind, both men looking uncertain. ØMy Lord is everything okay?Æ the blonde asked after a few moments of hesitation. Marvolo sighed and motioned for the two men to sit, both looking more than slightly uncomfortable. ØHarrison is upset with me,Æ he said, finding a measure of satisfaction in how uneasy they looked hearing those words. ØMy Lord?Æ Severus eventually said, his tone a question after a few moments of silence. ØHe disagrees with my plans for our brothers and sisters in Azkaban.Æ Severus sighed, his initial reservations towards his lordís return had been put to rest yet he too worried about releasing those imprisoned. Still his lord was nothing if not persuasive and his plan was solid, Severus could see it working. ØDid you explain it fully?Æ ØNot in explicit detail but enough that Harrison knows the plan.Æ ØWhat will you do?Æ Marvolo sighed, he didnít know what to doè he wanted to curse somethingè to go find his mate and demand he stop feeling such sadness, such emotionè he wanted his little raven here with him, happy and safe. ØI will let him calm down; I can feel he is still upset.Æ Severus sighed, ØMy Lord if you would, allow me to go to himè I think I can explain the plan fully for you without making the situation worse.Æ Marvolo snarled internally, how dare his follower think he would do better in regard to his mate, he should curse him, no kill him, make his beg for mercyè blinking those turbulent thoughts away, he turned to Severus. ØWhy would you think that?Æ he asked, with a tone conveying his displeasure. Severus gulped, ØI too initially held reservations, I think I can empathise with him and make him see that this is for the best.Æ Marvolo hummed, he could see the logic in the actions. Still, he didnít like it, but he was self-aware enough to realise it was the fact that he didnít want anybody else comforting his mate that he didnít like, not so much Severusís plan itself. ØVery well, you may go,Æ he commanded, Øbut Severus, if you make the situation worse you will regret it.Æ Severus stood and bowed, simultaneously relieved and worried. /// Severus didnít quite know what to expect when entering Grimmauld Place, but an almost frantic animagus was not it. Sirius looked wrecked, his grey eyes wild and hair in disarray. ØWhat are you doing here?Æ the Black Lord hissed quietly when he noticed the visitor. The volume, Severus realised, was probably due to the sleeping young wizard laid out on the couch. Whom, judging by the tear tracks on his face, had cried himself to exhaustion. ØMy Lord told me what had occurred, I thought I would come and see if I could help Harrison.Æ Sirius growled at that, ØCome to grovel like a dog for your lord?Æ he scoffed, lashing out as he was still wound up from hearing his sonís earlier cries. Severus sneered, ØI think we both know who out of the two of us is the dog.Æ Sirius went to respond, a scathing reply on the tip of his tongue, when a sob like breath caught his attention. Harrison woke to hissed whisperers, before grey eyes filled his vision. ØHarry, sweetheart, are you okay now?Æ Sirius whispered, hands hovering anxiously over his body. Harry nodded as he sat up, his eyes and face felt crusty, and his head was throbbing. ØI feel better, I apologise for getting so worked up,Æ he said, waving his hand to cast a refreshening charm over his face. Sirius shook his head, Øyou donít have to apologise pup.Æ Still, Harry scowled when he thought about his reaction, he had been so... uncontrolledè ØYour dog-father is right, you did nothing wrong Mr. Potter.Æ Hearing another voice Harry turned his head and finally noticed that Severus was also in the room. ØAs I had just explained to your father, I am here because I thought I could perhaps shed some light on the situation you have found yourself in.Æ Harry nodded, ØI shouldnít have left like I did, I should have at least heard him out. But I couldnít, I just felt overwhelmed and betrayed. It was too much; I knew if I didnít leave, I would have done or said something I would have regretted.Æ Severus tilted his head, ØI wonder,Æ he muttered seemingly to himself. ØOh?Æ Harry asked. ØMy Lord is, without question the sanest he has been in a long time. Yet I wonder if your connection perhaps attributed to yourè shall we say, emotive response. You are, I have noticed, not usually prone to extreme reactions.Æ Harry tilted his head, in his last life he had experienced visions and pain from the horcrux within is scar. It was possible, he supposed, that as Marvolo rebuilt his occulmency shields and regained his former power and skills, that his control could slip and as a result his emotions could influence Harry's own. Severus continued, Øthere is a lot not known about the bond between soul mates, and although my Lord is regaining his sanity more with each passing day, I wonder if perhaps being in close proximity to him is affecting you.Æ Harry could help but be impressed with the manís reasoning, he didnít know about the horcrux Harry held, but still came close to the truth, Øyou may be right professor. Still, I should apologise to him, I canít believe I just walked out, it must have hurt him.Æ Severus swallowed his urge to scoff, his lord even having feeling that could be hurt was till a notion that made him uncomfortable. ØI am sure my lord has already forgiven you if you did upset him, I doubt there is anything you could do that he would not forgive.Æ Harry nodded, allowing a thoughtful frown to appear on his face, Ønot that I donít appreciate the sentiment professor, but was there anything else you wanted?Æ ØMy Lord mentioned your reservations. No, this time he would give in to the hunger that had been steadily growing within himself. /// Opening the door Harry quickly took in the scene; Luna huddled in a ball on the floor, covered in blood and weakly whimpering as the savage bitch who had stalked him beat her like an animal. As soon as he realised his eyes were not lying him his magic exploded from him, lifting the redheaded witch from the ground and smacking her into the wall with a sickening crunch. Luna weakly looked at her lord, and smiled, ignoring the blood covering her teeth as she did so. Harry crouched down and gently caressed her face, Øoh my little moon, what did she do to you?Æ Øitís okay my lord. Iím okay.Æ Harry closed his eyes as he fought his anger, Luna had allowed herself to be in this potion as she thought it was necessary, Øyouíre not okay little moon. ØWhy was this necessary my little moon?Æ ØThe potion she had brewed, if it had worked you would have been lost. Even if we had recovered you, the damage would have been catastrophic. This week I discovered, as many of you are may now be aware, our very own Hogwarts School of Witchcraft and Wizardry will be hosting the Tri-Wizard Tournament. The competition, which challenges young witches and wizards making them compete in daring tasks, was discontinued in 1792, due to the extraordinarily high death toll of the competitors. Why our beloved headmaster had decided to bring it back I donít know, but that isnít the real story here. No, our story starts with how the competitors are chosen, which is via an ancient magical artifact, The Tri-Wizard Cup. Due to the aforementioned death toll, it was decided that only of-age competitors would take part this year. However, that didnít stop our young hero Harry Potter-Blackís name being called as Hogwarts champion. Heir Potter-Black, as many of us know, is only 14, yet somehow his name was selected by the cup. Now before any of you get upset, thinking the young man cheated, rest assured he didnít. No, when his name was called, heir Potter-Black refused to join. He was even forced to call on Lady Magic, in order to punish the person who entered him, as the headmaster was insistent on him competing even when it was clear to all that the young man didnít want to. And who was the despicable person who Lady Magic punished, you ask? why none other than the youngest child of the Weasley family, Ronald Weasley. Rest assured Heir Potter-Black will not be competing, as the punishment chosen by Lady Magic, quite justly in my opinion, was to make Mr Weasley compete instead. Why Mr Weasley would do something so horrific to one of his classmates isnít clear, and neither he nor the headmaster are willing to make a comment. Trilling happily to him and rubbing itself against him. He was not a bird damn it! ØI need to go, my Lord needs to know of my success. Off with you,Æ he said, shooing it away. The bird seemed to wilt slightly, unsure why his saviour didn't want him. ØHarrison and my lord will no doubt-Æ before he could continue the bird trilled excitedly and flamed out. Lucius stood for a moment before cursing. Feeling a new weight land on his shoulder, he turned his head and saw Fawkes was now sitting on him. Eyes widening he looked at the bird, fawkes was probably not long of a burning day judging by the worn look to his feathers. ØHello,Æ he said lightly, smiling softly as the firebird trilled excitedly, rubbing his face against his cheek. Fortunately Harrison gave me prior warning, such a considerate young wizard.Æ Marvolo inclined his head, he didn't truly understand the relationship his mate had with the goblins, but he appreciated it and although it might not show, he did respect the goblins themselves, Øhe is a true embodiment of the phrase, åones better halfí.Æ The goblin let out a rough laugh at that, Øat least you know your place.Æ The gathered Death Eaters all paused, some offended at the insult to their chosen lord, but the majority more fascinated by this new aspect of him they had never before seen. They had all had the chance to meet the boy known as Harry Potter, and they could all agree that their lord was lucky to have a mate with such potential. ØShall we get on to business my lord?Æ Lucius asked, ever the consummate businessman, after it became clear their lord was not offended. ØIndeed,Æ Marvolo agreed, settling in to read about his faithful followers' new identities. /// Harry returned to Malfoy manor alone after finishing at the ministry, Sirius going to Grimmauld to check in with Remus, only to find an almost tangible air of excitement. Marvoloís followers, who had steadily been recovering, were practically vibrating as they moved between themselves talking animatedly. Seeing his mate standing to the side nursing a glass of wine, Harry wove between the crowd, nodding in greeting to those who greeted him. ØIt went well then?Æ he asked as he approached. Marvolo opened his arms, allowing his raven to bury himself into his side, as always them being together soothed their souls and caused them both to relax, Øa veritable success.Æ ØI'm glad,Æ Harry replied, relishing in how their magic connected without conscious thought or effort, Øhow has Barty been?Æ Barty had only joined them days earlier, Harry hadn't even known he was at the manor until this morning when the man had appeared at breakfast. He had had to quickly penned a message to Ragnok to give him at least a small amount of warning when he had discovered Lucius had managed to recover the man. ØHe seems to be enjoying the freedom found being away from his fathers tender mercy,Æ Marvolo commented, indicating with his glass over to where the man was currently engaged in a whispered conversation with a blushing Rabastan Lestrange. Watching their interaction Harry let out a wistful sigh, Øwere they together before your fall?Æ he asked. Marvolo hummed, ØI didn't have the capacity to see it, but I think so, they were also very close with Evan Rosier.Æ ØHe was killed by Moody, right?Æ ØFrom what i've been told, though he took down several others with him.Æ ØWhat of his father, Crouch Sr?Æ Harry asked, drawing the conversation back to Barty who was clearly enjoying himself if the satisfied look on his face was any indication. ØBartemius Crouch Sr is currently under the imperious, and is to act as he always hasè for now.Æ ØHow did Barty take that decision?Æ ØRevenge will be his once our plans are finalised, for now he understands the need for subtly. They all do.Æ ØNo more war,Æ Harry warned. ØWhat need do we have for war, my soul? when the world will be ours for the taking without it.Æ ØSuch confidence, it truly must have gone very well for you today.Æ ØOur greatest enemy lies powerless in a hospital bed, magically crippled and reputation destroyed, guarded by the very people who once worshipped him. My followers have all been given a chance to one day rejoin society freely, and you my raven, are here in my arms. So, youíll have to forgive me for my confidence but today is a good day.Æ Harry hummed, reaching out with his magic he basked in their combined power. After a few moments enjoying the feel he withdrew his magic, before deciding on a while to steal the half full wine glass from Marvoloís hand and took a deep swallow, pulling a face as the flavours did not agree with his palate. ØI feel the need to remind you that you're currently underage,Æ Marvolo commented, accepting the glass back and draining it of its contents, making a point to drink from the same spot as Harrison, enjoying the dark look the younger sent him. ØAnd you say I'm the one testing you?Æ ØPayback darling, is a bitch, or so I'm told.Æ Shaking his head in amusement Harry looked around the room, ØToday may be a win, but I doubt it's over yet, Albus will have back up plans, he's not one to give up or go down without a fight.Æ ØOf that I have no doubt, but for today we shall relish in our victory.Æ /// Consciousness did not come gently to him as Albus awoke with a cry of pain, a deep soul searing emptiness filled him and he couldn't help but wish for the oblivion that unconsciousness offered. ØMr Dumbledore?Æ a stern voice asked, drawing his attention from his pain, Øare you with us?Æ ØWhere?Æ he asked, forcing himself to focus on where he was.. He had 45 minutes to get to the ministry before he would be barred entry to the emergency meeting. Quickly entering their ensuite, he washed and dressed in the ghastly robes provided to members of the Wizengamot before speedily walking to the floo. Entering the ministry, he saw a sea of puce robes indicating that most of the lords and ladies on the Wizengamot had managed to get in on time. ØDo you think if you-know-who takes over he can get these ugly things scrapped?Æ a petulant voice whined to him, drawing his attention to Sirius who looked to be half asleep. Lucius hated to agree with him butè Øone can only hope.Æ ØShall I add it to my list?Æ The question caused both men to jump. ØLord Gaunt,Æ Lucius greeted, before steering their trio over to where the dark faction had congregated. After accepting their greeting, many which bordered on fanatical, Marvolo briefly explained why the meeting had been called, watching as their faces morphed for shock, to disgust before settling on rage. ØI've always said it, but now he's proven it. The trial will go ahead in one week. That gives time for the accused to gather evidence and prepare his defence.Æ Marvolo hummed, a week, one week and then Albus would be ruined, one week and then he could make a start on the final stages of his plans. /// Albus couldn't believe what he was hearing. ØDo you understand the charges against you?Æ Amelia repeated. ØYou're making a grave mistake my dear girl,Æ he warned, how had it come to this? As long as you're still you then it doesn't matter, all I care about is your happiness and safety.Æ Harry blinked back the tears that had gathered in his eyes, internally cursing his ridiculous teenage body. He had noted recently that his emotions were more erratic, he had been utilising him occlumency more often, and while he would love to blame his bond with Marvolo, he was pretty sure it wasn't all his fault, but rather an unfortunate mixture of both teenage hormones as well as formally insane soul mate. ØStill, I will write to the twins a few suggestions, they've already lost their mother, at least as far as their relationship is concerned, I don't want them to lose their father as well. /// Albus stared at the ceiling of his room, he had counted the ceiling tiles 12 times as a way to keep himself busy after his repeated attempts at talking to his guards had resulted in them threatening to silence him. Still he rallied and was about to try overpowering them when he felt itè A static in the air, the type of sensation you feel moments before electricity strikes. Sitting up as much as he was able with his bindings on, he noticed the wary looks of the guards, they too had clearly felt the same thing as him. Øwhatè?!Æ the younger one said before slumping over with a flash of red light, his colleague spun in alarm before joining him on the floor. Albus eyed the room suspiciously, unsure if those who had attacked his guards were friends or foes, he wouldn't put it past Tom to take advantage of his current situation. ÆWhoè?Æ he started to say, but he words died in his mouth as young Nymphadora Tonks appeared from under an invisibility cloak with her mentor Hestia Jones at her side. ØGreat Circe Albus, how dare they treat you like this?!Æ came another voice that filled the headmaster with Glee as Dedalus Diggle appeared. ØThey're simply misguided souls, they have fallen under the sway of the dark, Ø he offered as the two witches set about freeing him of his bindings. ØStill, to treat you like a common criminal after all you have done for usè it's a disgrace!Æ his old friend ranted as he sent spells at the guards, ones Albus recognised as memory alterations and sleeping charms. Once free, Albus felt a moment of panic as his magic was still gone, though he tried not to show it, he had hoped the bindings were magic suppression but based on how sluggish and drained he felt, he suspected that his leeches had fallen and he was once again powerless. ØCome, we must move quickly,Æ Diggle warned. ØDo you have a plan?Æ Albus asked as he approached him, taking the offered cloak with a look of barely concealed disgust, once upon a time he wouldn't have needed such cheap tricks. ØA safe house with others who support you,Æ the other man offered, Øwe won't let the dark forces besmirch your name and lead us to anarchy.Æ ØWe need to go now sir, the enchantment I cast on the door won't last for long,Æ Nymphadora interrupted hesitantly. Albus cast a look over the young Metamorphmagus, when she had been a student he had taken her under his wing and helped steer her towards the light, even introducing her to Hestia so she could be taken under the older witches wing. She was clearly nervous, her hair a ghostly white instead of one of the usual vibrant colours she seemed to favour. Still that she had chosen to join him, to fight for the light was a good sign. It seemed not all was lost. ØThen let's take our leave,Æ Albus said, waving his arm towards the door, he would have to follow their lead until he managed to sort out his magic issues. /// Chapter End Notes Still alive, just very busy & not thriving Chapter48 Chapter Notes I am alive Harry Potter and the Shadowed Light Chapter 48 ~ Previously ØWe need to go now sir, the enchantment I cast on the door won't last for long,Æ Nymphadora interrupted hesitantly. Albus cast a look over the young Metamorphmagus, when she had been a student he had taken her under his wing and helped steer her towards the light, even introducing her to Hestia so she could be taken under the older witches wing. If not, then he was more than happy to assist them beyond this mortal plane. After washing and dressing, he entered the Malfoys sitting room, not surprised to see the Head of the DMLE was sitting nursing a large cup of coffee. ØApologies for helping myself but Iím running on fumes,Æ the woman said without preamble. Marvolo waved a hand in the face of her words. He didnít mind. He could imagine her workload had multiplied drastically in recent days, ØI do not mind, I can only imagine the stress youíre currently under.Æ Marvolo was also keenly aware that in the manor alone there were countless criminals who had yet to change their features to match their new goblin made identities, so allowing the woman a cup of coffee seemed like a small price considering if she knew just who shared this roof with her she would probably pass out. Amelia felt her face tighten, she was planning to go home and sleep after this but for how long she didnít know, it felt like she hadnít had more than 3 hours of consecutive sleep since the night of the ball. With each new discovery she felt a part of her shatter, she was ashamed that Dumbledore had managed to trick her and her staff for so long. She had been blind to his manipulations and treacherous ways. Draining the rest of her cup she placed it down and looked at the young lord before her. Why he was the target of Dumbledore's delusions she didnít know but she felt a measure of pity for him. He hasnít been here for long from what background work she had done on him, and honestly, she wouldnít blame him for leaving back to where he came from as the current situation left much to be desired. ØIím not one to mince words, and usually I would sit on this information, but in light of everything I felt it important for you to know. You will regret standing against the light. I wonít let you win, Voldemort. The light will triumph. ~ ØYou think he will attack me?Æ Marvolo asked faux calmly, juxtaposing the overwhelming rage swirling within him. How dare the old goat threaten his soulmate? Oh he would make the fool pray for death for the audacity. ØBased on his previous actions I wouldnít put it past him,Æ Amelia confirmed, looking for a sign of the darkness she had seen just moments ago, but not finding it. She wondered briefly if in her current exhausted state she had simply imagined it. ØI see,Æ he replied, he prayed the man would come, how he longed to put him in his rightful place. ØI have to ask, are you seeing somebody romantically at the moment? as the note also threatens them.Æ ØNo, I am still new to England and although I have found friends I have yet to explore romance,Æ he lied smoothly. Rage building at the reminder of the treat to Harrison. Amelia nodded, she didnít quite believe the man, but she wouldnít push. A part of her prayed Dumbledore didnít mean Harrison; their dancing at the ball had made it into the papers and honestly, she wouldnít put it past the man in his delusions to go after the teen again. Still, to call a child a whoreè she hoped Dumbledore hadnít fallen so far. ØI can have aurors set to guard you, although I doubt you will accept such an offer.Æ ØAs generous as it is, you are correct. Their time will be better spent trying to find the madman. I am currently staying here while my own home is under construction and I am not often out in public.Æ Amelia sighed, Øif you change your mind please donít hesitate to contact my officeè with his recent actions I wouldnít put it past him to do something drastic.Æ /// Harry knew before he even woke up that something had happened, the anger flaring through his bond with Marvolo tipping him off before his mind had even cleared of his dreams. A true supporter. Diggle had passion and his voice on the Wizengamot still held some sway among the light families - but his age was fast catching up to him. Yet, even his connections to the shadier side of magical society had seemingly dried up, nobody had time for a low level thief. Peter was, as always, a disappointment. No, he would find no allies in Hogwarts. And so many of his once allies had simply perished in the former war, so many good soldiers lost to the dark forces. Heíll be in the stands, observing, his guard will be down. That is our opening.Æ Fletcher rubbed his hands together. ØAnd the wards? The spectators? The Aurors? Weíll have to move fast.Æ Albusí lip curled. ØI have calculated every variable. No detail overlooked. We will use the chaos of the task to our advantage, and the boy will beè removed before anyone notices.Æ Tonks stood in the corner, arms crossed, jaw tight. Every syllable from Albus made her stomach twist. She had followed his orders before, reluctantly, but this - targeting a child, any child - felt wrong, felt like a line she could not cross. Enough was enough. Her mind raced, plans forming even as Albus spoke. She needed leverage, an angle to protect Harry without alerting Albus. She would not be complicit. Not this time. Not ever again. Albus turned suddenly, catching her gaze. ØTonks,Æ he said, sharp. ØYou are quiet. Speak. Do you understand your role?Æ ØYes,Æ she said softly, forcing calm into her voice. Inside, however, every nerve screamed in opposition. She would warn Harry. She would delay, misdirect, anything necessary. And if neededè she would betray Albus outright. ØGood,Æ he said, dismissively, returning to the map. ØThe final task is ideal. The chaos of the maze, the dragons, the cheering crowds, the championsí focus elsewhereè all perfect conditions. Harry Potter will be our prize. I want him in the dungeons before people even realise he's gone. Understood?Æ ØYes, sir,Æ Tonks whispered, voice tight. But her eyes were cold, determined. I wonít let you hurt him. Peter glanced nervously at her. ØButè the boy - heís never alone, always with other students.Æ ØNot always, and he is a student himself, which makes him practically defenseless,Æ Albus interjected. ØPerfectly vulnerable. We strike, remove him quietly, and our advantage is secured.Æ Tonksí hands clenched into fists. ØQuietly,Æ she echoed silently, her mind already racing with contingencies: a diversion, misdirection, a warning spellè anything to keep Harry safe without giving herself away too soon. Albus continued speaking, outlining ward dispersal, timing, escape routes, contingency plans. Every detail confirmed her worst fears. But it also gave her the information she needed. Her resolve hardened. I will not be a pawn in this. The final task would be the turning point. Not for Albus. Not for the Dark Lord. But for her, for Harry, and for the moment she decided that justice - and morality - trumped blind obedience. /// It was shocking how hard returning to Hogwarts was, each time it felt like leaving a piece of himself. In his last life, Hogwarts was his home, it called to him in a way that a lighthouse calls to a lost boat. Yet standing on the platform, Harrison had to force himself to walk away and get onto the train. ØIt's a few weeks, I will attend the second task,Æ Marvolo had promised when they said their goodbyes last night. Harrison felt changed since Yule, since marking Neville and Luna, since the night he spent with Marvolo in the Malfoy Dungeons. Settled, yet somehow alight with magic, he could feel it under his skin, stronger, darker, yet more his. ØCome on my lord,Æ Luna murmured, steering him onto the train. The compartment they found was large, with enough space for everybody and then some. Pulling out a book on runes, Harrison settled in and started to read as the noise grew - people saying their goodbyes, searching for friends, lamenting about returning to school. Soon enough their compartment filled- Draco was quickly talking to Hannah and Susan about who wore what to the ministry's annual Yule ball, Neville and Pansy were studying together .or rather Neville was telling Pansy about the plants he had received and the dark haired Slytherin was taking notes on what looked conspicuously like their herbology assignment. The twins, Blaise and Luna were gossiping about something regarding a rumbled looking professor Sinista leaving a broom cupboard not long after Professor Babbling. Time passed quickly like this, all of them for a moment simply existing, no worries, only friendship. Soon Harrison found himself with the twins. ØSoèÆ ØKarkaroff,Æ they asked. Harrison hummed. Thinking back to what they had done. What he had done. /// The dungeons beneath Malfoy Manor were never silent. Stone remembered screams the way skin remembered scars, and the air itself seemed to breathe, damp and expectant. Torches burned low along the walls, their flames steady despite the absence of any draft, as though the magic here did not permit flickering uncertainty. Igor Karkaroff had hung suspended by enchanted chains, wrists above his head, boots barely brushing the flagstones. His once-fine robes were torn, stained dark in places where spells had already found their mark. What remained of his bravado had long since drained away, leaving only the sharp, uneven rise and fall of his chest. Marvolo had stood before him, hands folded neatly behind his back, posture relaxed in a way that made the scene infinitely worse. His expression was calm - almost bored - but his magic pressed outward like a coiled serpent, heavy enough that Karkaroff whimpered without understanding why. Harrison had lingered a step behind and to Marvoloís right, shadows clinging to him as though they had chosen him deliberately. His face was composed, eyes bright with an eerie, thoughtful light. There was no hesitation in him. No uncertainty. ØYou ran,Æ Marvolo had said softly, his voice carrying effortlessly in the stone chamber. ØYou hid, you betrayed us, betrayed me.Æ Karkaroff swallowed. ØM-my Lord-Æ The word earned him a flick of Marvoloís fingers. The chains tightened. Not enough to break bone - Marvolo was far too precise for that - but enough to force a scream from Karkaroffís throat, raw and uncontrolled. The sound echoed, then faded, absorbed hungrily by the walls. Harrison watched closely. Not with cruelty for crueltyís sake, but with the quiet attention of someone learning the shape of a thing they had already decided to master. ØYou sold names,Æ Harrison said, his tone almost conversational. ØLives. Families. You bartered loyalty like coin and called it survival.Æ Karkaroffís head lolled forward, sweat slicking his temples. ØI did what I had to-Æ Harrison stepped closer. The air shifted. Whatever he did did not leave marks that could be easily named. There was no flash, no incantation spoken aloud. Just a subtle tightening in the world, a pressure that crawled beneath the skin and sank deep, searching for every regret, every memory Karkaroff had buried to sleep at night. He screamed again. Marvolo had closed his eyes briefly, savoring the resonance of it - not the sound, but the truth behind it. Fear unfiltered. Guilt stripped bare. ØYou always were disappointing,Æ Marvolo murmured. ØToo weak to be loyal. Too proud to be honest about it.Æ Harrison withdrew the pressure slowly, deliberately, leaving Karkaroff shaking, breath stuttering as if his body had forgotten the rhythm of living. ØHe doesnít deserve a quick end,Æ Harrison said quietly. Marvolo had opened his eyes and looked at him then - really looked. At the certainty in his posture. At the way his magic hummed in perfect harmony with the wards etched into the stones. ØNo,Æ Marvolo agreed. ØHe doesnít.Æ Time lost meaning after that. There were screams that dissolved into hoarse sobbing. Questions asked not for answers, but to hear the breaking voice that followed. Magic layered carefully, expertly - never enough to kill, always enough to remind Karkaroff that death was a mercy being deliberately withheld. Eventually, there was nothing left to take. Karkaroff sagged in his chains, unconscious or something close to it, his mind shattered beyond repair. Marvolo stepped back at last, satisfaction settling over him like a well-fitted cloak. He turned to Harrison, eyes dark and intent. ØYou didnít have to bring him to me yourself,Æ he said. Harrison met his gaze, unflinching. ØI wanted to.Æ For a moment, the dungeons had felt very still. Marvolo had reached out, resting his hand at the nape of Harrison's neck - possessive, reverent, unmistakably intimate. His thumb had brushed warm skin, grounding, grateful. ØThank you,Æ he said softly. ØFor my gift.Æ Harrison had smiled. / // ØKarkaroffèÆ Harrison began, then paused, clearly considering his words, Ølet's say that Drumstrang will be looking for a new headmaster.Æ The twins nodded, taking in the way that Harrison held himself as he spoke. Fred noticed it first. He didnít say anything at the time - just tilted his head, the way he did when something didnít quite fit the pattern but felt important enough to store away. Harrison was sitting by the window, half-turned toward the light, magic humming around him like a low note only certain people could hear. George felt it a heartbeat later. They always did things like that. Noticing the silence where there should have been restlessness. The way the air leaned toward him. The way people unconsciously angled their bodies, listening before heíd even spoken. He wasnít trying to command the room. That was the strange part. He was justè being. ØYou see it too, donít you?Æ George murmured, not looking away. Fred snorted softly. ØThought I was imagining it at first. Figured Iíd finally lost what little grip on reality I had left.Æ They drifted back to their corner of the compartment. Watching as Harrison laughed at something Blaise said, the sound was warm and real - it didnít feel as forced as it used to. It didnít feel like a mask, or a shield hurriedly slapped on to hide the cracks underneath. ØHeís different,Æ George said finally. ØYeah,Æ Fred agreed. ØBut not in a årun for your lifeí sort of way.Æ George smirked. ØMore like a åstand very still and pay attentioní sort of way.Æ There was power there now. Not the loud, showy kind that needed spells and sparks and shouting to be convincing. This was quieter. Heavier. Like gravity had decided Harrison was worth orbiting. Fred frowned, thoughtful. ØYou ever get the feeling heís more than he lets on?Æ George hummed. ØDefinitely.Æ ØReckon we should be worried?Æ George watched Harrison for a long moment. The boy - no, their lord, if they were being honest - reached out to steady Neville as he passed, fingers brushing his sleeve in a way that was instinctive, grounding. Protective. ØNo,Æ George said easily. ØNot even a little.Æ Fred nodded. ØYeah. Me neither.Æ Because there was something else threaded through the darkness that Harrison gave off. Something clean. Purposeful. Like shadow that existed only because the light was strong enough to cast it. ØHeís more settled,Æ Fred said slowly. ØLike he finally fits in his own skin.Æ ØLike he stopped trying to be what everyone told him he should be,Æ George added, Øand became what he was always meant to be instead.Æ They shared a glance, identical grins flickering between them - sharp, knowing, unafraid. ØDarker magic,Æ Fred said. ØLighter soul,Æ George replied. ØAnd somehow,Æ Fred finished, Øthat makes all the difference.Æ Harrison turned then, catching them staring. One eyebrow lifted in mild curiosity, a ghost of a smile tugging at his mouth. The twins didnít look away. Didnít bow. Didnít joke. They simply inclined their heads - just enough to be deliberate. Harrison's smile softened, something like understanding passing between them. George exhaled, satisfied. ØGlad we chose him.Æ Fred grinned wider. ØBest decision weíve made all year.Æ /// The Great Hall had not felt this unsettled in decades. The candles still floated overhead, the enchanted ceiling still mirrored a winter-dark sky dusted with stars, and yet something fundamental had shifted. Conversations were muted, laughter restrained, eyes darting toward the High Table far more often than usual. Minerva McGonagall rose from her seat. The sound was small - just the scrape of chair legs against stone - but it carried. One by one, voices fell silent until the Hall was hushed, hundreds of students turning their attention toward the witch standing straight-backed in emerald robes, tartan clasp gleaming at her throat. She did not smile. ØStudents,Æ she began, her voice clear and steady, carrying effortlessly to the farthest corners of the Hall, ØI will not insult your intelligence by pretending you are unaware that recent events haveè unsettled our school.Æ A ripple of movement followed - heads nodding, shoulders stiffening. ØIn light of the attack at the Triwizard Yule Ball,Æ she continued, expression hardening, Øand the subsequent issuance of an arrest warrant for Albus Dumbledore, I will be acting as Headmistress of Hogwarts School of Witchcraft and Wizardry until further notice.Æ The words landed like a dropped plate. Whispers burst forth instantly, restrained but urgent. Arrest warrant-She said attack-On Harrison Potter-Black and Lord Gaunt-I knew something was wrong with Dumbledore-McGonagall raised a single hand. Silence followed immediately. ØI am aware that many of you are frightened,Æ she said, and this time her tone softened just a fraction. ØLet me be absolutely clear: Hogwarts remains safe. The wards are intact, strengthened, and monitored constantly. Aurors are posted discreetly. Professors are on heightened alert. No student will be left unprotected under my watch.Æ Her gaze swept the Hall, sharp and unyielding. ØI promise you this.Æ There was a collective exhale, the kind that came from people who hadnít realized theyíd been holding their breath. ØAnd now,Æ McGonagall said, straightening once more, Øthere is another matter which must be addressed.Æ The Beauxbatons and Durmstrang tables visibly stilled. ØIt has come to the attention of the Ministry and the International Confederation of Wizards that the Headmaster of Durmstrang Institute has gone missing.Æ The Hall erupted. ØMissing-?Æ ØWhen-?Æ ØGone how-?Æ McGonagall allowed the noise to crest before continuing, her eyes flicking briefly toward the pale-faced Durmstrang students. ØAt present, there is no evidence of foul play,Æ she said carefully, a phrasing that did little to calm anyone. ØHowever, given the current circumstances, we are treating his disappearance with the utmost seriousness.Æ Whispers spread like wildfire. ØBet it was Dumbledore-Æ ØHeís already attacked people-Æ ØWhat if heís going after anyone connected to the Tournament-Æ The Beauxbatons students leaned close together, elegant composure fraying at the edges. Several Durmstrang students looked outright shaken, faces tight with worry, hands clenched around goblets or the edges of the table. ØIf any student,Æ McGonagall continued firmly, Øhas information -no matter how small- that may be relevant, I urge you to come forward. You may speak to myself, your Head of House, or the Auror liaison assigned to the school. You will not be punished for honesty.Æ Her gaze lingered on the Durmstrang students, not unkindly. In particular their champion, Victor looked stricken. ØYou are guests here,Æ she said. ØAnd you are under Hogwartsí protection.Æ That, more than anything, seemed to steady them. McGonagall drew herself to her full height, shoulders squared. ØHogwarts has endured wars, dark lords, and betrayals before,Æ she said quietly. ØIt will endure this as well. Classes will continue. Meals will continue. Your education will not be disrupted.Æ Her eyes hardened, steel beneath the silk. ØAnd anyone who believes this school to be vulnerable would do well to remember exactly who stands guard over it.Æ She inclined her head once. ØThank you. You are dismissed to finish your meal.Æ As she sat, the Hall erupted again - this time louder, sharper, buzzing with fear, speculation, and a single unspoken thought threading through it all: Change was coming. /// The infirmary was too white. Clean. Pure. Ron felt dirty compared to it. Too open. Too bright. It made everything feel unreal, as though Ron might wake up any moment and discover the dragon had never been real, that the fire hadnít touched him, that the pain wasnít still sitting deep in his bones like something waiting to flare again. He lay propped against a mountain of pillows, staring at the ceiling as Madam Pomfrey fussed somewhere out of sight. His left arm was wrapped in layers of enchanted bandages, the skin beneath warped and tight, faintly shimmering where dragonfire had scorched more than flesh. His magic - what was left of it - felt thin. Threadbare. Like a jumper unravelled stitch by stitch. Dumbledore had destroyed him. Not Potter, not the Dark Lord, but Dumbledore. He tried to reach for his magic. Nothing answered. Ron swallowed hard and turned his head toward the window, where grey winter light pressed weakly against the glass. He felt hollow. Not just tired, but emptied, scraped out from the inside. He had believed. That was the worst part. He had believed in Dumbledore with the same certainty heíd once believed in Christmas morning, in Mumís hugs, in the idea that if you did the right thing long enough it would all work out in the end. Dumbledore had been the light, hadnít he? The good guy. The one who knew what he was doing. Now Ron knew better. The faith was gone, burned away as surely as his magic had been scorched. He could see the truth now, clear and ugly: heíd been used. Pushed. Set on a path that hadnít cared whether he survived it or not. His hand clenched weakly in the sheets. ØI was stupid,Æ he muttered, voice rough. The words felt heavy, but honest. The healers hadnít sugar-coated it. They never did. If he didnít compete - if he didnít finish the Tournament - his magic would continue to degrade. The binding had already been activated. Backing out now wouldnít save him. It would end him. A squib. A muggle. The thought used to terrify him. Nowè he wasnít sure. Ron closed his eyes, imagining a life without magic. No spells that failed him when he needed them most. No wands that burned his hand with recoil. No ancient tournaments, no dragons, no expectations he could never quite meet. Justè quiet. A job. A small flat. Maybe fixing things with his hands instead of magic. Maybe being normal wouldnít hurt so much. Maybe magic was the problem. It had promised him belonging, power, worth - and then turned its back on him the moment he faltered. The moment he trusted the wrong person. Dumbledore, with his twinkling eyes and promises of power, of being a hero. Oh, what a fool he had been, a stupid trusting fool. Was it worth it? Facing another task. Another creature. Another chance to lose what little he had left - or his life. Ron laughed softly, the sound brittle. ØWhat a joke,Æ he whispered. Compete and maybe die. Quit and definitely lose his magic. Either way, the boy heíd been - the one whoíd dreamed of being brave, of standing beside heroes - was already gone. He turned his face into the pillow, eyes burning, chest aching with grief that felt far too big for one person. For the first time in his life, Ron Weasley didnít know whether surviving was worth the cost. /// The Room of Requirement was one of Harrison's favourite places in the castle. It seemed to hum with magic, older and deeper than anywhere else, and as always when asked it answered. It had shaped itself into a circular chamber of smooth black stone veined with faint silver runes, the walls curving inward like a sanctuary rather than a classroom. Candles burned without wax, their flames steady and low, casting overlapping shadows that moved with purpose rather than chance. The air was thick with magic - old, patient, and attentive. Harrison stood at the center. Not a boy. Not anymore. He wore simple black robes, unadorned, his wand resting in his palm more as a focus than a necessity. Power coiled beneath his skin, familiar now, no longer startling when it answered him. He could feel the Room responding to that power, recognizing him. Waiting. Around him stood the others. Fred and George, uncharacteristically still, their usual irreverence replaced by something solemn and bright-eyed. Draco straight-backed and composed, silver-grey gaze sharp with anticipation. Blaise relaxed but alert, magic humming beneath his calm exterior. Pansy poised and unflinching, chin lifted in defiance of any world that might dare judge her. Hannah and Susan close together, hands linked, fear edged with resolve. Neville and Luna stood apart from the circle. Their marks - triskelions etched in magic rather than ink - glowed softly through fabric and skin alike, a quiet echo of what was to come. They were settled in themselves now, grounded in a way Harrison recognized intimately. He drew a slow breath. I was never this, he thought. In another life, he would have been shaking. Unsure. Waiting for permission from teachers who never truly saw him, clinging to a morality shaped by manipulation rather than understanding. He would have stumbled into greatness by accident and paid for it in blood and grief. A fool lead willingly to his own demise. That boy had survived by chance. But he had never lived. ØThis feels big,Æ George murmured, breaking the silence gently. ØNot in a terrifying way. More likeè history-making.Æ Fred grinned. ØNo pressure, Your Lordship.Æ Harrison huffed a soft laugh, then sobered again. His gaze flicked to Luna, who was watching him with her usual unsettling clarity, head tilted, eyes luminous. ØYouíre thinking too hard,Æ she said lightly. ØI usually do,Æ he admitted. She stepped closer, close enough that her presence brushed against his magic like moonlight on water. ØYou think youíve changed too much. That youíve strayed from who you were meant to be.Æ Harrison hesitated. ØI know Iím not who I was,Æ he said quietly. Luna smiled. ØYou never were.Æ Her words settled, not as a rebuke, but as a truth long waiting to be spoken. ØYou were always destined for greatness,Æ she continued softly. ØNot the loud kind. Not the kind that saves the world by accident. The kind that remakes it. ThisÆ - she gestured to the room, to the circle, to him - Øis simply you stepping into yourself.Æ He closed his eyes for a moment. When he opened them, the doubt was gone. ØStep forward,Æ Harrison said. They did, one by one, forming a ring around him. He raised his wand - not pointing, but offering - and magic unfurled from him in slow, deliberate waves. It did not burn. It did not cut. It wrote. The triskelion formed in the air between them: three interlocking spirals, motion eternal, balance in flux. Light, dark, and grey. Life, death, and rebirth. Past, present, and becoming. The ritual took shape, the words he had used once before falling from his lips and theirs. When the first mark settled - Fred, then George - it sank into skin like a remembered truth. Power answered power, sealing not ownership, but allegiance freely given. Draco followed, eyes steady. Blaise next, a quiet nod of respect. Pansy accepted hers with a sharp inhale and a fierce smile. Hannah trembled but did not falter. Susanís eyes shone with unshed tears as the magic embraced her. Each mark glowed brieflyîthen faded, settling deep. The Room thrummed. Harrison lowered his wand, breath steady, magic vast and contained. Once it was over a profound silence settled for a moment. ØI do not claim you,Æ he said, voice carrying without effort. ØYou stand with me by choice, bound by trust, not chains. We are not light or dark alone - but whole.Æ He straightened, spine aligning as something ancient and undeniable clicked into place. ØI am Harrison Potter-Black. Keeper of balance. Knowing everything the old man had built - his life, his reputation, his name - would soon be destroyed, was a balm to his broken soul. Marvolo knew it would happen - but - seeing it, being here to watch it, was a gift he felt almost unworthy of. He sat with an expression that was carved from stone, dark eyes cold as the memories played.
